oneSentenceDescription: U.S. commercial-scale solar owner-operator with more than 1 GW of operating assets across 26 states; Altus closed its transaction with TPG in April 2025 and now describes itself as a TPG Rise Climate portfolio company.
ownershipDetails: Firm: TPG | Fund: TPG Rise Climate Transition Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Altus Power develops, owns, and operates commercial-scale solar generation and related clean energy assets. Its customers include commercial and industrial users, schools, hospitals, municipalities, and community solar subscribers. The business follows an asset-heavy owner-operator model in which long-lived solar assets generate recurring contracted revenue. The company operates across the United States and public filings describe more than 1 GW of operating assets. TPG agreed to acquire Altus Power through TPG Rise Climate Transition Infrastructure in February 2025, and the transaction closed in April 2025.
historicalMilestones: 2010 - Altus energized its first solar system in Bridgeport, Connecticut.
2014 - Blackstone made its initial investment in Altus.
2015 - Altus completed its first community solar project in Massachusetts.
2021 - Altus listed on the New York Stock Exchange following a business combination.
2024 - Altus surpassed 1 gigawatt of operating assets.
2025-02-06 - TPG announced an agreement to acquire Altus Power.
2025-04-16 - TPG completed the acquisition of Altus Power.

oneSentenceDescription: South Florida-based telecom infrastructure platform that owns, operates, and manages tower and telecom real-estate assets across the United States, backed by up to $1 billion from TPG Peppertree.
profileSubsector: Wireless towers and telecom real estate
ownershipDetails: Firm: TPG Peppertree | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Connected Infra Group owns, operates, and manages tower and telecom real-estate assets. Its counterparties include wireless carriers, network operators, property owners, and municipalities. The platform follows an asset-heavy digital infrastructure model built around site ownership, leasing, and site management. The company is based in South Florida and is pursuing assets across the United States, although public disclosures do not specify current tower count. TPG Peppertree and Bernard Borghei announced the platform and up to $1 billion of investment capital in February 2026.
historicalMilestones: 2026-02-17 - TPG Peppertree and Bernard Borghei announced the formation of Connected Infra Group.
2026-02-17 - TPG Peppertree committed up to $1 billion of investment capacity to the platform.
2026-02-17 - Connected Infra Group launched as a South Florida-based telecom infrastructure platform.
2026-02-17 - Bernard Borghei was named Chief Executive Officer and Dan Marinberg Chief Legal Officer.

portfolioCompany: Digital Realty Northern Virginia Hyperscale Data Center JV
oneSentenceDescription: TPG Real Estate acquired an 80% stake in a three-asset Northern Virginia hyperscale data-center portfolio totaling roughly 104 MW of IT capacity in a joint venture with Digital Realty.
profileCompanyName: Digital Realty Northern Virginia Hyperscale Data Center JV
ownershipDetails: Firm: TPG Real Estate | Fund: TPG Real Estate Partners (TREP) | Ownership Interest: 80%
Firm: Digital Realty | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 20%
companyOverview: This joint venture owns three stabilized hyperscale data center buildings in Northern Virginia. The assets serve investment-grade hyperscale customers that require large-scale data center capacity in one of the largest data center markets in the United States. The venture is an asset-heavy leased infrastructure platform, and Digital Realty manages day-to-day operations. The portfolio is concentrated in Virginia and totals about 104 MW of IT capacity. TPG Real Estate acquired an 80% interest in July 2023, while Digital Realty retained a 20% stake.
historicalMilestones: 2023-07-27 - Digital Realty and TPG announced a Northern Virginia hyperscale data center joint venture.
2023-07-27 - TPG Real Estate agreed to acquire an 80% interest in the three-data-center portfolio.
2023-07-27 - Digital Realty retained a 20% interest and continued to manage the assets.
2023-07-27 - The transaction valued the portfolio at approximately $1.5 billion.

oneSentenceDescription: IPX was launched from Intersect’s grid-tied business as an independent power producer focused on co-located solar and battery storage, with 4.4 GW of solar PV and 8.8 GWh of battery storage serving utilities and other customers across California and Texas.
ownershipDetails: Firm: TPG | Fund: TPG Rise Climate | Ownership Interest: Majority
Firm: Climate Adaptive Infrastructure | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Greenbelt Capital Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: IPX Power is an independent power producer focused on developing, owning, and operating large-scale solar and battery storage assets. Its customers include utilities, community choice aggregators, large corporate buyers, and energy service providers. The company follows an asset-heavy generation model centered on grid-tied utility-scale projects rather than equipment manufacturing or pure development fees. Public disclosures describe a pipeline of 4.4 GW of solar photovoltaic capacity and 8.8 GWh of battery storage, with activity concentrated in California and Texas. The company was launched in 2026 from Intersect's grid-tied business following Google's acquisition of Intersect, with majority backing from TPG Rise Climate and additional support from Climate Adaptive Infrastructure and Greenbelt Capital Partners.
historicalMilestones: 2025-12-22 - Alphabet announced an agreement to acquire Intersect Power.
2026-03-10 - TPG announced the completion of the sale of Intersect Power to Google.
2026-03-10 - IPX Power launched as an independent power producer holding Intersect's grid-tied portfolio.
2026-03-10 - IPX launched with 4.4 GW of solar generation and 8.8 GWh of battery storage capacity.

oneSentenceDescription: Monarch develops farm-based renewable natural gas infrastructure in the U.S., converting methane from Smithfield hog farms into carbon-negative RNG, and its site lists operating projects in Missouri.
ownershipDetails: Firm: TPG | Fund: TPG Rise Climate | Ownership Interest: 33.3%
Firm: Roeslein Alternative Energy | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 33.3%
Firm: Smithfield Foods | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 33.3%
companyOverview: Monarch Bioenergy develops and operates farm-based renewable natural gas infrastructure that captures methane and upgrades it into pipeline-quality fuel. Its end markets include gas utilities, transportation fuel buyers, and other customers seeking lower-carbon gaseous fuel. The platform uses an asset-heavy operating model with digesters, gas collection systems, and upgrading equipment connected to livestock operations. Public disclosures identify operating projects in Missouri, and the company website lists nine operating projects in that state. Monarch was launched in 2018 by Roeslein Alternative Energy and a Smithfield Foods subsidiary, and TPG Rise Climate invested as an equal partner in March 2022.
historicalMilestones: 2018 - Monarch Bioenergy launched as a joint venture of Roeslein Alternative Energy and a Smithfield Foods subsidiary.
2019 - Monarch reported record-low CARB carbon intensity scores for RNG produced from hog waste.
2020 - Monarch began construction of a multi-state project pipeline.
2022-03-10 - TPG Rise Climate announced an equal-partner investment in Monarch Bioenergy.
2022 - Monarch reported 88 completed lagoons across nine hog farms.

oneSentenceDescription: Long Beach-based operator of two storage terminals, including one at the Port of Long Beach, plus an interconnected regional pipeline network that handles renewable fuels and refined products.
ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Olympus Terminals owns and operates storage terminals and related pipeline infrastructure for renewable fuels, refined products, crude oil, and chemical products. Its customers are fuel marketers, refiners, traders, and other shippers that need marine-access storage and regional product movements in Southern California. The company follows an asset-heavy midstream logistics model built around tank storage, dock access, and an interconnected pipeline network. Public disclosures describe two terminals in Carson and Long Beach, including a marine terminal at the Port of Long Beach. TPG Rise Climate announced an agreement to acquire the company from Davidson Kempner and Intrepid Investment Management in May 2024, but public materials reviewed do not disclose the current post-signing ownership structure.
historicalMilestones: 1984 - Olympus Terminals traces its operating history to 1984.
2021-12-20 - An investor group acquired Chemoil Terminals.
2021-12-20 - Following the acquisition, Chemoil Terminals was renamed Olympus Terminals.
2024-05-07 - TPG Rise Climate signed a definitive agreement to acquire Olympus Terminals.

oneSentenceDescription: Pike is a leading U.S. turnkey infrastructure engineering and construction provider for electric utilities, and TPG said in its February 2026 earnings call that it acquired a majority stake in Q4 2025.
profileSubsector: Electric and gas utility infrastructure services
principalLocationOfOperations: North Carolina and multi-state United States
ownershipDetails: Firm: TPG | Fund: TPG Rise Climate | Ownership Interest: Majority
Firm: La Caisse | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Significant minority
Firm: Pike management and other existing investors | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Pike Corporation provides engineering, construction, and maintenance services for electric and gas utilities and telecommunications customers. Its customer base includes utilities that outsource transmission, distribution, substation, storm response, and related field services. The business operates an asset-light services model supported by labor, equipment, and a nationwide field organization rather than a concession or merchant generation structure. Public disclosures cite about 12,000 employees and more than 400 customers across the United States. TPG Rise Climate announced a majority investment in November 2025, and TPG stated in February 2026 that the acquisition closed during the fourth quarter of 2025 with La Caisse as a significant minority partner.
historicalMilestones: 1945 - Pike Corporation was founded.
2002 - Lindsay Goldberg made its first investment in Pike Electric Corporation.
2005 - Pike completed its initial public offering.
2020-12-21 - Lindsay Goldberg affiliates acquired a majority interest in Pike.
2025-11-17 - TPG Rise Climate, La Caisse, and management announced an acquisition of Pike.
2025-12 - Lindsay Goldberg exited its remaining stake in Pike.

oneSentenceDescription: Summit is developing a Midwest carbon capture, transport, and storage system intended to capture and permanently store up to 20 million tons of CO2 annually, and TPG Rise Climate inves…
principalLocationOfOperations: Iowa, Minnesota, Nebraska, North Dakota, and South Dakota
ownershipDetails: Firm: TPG | Fund: TPG Rise Climate | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Tiger Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Continental Resources | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Summit Carbon Solutions is developing a carbon capture, transportation, and permanent sequestration system for ethanol plants and other industrial facilities in the Midwest. Its end market is the low-carbon fuels and industrial decarbonization value chain, with customers that need lower lifecycle carbon intensity and permanent CO2 storage. The company follows an asset-heavy infrastructure model built around pipeline transportation and geological sequestration rather than a services-only offering. Public disclosures describe a system of about 2,000 miles of pipeline designed to capture up to 20 million tons of carbon dioxide per year across Iowa, Minnesota, Nebraska, South Dakota, and North Dakota. TPG Rise Climate invested $300 million in May 2022 as part of Summit's more than $1 billion equity raise alongside existing sponsors including Tiger Infrastructure Partners and Continental Resources.
historicalMilestones: 2019-12-02 - Summit Agricultural Group announced the creation of Summit Carbon Solutions.
2021-04-20 - Tiger Infrastructure agreed to invest in Summit Carbon Solutions.
2022-05-10 - SK E&S agreed to acquire a 10% stake in Summit Carbon Solutions for $110 million.
2022-05 - Summit announced more than $1 billion of equity commitments, including $300 million from TPG Rise Climate.

oneSentenceDescription: U.S. utility holding company with natural gas distribution plus water and wastewater operations serving more than 233,000 customers.
profileSubsector: Natural gas distribution and water utilities
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ullico | Fund: UIF | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Hope Utilities is a West Virginia utility platform with natural gas distribution and water and wastewater operations. Its customers are residential, commercial, and industrial users, and public materials describe service to more than 233,000 utility customers across the platform. The business follows an asset-heavy regulated utility model with distribution mains, gathering and header pipelines, and local water and wastewater systems. Public disclosures state that Hope Gas serves more than 111,000 customers in 35 West Virginia counties and operates roughly 3,200 miles of mains plus about 2,000 miles of header and gathering lines. Ullico, through Hearthstone Utilities, signed an agreement to acquire Hope Gas from Dominion Energy in February 2022 and announced the closing in August 2022.
historicalMilestones: 1898 - Hope Gas traces its origins to 1898.
2020 - Hope acquired gathering assets from Dominion Energy Transmission.
2022-02 - Ullico signed an agreement to acquire Hope Gas from Dominion Energy.
2022-08-31 - Hearthstone closed the acquisition and rebranded the utility as Hope Gas.
2023-12 - Hope Gas acquired Southern Public Service Company.
2024-05-07 - Hope Gas agreed to acquire Consumers Gas Utility Company.

oneSentenceDescription: U.S. (Puerto Rico) toll-road concessionaire operating PR-22, PR-5, PR-52, PR-53, PR-66, and PR-20 under concession agreements with the Puerto Rico Highways and Transportation Authority.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Abertis Infraestructuras | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Ullico | Fund: UIF | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Metropistas is a Puerto Rico toll-road concessionaire that operates a network including PR-22, PR-5, PR-52, PR-53, PR-66, and PR-20 under concession agreements. Its customers are passenger and freight road users, and its end market is transportation access across the San Juan metropolitan area and the broader island road network. The company follows an asset-heavy concession model in which toll revenue supports operations, maintenance, and capital investment over a long concession term. Operations are concentrated in Puerto Rico, and public materials identify PR-22 and PR-5 as held under concession through 2061. Ullico invested in the concessionaire in 2020 alongside Abertis, while current ownership percentages disclosed in recent public materials are not available.
historicalMilestones: 2011-06-27 - The concession agreement for PR-22 and PR-5 was signed.
2020 - Ullico closed an investment in Metropistas alongside Abertis.
2023-10-17 - Metropistas reached commercial close on the concession for PR-20, PR-52, PR-53, and PR-66.
2023-12-14 - Financial close was completed on the additional Puerto Rico toll roads concession.

oneSentenceDescription: U.S. airport-terminal public-private partnership in which UIF invested as an equity sponsor to develop JFK’s proposed 2.4 million-square-foot New Terminal One.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ferrovial Airports | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: JLC Infrastructure | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Ullico | Fund: UIF | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Carlyle | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: JFK New Terminal One is the public-private partnership developing and operating a new international terminal at John F. Kennedy International Airport. Its end customers are airlines and air passengers using New York's international gateway infrastructure. The project follows an asset-heavy concession and terminal operating model rather than an asset-light services model. The development is located in Queens, New York, and public disclosures describe a planned 2.4 million-square-foot terminal with $5.9 billion of project bond financings issued to date as of July 2025. Ullico participates as an equity sponsor in a consortium with Ferrovial Airports, JLC Infrastructure, and Carlyle, and the project broke ground in September 2022.
historicalMilestones: 2021-12-13 - New York announced a revised agreement with the New Terminal One consortium.
2022-09-08 - The New Terminal One project officially broke ground.
2024 - The project completed a $2.55 billion green bond issuance.
2025-07-23 - The project completed a $1.367 billion Series 2025 green bond issuance.
2025-07 - Assured Guaranty stated that the project had issued $5.9 billion of bonds across three transactions.

oneSentenceDescription: U.S./Canada marine and intermodal transportation provider operating tugs, barges, and terminals along the Columbia and Snake Rivers and Vancouver-area waterways.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ullico | Fund: UIF | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Tidewater Transportation and Terminals provides marine and intermodal transportation services using tugs, barges, and terminal infrastructure. Its customers include fuel, bulk commodity, and industrial shippers that move products on the Columbia and Snake River systems and related Pacific Northwest waterways. The business follows an asset-heavy operating model centered on owned or controlled vessels, barges, and terminals. Its operations are concentrated in Oregon, Washington, and adjacent cross-border trade corridors, although public disclosures reviewed do not provide a current vessel count. Ullico identifies the company as an active investment, and in 2025 Tidewater contracted for four new liquid refined product barges, while current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 1932 - Tidewater traces its operating history to 1932.
2017-09 - Tidewater acquired Island Tug and Barge and ITB Subsea.
2019-01 - Upper Bay Infrastructure Partners acquired Tidewater from Stonepeak.
2021 - Tidewater added a bio-blending system at its Umatilla terminal.
2022 - Island Tug and Barge was rebranded as Tidewater Transportation Canada.
2023 - Tidewater added new tugs and placed Barge 35 into service.
2025-03-21 - Tidewater contracted for four new liquid refined product barges.

oneSentenceDescription: U.S./Canada school transportation operator and one of the largest providers in North America, serving 325+ school districts.
ownershipDetails: Firm: La Caisse | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Ullico | Fund: Ullico Infrastructure Master Fund | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Student Transportation Inc. provides outsourced school bus transportation services to school districts and school boards. Its customers are public and private educational institutions that contract for route-based student transportation rather than operating fleets directly. The business follows an asset-heavy operating model built around buses, depots, drivers, and long-term service contracts. The company operates across the United States and Canada, and the provided source list describes service to more than 325 school districts. A group of investors led by La Caisse announced an acquisition of the company in 2018, with Ullico Infrastructure Master Fund participating in the transaction.

2004-09-22 - Student Transportation Inc. was incorporated.
2018-02-27 - Student Transportation announced an agreement to be acquired by a CDPQ-led investor group.

oneSentenceDescription: U.S. wireless infrastructure platform that sources, owns, manages, and operates wireless infrastructure assets nationwide.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ullico | Fund: UIF | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Meritage Group | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: TowerPoint Capital | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: TowerPoint Infrastructure Partners is a wireless infrastructure platform that sources, owns, manages, and operates wireless tower and related site assets. Its customers are wireless carriers and other network operators that need long-term access to communications sites. The company follows an asset-heavy lease-based digital infrastructure model with recurring cash flow from site ownership and management. Operations are focused on the United States, and public disclosures reviewed do not specify current tower count or revenue. The platform was formed following a recapitalization announced in early 2025, and Ullico closed an investment in the business in February 2025 alongside Meritage Group and TowerPoint Capital.

2025-02-19 - TowerPoint Infrastructure Partners was launched.

oneSentenceDescription: California contracted generation and energy-storage portfolio that includes two natural-gas generation assets and battery storage.

ownershipDetails: Firm: AES | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 50.1% of Southland Energy's interest
Firm: Ullico | Fund: UIF | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Southland Energy is a portfolio of two combined-cycle gas generation assets and two battery storage assets. Its customers are investment-grade utilities under long-term power purchase agreements. The platform follows an asset-heavy contracted generation model rather than a merchant-only power strategy. Public disclosures describe about 1.4 GW of capacity across assets located in Southern California and Arizona. Ullico made an initial investment in 2020 and increased its position in 2022, while AES disclosed that it retained 50.1% of Southland Energy's interest after the 2022 transaction.

oneSentenceDescription: New Jersey-based 751 MW combined-cycle natural-gas power generation facility in West Deptford.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ullico | Fund: UIF | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: West Deptford Energy Center is a combined-cycle natural gas power plant in West Deptford, New Jersey. Its end market is the regional wholesale electricity system, although public disclosures reviewed do not specify the current mix of contracted and merchant revenues. The facility follows an asset-heavy generation model based on a single large power station. Public disclosures describe the plant as having 751 MW of generating capacity and operations centered entirely in New Jersey. Ullico identifies the asset as an active investment, but current ownership percentages, acquisition date, and co-owner details are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2014-11 - West Deptford Energy Center achieved commercial operation.
2016-09-14 - A Marubeni-led investor group acquired a majority interest from LS Power.
2016-09-14 - LS Power remained a co-owner and operator after the transaction.

oneSentenceDescription: Connecticut-based 785 MW combined-cycle natural-gas power generation facility in Oxford.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ullico | Fund: UIF | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Towantic Energy Center is a combined-cycle natural gas power plant located in Oxford, Connecticut. Its end market is the New England electricity market, although public disclosures reviewed do not specify the current split between contracted and merchant revenues. The facility follows an asset-heavy generation model built around a single large thermal power station. Public disclosures describe the plant as having 785 MW of capacity and commencing operations in 2018. An Ullico subsidiary acquired a partial stake from GE Energy Financial Services in 2016, and current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2016-08-29 - Ullico agreed to acquire a 13.7% stake in Towantic from GE Energy Financial Services.
2016-10-04 - Ullico announced the closing of its investment in CPV Towantic.

Firm: Not publicly disclosed. | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Carroll County Energy is a combined-cycle natural gas generation facility in Carroll County, Ohio. Its end market is the regional electricity system, although public disclosures reviewed do not specify the current mix of contracted and merchant exposure. The plant follows an asset-heavy operating model centered on a single large baseload generation asset. Public disclosures describe the facility as having 700 MW of capacity and entering operations in December 2017. Ullico's disclosed ownership interest is 10.5%, while public materials reviewed do not fully identify the remaining current ownership structure.

oneSentenceDescription: U.S. district-energy platform, formerly Enwave USA, serving 400+ buildings and operating the largest thermal ice storage facility while building the largest sewer heat recovery system in North America.

Firm: Ullico | Fund: UIF | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: CenTrio is a district energy platform that provides heating, cooling, and related utility services through centralized energy systems. Its customers are commercial, institutional, and mixed-use buildings that connect to district energy networks instead of maintaining all systems on site. The company follows an asset-heavy infrastructure model based on thermal plants, distribution piping, and long-term service relationships. Public disclosures describe operations in cities including Chicago, Houston, New Orleans, Seattle, Portland, Los Angeles, Syracuse, and Denver, serving more than 400 buildings. QIC and Ullico completed the acquisition of the platform in July 2021.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Ullico | Fund: UIF | Ownership Interest: Majority
companyOverview: Vista Ridge is a Texas water transmission public-private partnership that moves groundwater from Burleson County to the San Antonio area. Its end customer is municipal water demand, with the system supporting long-term regional water supply needs. The project follows an asset-heavy infrastructure model built around a long-distance transmission pipeline rather than retail distribution. Public disclosures describe a 142-mile pipeline and operations concentrated entirely in Texas. Ullico acquired a majority equity interest in Vista Ridge in October 2024 from Ridgewood affiliates, while any retained minority interests are not publicly disclosed.

oneSentenceDescription: California water and wastewater concession operating under a 30-year public-private partnership agreement with the City of Rialto.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ullico | Fund: UIF | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Table Rock Capital | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Rialto Water Services operates the water and wastewater system serving the City of Rialto under a long-term public-private partnership. Its end users are residential, commercial, and industrial customers in the Inland Empire, and public materials cite service to about 100,000 people. The business follows an asset-heavy utility concession model, while Veolia North America operates the systems under the partnership structure. Operations are concentrated in Rialto, California, and the platform has raised capital for infrastructure upgrades, including a $68 million WIFIA loan. Ullico Infrastructure Fund and Table Rock Capital are identified as owners in public disclosures, while the original acquisition date is not publicly disclosed in the materials reviewed.
historicalMilestones: 2012 - The City of Rialto entered into a 30-year concession with Rialto Water Services.

oneSentenceDescription: New York transmission company owning the 66-mile Volney-Marcy high-voltage electric transmission line in central New York.

companyOverview: Upstate Transmission is the indirect owner of the Volney-Marcy high-voltage transmission line in central New York. Its end market is electricity transmission service within the New York grid rather than retail power sales. The business follows an asset-heavy utility infrastructure model centered on a single long-distance transmission line. Public disclosures describe the asset as a 66-mile line located in New York. Ullico acquired Upstate Transmission in January 2023, and public materials reviewed do not disclose additional co-owner interests.
historicalMilestones: 1986 - The Volney-Marcy transmission line entered service.

oneSentenceDescription: U.S. interstate natural-gas pipeline system with more than 5,800 miles of steel pipe plus storage and compressor assets.
principalLocationOfOperations: Kansas, Oklahoma, Missouri, Wyoming, Colorado, Nebraska, and Texas
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ullico | Fund: UIF | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Southern Star owns and operates an interstate natural gas transmission and storage system. Its customers include utilities, marketers, producers, and industrial users that need transportation and storage across the Mid-Continent region. The business follows an asset-heavy pipeline model supported by long-lived pipeline, storage, and compressor assets. Its customers are public safety answering points, telecom providers, enterprises, and government agencies that rely on high-availability communications infrastructure. The business has a service-led model supported by software, networks, and critical communications platforms rather than by discretionary technology spending alone. Public materials emphasize a long operating history in emergency communications and a large installed base across the United States. Stonepeak agreed to acquire Intrado's life and safety business in 2022 and completed the transaction in January 2023.

2023-01-31 - Stonepeak completed the acquisition of Intrado Life & Safety.

oneSentenceDescription: KAPS is a roughly 560-kilometer Canadian NGL pipeline system linking Northwest Alberta supply to Edmonton/Fort Saskatchewan, with Stonepeak acquiring a 50% interest.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Stonepeak | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 50%.
Firm: Keyera Corp. | Fund: Not applicable. | Ownership Interest: 50%.
companyOverview: KAPS is a dual-pipeline system in Alberta designed to transport natural gas liquids and condensate from the Karr area to Keyera's processing and fractionation network. Its customers are upstream producers and downstream infrastructure users that require contracted liquids takeaway and connectivity into the basin's gathering and processing system. The project is an asset-heavy midstream business with revenue tied to pipeline transportation rather than commodity merchandising. Public sources describe roughly 560 kilometres of pipeline and planned throughput capacity of up to 350,000 barrels per day on the NGL line. Stonepeak agreed to acquire a 50% interest in the project in December 2022, the transaction closed in April 2023, and Keyera continues to operate the system.
historicalMilestones: 2022-12-12 - Stonepeak announced an agreement to acquire a 50% interest in KAPS.
2023-04-26 - Stonepeak completed the acquisition.
2023-10-04 - Keyera announced completion of the KAPS pipeline system.
2023 - Keyera retained operatorship of the asset after the sale.
2025 - KAPS continued operating as a jointly owned Alberta liquids transportation system.

oneSentenceDescription: Lineage is a leading temperature-controlled logistics and cold-storage platform with a large global network anchored by a major North American footprint.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Stonepeak | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Lineage operates temperature-controlled warehouses and cold-chain logistics infrastructure for food and consumer supply chains. Its customers include food producers, processors, retailers, and distributors that outsource storage, handling, and logistics for frozen and refrigerated products. The business follows an asset-heavy model built around owned and leased cold-storage facilities, transportation coordination, and value-added services. Public materials describe a global footprint of more than 485 warehouses in about 20 countries with over 3 billion cubic feet of capacity. Stonepeak first invested in the company in 2018 and remained an investor through the company's later expansion and public listing.
historicalMilestones: 2008 - Lineage was founded with a single warehouse.
2018-05 - Stonepeak made its initial investment in Lineage.
2019-02 - Lineage completed its acquisition of Preferred Freezer Services.

2021 - Lineage announced a further strategic equity capital raise with support from Stonepeak.
2024 - The company completed its initial public offering while Stonepeak remained an investor.

oneSentenceDescription: LOGISTEC operates cargo-handling and marine services across about 60 ports and 85 terminals in North America and also provides environmental infrastructure services.

companyOverview: LOGISTEC provides marine cargo handling, port services, transportation logistics, and environmental remediation services in Canada and the United States. Its customers include port users, industrial shippers, infrastructure owners, and public-sector counterparties that require recurring essential-services support. The business combines asset-backed terminal operations with service-led environmental activities, producing a diversified infrastructure-services profile rather than a single-asset concession model. Public materials describe a broad network of marine terminals and environmental service operations across North America. Blue Wolf Capital Partners agreed to acquire the company in 2023, and Stonepeak partnered in the transaction by providing significant investment support that closed in January 2024.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Stonepeak | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Longview Infrastructure is a newly formed electric transmission development and investment platform in the United States. Its customers are electric utilities, grid operators, and end users indirectly served by new transmission capacity rather than retail consumers. The platform follows a development-led utilities model focused on originating, permitting, financing, and executing transmission projects. Public materials state that the management team has experience across every major U.S. RTO and ISO and has participated in more than $8 billion of energy infrastructure development. Stonepeak partnered with Longview in March 2025 by providing an equity commitment to support the platform's project pipeline.

The business is asset-heavy, with revenues tied to biogas collection, upgrading, and pipeline-quality RNG production from farm-based facilities. Public materials describe more than 85 digesters in operation and another 30-plus in construction or development across several agricultural states. Stonepeak invested in Maas Energy Works in November 2022, and the current ownership percentage is not publicly disclosed.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Stonepeak | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.

ownershipDetails: Firm: WhiteWater | Fund: Not applicable. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.

companyOverview: Pelican Pipeline is a new-build natural gas transmission project being developed in Louisiana. Its customer base is expected to be shippers moving Haynesville gas toward Gulf Coast markets under firm transportation agreements, which gives the project a contracted midstream model rather than merchant commodity exposure. Public materials describe a roughly 170-mile pipeline running from Williams, Louisiana to the Gillis Hub near the Gulf Coast, with expansion plans that increased targeted capacity to about 2.5 Bcf per day. The project remains under construction and is expected to enter service in 2027. Stonepeak agreed to invest alongside WhiteWater, FIC, and Trace Capital Management in 2024, and detailed ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.

Stonepeak announced the acquisition of Rinchem in January 2022, and in January 2026 the company announced a combination with Dupré under common Stonepeak ownership.

Its end market is hyperscale, cloud, and other high-density compute demand, and the business model is asset-heavy development and leasing of powered data center capacity. Public materials do not disclose the legal entity name, founding year, customer roster, or detailed ownership percentages for the project. SDC continues to identify the campus as a current investment, and third-party market reporting in 2025 linked SDC to a 112-acre, 600MW Ashburn site.
historicalMilestones: 2024 - SDC's portfolio materials listed a multi-building 600MW data center campus in Ashburn, Virginia.

2026 - The campus remained part of SDC's active digital infrastructure portfolio.

ownershipVehicle: Majority interest acquired by funds managed by SDC
oneSentenceDescription: IQ Fiber is a Jacksonville-based residential fiber operator in North Florida, and SDC disclosed both the original majority investment and a later $150 million follow-on equity commitment.
principalLocationOfOperations: Florida, Georgia, South Carolina, Delaware, and Maryland
ownershipDetails: Firm: SDC Capital Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Majority
companyOverview: IQ Fiber is a residential and small-business fiber broadband provider headquartered in Jacksonville, Florida. The company sells 100% fiber internet and managed Wi-Fi services to households and businesses and emphasizes simple pricing and local support. Its model is asset-heavy, with owned fiber network deployment across Southeastern and Mid-Atlantic markets. Public company materials say the business launched in August 2021 and by 2026 had expanded beyond North Florida into Georgia, South Carolina, Delaware, and Maryland through organic builds and acquisitions. Funds managed by SDC acquired a majority interest in 2021 and provided follow-on equity in 2023 to support network expansion.
historicalMilestones: 2021-09-15 - SDC announced that funds it manages acquired a majority interest in IQ Fiber.
2021-08 - IQ Fiber launched operations.

ownershipVehicle: Majority interest acquired by funds managed by SDC
oneSentenceDescription: Smartaira provides broadband and related communications services to multifamily properties across western U.S. metros, and Smartaira disclosed that SDC-managed funds acquired a majority interest in 2022.
profileCompanyName: Consolidated Smart Systems, LLC d/b/a Smartaira
principalLocationOfOperations: Western United States and multi-state United States
ownershipDetails: Firm: SDC Capital Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Majority
companyOverview: Smartaira provides internet, managed Wi-Fi, voice, and entertainment services to multifamily property owners, managers, and residents. Its customer base is concentrated in apartment and other multiple-dwelling-unit communities, where the company offers both bulk and retail service models. The operating model combines network ownership, managed service delivery, and property-level recurring contracts, making it more asset-intensive than a pure reseller. Company materials state that the platform has served the multifamily market since the 1980s and expanded from video into internet services and community-wide managed Wi-Fi. Funds managed by SDC acquired a majority interest at the end of 2021 and committed additional capital to support organic growth and acquisitions.
historicalMilestones: 1980s - Smartaira traces its operating history in the multifamily market to the 1980s.
2010 - The company expanded its service offering to include internet services.

2023-01-24 - Smartaira acquired Direct Plus to expand into additional multifamily markets.
2023 - Smartaira continued expanding managed Wi-Fi and bulk connectivity services for property owners.

ownershipVehicle: Not publicly disclosed on current page
oneSentenceDescription: BIG Fiber operates dark-fiber networks in the San Francisco Bay Area, Greater Atlanta, and Greater Portland, and its current leadership page still lists SDC founder Todd Aaron as an investor.

The platform focuses on newly built, 100% underground fiber infrastructure in the San Francisco Bay Area, Greater Atlanta, and Greater Portland markets. Its model is asset-heavy and long-duration because it owns the network plant and leases high-capacity fiber connectivity to mission-critical users. Public materials provide headquarters and network scale indicators, including more than 320 route miles and 65 data centers on its Bay Area footprint, but do not disclose a founding year. SDC lists the company as a current investment, and the business rebranded from Bandwidth IG to BIG Fiber in 2025 as it continued to expand its dark fiber platform.

ownershipVehicle: Funds managed by SDC invested for an approximately 48% stake
oneSentenceDescription: ALLO is a Midwestern FTTP platform, and Nelnet disclosed in 2020 that SDC-managed funds invested $197 million for an approximately 48% ownership stake.

Its customers include residential users, enterprises, schools, and public-sector organizations that require high-capacity last-mile connectivity. The company follows an asset-heavy fiber owner-operator model and public materials state that it began building its first networks in 2003. ALLO reports service in 53 cities and more than 1.4 million people across its footprint, giving it meaningful regional scale. Funds managed by SDC acquired an approximately 48% stake in 2020, with Nelnet and ALLO management retaining the balance of the equity.

Its customers include wireless carriers, fixed wireless providers, public-sector users, and other network operators that need vertical real estate and backhaul infrastructure. The platform is asset-heavy because it builds and leases physical communications sites under long-duration infrastructure arrangements. Public materials describe a national footprint with hundreds of sites under development, but they do not disclose a founding year or ownership percentage for SDC's investment. SDC identifies Verta as a current portfolio company, and the platform expanded in 2025 through the acquisition of Arcadia Towers.

oneSentenceDescription: CleanCapital develops, builds, owns, operates, and invests in U.S. solar and storage projects, and Manulife has publicly disclosed additional capital commitments to the platform.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Manulife Investment Management | Fund: Manulife Infrastructure Fund II | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: BlackRock | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: CleanCapital develops, constructs, owns, operates, and invests in distributed solar and energy storage assets across the United States. Its counterparties include project developers, commercial and institutional energy users, and other customers participating in distributed generation and community solar markets. The platform follows an asset-heavy owner-operator model with recurring cash flows from operating projects and portfolio acquisitions. Company materials state that CleanCapital was founded in 2015 and by 2024 had grown into one of the larger investors and owners of distributed solar and battery generation projects in the country, with a footprint across 23 states and one U.S. territory. Manulife committed $300 million in 2021 and an additional commitment of up to $500 million in 2023, making it a key capital partner in the company's current ownership and growth strategy.

oneSentenceDescription: Cleco is a Louisiana regulated utility holding company, and current Cleco disclosures still show John Hancock/Manulife representation in governance.
profileSubsector: Regulated electric utility and wholesale generation

companyOverview: Cleco owns a regulated electric utility and a wholesale power business serving Louisiana. Public company materials state that Cleco Power serves approximately 295,000 retail customers across 24 Louisiana parishes and also supplies wholesale power in the state. The operating model is asset-heavy and rate-regulated on the retail side, supported by owned generation, transmission, and distribution infrastructure and an unregulated generation portfolio. Public materials cite approximately 3,035 MW of generation capacity, about 1,335 miles of transmission lines, and more than 12,000 miles of distribution lines, indicating utility-scale operations within a single-state footprint. A North American investor group led by Macquarie and British Columbia Investment Management Corporation completed the take-private acquisition in 2016, and Manulife lists the investment in Manulife Infrastructure Fund I. Current public sources do not disclose Manulife's exact ownership percentage within the consortium.
historicalMilestones: 1935 - The company began as Louisiana Ice & Electric Company in Bunkie, Louisiana.

2016-04-13 - The investor group completed the acquisition and Cleco became privately held.
2019-02-04 - Cleco completed the acquisition of NRG South Central Generating through Cleco Cajun.
2025-02-04 - Cleco marked 90 years of operating history and continued transmission and generation investment planning in Louisiana.

Its customer base includes wireless carriers, infrastructure tenants, landlords, and enterprise venue users that require tower, rooftop, DAS, small-cell, and related fiber solutions. The company operates an asset-heavy model built around owned tenanted sites as well as long-term managed properties for third-party asset owners. Diamond states that it owns and manages more than 4,000 tenanted wireless sites and markets more than 500,000 third-party properties across all 50 states, which provides national operating scale. Ontario Teachers' acquired a significant equity position in 2023, joining existing investors including Sculptor Real Estate and Manulife Investment Management, and Manulife's infrastructure team continues to identify Diamond as a Fund I investment. Public disclosures do not state the exact ownership split among the investors.

Company materials state that it serves approximately 605,000 customers in Allegheny and Beaver counties across an approximately 817-square-mile service territory. The operating model is fully utility-based, with earnings supported by regulated network investment rather than merchant power generation. Public disclosures emphasize a dense urban and suburban footprint and ongoing grid modernization and clean-energy transition work within southwestern Pennsylvania. Duquesne's current shareholder group is publicly identified on its investor site, with GIC as the largest owner and Three Rivers Utility Holdings, the Manulife and PGGM vehicle, holding a 30.4% interest; Manulife's infrastructure team links the investment to Fund I. The current consortium structure reflects ownership changes following the 2007 take-private and later secondary transfers in 2011, 2016, and 2017.

oneSentenceDescription: Elizabeth River Crossings operates the DBFOM tunnel/freeway concession connecting Norfolk and Portsmouth through the Downtown and Midtown tunnel system.

companyOverview: Elizabeth River Crossings finances, operates, and maintains the Elizabeth River Tunnels project in the South Hampton Roads region of Virginia. The asset serves passenger and freight traffic moving between Portsmouth and Norfolk through the Downtown and Midtown Tunnels and related roadway infrastructure. Its operating model is concession-based and asset-heavy, with long-dated toll-backed cash flows and contracted responsibilities for operations, maintenance, and customer service. Public materials identify the business as a public-private partnership concession with a remaining term extending to 2070 and a headquarters location in Portsmouth, Virginia. Abertis and Manulife Investment Management acquired the concession in late 2020, and ERC's current ownership page states that Abertis holds 55.2% while Manulife holds 44.8%. Manulife's infrastructure team lists the investment under Fund II.

Public materials describe ExteNet as the largest privately held U.S. provider focused on DAS, small cells, fiber, and other advanced connectivity solutions. Its footprint is national, and the company relocated its headquarters to Frisco, Texas while continuing to serve carrier and enterprise customers across major U.S. markets. John Hancock led the acquisition of an approximately 30% stake in 2020, and ExteNet announced the closing of the Manulife-led investment in 2021 alongside existing owners Digital Colony and Stonepeak. The customer base is the Texas power system and counterparties that value flexible capacity, congestion management, and grid services rather than retail end users. The operating model is asset-heavy and project-based, with capital committed to the development and ownership of batteries and related interconnection infrastructure. Public announcements from Hunt state that by 2024 the related platform had 270 MW operating and 80 MW under development, providing a disclosed scale indicator for the Texas footprint. Manulife announced an initial commitment in 2021 and continues to identify HEN Infrastructure as a Fund II investment, while detailed ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.

Kennedy International Airport and to manage Terminal 7 during the transition period. The project serves airlines and airport passengers and forms part of the broader redevelopment of JFK's international terminal complex. Its operating model is concession-based and asset-heavy, combining airport development, long-term terminal operations, and commercial tenant management. Public materials state that Terminal 6 is a $4.2 billion, approximately 1.2 million-square-foot redevelopment, which provides a clear scale indicator for the project. The consortium includes Vantage Group, American Triple I, RXR, and Manulife Investment Management, and Manulife's infrastructure team links the investment to Fund II. Public sources reviewed for this workbook do not disclose the partners' individual ownership percentages.

2024 - JFK Millennium Partners completed a nearly $2 billion bond issuance for the Terminal 6 redevelopment.

2026 - Public reporting indicated the phase-one opening preparations for Terminal 6.
2026 - Manulife continues to identify JFK Millennium Partners as an active Fund II portfolio company.

oneSentenceDescription: Manulife announced the formation of Lakefront Fiber in 2025 as a JV with Midwest Fiber Networks to serve hyperscale, carrier, and municipal customers in the Midwest.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Manulife Investment Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Midwest Fiber Networks | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Lakefront Fiber is a fiber infrastructure platform formed to serve hyperscale, carrier, and municipal customers in the Midwest. Public Manulife materials state that the platform builds on Midwest Fiber Networks' regional operating experience and that its first acquisition added nearly 650 route miles in Illinois. The customer base is wholesale and institutional rather than retail, with an asset-heavy model centered on owned network infrastructure and capacity sales. Public disclosures indicate a Midwest footprint with Illinois as the initial operating market and a stated focus on high-capacity connectivity for carrier, hyperscale, and municipal use cases. Manulife announced the formation of Lakefront Fiber in 2025 and concurrently disclosed the platform's first transaction, the purchase of select Everstream network assets. Detailed equity percentages between Manulife and its operating partner are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2024-06-07 - Midwest Fiber Networks LLC was organized in Illinois, according to state records filed in the Illinois Commerce Commission docket.
2025-05-16 - Lakefront Fiber closed its first transaction, acquiring select Everstream network assets in Illinois.
2025-05-19 - Manulife announced the formation of Lakefront Fiber.
2025-05-19 - Manulife disclosed that the Everstream acquisition added nearly 650 route miles to the platform.
2025-06-02 - BGL announced that it had advised Lakefront Fiber on the Everstream asset acquisition.

oneSentenceDescription: Landmark remains an active Manulife-backed student-housing platform, with Manulife’s December 2025 press release calling Auburn the fifth project in its build-to-core JV with the Manulife Infrastructure Fund pool.
principalLocationOfOperations: Georgia, Michigan, Texas, South Carolina, Arizona, California, and other U.S. states
ownershipDetails: Firm: Landmark Properties, Inc. | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Manulife Investment Management | Fund: Manulife Infrastructure Fund II | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Landmark develops, builds, and operates student housing and related residential communities across U.S. university markets. Its customer base is primarily students and universities, with activity spanning development, construction, property management, and investment management. The platform operates through an asset-heavy real estate model and public materials show a national footprint anchored by Athens, Georgia, with projects and communities across numerous states. Landmark stated in its 2024 ESG report that it had developed or was developing more than 120 residential projects totaling roughly 74,000 beds and more than $15 billion of assets under management. Manulife Investment Management has invested alongside the platform through Manulife Infrastructure Fund II, and Landmark said in December 2025 that a sixth project was commencing under the parties’ build-to-core joint venture.
historicalMilestones: 2004 - Landmark was founded in Athens, Georgia.
2021-03-17 - Landmark and Manulife announced a joint venture focused on student-housing development.
2023-08-03 - Landmark and Manulife announced construction start on The Standard at Berkeley, a project within their venture.
2024 - Landmark reported more than 120 residential projects representing about 74,000 beds and over $15 billion of assets under management.
2025-12-18 - Landmark and Manulife announced construction start on The Metropolitan on South U in Ann Arbor, which Landmark described as the sixth project in the venture.
2025 - Landmark said it had reached a milestone of more than 115 communities and over 72,000 beds nationwide.

oneSentenceDescription: Midland Cogeneration Venture is a major Michigan gas-fired cogeneration plant that Capital Power and Manulife Fund II acquired in 2022.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Capital Power Corporation | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 50%
Firm: Manulife Investment Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 50%
companyOverview: Midland Cogeneration Venture owns a large natural gas-fired cogeneration facility in Midland, Michigan that supplies both electricity and steam. The plant serves Consumers Energy under a long-term power purchase arrangement and also provides steam to industrial customers in the surrounding chemical manufacturing complex. Its operating model is asset-heavy and largely contracted, with Capital Power responsible for operations and maintenance under the joint ownership structure. Capital Power describes the facility as the largest natural gas-fired combined electrical and steam cogeneration plant in the United States, with 1,633 MW of generation capacity and significant steam output. Capital Power and Manulife announced the acquisition in July 2022, completed it in September 2022, and in December 2025 Capital Power said the owners had secured a new contract with Consumers Energy through 2040.
historicalMilestones: 1989 - Commercial operations began at the Midland cogeneration facility.
2022-07-12 - Capital Power and Manulife announced an agreement to acquire Midland Cogeneration Venture.
2022-09-23 - Capital Power reported closing of the acquisition.
2024 - Capital Power continued to identify Midland as part of its operated generation fleet.
2025-12-23 - Capital Power announced a new long-term contract with Consumers Energy extending through 2040.
2026 - Capital Power continued to describe Midland as the largest natural gas-fired combined electrical and steam cogeneration plant in the United States.

oneSentenceDescription: Manulife’s infrastructure page surfaced Nexamp’s 2024 $520 million raise, and Nexamp describes itself as a national clean energy company that develops, manages, and maintains solar assets across the U.S.
principalLocationOfOperations: Massachusetts, Illinois, Maine, Minnesota, and other U.S. states
ownershipDetails: Firm: Mitsubishi Corporation | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Majority
Firm: Generate Capital | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Manulife Investment Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Nexamp develops, owns, and operates distributed solar and storage assets serving utilities, municipalities, businesses, and community solar subscribers. Its revenue model is primarily asset-based and contracted, supported by power purchase agreements, community solar subscriptions, and related project-level financing. Public materials show a multistate operating footprint, with project activity and customer programs across numerous U.S. power markets. Mitsubishi said in 2024 that it was the majority owner of Nexamp, and Nexamp reported that it generated more than 1.2 billion kilowatt-hours in 2025 after raising more than $1.5 billion that year. In August 2024 Mitsubishi announced a $520 million capital raise for the company that was underwritten by lead investor Manulife Investment Management with participation from existing shareholder Generate Capital.
historicalMilestones: 2007 - Nexamp was founded in Boston.
2018 - Mitsubishi Corporation announced an investment in Nexamp.
2021 - Generate Capital became an investor in Nexamp.
2024-08-29 - Mitsubishi announced a $520 million capital raise for Nexamp led by Manulife Investment Management.
2025 - Nexamp said it raised more than $1.5 billion during the year and generated over 1.2 billion kilowatt-hours.
2025-11-18 - Nexamp announced $340 million of financing for a 39-project portfolio totaling 150 MW of solar and 37 MWh of storage.

oneSentenceDescription: NineDot develops distributed battery storage in the New York City metro area, and Manulife’s infrastructure team maps the investment to Fund III.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Manulife Investment Management | Fund: Manulife Infrastructure Fund III | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Carlyle | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: NineDot develops, owns, and operates distributed battery energy storage systems designed for the New York City metropolitan area. Its customer base includes the electric grid and local communities, with projects positioned to support reliability, resiliency, and distributed energy needs in dense urban load pockets. The business follows an asset-heavy infrastructure model centered on development, financing, construction, and operation of front-of-the-meter storage projects. Company materials state that the platform was founded in 2015, now has more than 80 employees, and is building a portfolio across the New York metro area. In January 2024 NineDot announced a $225 million equity financing led by Manulife Investment Management alongside Carlyle to fund construction and operation of its battery storage pipeline.
historicalMilestones: 2015 - Company materials state that NineDot was founded in Brooklyn.
2021 - NineDot said it launched as an evolution of Certain Solar’s development platform.
2023-06-28 - NineDot announced the activation of its first battery storage project in the Bronx.
2024-01-10 - NineDot announced a $225 million equity financing led by Manulife Investment Management with Carlyle.
2025 - NineDot published an updated fact sheet describing a growing portfolio of New York City-area storage projects.
2026 - NineDot reported a workforce of more than 80 employees and an expanded metro-area development pipeline.

oneSentenceDescription: Patriot Utility Privatizations was formed with John Hancock/Manulife affiliates to acquire a 50% interest in Dominion Energy’s private utility holdings business.
profileSubsector: Private utility systems and utility privatization
principalLocationOfOperations: Virginia, South Carolina, North Carolina, and other U.S. states
ownershipDetails: Firm: Foundation Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: John Hancock Life Insurance Company (U.S.A.) and affiliates | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Patriot Utility Privatizations is an infrastructure vehicle formed to invest in regulated-style private utility systems serving military bases, campuses, and other government-related facilities. Its end markets are long-term water, wastewater, electric, and gas utility concession and service agreements, which typically produce contracted and essential-service cash flows. Public materials tie the platform to Dominion Energy’s private utility holdings business, including utility assets operating on or for U.S. military installations. The platform follows an asset-heavy model built around long-duration concession-style infrastructure ownership and operation. Foundation Infrastructure Partners announced Patriot’s formation in February 2022 with John Hancock Life Insurance Company (U.S.A.) and affiliates, and public filings subsequently described Patriot as the joint venture partner owning 50% of Dominion Utility Privatization LLC.
historicalMilestones: 2022-02-25 - Foundation Infrastructure Partners announced the formation of Patriot Utility Privatizations with John Hancock Life Insurance Company (U.S.A.) and affiliates.
2022-02-25 - Patriot announced an agreement to acquire a 50% interest in Dominion Energy’s private utility holdings business.
2022 - Dominion Utility Privatization LLC continued operating utility systems under the new joint ownership structure.
2025 - Foundation Infrastructure continued to list Patriot as an active investment on its portfolio page.
2026-03-17 - Dominion Energy’s Form 10-K described Patriot as the joint venture between Foundation and John Hancock Life Insurance Company (U.S.A.) and affiliates.

oneSentenceDescription: PennBridges is surfaced on Manulife’s current portfolio materials and aligns with PennDOT’s Major Bridge P3 program in Pennsylvania.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Macquarie Capital Investment Holdings, Inc. | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: S&B USA Concessions LLC | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Not publicly disclosed. | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Bridging Pennsylvania Developer I is the project company delivering a portfolio of major bridge replacements under the Pennsylvania Department of Transportation’s Major Bridges public-private partnership program. Its customer is PennDOT, and its revenue model is based on long-term availability payments tied to delivery and performance rather than traffic demand. The project is asset-heavy and construction-led, covering the replacement, financing, and long-term maintenance of six bridges across the state. Public project materials identify the developer team as being led jointly by Macquarie Capital and S&B USA Concessions, with construction entering active delivery in 2025 and substantial completion targeted for 2028. Manulife lists PennBridges in its infrastructure materials, but public project documents do not disclose the full economic ownership structure or investor percentages.
historicalMilestones: 2022-11-29 - PennDOT selected the Bridging Pennsylvania Partners team for the Major Bridges P3 initiative.
2023 - Bridging Pennsylvania Developer I was established to develop, finance, and maintain the first bundle of six bridges.
2024 - PennDOT reported continued preconstruction and permitting progress for the bridge bundle.
2025-01 - PennDOT’s P3 board report stated that construction had started on the project.
2028-Summer - PennDOT’s board report targets substantial completion of the six-bridge bundle by summer 2028.

oneSentenceDescription: Phoenix Tower is a wireless-infrastructure owner/operator with U.S. roots and broader Americas operations, and company disclosures still cite John Hancock among investors.
principalLocationOfOperations: Florida and international operations across the United States, Latin America, the Caribbean, and Europe
ownershipDetails: Firm: Blackstone | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Grain Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: BlackRock | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Wren House | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: USS | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Phoenix Tower International owns and operates wireless tower and related telecom site infrastructure for mobile network operators. Its customers are primarily carriers and telecom service providers, and its revenue model is asset-based and largely contracted through long-term site leases and colocation agreements. The company states that it is headquartered in Boca Raton, Florida and operates from 23 offices serving markets in the United States, Latin America, the Caribbean, and Europe. PTI’s official materials say it was founded in October 2013 and, as of 2025 and 2026 transaction announcements, remained privately owned by investor groups including Blackstone, Grain Management, BlackRock, Wren House, and USS. Blackstone acquired a 35% stake from Manulife Investment Management in 2022, and PTI continued expanding through acquisitions including the closing of a 3,700-site French transaction in February 2026.

2026 - PTI continued to identify Blackstone, Grain Management, BlackRock, Wren House, and USS as its investors.

oneSentenceDescription: PowerFlex provides onsite solar, storage, EV charging, and energy-management solutions, and Manulife publicly disclosed a $100 million investment in 2022.
profileSubsector: Behind-the-meter solar, storage, EV charging, and energy management
ownershipDetails: Firm: EDF Renewables North America | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Majority
Firm: Manulife Investment Management | Fund: Manulife Infrastructure Fund II | Ownership Interest: Minority
companyOverview: PowerFlex provides onsite solar, battery storage, electric-vehicle charging, and energy-management solutions for commercial, industrial, municipal, educational, and healthcare customers. Its business model combines software, engineering, financing, and long-term asset operations, giving it exposure to both equipment deployment and recurring managed-service revenues. The company operates nationally from a U.S. base in California and public materials emphasize a portfolio built around distributed energy assets under long-term customer arrangements. In March 2025 PowerFlex reported more than 500 MW of solar, over 50 MWh of storage, and more than 50,000 EV chargers under management. Manulife Investment Management announced a $100 million minority investment in December 2022, while EDF Renewables North America said it retained majority ownership.
historicalMilestones: 2019 - EDF Renewables acquired PowerFlex Systems and combined it with EDF’s EV charging activities in North America.
2022-12-13 - Manulife Investment Management announced a $100 million minority investment in PowerFlex.

2025-03-12 - PowerFlex announced that it had surpassed 500 MW of solar, 50 MWh of storage, and 50,000 EV chargers under management.
2026 - PowerFlex continued operating as a distributed energy platform within EDF Renewables North America.

oneSentenceDescription: Rocky Mountain Midstream is surfaced on Manulife’s current portfolio materials and is still referenced by the infrastructure team as a Fund I midstream investment.
ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Rocky Mountain Midstream is a natural gas gathering and processing system serving producing basins in the Rocky Mountain region. Publicly available system materials describe operations connected to the Greater Green River, Piceance, and Uinta basins, supporting upstream producers with transportation and related midstream services. The platform follows an asset-heavy fee-based operating model centered on gathering pipelines, treating facilities, and processing infrastructure. Williams identifies Rocky Mountain Midstream as part of its regional system information, and Manulife continues to include Rocky Mountain Midstream in its direct infrastructure materials. Public sources reviewed do not disclose a current founding year, asset count, or the present economic ownership structure in sufficient detail.
historicalMilestones: 2015 - Colorado regulatory records identify Rocky Mountain Midstream as an operator in the state.

2020 - Manulife’s Equator Principles reporting period included several North American power and infrastructure financings, after which Rocky Mountain Midstream later appeared in its portfolio materials.

oneSentenceDescription: Sabey is one of the largest privately owned U.S. data-center platforms, and Manulife’s infrastructure team maps the investment to Fund II.
principalLocationOfOperations: Washington, Virginia, New York, and Texas
ownershipDetails: Firm: Sabey Corporation | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: National Real Estate Advisors, LLC | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Manulife Investment Management | Fund: Manulife Infrastructure Fund II | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Sabey Data Centers develops and operates wholesale and hyperscale data center campuses for enterprise, cloud, and technology customers. Its operating model is asset-heavy and infrastructure-like, built around owned campuses, long-term customer contracts, and ongoing capacity expansion. The company states that it was created in 2010 through a partnership between Sabey Corporation and National Real Estate Advisors and today operates campuses in Washington, Virginia, New York, and Texas. Public materials describe six operating campuses, additional development activity, and more than 70 MW of new power availability scheduled to come online across key sites by 2027. In February 2021 Sabey and National Real Estate Advisors announced a $150 million investment from Manulife Investment Management into Sabey Data Center Properties LLC.

oneSentenceDescription: Manulife announced it acquired a controlling interest in Serverfarm in 2023, and Serverfarm says it develops and operates data centers across North America and EMEA.
principalLocationOfOperations: Texas, Illinois, Georgia, New Jersey, Washington, Ontario, and other North American and EMEA markets
ownershipDetails: Firm: Manulife Investment Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Controlling
Firm: Existing Serverfarm management shareholders | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Serverfarm develops, acquires, and operates data centers for hyperscale, enterprise, and cloud customers. Its operating model is asset-heavy and centered on owned or controlled campuses, colocation facilities, and build-to-suit development tied to long-term customer demand. Public materials describe a North American and EMEA footprint and recent expansion into large-scale hyperscale campus development, including more than 500 MW of potential capacity added in Houston. Manulife Investment Management announced in August 2023 that it had signed a definitive agreement to acquire a controlling interest in the platform and later announced completion of the transaction. Since that acquisition Serverfarm has expanded through additional campus acquisitions and in March 2026 announced a $3.0 billion credit facility to support hyperscale development across North America.
historicalMilestones: 2023-08-15 - Manulife Investment Management announced a definitive agreement to acquire a controlling interest in Serverfarm.

2026-03-04 - Serverfarm announced a $3.0 billion credit facility to accelerate North American hyperscale campus development.
2026 - Serverfarm continued to market operations across North America and EMEA.

oneSentenceDescription: Silicon Ranch is a large U.S. solar platform, and Manulife’s team page places the investment in Fund II.
principalLocationOfOperations: U.S. operations across multiple states and Canada
ownershipDetails: Firm: AIP Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Manulife Investment Management | Fund: Manulife Infrastructure Fund II | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Shell | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: TD Asset Management / Greystone Infrastructure Fund | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Mountain Group Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Silicon Ranch develops, owns, and operates utility-scale solar and battery storage projects serving utilities, corporations, and other large power buyers. Its revenue base is primarily contracted through long-term power purchase agreements, and the company also performs asset management and land stewardship across its operating fleet. Public materials state that the company was founded in 2011 and now has more than 180 projects across 15 states, with over 4,000 MW in operation and more than 7 GW either contracted, under construction, or operating across the United States and Canada. The platform is asset-heavy and geographically diversified across North American renewable-power markets. In October 2022 Silicon Ranch announced completion of a $775 million equity raise led by Manulife Investment Management on behalf of Manulife Infrastructure Fund II and John Hancock, and in 2025 it announced an additional investment from AIP Management.

oneSentenceDescription: Sunrun Mars is a Sunrun-related distributed solar portfolio/vehicle surfaced on Manulife’s current public portfolio materials and tied to Fund I on the team page.
ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Sunrun Mars is a Sunrun-related distributed solar investment vehicle identified in public filings and Manulife’s infrastructure portfolio materials. The underlying business activity is residential solar system ownership and related customer contracts, with cash flows typically derived from long-term leases, power purchase agreements, and financing structures tied to installed rooftop systems. Publicly available sources reviewed do not provide vehicle-level operating metrics, founding details, or a complete current ownership breakdown for the Mars entities. Sunrun’s public SEC subsidiary lists confirm the existence of Sunrun Mars holdco and portfolio entities, while Equator Principles project disclosures identify Sunrun Mars Portfolio 2020-B LLC as a U.S. power financing closed in 2020. Manulife continues to include Sunrun Mars in its direct infrastructure materials, but detailed vehicle economics and current co-owner percentages are not publicly disclosed.

oneSentenceDescription: Tract is a U.S. data-center land acquisition and development platform focused on master-planned campuses.
principalLocationOfOperations: Nevada, Arizona, Utah, Iowa, and Texas
ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Tract acquires, entitles, and develops master-planned land campuses for data center and digital infrastructure customers. Its end market is hyperscale and large enterprise computing demand, with the platform providing entitled land, utilities planning, and development-readiness rather than retail colocation services. The model is asset-heavy and land-intensive, focused on assembling large sites in markets with power access and long-term digital infrastructure demand. Public materials show a growing U.S. footprint that includes large holdings in greater Reno as well as projects in Arizona, Utah, Iowa, and Texas. Public sources reviewed do not disclose the company’s founding year or full ownership structure, but company announcements in 2025 show continued expansion through new land acquisitions and rezoning activity across multiple markets.

2025-03-05 - Tract announced entry into the Des Moines market through the acquisition of 453 acres in Altoona, Iowa.
