ownershipVehicle: Direct Temasek holding; 100% stake disclosed in Temasek’s 2025 major-investments list.
oneSentenceDescription: Critical transport-infrastructure operator whose North American footprint includes PSA Halifax’s two Canadian container terminals, Ashcroft inland terminal, and U.S. operations including Penn Terminals and Philadelphia-headquartered PSA BDP.
principalLocationOfOperations: North America, Asia, Europe, and other global markets
ownershipDetails: Firm: Temasek | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: PSA International owns and operates container terminals, port infrastructure, and related supply-chain platforms serving global shipping lines and cargo owners. Its operating model is asset-heavy and infrastructure-like, with revenues supported by port handling, terminal operations, and adjacent logistics services across a broad international network. PSA states that its origins date to the Port of Singapore Authority in 1964, while the current PSA International holding structure was formed in 2003 to coordinate global investment and expansion. Public materials describe more than 70 deepsea, rail, and inland terminals in over 180 locations across 45 countries, including North American operations such as PSA Halifax, Penn Terminals, and PSA BDP. In January 2026 PSA reported record 2025 throughput of 105 million TEUs globally, underscoring the scale of the platform within Temasek’s portfolio.
historicalMilestones: 1964-04-01 - The Port of Singapore Authority was formed.
1996 - PSA’s regulatory functions transferred to the Maritime and Port Authority of Singapore, allowing PSA Corporation to focus on commercial terminal management.
2003 - PSA International was formed as the group’s global investment and strategic holding company.
2024 - PSA surpassed 100 million TEUs of annual throughput for the first time.
2025 - PSA handled a record 105 million TEUs across its global terminals.
2026-03-09 - PSA published its FY2025 financial results.

In North America the company has active operations and development in Canada and Mexico, including battery storage and solar projects, while its broader footprint extends across Europe, Australia, and Latin America. Neoen states that it was founded in 2008, and Brookfield’s 2025 offer materials described a portfolio with more than 8 GW in operation or under construction worldwide. Brookfield acquired control through a 2025 public offer, while Temasek disclosed that it partnered in the investment and held a minority interest in the acquisition structure.

oneSentenceDescription: Temasek describes Luminace as North America’s largest distributed energy generation platform, and the company says its 1,500+ MW portfolio spans 29 U.S. states, Washington, D.C., Puerto Rico, and Canada.
profileSubsector: Distributed generation, community solar, and battery storage
ownershipDetails: Firm: Brookfield | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Temasek | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Luminace develops, owns, and operates distributed generation, community solar, and battery storage assets across North America. Its customer base includes utilities, commercial and industrial counterparties, municipalities, and community solar subscribers, with revenues largely supported by long-term contracts and subscription arrangements. The platform follows an asset-heavy distributed infrastructure model and its website states that it has more than 1,700 projects across 29 states serving over 10,000 customers. Luminace says it was formed in 2021 through the combination of several Brookfield distributed generation businesses, while legacy operations date back to 2007. In 2025 Temasek described Luminace as North America’s largest distributed energy generation platform, and European Commission merger materials identified Brookfield and Temasek as acquiring joint control.
historicalMilestones: 2021 - Luminace was formed from Brookfield distributed generation businesses.
2024 - Luminace continued building out its distributed generation and storage footprint across the United States.
2025 - Temasek described Luminace as North America’s largest distributed energy generation platform.
2025 - European Commission merger review materials identified Brookfield and Temasek as acquiring joint control of Luminace.
2026 - Luminace continued to market more than 1,700 projects across 29 states.

ownershipVehicle: Temasek investment disclosed; vehicle not publicly disclosed.
oneSentenceDescription: Calgary-based advanced geothermal company developing closed-loop systems designed to deliver scalable dispatchable baseload heat and power, with Temasek still named among supporting equity investors.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Temasek | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Vickers Venture Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: bp Ventures | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: BDC Capital | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Canada Growth Fund | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Eavor develops closed-loop geothermal systems intended to provide dispatchable, baseload clean heat and power without the need for naturally occurring hydrothermal resources. Its end markets include utilities, industrial users, and district energy applications, and its business model combines technology development with project deployment and infrastructure ownership. The company is headquartered in Calgary and states that it was founded in 2017. Public financing announcements show a scaling platform supported by strategic and institutional investors as it advances demonstration and commercial projects, including its Geretsried project in Germany. Temasek participated in Eavor’s earlier financing rounds and remains one of the publicly disclosed investors in the company’s growth capital base.
historicalMilestones: 2017 - Eavor was founded in Calgary.
2021-10-06 - Eavor announced a US$40 million financing led by strategic energy investors including Temasek-backed participants.
2023-10-24 - Eavor announced a US$182 million capital raise to support commercialization.
2025-07-23 - Eavor announced first electricity production at its Geretsried site in Germany.
2025-09-09 - Canada Growth Fund announced an additional investment in Eavor.
2026 - Eavor continued advancing closed-loop geothermal commercialization.

oneSentenceDescription: Technology-enabled marketplace connecting solar and storage buyers with manufacturers to streamline procurement and reduce sourcing costs.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Energy Capital Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Angeleno Group | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Anza provides a software-enabled marketplace and analytics platform used by solar and storage developers, independent power producers, and corporate energy buyers to source equipment. Its operating model is asset-light relative to power generation platforms, with revenues tied to subscription, transaction support, and procurement services rather than ownership of operating plants. The business focuses on helping customers evaluate module, inverter, and storage options using market data and comparative analytics. The company states that it was formally launched in 2022 and completed its separation from Borrego in 2023 with new backing from an Energy Capital Partners-led consortium that included Angeleno Group. Anza reported in early 2026 that its platform had supported more than 2 GW of solar safe-harbor procurements during 2025 and more than 9.2 GW of solar purchase orders over the prior five years.
historicalMilestones: 2022-09 - Anza was formally launched.
2023-05-23 - Anza completed its separation from Borrego and announced new investment from an Energy Capital Partners-led consortium.
2024 - Anza continued expanding its procurement and analytics platform for solar and storage buyers.
2025 - Anza said its platform supported more than 2 GW of solar safe-harbor procurements during the year.
2026-02-25 - Anza published updated safe-harbor procurement activity and historical purchase-order data.

ownershipVehicle: Take-private led by ECP and co-investors
oneSentenceDescription: Owner, operator and developer of a large portfolio of contracted renewable, storage, efficient gas, transmission and water assets, with operations located primarily across North America, Europe and South America.
profileSubsector: Contracted renewable, storage, transmission, and water infrastructure
principalLocationOfOperations: United States, Canada, Mexico, and South America
ownershipDetails: Firm: Energy Capital Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Temasek | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Co-investors | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Atlantica Sustainable Infrastructure owns and manages a diversified portfolio of renewable energy, energy storage, efficient natural gas, transmission, and water assets. The company’s revenue profile is predominantly contracted, with management disclosures indicating that the vast majority of expected cash flow is supported by long-term agreements. Its footprint includes North American assets across the United States, Canada, and Mexico, alongside additional operations in South America and Europe. Atlantica’s December 2024 factsheet described approximately 2.2 GW of operating renewable generation, 355 MW of storage, and more than 1,200 miles of transmission lines. Energy Capital Partners agreed to acquire Atlantica in 2024 and completed the take-private in December 2024 with co-investors, after which the company continued operating as a contracted clean-energy infrastructure platform.
historicalMilestones: 2014 - Atlantica was established as a listed yield-oriented infrastructure company.
2024-05-29 - Atlantica announced an agreement to be acquired by Energy Capital Partners and co-investors.
2024-12-10 - Atlantica announced court approval for the acquisition scheme.
2024-12-12 - Atlantica closed the acquisition by Energy Capital Partners and co-investors.
2025 - Atlantica continued operating a diversified portfolio of contracted infrastructure assets.
2026 - Atlantica remained an operating platform spanning renewable, storage, transmission, and water assets.

ownershipVehicle: Joint venture by ECP and Avolta, LLC
oneSentenceDescription: Renewable natural gas developer focused on U.S. dairy projects that capture methane and upgrade biogas into pipeline-spec RNG, alongside affiliated Nacelle services.
profileSubsector: Renewable natural gas from dairy waste
principalLocationOfOperations: California, Idaho, and other U.S. dairy markets
ownershipDetails: Firm: Energy Capital Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Avolta, LLC | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Avolta Renewable Holdings is a renewable natural gas platform focused on dairy projects that capture biogas from manure and upgrade it to pipeline-quality fuel. Its end markets include gas utilities, transportation fuel markets, and environmental credit programs, with cash flows supported by offtake and regulatory value streams tied to low-carbon fuel production. The platform follows an asset-heavy development and ownership model built around digester infrastructure, gas upgrading facilities, and pipeline interconnections. Energy Capital Partners said in February 2022 that it had committed capital to a newly formed joint venture with Avolta to develop a series of U.S. dairy RNG projects. Public materials since then continue to describe the company as a growing dairy RNG developer with a project pipeline across major livestock regions.
historicalMilestones: 2022-02-01 - ECP announced its commitment to a newly formed joint venture with Avolta.
2022 - Avolta Renewable Holdings was formed to develop a portfolio of dairy RNG projects.
2024 - Avolta continued advancing projects and development activities across U.S. dairy markets.
2025 - Avolta continued marketing its RNG development platform and project pipeline.
2026 - ECP continued to list Avolta Renewable Holdings as a current portfolio company.

oneSentenceDescription: Owner of the Come By Chance, Newfoundland refinery conversion project, now producing renewable diesel with expansion potential into SAF and green hydrogen.
profileSubsector: Renewable diesel and low-carbon fuels refining
ownershipDetails: Firm: Energy Capital Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Cresta Fund Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: North Atlantic Refining Corp. managed by Silverpeak | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Braya Renewable Fuels owns the Come By Chance refinery complex in Newfoundland and Labrador and is converting and operating the site for renewable and low-carbon fuel production. Its customer base is tied to fuel markets and distributors, with economics influenced by physical refining margins and low-carbon fuel programs rather than regulated utility-style revenues. The platform is highly asset-heavy, centered on a large existing refinery and associated logistics infrastructure that has been repurposed for renewable diesel production. Energy Capital Partners announced a $300 million investment in 2023, and Braya said in early 2024 that the facility had commenced commercial operations for renewable fuel production. Newfoundland and Labrador government materials in 2025 identified the ownership group as including Cresta Fund Management, North Atlantic Refining Corp. managed by Silverpeak, and Energy Capital Partners.
historicalMilestones: 2021 - The Come By Chance refinery changed ownership and began repositioning toward renewable fuels.
2023-12-20 - ECP announced a $300 million investment in Braya Renewable Fuels.
2024-02-22 - Braya announced commencement of commercial operations.
2025-01 to 2025-06 - Newfoundland and Labrador government materials referenced a temporary shutdown period at the facility.
2025-09-11 - Provincial materials identified the Braya ownership group as including Cresta, North Atlantic Refining Corp. managed by Silverpeak, and ECP.
2026 - Braya continued operating the converted renewable fuels facility.

oneSentenceDescription: Energy-storage platform with commercial, industrial and utility-scale assets and more than $1 billion invested or committed across North America.
profileSubsector: Battery storage and distributed energy infrastructure
principalLocationOfOperations: United States, Canada, and the United Kingdom
ownershipDetails: Firm: Energy Capital Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Convergent develops, owns, and operates battery storage and related distributed energy assets for utilities, commercial and industrial customers, and public-sector counterparties. Its business model is asset-heavy and contract-based, with revenues generated from power market participation, resilience services, and long-term customer arrangements. The company states that it was founded in 2011 and has built a portfolio spanning North America and the United Kingdom. Its 2024 sustainability report cited more than 800 MW and 1 GWh of energy storage developed or acquired across its platform. Energy Capital Partners acquired Convergent in 2019 and remains the publicly disclosed sponsor behind the business.
historicalMilestones: 2011 - Convergent was founded.
2019-05-09 - Energy Capital Partners announced the acquisition of Convergent Energy and Power.
2023 - Convergent continued expanding its storage development and operating portfolio.
2024 - Convergent’s sustainability report cited more than 800 MW and 1 GWh of projects developed or acquired.
2026 - ECP continued to list Convergent as a current portfolio company.

oneSentenceDescription: 2.5 GW PJM natural gas fleet spanning Waterford and Darby in Ohio plus Lawrenceburg in Indiana; ECP agreed its sale to Talen in January 2026, but closing is expected in the second half of 2026.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Energy Capital Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Cornerstone Generation owns a portfolio of PJM gas-fired generation assets consisting of Waterford in Ohio, Lawrenceburg in Indiana, and Darby in Ohio. Its customers are wholesale power markets and capacity markets, and its earnings profile is therefore more merchant than contracted compared with regulated or availability-based infrastructure businesses. The platform is asset-heavy and ECP has described it as a 2.5 GW or approximately 2.6 GW fleet across the three facilities. Public deal materials show that ECP acquired the business from Talen in 2022 and later agreed to sell it back to Talen in 2026, subject to regulatory approvals. Pending completion of that sale, Cornerstone remains an ECP-backed merchant generation platform in the Midwest PJM market.
historicalMilestones: 2022 - ECP acquired the portfolio from Talen and established Cornerstone Generation.
2024 - Cornerstone continued operating Waterford, Lawrenceburg, and Darby in PJM.
2026-01-15 - ECP and Talen announced an agreement for Talen to acquire Cornerstone Generation for $3.5 billion.
2026-02-17 - FERC-related filings showed the transaction moving through the approval process.
2026-H2 - Talen stated that the transaction was expected to close in the second half of 2026.

oneSentenceDescription: North American recycler of lead-acid batteries operating major facilities in Eagan, Minnesota and Tampa, Florida.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Energy Capital Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Gopher Resource recycles lead-acid batteries and related materials, producing recycled lead and polypropylene for battery manufacturers and industrial customers. Its operations are asset-heavy, anchored by large recycling plants in Eagan, Minnesota and Tampa, Florida, and supported by a nationwide battery collection network. The company describes itself as a long-established industrial recycler with approximately 80 years of operating history. Public materials indicate that it recycled about 600 million pounds of lead and 41 million pounds of plastic in 2025. Energy Capital Partners announced the acquisition of Gopher Resource in 2018 and remains the publicly disclosed owner.
historicalMilestones: 1946 - Gopher Resource traces its operating history back roughly 80 years.
2018-03-26 - Energy Capital Partners announced the acquisition of Gopher Resource.
2024 - Gopher continued operating major recycling facilities in Minnesota and Florida.
2025 - Gopher reported recycling roughly 600 million pounds of lead and 41 million pounds of plastic.
2026-03-18 - Gopher highlighted its 2025 recycling volumes in a Global Recycling Day release.

ownershipVehicle: Funds managed by ECP recapitalization investment
oneSentenceDescription: One of Canada’s largest diversified infrastructure companies, offering vertically integrated infrastructure solutions for public and private projects, with current ECP board representation.
profileSubsector: Integrated heavy civil construction, asphalt, and aggregates
ownershipDetails: Firm: Energy Capital Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: GFL Environmental Inc. | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 30.1%
Firm: HPS Investment Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Patrick Dovigi | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Green Infrastructure Partners is a vertically integrated heavy civil contractor serving transportation and broader public and private infrastructure markets in Canada. Its services span aggregates, asphalt production and paving, construction engineering, and major civil works, making it a self-perform, asset-heavy operating platform rather than a concession asset owner. The company’s operating footprint is centered in Ontario, where it supplies materials and construction services for roads, bridges, airports, and related infrastructure. Public disclosures state that GIP was established in 2022 and in 2025 ECP agreed to recapitalize the company at a C$4.25 billion enterprise value. Following that transaction announcement, GFL said it would retain an approximately 30.1% equity interest, with ECP becoming the lead new investor alongside continuing minority holders.
historicalMilestones: 2022-04 - Green Infrastructure Partners was established by combining infrastructure businesses under GFL ownership.
2024 - GIP continued operating an integrated Ontario construction materials and civil works platform.
2025-07-15 - GFL announced an agreement to recapitalize GIP at a C$4.25 billion enterprise value with investment from Energy Capital Partners.
2025 - GFL stated that after the recapitalization it would retain an approximately 30.1% equity interest.
2026 - GIP continued marketing vertically integrated infrastructure solutions across Ontario.

ownershipVehicle: Formed by Harvestone Group and ECP
oneSentenceDescription: Low-carbon ethanol and carbon-capture platform owning the Blue Flint, Dakota Spirit and Iroquois Bioenergy plants in North Dakota and Indiana.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Harvestone Group | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Energy Capital Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Harvestone Low Carbon Partners is a low-carbon fuels platform that owns and operates ethanol and related carbon management assets. Its facilities serve fuel and agricultural markets, with economics supported by ethanol sales, feed coproducts, and increasingly carbon-capture and low-carbon fuel attributes. The business is asset-heavy, with operating plants at Blue Flint in Underwood, North Dakota, Dakota Spirit in Spiritwood, North Dakota, and Iroquois Bio-Energy in Rensselaer, Indiana. Harvestone states that the platform was formed in 2022 by Harvestone Group and Energy Capital Partners and has capacity of more than 200 million gallons per year. In 2024 the company announced a $205 million carbon-capture tax-equity financing for the Blue Flint CCS project, further shifting the platform toward lower-carbon fuel production.
historicalMilestones: 2022 - Harvestone Low Carbon Partners was formed by Harvestone Group and Energy Capital Partners.
2023 - The platform continued integrating Blue Flint, Dakota Spirit, and Iroquois Bio-Energy under a low-carbon strategy.
2024-09-17 - Harvestone announced $205 million of tax-equity financing for the Blue Flint carbon capture project.
2025 - Harvestone continued operating more than 200 million gallons per year of ethanol capacity.
2026 - ECP continued to list Harvestone Low Carbon Partners as a current portfolio company.

oneSentenceDescription: Building-efficiency and clean-energy financing platform funding decarbonization, resiliency and infrastructure upgrades through a no-upfront-cost model.
profileSubsector: Energy-as-a-service financing for efficiency and distributed energy
ownershipDetails: Firm: ECP | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Metrus Energy finances, owns, and manages energy efficiency, renewable energy, and resiliency upgrades for commercial, industrial, higher education, and other institutional customers. Its customers use the company's energy-as-a-service structure to procure building and onsite energy infrastructure without upfront capital spending, while projects are installed and maintained through partner contractors. The business follows an asset-heavy, contracted model in which project cash flows are tied to long-term service agreements rather than merchant energy sales. Public disclosures state that Metrus has operational projects in 32 states across more than 760 sites, giving it a broad national footprint in distributed decarbonization infrastructure. ECP acquired the company in July 2022, and Metrus has since continued to scale its platform around energy efficiency, distributed generation, cooling, heating, and related low-carbon infrastructure.
historicalMilestones: 2009 - Metrus Energy was founded and launched its energy-as-a-service financing model.
2022-07-07 - Metrus announced its acquisition by ECP.
2023 - Metrus continued scaling its national efficiency and clean energy financing platform under ECP ownership.
2024 - Metrus reported more than 760 project sites across 32 states in its public materials.

ownershipVehicle: Formed by ECP via Borrego Solar development spin-off
oneSentenceDescription: U.S. solar and storage developer focused on commercial, community and utility-scale projects, with a 15 GW solar and 7 GW storage pipeline.
ownershipDetails: Firm: ECP | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: New Leaf Energy develops utility-scale, commercial, and community solar and storage projects for utilities, corporate offtakers, and community energy programs in the United States. The company operates as a development platform that originates sites, secures permits and interconnection, advances offtake arrangements, and manages projects through financing and construction handoff. Its model is development-led and contracted rather than merchant, with value tied to a pipeline of permitted and construction-ready projects across multiple power markets. ECP and company materials describe a development portfolio of approximately 15 gigawatts of solar and 7 gigawatts of storage, which provides a large national scale indicator for the platform. The business was formed in July 2022 through the spin-off of Borrego's development business backed by ECP, and it now operates as a standalone renewables developer under that ownership.
historicalMilestones: 1980 - Borrego Solar, the predecessor development platform from which New Leaf Energy was later formed, traces its roots to 1980.
2022-07-28 - ECP announced the formation of New Leaf Energy through the spin-off of Borrego's development business.
2022 - New Leaf Energy launched as a standalone U.S. solar and storage development company.
2024 - Public materials described the platform as having approximately 15 GW of solar and 7 GW of storage in development.

oneSentenceDescription: Developer and operator of the Project Traveler facility on the Houston Ship Channel, producing alkylate blendstock that lowers vehicle emissions.
profileSubsector: Alkylate production, processing, and fuels logistics
ownershipDetails: Firm: ECP | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Next Wave Energy Partners develops, owns, and operates downstream and midstream assets focused on alkylate, natural gas liquids, petrochemicals, and related logistics services. Its customers include refiners, blenders, and other industrial counterparties that require toll processing, manufacturing, and logistics solutions for fuels and petrochemical value chains. The company follows an asset-heavy operating model centered on owned processing and terminal infrastructure rather than an advisory or marketing model. Its principal operating footprint is in the Houston Ship Channel area, where the company developed Project Traveler on a 53-acre site in Pasadena, Texas. ECP committed up to $500 million to the company in 2015, and the platform reached a major operating milestone when Project Traveler entered commercial operations in 2024.
historicalMilestones: 2014 - Next Wave Energy Partners was formed.
2015-04-14 - Next Wave announced an investment of up to $500 million from ECP.
2019-11-20 - The company announced a final investment decision to build Project Traveler near the Houston Ship Channel.
2024-03-20 - Project Traveler entered commercial operations in Pasadena, Texas.
2024-05-28 - Public disclosures described startup completion of the stand-alone alkylation complex.

oneSentenceDescription: Commercial and community solar platform developing, financing and managing distributed projects across the United States.
profileSubsector: Distributed and community solar and storage
ownershipDetails: Firm: ECP | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Pivot Energy develops, finances, builds, owns, and manages commercial, community-scale, and utility-oriented solar and energy storage projects in the United States. Its customers and counterparties include businesses, utilities, cooperatives, municipalities, community solar subscribers, and tax credit or capital providers. The platform operates as an asset-heavy independent power producer and developer, with contracted project revenues and development value rather than merchant retail power exposure. Public materials state that Pivot has nearly 1,900 projects completed or under development totaling more than 4 gigawatts of solar capacity nationwide. ECP acquired Pivot in June 2021, and the company has continued to expand under that ownership through project financing and new offtake arrangements.
historicalMilestones: 2009 - Pivot Energy was founded.
2021-06-01 - ECP completed its acquisition of Pivot Energy.
2024-08-08 - Pivot announced a framework agreement with Microsoft for up to 500 MWac of community-scale solar development.
2025 - Pivot reported 3.5 GW of renewable energy completed or in development in its 2024 ESG reporting.
2026-01-29 - Pivot disclosed that projects completed or under development had reached roughly 4.3 GW.

sectorVertical: Electricity infrastructure / gas turbines & peaking generation
oneSentenceDescription: Sedalia, Missouri-based aeroderivative gas-turbine platform combining WattBridge’s 2.4 GW ERCOT peakers, AeroAdvantage services and PowerFLX OEM/EPC capabilities.
profileSubsector: Fast-start gas generation equipment, services, and contracted power
ownershipDetails: Firm: ECP | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Majority
companyOverview: PROENERGY manufactures, services, and operates aeroderivative power generation equipment and related contracted generation assets for utilities, municipalities, data centers, and industrial customers. Its business spans turbine and package manufacturing, turnkey project execution, contracted power, and lifecycle maintenance, which gives the company exposure to both equipment and operating infrastructure cash flows. The operating model is asset-heavy and service-intensive, with revenue supported by long-lived power assets, maintenance contracts, and engineered equipment delivery. Public disclosures tie the platform to headquarters and manufacturing operations in Sedalia, Missouri and to the WattBridge portfolio of approximately 2.4 gigawatts of contracted gas peaking capacity in ERCOT. ECP acquired a majority stake in August 2024, positioning the company around fast-start generation, aero services, and flexible power solutions.
historicalMilestones: 2002 - PROENERGY was founded.
2024-08-13 - ECP announced the acquisition of a majority stake in PROENERGY.
2024 - ECP's transaction disclosure highlighted the inclusion of the WattBridge portfolio in ERCOT.
2025 - PROENERGY continued operating across manufacturing, lifecycle care, and contracted power lines under ECP ownership.

oneSentenceDescription: Closed-loop fresh-oil delivery and used-cooking-oil collection network serving roughly 32,000 restaurants and food-service customers across the U.S.
profileSubsector: Commercial kitchen oil management and back-of-house services
ownershipDetails: Firm: ECP | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Restaurant Technologies provides automated cooking oil management, hood and flue cleaning, and related back-of-house services to restaurants and other foodservice operators. Its customer base includes quick-service and full-service restaurant chains, independent restaurants, grocery delis, hotels, casinos, universities, and hospitals. The company operates a route-based, service-heavy model supported by installed equipment and a depot network rather than a purely asset-light software or marketplace model. Public disclosures state that it serves more than 45,000 customer locations through 41 depots with more than 1,200 employees across the United States. ECP agreed to acquire the company in February 2022, and Restaurant Technologies now operates as an essential outsourced kitchen services platform under that ownership.
historicalMilestones: 1999 - Restaurant Technologies was founded.
2022-02-27 - ECP announced an agreement to acquire Restaurant Technologies from Goldman Sachs Asset Management.
2022 - The company transitioned to ECP ownership.
2025 - Public company materials reported a national footprint of more than 45,000 customers served through 41 depots.

oneSentenceDescription: Mid-Atlantic broadband, fiber and tower operator; ECP backed the Horizon Telcom acquisition, the combined network reached about 15,400 fiber route miles across seven states at closing, and ECP still has current board representation.
profileSubsector: Rural fiber broadband, wireless towers, and telecom networks
principalLocationOfOperations: Virginia, West Virginia, Maryland, and Pennsylvania
ownershipDetails: Firm: ECP | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Shenandoah Telecommunications Company, or Shentel, provides broadband, fiber, tower, and related communications infrastructure services in rural and secondary markets in the Mid-Atlantic. Its customers include residential broadband subscribers, enterprise and wholesale telecom users, and wireless carriers using tower and backhaul infrastructure. The business follows an asset-heavy network ownership model with recurring service and leasing revenues tied to fiber systems, cable and broadband assets, and communications towers. Public disclosures describe a footprint across Virginia, West Virginia, Maryland, and Pennsylvania, supported by thousands of route miles of fiber and a macro tower portfolio that was partially monetized in 2024. ECP completed a preferred equity investment in October 2023 as an existing shareholder, and the company expanded its regional footprint further through the acquisition of Horizon Telcom in April 2024.
historicalMilestones: 1902 - The business was founded as the Farmers Mutual Telephone System of Shenandoah County.
2023-10-24 - ECP completed a preferred equity investment in Shentel.
2024-03-01 - Shentel closed the sale of its tower portfolio and entered a leaseback arrangement for wireless locations.
2024-04-01 - Shentel completed its acquisition of Horizon Telcom.
2025 - Shentel continued integration and expansion of its Mid-Atlantic fiber and broadband platform.

oneSentenceDescription: Utility-scale renewable developer focused primarily on wind projects in the central and western United States.
profileSubsector: Utility-scale wind and hybrid renewable development
ownershipDetails: Firm: ECP | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Triple Oak Power develops utility-scale renewable energy projects with a primary focus on wind generation and selected hybrid solar and storage opportunities. Its customers and counterparties are utilities, corporate power buyers, and other offtakers that contract for large-scale renewable generation output. The company operates a development-led model in which value is created through siting, permitting, interconnection, and offtake origination rather than through retail power sales. Public disclosures state that the platform manages an 8 gigawatt-plus project pipeline spanning more than a dozen states in the central and western United States. The business was formed in 2020 and ECP acquired the company in late 2023, providing additional capital to advance its development portfolio.
historicalMilestones: 2020 - Triple Oak Power was established.
2023-12 - ECP acquired Triple Oak Power.
2024-01-09 - Triple Oak announced new ownership by ECP and disclosed an 8 GW-plus project pipeline.
2025-09-03 - The company announced a $60 million financing arrangement from First Citizens Bank.
2026-03-10 - Triple Oak disclosed a pipeline spanning 15 states in connection with new project advancement.

ownershipVehicle: Owned by employees, ECP and Goldman Sachs
oneSentenceDescription: Developer, owner and operator of large-scale multimodal logistics centers and energy-related midstream infrastructure across North America.
profileSubsector: Multi-modal energy logistics terminals and infrastructure
ownershipDetails: Firm: ECP | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Goldman Sachs | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: USD management and employees | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: USD Group designs, develops, owns, and manages large-scale multi-modal logistics centers and energy-related midstream infrastructure across North America. Its customers are producers, refiners, and marketers that require origin-to-destination logistics solutions linking Western Canada, Cushing, and the U.S. Gulf Coast energy corridor. The company operates an asset-heavy model built around terminal, storage, rail, and other logistics infrastructure rather than an asset-light brokerage model. Public materials describe a portfolio of irreplaceable logistics terminals and identify Houston, Texas as the corporate base for the platform's North American operations. ECP partnered with the company in 2014, and current public disclosures state that USDG is owned by its employees, ECP, and Goldman Sachs.

oneSentenceDescription: Vision Ridge lists Sparkfund in SAF I, and Sparkfund says it deploys distributed energy resources for utilities from grid edge to grid core.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Vision Ridge Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Sparkfund develops and manages distributed energy deployment programs and energy-as-a-service offerings for utilities, energy companies, and building owners. Its customer base spans utilities, energy majors, and large-load or commercial hosts that need distributed assets deployed at scale to support grid and building use cases. The company operates a contracted, service-led model that coordinates program design, host engagement, asset deployment, and value-chain management rather than merchant power sales. Public materials describe more than a decade of operating history and a national role in distributed capacity procurement and other distributed energy resource deployment programs. Vision Ridge invested in the platform and continues to identify Sparkfund as an active portfolio company focused on scaling distributed energy infrastructure in the built environment.
historicalMilestones: 2013 - Sparkfund was founded.
2017 - Vision Ridge participated in Sparkfund's Series B financing.
2025-03-18 - Sparkfund announced the appointment of a chief financial officer as it continued scaling utility and DER deployment services.

oneSentenceDescription: Vision Ridge lists Homer in both SAF I and SAF II as a California water platform with water in storage, water rights, and water-storage infrastructure.
profileSubsector: Water rights, storage, and groundwater infrastructure
ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Homer is a California water asset platform comprising water in storage, water rights, and related water storage infrastructure. The company follows an asset-heavy model based on owned gas collection, processing, and interconnection infrastructure supported by long-term operating and offtake arrangements. Vision Ridge committed an initial $100 million to establish the platform in 2021, and Vision RNG has since continued adding project agreements in the landfill gas market.
