oneSentenceDescription: Canadian developer and owner of carrier-neutral communications infrastructure; Northleaf committed an initial C$100 million in March 2025 and later identified Shared Tower as a NICP IV investment.
profileSubsector: Carrier-neutral towers, fiber, and edge colocation
ownershipDetails: Firm: Northleaf Capital Partners | Fund: NICP IV | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Shared Tower develops and owns carrier-neutral communications infrastructure, including tower sites, fiber assets, and edge colocation facilities. Its customers are wireless and wireline network operators that use shared infrastructure to expand coverage and capacity without duplicating capital investment. The business follows an asset-heavy, long-term contracted model in which site development and infrastructure ownership support recurring lease and service revenues. Public disclosures describe a Canada-wide development strategy from a base in Oakville, Ontario, with facilities designed to connect to diverse network providers and data center applications. Northleaf committed an initial C$100 million to the company in March 2025 through NICP IV after Shared Tower had been established in 2021.
historicalMilestones: 2021 - Shared Tower was established.
2021 - The company launched its Oakville, Ontario platform for neutral-host communications infrastructure.
2025-03-12 - Northleaf committed an initial C$100 million to Shared Tower.
2025 - Northleaf identified Shared Tower as an NICP IV investment.
2026 - Shared Tower continued marketing tower, fiber, and edge colocation development services across Canada.

ownershipVehicle: Northleaf-managed funds + AVALT (specific fund not named)
oneSentenceDescription: North American route-based laundry infrastructure services company serving the multi-residential market, with growth focused on core markets in the U.S. and Canada.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Northleaf Capital Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: AVALT Holdings | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: WASH provides route-based common-area laundry services and related equipment infrastructure to multifamily apartments, universities, and other shared-housing properties. Its customers are property owners and operators that outsource machine ownership, installation, servicing, payment systems, and maintenance under long-term contractual arrangements. The company follows an asset-heavy model supported by a large installed base of laundry machines, branch operations, and field service capabilities rather than a pure equipment reseller model. Public disclosures describe more than 34 U.S. and Canadian branches, roughly 660,000 installed machines, and service to more than 80,000 locations across North America. Northleaf and AVALT announced the acquisition of WASH from EQT in July 2025, with closing expected later that year.
historicalMilestones: 1947 - WASH was established.
2015 - EQT Infrastructure II acquired WASH.
2025-07-15 - Northleaf and AVALT announced the acquisition of WASH from EQT.
2025 - Public disclosures described approximately 660,000 installed machines across more than 80,000 locations.
2025 - The company continued serving multifamily and university customers under long-term contracts across the United States and Canada.

oneSentenceDescription: EV charging platform deploying Level 2 and Level 3 chargers across North America under an infrastructure-as-a-service model, with Northleaf committing $200 million.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Northleaf Capital Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Controlling stake
companyOverview: EVPassport deploys and operates electric vehicle charging infrastructure for enterprise property owners, parking operators, hospitality venues, multifamily properties, and other commercial customers. Its offering combines hardware deployment, software, payment functionality, and ongoing operations in an infrastructure-as-a-service model designed to reduce customer upfront capital requirements. The business is asset-heavy because capital is committed to charger deployment and network operations, while revenues are supported by long-term commercial site relationships rather than retail vehicle sales. Public reporting states that the platform had expanded to 35 U.S. states as well as Canada and Mexico, and the company markets itself as an enterprise charging network across North America. Northleaf committed US$200 million in October 2023 and acquired a controlling stake to accelerate the build-out of EVPassport's charging systems.
historicalMilestones: 2020 - EVPassport was founded, according to public reporting.
2022 - EVPassport partnered with Phoenix Sky Harbor International Airport on a Level 2 charging deployment.
2023-10-12 - Northleaf committed US$200 million to EVPassport and acquired a controlling stake.
2023 - Public reporting described EVPassport as active in 35 U.S. states plus Canada and Mexico.
2025-12-09 - EVPassport said Northleaf continued to support its capital program through infrastructure funds.

oneSentenceDescription: Ontario-based sub-metering and building-automation provider; Northleaf later used Provident for a 2025 tuck-in acquisition of PowerStream, confirming it remains an existing infrastructure portfolio investment.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Northleaf Capital Partners | Fund: NICP IV | Ownership Interest: Majority
Firm: Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Minority
companyOverview: Provident Energy Management provides sub-metering, billing, and building automation services focused primarily on the multi-residential new-construction and existing-building market. Its customers are condominium, rental, and other residential property owners and developers that outsource utility metering, energy management, and monitoring infrastructure. The business follows a contracted infrastructure-services model anchored by installed metering systems, automation controls, long-term billing relationships, and ongoing service revenues. Public materials describe the company as an Ontario-based platform with longstanding specialization in the Greater Toronto Area multi-residential market. Northleaf acquired a majority stake in August 2023 through NICP IV while management retained a minority interest, and the platform completed the tuck-in acquisition of PowerStream Energy Services in October 2025.
historicalMilestones: 1985 - Provident was incorporated.
2023-08-02 - Northleaf acquired a majority stake in Provident while management retained a minority interest.
2023 - Northleaf identified Provident as an NICP IV investment.
2025-10-07 - Provident acquired PowerStream Energy Services.
2026 - Provident continued marketing sub-metering and building automation services in Ontario.

oneSentenceDescription: New York-based wholesale FTTP fiber platform that Northleaf backed with an initial $200 million to expand underserved U.S. neighborhoods.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Northleaf Capital Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Tillman Global Holdings | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Tillman Fiber designs, builds, and owns open-access fiber broadband infrastructure for residential, business, and wholesale network customers. Its business model is asset-heavy and centered on fiber-to-the-home construction and ownership, with wholesale access revenues rather than a legacy retail telecom footprint. The company targets underserved neighborhoods in the United States where new-build fiber can support long-duration network utilization and third-party service relationships. Public materials state that Tillman Fiber was founded in 2021, is based in New York, and aims to provide wholesale network access across industries and geographies. Northleaf announced a strategic partnership with an initial US$200 million investment in August 2023 and later expanded the commitment to US$500 million in December 2023.
historicalMilestones: 2021 - Tillman FiberCo was founded.
2023-08-10 - Northleaf announced an initial US$200 million investment in Tillman FiberCo.
2023-08-10 - Northleaf and Tillman FiberCo formalized a strategic partnership to scale open-access fiber deployment.
2023-12-20 - Tillman FiberCo and Northleaf expanded the funding relationship to US$500 million.
2026 - Tillman continued to market itself as an open-access fiber network owner and developer.

oneSentenceDescription: Provider of solar-powered electric-fence and perimeter security infrastructure across the continental United States, where Northleaf and AVALT acquired a majority interest.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Northleaf Capital Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Majority
Firm: AVALT Holdings | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: AMAROK installs, operates, monitors, and maintains electric fencing and related perimeter security infrastructure for commercial and industrial properties. Its customers span logistics, equipment, distribution, manufacturing, and other operating sites that need outsourced perimeter protection rather than in-house guard operations. The company follows an asset-heavy contracted model in which fencing, monitoring, surveillance, and service infrastructure support recurring revenues under ongoing agreements. Public disclosures state that the business operates thousands of installed sites across the continental United States and has roots dating back to a guard-dog security service founded in 1973. Northleaf and AVALT acquired a majority interest in December 2021 to support the company's expansion under its perimeter security infrastructure model.
historicalMilestones: 1973 - The business that became AMAROK was founded by Bill Mullis.
2021-12-13 - Northleaf and AVALT announced the acquisition of AMAROK.
2021 - Public disclosures described approximately 5,000 installed sites under service agreements at the time of the acquisition.
2026-03-24 - South Carolina announced AMAROK's headquarters expansion in Columbia.
2026 - AMAROK reported that it secures more than 9,000 commercial businesses.

oneSentenceDescription: 210.1 MW wind farm located in northwest Texas.
ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Canadian Breaks is an operating onshore wind asset in northwest Texas that supplies renewable power into the regional market. Its end market is wholesale electricity, with cash flows supported by the operating characteristics and contracting profile of the wind project rather than a diversified utility platform. The asset follows an asset-heavy renewable generation model based on owned turbines, interconnection, and long-term operating infrastructure. Public sources describe a 210.1 MW project in Deaf Smith County, Texas, and Northleaf lists the asset as an active infrastructure investment made in 2019. Northleaf's current ownership interest is not publicly disclosed, but the firm continues to identify Canadian Breaks as part of its infrastructure portfolio.
historicalMilestones: 2019 - Northleaf made its investment in Canadian Breaks.
2019 - Public records indicate the project entered operation in 2019.
2024-05-01 - a Texas PUC filing updated the project's Renewable Energy Credit metering configuration.
2026 - Northleaf continued to identify Canadian Breaks as an active infrastructure investment.

ownershipVehicle: Infrastructure funds managed by Northleaf (100% acquired)
oneSentenceDescription: Calgary-based contracted energy platform; CSV disclosed that Northleaf infrastructure funds acquired 100% of CSV Midstream, including the Resthaven and Pipestone South assets.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Northleaf Capital Partners | Fund: Infrastructure funds managed by Northleaf | Ownership Interest: 100%
companyOverview: CSV Midstream develops, owns, and operates natural gas and natural gas liquids processing infrastructure in Alberta's Montney region. Its customers are upstream producers that require gas processing, compression, gathering, and related midstream services under contracted arrangements. The business follows an asset-heavy model centered on gas plants, compressor stations, and associated facilities rather than a pure marketing or advisory approach. Public materials state that CSV has grown from a single gas plant in 2019 to a network of six plants serving Grande Prairie, Grande Cache, and surrounding areas of northern Alberta. Northleaf acquired 100% of CSV in June 2019, including the Resthaven gas processing facility and Pipestone South compressor station, and has remained the company's disclosed infrastructure sponsor.
historicalMilestones: 2014 - CSV Midstream was founded in Alberta.
2018 - Resthaven came online as CSV's first greenfield gas plant.
2019-06-28 - Northleaf acquired 100% of CSV Midstream.
2019-08-01 - CSV completed the acquisition of the Karr 6-18 natural gas facility from Paramount Resources.
2026 - CSV's public materials described a network of six midstream plants in northern Alberta.

ownershipVehicle: Funds managed by Northleaf (49% OTUS stake)
oneSentenceDescription: Portfolio of bulk liquid storage terminals in Texas and South Carolina; Odfjell disclosed that Northleaf funds bought the 49% shareholding in its U.S. terminals business.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Northleaf Capital Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 49%
Firm: Odfjell SE | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 51%
companyOverview: Odfjell Terminals US owns and operates bulk liquid storage terminals that handle chemicals, oil products, and other specialty liquids at major shipping locations. Its customers are industrial and trading counterparties that require marine-connected storage and handling infrastructure rather than inland distribution assets. The business follows an asset-heavy contracted terminal model built around tanks, marine access, and associated operating infrastructure at strategic ports. Public materials describe U.S. terminals in Houston, Texas, and Charleston, South Carolina as part of Odfjell's global terminal network. Funds managed by Northleaf acquired a 49% shareholding in June 2019, with Odfjell SE retaining 51% and continuing as the industrial partner.
historicalMilestones: 2019-06-07 - Odfjell announced the closing of Northleaf's acquisition of a 49% stake in Odfjell Holdings (US) Inc.
2019 - Northleaf listed Odfjell Terminals as an active infrastructure investment.
2024-12-23 - Odfjell disclosed a dispute with Northleaf regarding leverage at the U.S. terminals joint venture.
2026 - Odfjell continued to report U.S. terminal operations in Houston and Charleston.

oneSentenceDescription: 8.8-mile toll road south of Richmond, Virginia.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Northleaf Capital Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Globalvia | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Pocahontas Parkway is a tolled highway south of Richmond that connects key corridors around the Richmond International Airport and the James River crossing. Its end users are passenger and commercial vehicle drivers, while cash flow is supported by toll collections under a long-dated concession structure rather than a regulated utility framework. The asset follows an asset-heavy transportation concession model requiring ongoing roadway, bridge, tolling, and customer-service infrastructure. Public sources describe an 8.8-mile, four-lane road in Virginia, and Northleaf lists the asset as an active investment made with Globalvia in 2017. Northleaf's exact ownership percentage is not publicly disclosed, but the firm invested alongside Globalvia after Globalvia acquired the concession in late 2016.
historicalMilestones: 1998 - Construction began on the Pocahontas Parkway project.
2002 - The toll road opened to traffic in stages.
2006 - Virginia's 99-year concession structure was in place for the asset.
2016-12-20 - Globalvia completed its purchase of the Pocahontas Parkway concession.
2017 - Northleaf recorded its investment in Pocahontas Parkway alongside Globalvia.

ownershipVehicle: Majority ownership stake by Northleaf; partner Apex Clean Energy
oneSentenceDescription: 217 MW Texas renewables portfolio comprising Cotton Plains Wind, Old Settler Wind, and Phantom Solar; Northleaf bought a majority stake from Apex.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Northleaf Capital Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Majority
Firm: Apex Clean Energy | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Cotton Plains Portfolio is a diversified Texas renewable generation portfolio comprising wind and solar assets. Its end markets are wholesale electricity and contracted power offtake, with revenues supported by operating renewable assets rather than development-stage land positions alone. The portfolio follows an asset-heavy generation model built around three projects and their related interconnection and operating infrastructure. Public materials describe a 217 MW portfolio consisting of the 50.4 MW Cotton Plains Wind facility, the 151.2 MW Old Settler Wind facility, and the 15.4 MWac Phantom Solar facility. Northleaf acquired a majority ownership stake from Apex Clean Energy in July 2016 and continues to list the portfolio as an active infrastructure investment.
historicalMilestones: 2016-07-14 - Apex sold a majority ownership stake in the 217 MW portfolio to Northleaf.
2016 - Public disclosures identified Cotton Plains Wind, Old Settler Wind, and Phantom Solar as the portfolio assets.
2016 - The transaction supported the U.S. Army's renewable energy power purchase arrangement at Fort Hood.
2026 - Northleaf continued to identify Cotton Plains Portfolio as an active infrastructure investment.

oneSentenceDescription: Underground downtown Chicago parking concession; FHWA says Northleaf and AMP Capital acquired the concession through Millennium Parking Garages LLC in 2016.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Northleaf Capital Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: AMP Capital | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Millennium Parking Garages operates the underground downtown Chicago parking concession beneath Millennium, Grant, and Maggie Daley Parks. Its end users are commuters, residents, tourists, and event visitors, while revenues are derived from parking fees under a long-term concession framework. The asset follows an asset-heavy transportation model based on four underground garages, traffic management systems, and customer access infrastructure. Public sources describe 9,176 parking spaces across 3.8 million square feet, making it the largest underground parking system in the United States. Northleaf and AMP Capital acquired the concession assets in 2016 through Millennium Parking Garages, LLC, and Northleaf continues to list the investment as active.
historicalMilestones: 2006 - The original 99-year lease for the Chicago Downtown Public Parking System was agreed.
2016-05 - Northleaf and AMP Capital completed the acquisition of the Millennium Garages concession assets.
2016 - FHWA identified Millennium Parking Garages, LLC as the concession purchaser.
2026 - The system continued operating four underground garages beneath downtown Chicago parks.

oneSentenceDescription: Provider of converged communications infrastructure and services addressing outdoor, real-estate, community, and enterprise advanced-connectivity needs in the United States.
profileSubsector: Distributed antenna systems, small cells, and metro fiber
ownershipDetails: Firm: Manulife Investment Management-led consortium | Fund: Manulife Infrastructure Fund II | Ownership Interest: Approximately 30%
Firm: DigitalBridge | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Stonepeak | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Extenet develops and operates distributed wireless networks, small cells, fiber, and related connectivity infrastructure for mobile operators, enterprises, venues, and real estate owners. Its business model is asset-heavy and based on owning or controlling communications infrastructure that supports recurring carrier and enterprise revenues rather than consumer subscriptions. The company serves advanced-connectivity use cases across outdoor, in-building, community, and enterprise environments in major U.S. markets. Public materials state that the business was founded in 2002 and later relocated its headquarters to Frisco, Texas while maintaining a national operating footprint. Northleaf lists Extenet as a November 2015 investment, while later public ownership disclosures in 2021 identified a Manulife-led consortium, Digital Colony, and Stonepeak as equity holders.
historicalMilestones: 2002 - ExteNet was founded.
2015-07-23 - ExteNet announced a capital restructuring involving Northleaf, Stonepeak, DigitalBridge's predecessor, and other investors.
2015-11 - Northleaf recorded its investment date for ExteNet.
2021-05-10 - ExteNet announced the closing of the Manulife-led investment representing approximately 30% ownership.
2022-12-15 - ExteNet rebranded as Extenet.

oneSentenceDescription: 38 MW operating geothermal facility on Hawaii Island.
ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Puna Geothermal Venture is an operating geothermal power facility on Hawai'i Island that supplies renewable electricity under power purchase arrangements. Its end market is utility-scale power generation, with revenues tied to contracted electricity sales rather than merchant retail activity. The asset follows an asset-heavy renewable generation model based on geothermal wells, plant equipment, and interconnection infrastructure located in the Puna District. Public sources describe a 38 MW operating facility and the only commercial geothermal power plant in Hawaii, with Ormat as the operating sponsor and development partner. Northleaf invested in April 2015 through a partnership with Ormat covering certain power plants that included Puna, but the specific current ownership percentages attributable to this facility are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2015-02-05 - Ormat announced a US$175 million agreement with Northleaf for a 40% equity investment in certain power plants.
2015-04 - Northleaf recorded its investment in Puna Geothermal Venture with Ormat as partner.
2020-11-05 - Ormat resumed operations at the Puna plant after the 2018 eruption-related shutdown.
2024-02-12 - Ormat secured approval for amendments to Puna's power purchase agreement and planned expansion.

oneSentenceDescription: Portfolio of three public-private partnership assets in Canada.
ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Maple PPP Portfolio is a portfolio of three operating public-private partnership assets in Canada. The end users are public-sector counterparties and communities that rely on essential infrastructure delivered under long-term concession or availability-based contractual structures. The portfolio follows an asset-heavy contracted model characteristic of PPP assets, with cash flows linked to long-duration project agreements rather than open-market demand alone. Public disclosures provide limited detail beyond the fact that the platform comprises three Canadian PPP assets and remains an active Northleaf infrastructure investment. Northleaf made the investment in December 2014, but the individual asset identities and current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed in the public sources reviewed.
historicalMilestones: 2014-12 - Northleaf made its investment in Maple PPP Portfolio.
2017-01 - Public reporting on Northleaf's second infrastructure fund identified Maple PPP Portfolio as an existing investment.
2026 - Northleaf continued to list Maple PPP Portfolio as an active infrastructure investment.

oneSentenceDescription: Clean-fuels developer with projects underway across British Columbia and Alberta, making it Quinbrook’s first Canadian investment.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Strategic ownership stake
companyOverview: Elemental Clean Fuels develops renewable fuel projects intended to support industrial decarbonization, transportation fuel switching, and broader clean-fuels deployment in North America. Its end markets include industrial users, transportation networks, and communities seeking supply from low-carbon fuels such as clean hydrogen, renewable natural gas, and low-carbon methanol. The platform follows an asset-heavy development model built around project origination, permitting, and future operating infrastructure rather than a fuel trading-only strategy. Public disclosures describe multiple projects underway across Alberta and British Columbia and position the company as Quinbrook's first investment in Canada. Quinbrook acquired a strategic ownership stake in October 2025, two years after the company was founded in 2023.
historicalMilestones: 2023 - Elemental Clean Fuels was founded.
2025-10-15 - Quinbrook announced a strategic ownership stake in Elemental Clean Fuels.
2025 - Quinbrook described the investment as its first in Canada.
2026 - Elemental continued advancing projects in Alberta and British Columbia.

oneSentenceDescription: Oregon RNG facility in the Pacific Northwest designed to produce about 730,000 MMBtu per year from agricultural residue.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Junction City Biomethane is an operating renewable natural gas facility that converts agricultural residue into pipeline-quality RNG. Its end markets include transportation and agricultural decarbonization applications that require low-carbon gaseous fuel supply rather than conventional fossil gas alone. The asset follows an asset-heavy processing model based on anaerobic digestion, gas upgrading, and pipeline interconnection infrastructure. Public materials state that the Oregon facility began commercial operations in 2021 and is designed to produce approximately 730,000 MMBtu of RNG per year. Quinbrook acquired the facility in 2025 and now identifies it as an active portfolio asset.
historicalMilestones: 2021 - The Junction City facility began commercial operations.
2021-09-28 - Shell announced initial RNG production at the Junction City plant.
2025 - Quinbrook acquired Junction City Biomethane.
2026 - Quinbrook continued to identify the facility as an active renewable natural gas asset.

oneSentenceDescription: Chicago-based developer, owner, and operator of standalone battery storage projects across the United States, with operating assets in PJM and ERCOT.
profileSubsector: Battery energy storage development and operation
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: GlidePath develops, owns, and operates standalone battery energy storage and related advanced energy assets across the United States. Its end markets are wholesale power systems and grid operators that need flexible storage capacity to support reliability and the integration of intermittent renewable generation. The platform follows an asset-heavy model built around battery storage development, construction, ownership, and market optimization rather than advisory or software-only services. Public materials describe the company as Chicago-based with a nationwide portfolio and development pipeline of battery storage and solar-plus-storage projects. Quinbrook acquired GlidePath in 2017 and continues to use the platform as a U.S. battery storage development and ownership vehicle.
historicalMilestones: 2017 - Quinbrook acquired GlidePath.
2018-01 - GlidePath acquired a wind power portfolio under Quinbrook ownership.
2020-10-27 - GlidePath announced a battery recycling partnership with Renewance.
2022 - GlidePath brought the Byrd Ranch storage project into operation in Texas.
2024-01-31 - GlidePath appointed a new chief commercial officer.

oneSentenceDescription: 50 MW / 50 MWh battery storage project in Sweeny, Texas, and GlidePath’s first transmission-interconnected BESS.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Byrd Ranch is a utility-scale lithium-ion battery storage project in Sweeny, Texas that provides grid support services in ERCOT. Its end markets are wholesale ancillary services and power market applications that reward flexible storage capacity rather than traditional contracted utility rate base revenues. The project follows an asset-heavy merchant storage model supported by transmission interconnection, battery systems, and market optimization infrastructure. Public materials describe a 50 MW / 50 MWh project that began operations in 2022 and is managed through Quinbrook's GlidePath platform. Quinbrook identifies Byrd Ranch as a project acquired in 2019, with construction and operational delivery completed under GlidePath.
historicalMilestones: 2019 - Quinbrook recorded the acquisition year for Byrd Ranch.
2022-01-26 - GlidePath announced the project as an addition to its Texas battery storage portfolio.
2022 - Construction and commissioning activities were completed for the project.
2022-11-09 - Byrd Ranch began operations.
2026 - Quinbrook continued to identify Byrd Ranch as an operational project.

oneSentenceDescription: 18 MW / 12 MWh battery storage asset in Somerset, Pennsylvania, repowered in late 2024 after GlidePath’s 2019 acquisition.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Meyersdale Energy Storage is an 18 MW / 12 MWh battery project in Somerset County, Pennsylvania. The asset provides grid-supporting storage capacity to the regional power system through a standalone battery installation. Its operating model is asset-heavy and centered on owned battery infrastructure rather than a diversified utility platform. Public project materials identify the site as originally built in 2015 and repowered in late 2024 after GlidePath acquired it in 2019. The asset remains part of Quinbrook's GlidePath storage portfolio.
historicalMilestones: 2015 - Public project materials identify the Meyersdale battery as originally built in Somerset County, Pennsylvania.
2019 - GlidePath acquired the asset.
2024 - Quinbrook reported that the project was repowered in late 2024.
2024 - Quinbrook continued to identify Meyersdale as an operational GlidePath battery asset.

oneSentenceDescription: 9.9 MW / 9.9 MWh battery project in West Columbia, Texas, and one of ERCOT’s earlier standalone storage assets.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Prospect Storage is a 9.9 MW / 9.9 MWh battery project in West Columbia, Texas. The project serves the Texas power system through a dedicated standalone storage installation in ERCOT. Its operating model is asset-heavy, with GlidePath acting as project owner and operator. Public disclosures described Prospect as one of ERCOT's earlier standalone battery projects and noted that construction began in 2019. The asset remains part of Quinbrook's GlidePath portfolio.
historicalMilestones: 2019-06-17 - GlidePath announced the start of construction on Prospect Storage in West Columbia, Texas.
2019 - GlidePath described Prospect as one of the early standalone battery projects in ERCOT.
2019 - GlidePath said the platform had more than 100 MW operating and more than 1 GW in development when Prospect entered construction.
2026 - GlidePath continued to list Prospect Storage as an operating project.

oneSentenceDescription: Quinbrook’s wholly owned solar-and-storage platform develops, finances, owns, and operates utility-scale solar PV and battery projects across North America.
profileSubsector: Utility-scale solar and battery storage development
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 100%
companyOverview: Primergy develops, finances, owns, and operates utility-scale solar photovoltaic and battery storage projects across North America. Its customers are utilities, corporate offtakers, and other power-market counterparties that contract for large-scale renewable generation and storage capacity. The business model is asset-heavy and development-led, with the platform taking projects from siting and financing through construction, ownership, and operations. Company materials state that lifetime closed transactions exceeded $5 billion as of March 2026. Quinbrook launched Primergy in 2020 as a wholly owned portfolio company.
historicalMilestones: 2020-10-07 - Quinbrook launched Primergy to develop solar and battery storage projects across North America.
2022-10-19 - Quinbrook and Primergy announced APG's investment in the Gemini Solar + Storage project.
2024-04-11 - Quinbrook closed its Valley of Fire continuation fund, which acquired a 51% interest in Gemini and the remaining Valley of Fire development portfolio.
2025-07-17 - Primergy announced commercial operation of the 408 MWac Ash Creek Solar project in Texas.
2026-03-10 - Primergy reported lifetime closed transactions of more than $5 billion.

ownershipVehicle: Majority-owned by Quinbrook; APG acquired 49% during construction
oneSentenceDescription: Nevada mega-project with 690 MWac of solar and 380 MW / 1,416 MWh of storage, delivering about 10% of Nevada’s peak demand.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Quinbrook Valley of Fire Fund | Ownership Interest: 51%
Firm: APG Asset Management on behalf of ABP | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 49%
companyOverview: Gemini Solar + Storage is a utility-scale solar and battery project in Nevada with 690 MWac of solar generation and 380 MW / 1,416 MWh of storage. The project supplies contracted renewable power and storage capacity to Nevada's grid and was publicly described as capable of meeting about 10% of the state's peak power demand. Its operating model is asset-heavy and based on long-life generation and storage infrastructure. Public financing materials identify Quinbrook's Valley of Fire continuation fund as the 51% owner and APG on behalf of ABP as the 49% owner. Quinbrook and Primergy advanced the project through development and construction, and commercial operations began in July 2024.
historicalMilestones: 2022-10-19 - Quinbrook and Primergy announced APG's agreement to acquire a 49% interest in Gemini on behalf of ABP.
2024-04-11 - Quinbrook said its Valley of Fire continuation fund acquired a 51% interest in Gemini.
2024-07-18 - Gemini reached commercial operations in Nevada.
2024-07-18 - Public disclosures described Gemini as a 690 MWac solar project paired with 380 MW / 1,416 MWh of battery storage.
2025 - Financing disclosures continued to identify the project ownership split as 51% Quinbrook Valley of Fire Fund and 49% APG on behalf of ABP.

oneSentenceDescription: 409 MW solar project in Hill County, Texas, designed to power roughly 90,000 homes annually.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Ash Creek Solar is a 408 MWac utility-scale solar project in Hill County, Texas. The project serves a contracted end market, with Primergy publicly disclosing a long-term power purchase agreement with Microsoft. Its operating model is asset-heavy and centered on owned generation infrastructure developed by Primergy. Public disclosures state that Primergy acquired the project from Orion Renewable Energy Group in 2021 and brought it to commercial operation in July 2025. The project sits within Quinbrook's Primergy platform.
historicalMilestones: 2021 - Primergy acquired the Ash Creek project from Orion Renewable Energy Group.
2024-03 - Primergy signed a long-term power purchase agreement with Microsoft for the project.
2025-07-17 - Primergy announced commercial operation of Ash Creek Solar.
2025 - Public disclosures described the project as a 408 MWac solar facility in Hill County, Texas.

oneSentenceDescription: 100 MW solar project in Ashley County, Arkansas, which achieved commercial operations in November 2024.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Prairie Mist Solar is a 100 MWac utility-scale solar project in Ashley County, Arkansas. The project serves a contracted corporate end market, with company materials identifying Amazon as the offtaker. Its operating model is asset-heavy and based on owned solar generation infrastructure developed and financed by Primergy. Public disclosures show the project closed debt and tax equity financing in 2023 and reached commercial operation in December 2024. The asset forms part of Quinbrook's Primergy portfolio.
historicalMilestones: 2023-11-28 - Primergy announced $300 million of debt and tax equity financing for Prairie Mist.
2024-12-18 - Primergy announced commercial operation of Prairie Mist Solar.
2024 - Company materials described Prairie Mist as a 100 MWac project in Ashley County, Arkansas.
2024 - Primergy identified Amazon as the customer for the project.

oneSentenceDescription: Eight-project solar portfolio across Nevada, Colorado, and Arizona totaling more than 2.2 GW of solar with potential for 2.5 GW of battery storage.
profileSubsector: Solar and battery storage development portfolio
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Quinbrook Valley of Fire Fund | Ownership Interest: 100%
companyOverview: Valley of Fire is Quinbrook's utility-scale solar and storage development portfolio across Nevada, Colorado, and Arizona. The portfolio is aimed at utilities and large power-market customers seeking renewable generation and storage capacity in western U.S. markets. Its operating model is asset-heavy and development-led, with projects progressing through siting, financing, construction, and long-term ownership. Quinbrook has publicly described the portfolio as including more than 2.65 GW of solar and up to 1.5 GW of battery storage, including Gemini. Quinbrook transferred the remaining Valley of Fire development portfolio into its continuation fund in April 2024.
historicalMilestones: 2022-10-19 - Quinbrook and Primergy announced APG's investment in the Gemini project within the Valley of Fire portfolio.
2024-04-11 - Quinbrook said its continuation fund acquired the remaining Valley of Fire development portfolio.
2024-07-18 - Gemini, the portfolio's flagship project, reached commercial operation.
2026-03-10 - Primergy announced $225 million of financing for the Valley of Fire portfolio and described the pipeline as more than 2.65 GW of solar and up to 1.5 GW of storage.

oneSentenceDescription: U.S.-based renewable fuels platform that converts food-and-beverage waste into biogas, clean water, and soil supplements through anaerobic digestion.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: PurposeEnergy develops, owns, and operates organics-to-energy infrastructure that converts food and beverage waste into biogas, renewable electricity, renewable natural gas, clean water, and related by-products. Its customers are food and beverage manufacturers, breweries, processors, and other industrial waste generators that need onsite or near-site treatment and energy recovery. The operating model is asset-heavy and based on anaerobic digestion and related treatment infrastructure tied to industrial waste streams. Company materials state that the platform has developed, owned, and operated multiple projects over the last 15 years. Quinbrook acquired PurposeEnergy in 2023 as its entry platform in renewable fuels.
historicalMilestones: 2007 - Public sources identify PurposeEnergy's founding year as 2007.
2023-04-12 - Quinbrook announced the acquisition of PurposeEnergy.
2023 - Quinbrook said the acquisition marked its move into renewable fuels infrastructure.
2024-10-24 - PurposeEnergy opened its Middlebury, Vermont facility.
2025-05-29 - PurposeEnergy opened its St. Albans, Vermont facility.

oneSentenceDescription: Vermont anaerobic-digestion project that exports renewable electricity to the grid via a 1.014 MW genset and began operations in October 2024.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Middlebury Resource Recovery Center is an anaerobic digestion facility in Middlebury, Vermont that processes food and beverage waste and exports renewable electricity to the grid. The project serves industrial and municipal organic waste streams and converts them into biogas and power. Its operating model is asset-heavy and based on owned treatment and generation infrastructure, including a 1.014 MW engine generator. Public construction materials state that the facility is designed to process about 80,000 gallons per day of liquid organic waste. The project was developed by PurposeEnergy and entered operation in October 2024 after Quinbrook acquired the parent platform in 2023.
historicalMilestones: 2022-12-28 - PurposeEnergy announced the start of construction on the Middlebury project.
2023-04-12 - Quinbrook acquired PurposeEnergy, the platform developing the project.
2024-10-24 - PurposeEnergy held the grand opening for the Middlebury Resource Recovery Center.
2024 - Public construction materials stated that the project was designed to process about 80,000 gallons per day of organic waste and generate just over 1 MW of renewable electricity.

oneSentenceDescription: Ohio anaerobic-digestion project being built to convert organic waste into biogas for a co-located dairy processor’s industrial boilers.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: PurposeEnergy Minster is an anaerobic digestion project in Minster, Ohio that converts organic waste into biogas for industrial boiler use. The project serves a co-located dairy processor rather than a broad merchant energy market. Its operating model is asset-heavy and centered on dedicated waste treatment and gas production infrastructure tied to an industrial site. Quinbrook's project materials identify Minster as a construction-stage asset acquired in 2023. The project sits within Quinbrook's PurposeEnergy platform.
historicalMilestones: 2023 - Quinbrook project materials identified Minster as acquired in 2023.
2023-04-12 - Quinbrook acquired PurposeEnergy, the platform developing the Minster project.
2023 - Public project materials described the facility as converting organic waste into biogas for industrial processes.
2026 - Quinbrook continued to list Minster as a PurposeEnergy project in Ohio.

oneSentenceDescription: Operating Vermont biogas facility that supplies renewable electricity to a co-located brewery under a net-metering arrangement.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: PurposeEnergy South Burlington is an operating anaerobic digestion facility in South Burlington, Vermont. The plant processes brewery waste and related organics and uses the resulting biogas to generate renewable electricity under a net-metering arrangement. Its operating model is asset-heavy and based on owned treatment and combined heat and power infrastructure located near the customer site. Public project materials identify Zero Gravity Brewery as the associated industrial customer. The project forms part of Quinbrook's PurposeEnergy platform.
historicalMilestones: 2019 - Public construction materials described the South Burlington anaerobic digester serving Zero Gravity Brewery.
2019 - Project materials said the facility would use combined heat and power equipment and net metering.
2023-04-12 - Quinbrook acquired PurposeEnergy, the parent platform for the project.
2026 - Quinbrook continued to identify the South Burlington project within the PurposeEnergy portfolio.

oneSentenceDescription: Vermont anaerobic-digestion project co-located with an ice-cream plant that began commercial operations in December 2024.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: St. Albans Industrial Treatment, or SAINT, is an anaerobic digestion and treatment facility in St. Albans, Vermont. The project processes food manufacturing waste streams and produces renewable electricity for export to the grid. Its operating model is asset-heavy and linked to long-term industrial waste handling needs, with Ben & Jerry's publicly identified as the anchor customer partnership. Public disclosures state that the facility began exporting electricity in December 2024 and held its grand opening in May 2025. The project was developed by PurposeEnergy after Quinbrook acquired the platform in 2023.
historicalMilestones: 2023-05-23 - PurposeEnergy announced plans to build the St. Albans biogas plant in partnership with Ben & Jerry's.
2023 - Construction began on the facility.
2024-12 - The project began exporting renewable electricity.
2025-05-29 - PurposeEnergy held the grand opening for the St. Albans facility.

oneSentenceDescription: Quinbrook-created U.S. hyperscale data-centre platform with 20+ sites nationwide and more than 5.8 GW of capacity.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Rowan Digital Infrastructure develops hyperscale data center campuses and related power and connectivity infrastructure across the United States. Its customers are hyperscale and large enterprise data center users that require large blocks of power, land, and network access. The operating model is asset-heavy and development-led, with Rowan securing sites, utility capacity, and financing before building or delivering campuses. Company materials describe a U.S. development pipeline exceeding 12 GW-IT and a point-to-point connectivity ecosystem spanning more than 50 interconnection locations. Quinbrook established the platform and continues to back its expansion.
historicalMilestones: 2020-11 - Rowan Digital Infrastructure was founded.
2022 - Quinbrook said the platform had sites across 20 U.S. states.
2023-01-13 - Quinbrook announced the expansion of Rowan's green data center platform in Texas.
2025-01-16 - Rowan secured $975 million of financing for its Maryland campus.
2026 - Quinbrook described Rowan's development pipeline as exceeding 12 GW-IT.

oneSentenceDescription: Frederick, Maryland, multi-building data-centre campus planned for 626 MW of utility load across three sites.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Rowan Bauxite is a hyperscale data center campus in Frederick, Maryland. The development is designed for large cloud and data center customers seeking significant utility load and expandable campus capacity. Its operating model is asset-heavy and campus-based, with Rowan arranging power, financing, and site development for multiple buildings. Public disclosures describe the campus as planned for 626 MW of utility load across three sites, with financings announced in January and February 2025. The project sits within Quinbrook's Rowan Digital Infrastructure platform.
historicalMilestones: 2025-01-16 - Rowan secured $975 million of financing for the first phase of its Maryland campus.
2025-02-25 - Rowan secured an additional $1.24 billion of financing for the second phase of the campus.
2025 - Public disclosures described the full Bauxite campus as planned for 626 MW across three sites.
2025 - Rowan identified Bauxite as its flagship Maryland hyperscale campus.

oneSentenceDescription: 220-acre San Antonio data-centre campus with 700 MW of secured power capacity targeted before 2030.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Rowan Cinco is a hyperscale data center campus in Medina County outside San Antonio, Texas. The project is designed for large data center customers that require secure land positions and substantial power availability. Its operating model is asset-heavy and development-led, with Rowan securing land, financing, and utility capacity before vertical construction. Public disclosures describe the site as a 220-acre campus with 300 MW in its initial development and long-term power capacity that Rowan has said will support a larger buildout before 2030. The campus is being developed within Quinbrook's Rowan platform.
historicalMilestones: 2025-08-07 - Rowan announced the start of construction on the Cinco data center campus outside San Antonio.
2025 - Rowan described Cinco as a 300 MW campus on approximately 220 acres.
2026-02-12 - Rowan closed $551 million of project financing for Rowan Cinco LLC.
2026 - Quinbrook continued to identify Cinco within Rowan's broader Texas expansion.

oneSentenceDescription: Broader Austin-region data-centre development where Rowan has secured 1.6 GW of power capacity expected online between 2026 and 2030.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Rowan Temple is a hyperscale data center development in the broader Austin region of Texas. The project targets large cloud and data center customers that need phased campus delivery and substantial power capacity. Its operating model is asset-heavy and based on staged campus construction supported by long-term utility interconnection and site development. Quinbrook has described Temple as Rowan's inaugural Texas campus, and later project materials said Rowan had secured 1.6 GW of power capacity there expected online between 2026 and 2030. The campus remains part of Quinbrook's Rowan platform.
historicalMilestones: 2023-01-13 - Quinbrook announced the expansion of Rowan's Texas data center platform, including the Temple campus.
2023 - Quinbrook said the first 220 MW stage at Temple was nearing completion.
2026-01-05 - Rowan announced the start of construction on a 300 MW Temple data center phase.
2026 - Rowan said it had secured 1.6 GW of power capacity at Temple expected online between 2026 and 2030.

sectorVertical: Digital / managed cloud, network & security infrastructure
oneSentenceDescription: Managed infrastructure services provider focused on network managed services, cloud, and security, with headquarters in Fairfield, New Jersey.
profileSubsector: Managed cloud, network, and security infrastructure
ownershipDetails: Firm: Tiger Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: 11:11 Systems provides managed cloud, network, security, and connectivity infrastructure services. Its customers are enterprise and mid-market organizations that outsource IT infrastructure, connectivity, security, and disaster recovery functions. The business model is service-led rather than utility-regulated, but it relies on owned and managed digital infrastructure capabilities delivered under recurring customer relationships. Company materials describe an integrated offering spanning managed network services, cloud, security, and voice. Tiger announced its investment when the platform launched in 2021 and remains the sponsor identified in public materials.
historicalMilestones: 2021-09-23 - 11:11 Systems launched as a new managed infrastructure platform.
2021-09-23 - Tiger Infrastructure announced its investment in 11:11 Systems.
2021 - Public launch materials identified Fairfield, New Jersey as the company's headquarters.
2026 - Company materials continued to describe the platform's core services as cloud, connectivity, security, and voice.

oneSentenceDescription: Owns and operates multi-fuel stations and wholesale fuel distribution across western Pennsylvania and eastern Ohio, and current company materials describe it as Pittsburgh-based.
profileSubsector: Multi-fuel retail and wholesale fuel distribution
ownershipDetails: Firm: Tiger Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: American Natural owns and operates multi-fuel retail fueling stations and wholesale fuel distribution operations in western Pennsylvania and eastern Ohio. Its customer base includes commercial fleets, retail motorists, and wholesale fuel users seeking conventional and alternative-fuel supply. The operating model is asset-heavy and based on station, logistics, and fuel-distribution infrastructure. Public materials describe the company as Pittsburgh-based and focused on natural gas, other alternative fuels, and conventional fuel supply. Tiger is the current sponsor identified in public portfolio materials.
historicalMilestones: 2011 - Public sources identify American Natural's founding year as 2011.
2017-12-07 - American Natural announced a series of strategic transactions to support its growth plan.
2020-12-15 - Tiger Infrastructure announced an investment in American Natural.
2026 - Tiger continued to identify American Natural as an active portfolio company.

oneSentenceDescription: Builds, owns, and operates facilities that convert end-of-life tires into recovered carbon black and other sustainable materials, with headquarters in Boulder, Colorado.
profileSubsector: Circular tire recycling and recovered materials
ownershipDetails: Firm: Tiger Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Bolder Industries develops and operates tire-recycling facilities that convert end-of-life tires into recovered carbon black, pyrolysis oil, and related products. Its customers include industrial material users seeking recycled inputs and waste generators requiring tire-processing capacity. The operating model is asset-heavy and based on specialized processing plants rather than outsourced service contracts. Public materials identify Boulder, Colorado as the headquarters and describe the Maryville, Missouri facility as processing more than 2 million tires per year, with further expansion underway. Tiger announced its growth investment in Bolder in 2025 and is the current sponsor identified in public materials.
historicalMilestones: 2020 - Bolder opened its Maryville, Missouri facility.
2024 - Bolder announced the groundbreaking for its Maryville Train 2 expansion.
2025-02-06 - Tiger completed its growth investment in Bolder Industries.
2025 - Public company materials described the Maryville plant as capable of processing more than 2 million tires per year.

oneSentenceDescription: Fiber developer/operator whose initial route links Toronto, Buffalo, and New York City, and whose current platform serves North America from a Toronto base.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Tiger Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Crosslake Fibre develops and operates subsea and terrestrial fiber networks in North America. Its customers are enterprise, carrier, cloud, and content-network users that require high-capacity connectivity between major metropolitan markets. The operating model is asset-heavy and based on owned long-haul fiber infrastructure and interconnection points. Company materials describe a network with more than 40 points of presence and an initial flagship route linking Toronto, Buffalo, and New York City. Tiger invested in Crosslake in 2017 and remains the sponsor identified in public portfolio materials.
historicalMilestones: 2016 - Crosslake Fibre was founded.
2017-06-27 - Tiger Infrastructure announced its investment in Crosslake Fibre.
2019 - Crosslake launched service on its Toronto-Buffalo-New York route.
2026 - Company materials described a footprint of more than 40 points of presence.

oneSentenceDescription: Owns the 500 MW gas-fired plant near Newburgh, New York and continues to advance its repowering plan on current company materials.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Tiger Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Tiger Infrastructure Partners Fund II | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Danskammer Energy owns the 500 MW gas-fired generating facility near Newburgh, New York. The plant serves wholesale power-market demand in New York and has also been advanced as the site for a repowering effort. Its operating model is asset-heavy and based on owned generation infrastructure. Tiger acquired the business through Fund II in 2017, and public portfolio materials continue to describe the company as part of the firm's active holdings. The asset remains one of Tiger's power investments in the northeastern United States.
historicalMilestones: 2017-09-14 - Tiger announced an agreement to acquire Danskammer Energy.
2017-12-27 - Tiger completed the acquisition of Danskammer Energy through Fund II.
2017 - Public disclosures described the asset as a 500 MW gas-fired plant near Newburgh, New York.
2026 - Tiger continued to list Danskammer Energy in its active portfolio.

sectorVertical: Energy Transition / utility locating & grid services
oneSentenceDescription: Provides outsourced utility locating services under long-term contracts for major public utilities, with corporate offices in Peoria, Illinois.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Tiger Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: ELM Utility Services provides outsourced utility locating and related field services for electric, gas, water, and telecommunications utilities. Its customers are major public utilities and other network operators that rely on outsourced ticket response and damage-prevention services. The operating model is service-based but supported by a large field fleet and distributed technician footprint under long-term utility relationships. Public materials describe operations across 13 states, more than 1,400 vehicles, service to over 60 utilities, and more than 3 million locate tickets annually. Tiger completed its investment in ELM in 2025 and is the current sponsor identified in public sources.
historicalMilestones: 1998 - ELM Utility Services was founded.
2025-11-03 - Tiger completed its growth investment in ELM Utility Services.
2025 - Public disclosures described ELM as serving more than 60 utilities across 13 states.
2025 - Public disclosures also cited a fleet of more than 1,400 vehicles and annual processing of more than 3 million locate tickets.

oneSentenceDescription: Tiger-created platform that acquires and partners with residential HVAC contractors across the United States using a recurring-revenue rental model.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Tiger Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Granite Comfort acquires and partners with residential HVAC contractors across the United States. Its customer base is residential homeowners that use the platform's recurring-payment heating, cooling, and plumbing offering through local operating brands. The operating model combines service operations with installed equipment ownership and a rental-style infrastructure-as-a-service structure. Public materials identify the platform as founded in 2018 and show continued acquisition-driven geographic expansion, including entry into Georgia in 2024. Tiger is the current sponsor identified in public materials for the platform.
historicalMilestones: 2018 - Granite Comfort was founded.
2021 - Tiger identified Granite Comfort as a portfolio company.
2024-09-03 - Granite Comfort expanded into Georgia through the acquisition of Anytime Heating, Cooling & Plumbing.
2024-11-19 - Granite Comfort announced an asset-based credit facility to support its HVAC infrastructure-as-a-service model.

oneSentenceDescription: Aviation services company specializing in aircraft painting, technical services, interiors, and engineering, with U.S. headquarters in Irvine, California.
profileSubsector: Aircraft painting, interiors, and technical aviation services
ownershipDetails: Firm: Tiger Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Tiger Infrastructure Partners Fund III | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: IAC provides aircraft painting, interiors, engineering, and other technical aviation services. Its customers are commercial airlines, lessors, OEMs, and private aviation operators that outsource heavy paint and technical work. The operating model is asset-heavy and labor-intensive, relying on specialized hangars, tooling, and technical staff across multiple facilities. Company and sponsor materials identify major operating locations in Irvine, California and Shannon, Ireland, alongside a broader international facility network. Tiger Fund III acquired IAC in 2022 and remains the public sponsor of the business.
historicalMilestones: 1980 - Public sources identify IAC's founding year as 1980.
2022-12-15 - Tiger Fund III completed the acquisition of IAC.
2022 - Sponsor materials described IAC as a market-leading aviation services provider.
2026 - Company materials continued to identify operations in Irvine, California and Shannon, Ireland.

oneSentenceDescription: Independent provider of student transportation and motorcoach charter services in Minnesota and western Wisconsin, with Minnesota Coaches describing the platform as headquartered in Hastings, Minnesota.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Tiger Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: MNC Transportation provides student transportation and motorcoach charter services in Minnesota and western Wisconsin. Its customers include school districts, student transportation authorities, charter clients, and related passenger transport users. The operating model is asset-heavy and route-based, built on owned and leased bus fleets, depots, and operating contracts. Public disclosures state that the business serves thousands of students each day across 36 school districts and has grown through multiple acquisitions under the Regan family. Tiger announced its investment in the platform in 2024 and is the current sponsor identified in public materials.
historicalMilestones: 1959 - Public sources identify the platform's founding year as 1959.
2024-12-10 - Tiger announced a growth investment in Minnesota Coaches, the core MNC Transportation platform.
2024 - Public materials stated that the company served thousands of students daily across 36 school districts.
2024 - Transaction materials said the Regan family had completed 11 acquisitions before Tiger's investment.

oneSentenceDescription: FBO platform operating across the United States; Tiger announced a full Fund II exit in 2023 but also said Tiger funds would reinvest alongside Apollo, and Modern currently says it is backed by Apollo and Tiger.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Apollo Infrastructure Funds | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Majority
Firm: Tiger Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Modern Aviation operates fixed-base operations that provide fueling, hangar, ramp, and related airport services for business and general aviation users. Its customers are corporate flight departments, charter operators, and private aircraft owners using non-airline airport infrastructure. The operating model is asset-heavy and based on airport concessions, leased facilities, and service infrastructure across multiple locations. Tiger disclosed in 2023 that the company had grown to 16 locations through nine acquisitions, and the company's current materials state that it is backed by Apollo and Tiger. Tiger formed the platform in 2018 and reinvested alongside Apollo when Apollo acquired a majority stake in 2023.
historicalMilestones: 2018 - Tiger formed Modern Aviation.
2023-11-20 - Tiger announced the sale of Modern Aviation from Fund II and said Tiger funds would reinvest alongside Apollo.
2023 - Tiger said the platform had reached 16 locations through nine acquisitions.
2023-11-20 - Apollo Infrastructure Funds acquired a majority stake in Modern Aviation.
2026 - Modern Aviation continued to identify Apollo and Tiger as its backers.

oneSentenceDescription: Developing cargo airport infrastructure at Ted Stevens Anchorage International Airport in Alaska under a 55-year lease for the South Park Campus.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Tiger Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Controlling stake
companyOverview: NorthLink Aviation is developing cargo airport infrastructure at Ted Stevens Anchorage International Airport in Alaska. The project is intended for air cargo carriers and logistics users that require dedicated terminal, warehouse, and apron capacity in Anchorage. Its operating model is asset-heavy and concession-based, anchored by a long-term airport lease and phased infrastructure development. Public disclosures describe a 120-acre South Park Campus under a 55-year lease and an initial phase including a 100,000-square-foot multi-tenant air cargo terminal. Tiger announced a controlling investment in 2021 and remains the sponsor identified in public materials.
historicalMilestones: 2021-11-16 - Tiger announced its investment in NorthLink Aviation.
2021 - Tiger said the project was based on a 55-year lease for the South Park Campus at Ted Stevens Anchorage International Airport.
2021 - Public materials described an initial 100,000-square-foot multi-tenant cargo terminal on a 120-acre site.
2026 - Tiger continued to list NorthLink Aviation as an active portfolio company.

oneSentenceDescription: Kansas City-area waste logistics platform operating transfer stations and hauling assets; Tiger’s 2025 announcement says it is headquartered in Grandview, Missouri.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Tiger Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Raptor Waste Solutions operates waste transfer and hauling infrastructure in the greater Kansas City area. Its customers are waste generators, haulers, and disposal counterparties that require transfer, transport, and logistics services. The operating model is asset-heavy and route-based, supported by transfer stations, hauling assets, and local operating infrastructure. Public disclosures identify Grandview, Missouri as the headquarters and describe the business as operating two transfer stations in addition to hauling capabilities. Tiger completed its growth investment in 2025, while the founders retained an ownership stake according to the transaction announcement.
historicalMilestones: 2025-12-03 - Tiger completed its growth investment in Raptor Waste Solutions.
2025 - Transaction materials identified Grandview, Missouri as the company's headquarters.
2025 - Tiger described the platform as operating two transfer stations with integrated hauling assets.
2025 - The transaction announcement said founders Shawn Polowniak and Kit Starr retained an ownership stake.

oneSentenceDescription: Designs, builds, finances, owns, and operates in-building wireless infrastructure under long-term contracts, and was still announcing leadership expansion in 2026.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Brookfield Infrastructure | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Tiger Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Strategic Venue Partners designs, finances, owns, and operates in-building wireless infrastructure for venues and property owners. Its customers are real estate owners, venues, wireless carriers, and enterprise users that need distributed antenna systems, Wi-Fi, fiber, and public-safety communications infrastructure without self-funding the installation. The operating model is asset-heavy and contract-based, with the company funding and owning systems under long-term venue agreements. Public materials describe the platform as established in 2018 and built around long-duration contracted infrastructure assets. Tiger acquired a minority equity interest from Brookfield in 2024, and both firms remain identified in public materials.
historicalMilestones: 2018 - Strategic Venue Partners launched as a wireless infrastructure platform.
2019-02-04 - Strategic Venue Partners announced the formation of a new holding company for its wireless infrastructure assets.
2024-12-12 - Strategic Venue Partners announced financial transactions that included Tiger's minority investment from Brookfield.

oneSentenceDescription: Developing a multi-state carbon capture and storage network for Midwestern biorefineries, with official materials listing Ames, Iowa as its location.
principalLocationOfOperations: Iowa; Nebraska; North Dakota; South Dakota; Minnesota
ownershipDetails: Firm: Tiger Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Summit Carbon Solutions is developing a carbon capture and sequestration network for Midwestern ethanol plants and related industrial emitters. Its customer base is centered on biorefineries and other emitters seeking transport and permanent storage solutions for captured carbon dioxide. The operating model is asset-heavy and based on pipeline, compression, and sequestration infrastructure rather than merchant commodity exposure. Public materials describe a planned system spanning multiple Midwest states and connecting about 30 ethanol plants with expected capture volumes of more than 10 million tons per year. Tiger invested in 2022 and remains the sponsor identified in public portfolio materials.
historicalMilestones: 2022-04-27 - Summit Carbon Solutions announced Tiger Infrastructure's investment.
2022 - Public project materials described a network serving approximately 30 ethanol plants.
2022 - Summit also described expected capture volumes of more than 10 million tons of CO2 per year.
2026 - Tiger continued to identify Summit Carbon Solutions as an active portfolio company.

oneSentenceDescription: Designs, builds, owns, and operates behind-the-meter microgrids for North American commercial and industrial customers, with headquarters in Greenwich, Connecticut.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Tiger Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Unison Energy designs, builds, owns, operates, and maintains behind-the-meter microgrids for commercial and industrial customers. Its customer base includes data centers, healthcare facilities, campuses, industrial sites, and other users that require resilient onsite energy infrastructure. The operating model is asset-heavy and contract-based, with Unison providing energy-as-a-service rather than selling equipment only. Company materials describe the platform as fully integrated across development, construction, ownership, and operations. Tiger completed its acquisition-backed growth investment in 2024 and is the current sponsor identified in public materials.
historicalMilestones: 2024-06-26 - Tiger closed its growth capital investment and acquisition of Unison Energy.
2024 - Tiger described Unison as a fully integrated energy-as-a-service microgrid platform.
2026 - Company materials continued to describe the business as designing, building, owning, operating, and maintaining microgrids.
2026 - Public materials identified the company's headquarters in Greenwich, Connecticut.

oneSentenceDescription: OTPP’s current portfolio materials show ACES Delta as a live holding, and OTPP says it invested via Haddington ESP in the company’s green-hydrogen platform, whose flagship project is helping decarbonize power in the western U.S.
ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: ACES Delta is a green hydrogen production and storage platform centered on Delta, Utah. The project is being developed to support western U.S. power decarbonization by producing and storing hydrogen for dispatchable power generation and related energy uses. Its operating model is asset-heavy and development-led, built around dedicated hydrogen production, storage, and associated energy infrastructure. Ontario Teachers' has said it invested in the platform through Haddington Energy Partners, and the holding remains listed in current portfolio materials. Public sources do not fully disclose the current ownership structure of ACES Delta itself.
historicalMilestones: 2022 - Haddington Energy Partners syndicated capital to support ACES Delta.
2022 - Ontario Teachers' said it invested in ACES Delta through Haddington ESP.
2023 - ACES Delta continued advancing its flagship Utah hydrogen project.

portfolioCompany: Autopista Arco Norte, S.A. de CV (Arco Norte)
oneSentenceDescription: OTPP’s portfolio and major-investments pages identify Arco Norte as a current holding; OTPP describes it as a 224 km toll road serving central Mexico.
profileCompanyName: Autopista Arco Norte, S.A. de C.V.
principalLocationOfOperations: State of Mexico; Hidalgo; Tlaxcala; Puebla
ownershipDetails: Firm: IDEAL | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 51%
Firm: CPP Investments | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 29%
Firm: Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 20%
companyOverview: Arco Norte is a toll road concession in central Mexico that serves passenger and freight traffic around the Mexico City metropolitan area. Its customers are motorists, logistics operators, and commercial freight users seeking a bypass route for central Mexican corridors. The operating model is asset-heavy and concession-based, with revenue generated from road usage rather than ancillary service businesses. Ontario Teachers' has described the asset as a 224 kilometer toll road and a current portfolio holding within its Mexican partnership with IDEAL and CPP Investments. Public disclosures identify IDEAL, CPP Investments, and Ontario Teachers' as the current co-owners.
historicalMilestones: 2016-07-26 - IDEAL, CPP Investments, and Ontario Teachers' created a strategic partnership to invest in Mexican infrastructure assets including Arco Norte.

oneSentenceDescription: OTPP still lists the holding as Compass Holdings LLC, while its company page describes Compass Datacenters as a Dallas-based developer/operator of large data-center campuses.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Brookfield Infrastructure | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Compass Datacenters develops and operates large-scale data center campuses for hyperscale and cloud customers. Its customer base is concentrated in major technology tenants that require standardized, expandable campuses with rapid delivery schedules. The operating model is asset-heavy and build-to-suit, with the company handling site development, construction, and long-term operation of data center infrastructure. Company materials identify Dallas as the base of the business and position the platform as a specialist in hyperscale campuses across U.S. markets. Brookfield Infrastructure and Ontario Teachers' agreed to acquire the business in 2023, and Ontario Teachers' continues to hold the investment through Compass Holdings LLC.
historicalMilestones: 2011 - Public sources identify Compass Datacenters' founding year as 2011.
2023-03-08 - Brookfield Infrastructure and Ontario Teachers' agreed to acquire Compass Datacenters.
2023 - Compass continued to describe itself as a Dallas-based developer and operator of hyperscale campuses.
2026 - Ontario Teachers' continued to list Compass Holdings LLC among its major investments.

oneSentenceDescription: OTPP says it owns 50% of Cubico, a renewable-energy and transmission platform whose portfolio spans multiple countries and has long included Mexico within its footprint.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 50%
Firm: PSP Investments | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 50%
companyOverview: Cubico Sustainable Investments owns and operates renewable power and transmission assets across multiple countries, including a longstanding presence in Mexico. Its end markets are power systems and counterparties that contract for renewable generation and network capacity. The operating model is asset-heavy and based on long-life infrastructure ownership rather than pure development fees. Ontario Teachers' has described Cubico as a renewable energy and transmission platform with an international footprint, and public materials identify offices in London, São Paulo, Milan, and Mexico. Ontario Teachers' and PSP Investments currently each own 50% of the company.
