oneSentenceDescription: OTG operates airport food-and-beverage concessions across the U.S. and Canada and a 2025 public transaction announcement states that Duration acquired control of OTG after the 2024 spinout from Oaktree.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Oaktree Capital Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Centerbridge Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Other investor consortium members | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: OTG develops and operates airport dining and retail concessions across North America. Its customers are airport authorities, brand partners, and airline passengers, and the operating model combines concession contracts, leasehold improvements, and operating expertise inside major airport terminals. Official company materials state that OTG has more than 350 locations across 11 major airports and serves millions of passengers annually. The business is concentrated in high-traffic North American airports including JFK, LaGuardia, Newark, Toronto Pearson, and other major hubs. OTG announced in January 2024 that an investor consortium led by Oaktree and Centerbridge would acquire the company and provide new financing, after first partnering with OTG in October 2021. The reviewed public materials do not disclose the detailed ownership percentages of the investor consortium.

2021-10 - OTG secured new financing from an investor group that later pursued control.

ownershipVehicle: Duration Ports America Capital Partners, L.P. (publicly referenced in a 2025 holding report)
oneSentenceDescription: Duration’s 2024 spinout announcement named Ports America as an existing portfolio company and a 2025 public holding report still referenced a Duration-named Ports America vehicle, although public disclosures around the ultimate ownership structure are mixed.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Canada Pension Plan Investment Board | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Oaktree Capital Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Ports America operates marine terminals, stevedoring, and other port logistics services across the United States. Its customers include ocean carriers, beneficial cargo owners, cruise operators, and government or terminal counterparties, and the business follows an asset-intensive operating model tied to terminal concessions, equipment, and labor management. Public materials describe Ports America as one of the largest U.S. port operators, with activity spanning container, breakbulk, cruise, and military cargo. The company serves major gateway and regional ports around the country, making it a national transportation infrastructure platform rather than a single-asset business. CPP Investments announced in September 2021 that it would acquire Ports America from Oaktree, and Oaktree later disclosed that it had reinvested and remained a minority owner. The reviewed public materials do not disclose the current exact ownership percentages of CPP and Oaktree.
historicalMilestones: 2014 - CPP Investments became a minority shareholder in Ports America.
2021-09-29 - CPP Investments announced an agreement to acquire Ports America from Oaktree.
2021-11 - Oaktree sold Ports America to CPP and later reinvested as a minority owner.

oneSentenceDescription: Rand transferred to Duration in the 2024 spinout, was still publicly described as a Duration portfolio company in 2024, and in 2026 rebranded as Mainstay Maritime after selling its Canadian operations to focus on its U.S. Jones Act business.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Duration Capital Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Oaktree Capital Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Mainstay Maritime, formerly Rand Logistics, provides marine transportation services on the Great Lakes. Its customers include dry bulk and liquid bulk cargo shippers, and the business operates an asset-heavy fleet model centered on Jones Act-compliant vessels, towing assets, and related marine operations. Public company materials state that the business operates one of the largest Jones Act-compliant fleets serving the Great Lakes. The operating footprint is concentrated on the U.S. Great Lakes, and the company repositioned toward that core market after selling its Canadian operations. Oaktree's transportation infrastructure business transferred the investment to Duration in the 2024 spinout, and the company rebranded from Rand Logistics to Mainstay Maritime in 2026. The reviewed public materials do not disclose the detailed ownership split between Duration and any continuing Oaktree interest.
historicalMilestones: 2024 - Rand Logistics acquired Andrie LLC and entered the liquid bulk transportation market on the Great Lakes.
2024 - Oaktree completed the spinout of its transportation infrastructure platform into Duration Capital Partners.

oneSentenceDescription: Oaktree’s official infrastructure portfolio lists REEF as a current investment and separately describes Neighborhood Property Group as a parking platform created through Oaktree Infrastructure’s strategic partnership with REEF.

companyOverview: REEF Technology operates and repurposes urban parking and adjacent real estate into neighborhood-oriented service and logistics platforms. Its customers include parking users, food-service operators, delivery partners, and local merchants, and the business combines an asset-backed real estate footprint with operating technology and service platforms. Public materials describe REEF as the largest operator of parking real estate and delivery restaurants in North America, with an ecosystem of about 5,000 locations and a workforce of more than 18,000 in 2022 disclosures. The company has a broad North American footprint because its assets are spread across urban parking facilities rather than concentrated in a single state or city. Oaktree participated in REEF's 2020 funding round and separately partnered with the company to form Neighborhood Property Group. The reviewed public materials do not disclose the current exact ownership percentages of REEF's investor syndicate.
historicalMilestones: 2020-11-02 - REEF announced a $700 million funding round with Mubadala, SoftBank, Oaktree, UBS Asset Management, and Target Global.

ownershipVehicle: Sponsored by Wind Point, Duration, and Oaktree
oneSentenceDescription: STG’s own 2024–2026 public releases identify it as a portfolio company of Wind Point, Duration, and Oaktree, and confirm that it continues operating through its Chapter 11 restructuring rather than liquidating.

Firm: Duration Capital Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Oaktree Capital Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: STG Logistics provides intermodal transportation, marine and rail drayage, warehousing, transloading, and over-the-road logistics services. Its customers are importers, exporters, and other freight users that need containerized logistics services, and the business combines transportation management with asset-based operations and nationwide port and rail connectivity. Company disclosures state that STG has more than 35 years of operating history and access to every major rail ramp and port in the country. The platform operates nationally and has built scale through acquisitions, including XPO's intermodal operations in 2022. Public materials from 2024 identify STG as a portfolio company of Wind Point Partners, Duration Capital Partners, and Oaktree Capital Management. In January 2026, STG filed for Chapter 11 with a pre-negotiated restructuring plan intended to continue operations rather than liquidate the business.
historicalMilestones: 1985 - STG Logistics was founded.
2022 - STG acquired XPO's intermodal operations in a recapitalization backed by Wind Point and Oaktree.
2023 - STG acquired Best Dedicated Solutions.
2024-10-04 - STG announced $300 million of financing and identified Wind Point, Duration, and Oaktree as sponsors.
2026-01-12 - STG filed for Chapter 11 restructuring and said it expected to continue operating.

oneSentenceDescription: Duration announced in June 2025 that it made a more than $600 million minority equity investment in Watco, a North American rail, port, terminal, and logistics operator, and that the transaction included Watco taking full ownership of Industrial Rail Services.
profileSubsector: Short line rail, terminals, ports, and logistics
ownershipDetails: Firm: Duration Capital Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Existing Watco shareholders and management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Watco owns and operates short line railroads, terminals, ports, and logistics services. Its customers span industrial, agricultural, chemical, and bulk commodity end markets, and the business combines owned rail and terminal assets with logistics and third-party transportation management services. Official company materials state that Watco was founded in 1983 and has grown into a network of short line railroads, terminals, and ports across North America and Australia. Sustainability reporting describes more than 8,000 track miles, 87 terminals and ports, and more than 1.1 million annual carloads as of 2022. Duration Capital Partners made a minority equity investment of more than $600 million in June 2025 to support growth and Watco's full ownership of Industrial Rail Services. The reviewed public materials do not disclose Duration's precise ownership percentage.

oneSentenceDescription: An official 2024 biography for a former founding Duration executive states he previously led Oaktree’s investment in LaserShip OnTrac, whose U.S. delivery network spans 31 states plus Washington, D.C., although the company does not appear on Oaktree’s published infrastructure portfolio page and should therefore be treated as the least-directly-disclosed name on this list.
ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: OnTrac provides parcel logistics and last-mile delivery services for e-commerce retailers, marketplaces, and other high-volume shippers. Its operating model is network-based and service oriented, with pickup, sortation, linehaul, and final-mile delivery rather than ownership of a national integrated air network. Official company materials state that the network reaches more than 75% of the U.S. population across 35 states and Washington, D.C., and that the company has been expanding service offerings beyond its traditional regional parcel product. The platform was formed by combining the historical LaserShip and OnTrac networks and is marketed under the OnTrac brand across the United States. American Securities announced the combination of LaserShip and OnTrac in 2021, and the rebranded business launched in 2023. The reviewed public materials do not clearly disclose the current ownership structure or current sponsor ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: 1991 - OnTrac states that the business began as California Overnight.

status: Active (listed on Vauban’s current assets page)
oneSentenceDescription: U.S. district-energy platform of eight systems providing essential energy services across New Jersey, Michigan, Delaware, Massachusetts, and Connecticut.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Vauban Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Cartier Energy Fund SCS; Cartier Energy Fund II SCS | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Cartier Energy is a U.S. district energy platform that owns and operates systems providing steam, chilled water, hot water, and electricity. Its customers include universities, hospitals, municipalities, and commercial and industrial users that rely on essential thermal and energy infrastructure. The business is asset-heavy and infrastructure based, with revenues supported by utility-style service arrangements tied to on-site and campus energy systems. Public materials describe a portfolio of eight district energy systems located across New Jersey, Michigan, Delaware, Massachusetts, and Connecticut. Vauban Infrastructure Partners identifies Cartier as its first North American investment, and public disclosures indicate the platform is owned through Vauban-managed investment funds. The reviewed public materials do not disclose the exact ownership percentages of those funds within the current structure.

ownershipVehicle: Strategic investment by Northampton Capital Partners LLC
oneSentenceDescription: Independent U.S. data-center platform developing two Austin-area campuses totaling 85MW, with Taylor (60MW) and Georgetown (25MW) targeted for 2026–2027 energization.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Northampton Capital Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Blueprint Data Centers is a U.S. data center development platform focused on new campus delivery for hyperscale and large enterprise demand. Its customer base is centered on cloud, AI, and other users that require utility-backed wholesale capacity rather than retail colocation services. The operating model is asset-heavy and development led, with site control, power procurement, construction, and eventual leasing or disposition of completed capacity. Public company materials describe two greater Austin campuses in Taylor and Georgetown totaling 85 MW, with energization targeted across 2026 and 2027. The platform is concentrated in Texas and is being built to serve one of the more active U.S. data center growth corridors. Northampton Capital Partners announced its investment in Blueprint in April 2025, and the reviewed public materials do not disclose the resulting ownership percentage.

ownershipVehicle: Royal Aggregator LP (affiliate of Northampton Capital Partners LLC; “Purchaser”)
oneSentenceDescription: Canadian renewable-energy royalty platform that completed its transition to private ownership on December 4, 2024 after Royal Aggregator LP acquired the public float, and ARR says its royalty interests include portfolios of leading U.S. wind and solar developers.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Altius Minerals Corporation | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 57%

companyOverview: Altius Renewable Royalties acquires and manages royalty interests tied to wind, solar, and other renewable energy projects. Its customers are effectively renewable project owners and developers that monetize a portion of future project revenues in exchange for growth capital, making the model contractual and asset-light rather than owner-operator based. Public materials describe a portfolio of royalty interests associated with multiple U.S. renewable developers and projects, with cash flows linked to project operation and generation. The company is headquartered in Canada while its royalty exposure is concentrated in North American renewable generation markets. On December 4, 2024, the business completed its transition to private ownership through a plan of arrangement under which Royal Aggregator LP, an affiliate of Northampton Capital Partners, acquired the public float while Altius Minerals retained its existing stake. Following that transaction, public disclosures state that Altius Minerals holds approximately 57% of the shares and Northampton holds approximately 43%.

companyOverview: Plenitude is an integrated energy transition company combining renewable generation, retail energy supply, and electric vehicle charging infrastructure. Its customer base includes retail households and businesses, energy users purchasing solutions and services, and EV charging users, and the model blends contracted retail activities with owned renewable and charging assets. Official company materials state that by early 2026 it operated in more than 15 countries with 5.8 GW of renewable capacity, about 10 million customers, and roughly 23,000 charging points. A consortium of funds managed by Fiera Infrastructure and Palisade Infrastructure Group acquired 100% of Amp US in June 2023 and rebranded the platform as PureSky Energy, while current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.

2017 - The business launched its first battery energy storage solution in Massachusetts.
2020 - The company brought its first New York community solar farm into operation.
2021 - The platform passed 100 MW in operating U.S. solar farms.
2022 - The platform passed 150 MW in operating and under-construction solar projects.
2023-06-12 - Funds managed by Fiera Infrastructure and Palisade Infrastructure Group completed the acquisition of 100% of Amp US and rebranded the business as PureSky Energy.
2023-09-06 - PureSky announced leadership changes, naming Jared Donald as CEO and Nicole LeBlanc as COO.
2023-11-14 - PureSky closed a 3-year $150 million revolving credit facility led by Nomura.
2025-12-30 - PureSky stated that it had surpassed 200 MW of operational solar and described a portfolio of approximately 241 MW across 52 sites or near-term projects.

oneSentenceDescription: West Coast Hydro consists of two run-of-river hydroelectric facilities—one in Oregon and one in British Columbia—each operating under long-term PPAs.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Fiera Infrastructure | Fund: EICLP | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: West Coast Hydro is a two-asset run-of-river hydroelectric portfolio. Its assets are Dorena in Oregon and Raging River in British Columbia, and both sell power under long-term PPAs to regulated utilities. The portfolio is asset-heavy and fully contracted rather than merchant, with 15.5 MW of aggregate capacity according to Fiera Infrastructure. The operating footprint is limited to the Pacific Northwest and British Columbia. Fiera Infrastructure lists the initial investment in 2012 through EICLP, and public sources tie the Raging River acquisition financing to Aquila Infrastructure Management's investment platform in 2012. Current portfolio-level ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2012 - Fiera Infrastructure lists the initial investment year for West Coast Hydro as 2012.
2012-10-30 - Travelers Capital arranged acquisition financing for the 8 MW Raging River hydroelectric facility for Aquila Infrastructure Management's investment platform.
2012 - Travelers Capital stated that Raging River had reached commercial operation on January 28, 2012.
2014-06 - Oregon water-right materials stated that the Dorena hydropower project was under construction and expected online in the summer of 2014.
2014-12 - The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers later stated that the non-federal Dorena hydropower project began operations in December 2014.
2020 - Fiera Capital's infrastructure profile continued to list Dorena Hydro and Raging River Power and Mining within the portfolio.

oneSentenceDescription: Woodford is a Canadian portfolio of six PPP assets spanning the social, telecom, and transportation subsectors.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Fiera Infrastructure | Fund: EICLP | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Woodford Social Infrastructure is a Canadian portfolio of public-private partnership assets spanning social, telecom, and transportation subsectors. Its assets operate under long-term concession agreements with investment-grade public counterparties and include courthouse, corrections, social housing, data-centre, and airport-access infrastructure. The platform is asset-heavy and contract-based, with cash flows linked to long-duration PPP concession structures rather than merchant demand. Fiera Infrastructure currently lists six assets in the portfolio, located in Ontario and British Columbia. Public disclosures show that Fiera first assembled the platform in 2016 through acquisitions from Forum and later added Enterprise Data Centre Borden in 2019. Current asset-level ownership percentages are not fully disclosed, although Fiera Infrastructure lists the investment in EICLP and previously disclosed 100% ownership of several assets and a 50% stake in Quinte at initial acquisition.
historicalMilestones: 2013-07 - Construction of the Quinte Consolidated Courthouse was completed.
2013-08 - Court operations began at the Quinte Consolidated Courthouse in late August 2013.
2014-02 - The Surrey Pretrial Services Centre Expansion officially opened.
2014-07-28 - The South West Detention Centre officially opened.
2015-07-30 - The Billy Bishop Toronto City Airport Pedestrian Tunnel officially opened.
2016-04-20 - Fiera announced the acquisition of five PPP assets from Forum, including 100% stakes in Billy Bishop, Surrey, social housing, and South West Detention, plus a 50% stake in Quinte.
2016-04-19 - Fiera reached financial close on Quinte, Billy Bishop, and Surrey, with South West Detention and the social housing project scheduled to follow later in 2016.
2019-04-10 - Fiera announced the acquisition of a 100% equity interest in the Enterprise Data Centre Borden PPP Project.

oneSentenceDescription: Amwaste LLC is a vertically integrated, regional provider of non-hazardous solid waste and recycling services serving over 300,000 residential, commercial, and industrial customers across Alabama, Georgia, and Louisiana.
profileSubsector: Solid waste collection, transfer, landfill, and recycling
ownershipDetails: Firm: 3i Group | Fund: 3i North American Infrastructure Fund | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Amwaste provides non-hazardous solid waste collection, transfer, landfill, and recycling services in the Southeastern United States. Its customer base includes residential, commercial, construction and industrial end markets across Alabama, Georgia, and Louisiana. The business operates an asset-heavy, vertically integrated model supported by owned disposal sites, transfer stations, and recycling infrastructure. Public materials describe a platform with more than 300,000 customers, eight landfill sites, ten transfer stations, and a materials recovery facility. The company has expanded through bolt-on acquisitions and local contract wins to deepen density in its core Southeastern markets. 3i invested growth capital through the 3i North American Infrastructure Fund in July 2023, and current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2010 - Founded by Chip Russell as Matter Management Enterprises in the Birmingham market.
2020 - Expanded into Louisiana and won the City of West Point, Georgia residential collection contract.
2020 - Acquired the Sylvan Springs Waste Complex from Meredith Environmental.
2023-07 - 3i North American Infrastructure Fund completed its growth-capital investment in Amwaste.
2023 - Acquired the Whitestone Valley C&D Landfill and four transfer stations from Cash Environmental Resources.
2024-02 - Tom Vaughn joined as President.
2024-11 - Acquired Waste Away Environmental in Milledgeville, Georgia.

oneSentenceDescription: EC Waste is the largest vertically integrated provider of non-hazardous solid waste services in Puerto Rico, serving over 100,000 residential clients and more than 9,000 commercial and industrial customers.
profileSubsector: Solid waste collection, transfer, and landfill services
ownershipDetails: Firm: 3i Group | Fund: 3i North American Infrastructure Fund | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: EC Waste management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: EC Waste provides non-hazardous solid waste collection, transfer, recycling, and disposal services across Puerto Rico. Its customers include residential, commercial, industrial, and municipal accounts served through an island-wide operating footprint. The business uses an asset-heavy, vertically integrated model supported by disposal sites, transfer infrastructure, hauling operations, and related environmental services. 3i has described the company as the largest vertically integrated waste-services provider in Puerto Rico, with more than 80,000 customers and multiple disposal and transfer assets. The platform has grown through acquisitions, including the 2020 purchase of Republic Services of Puerto Rico and the 2025 acquisition of ARB Waste Services. 3i acquired a majority interest through the 3i North American Infrastructure Fund in 2021, with management reinvesting alongside the fund.
historicalMilestones: 1961 - Founded in Humacao, Puerto Rico as El Coqui Waste Disposal.
2014-04 - Post Capital Partners and CEO Randy Jensen acquired EC Waste from Waste Management, Inc.
2020-09 - Acquired Republic Services of Puerto Rico, adding landfills, a transfer station, and hauling operations.
2021-11 - 3i acquired a majority stake in EC Waste through the 3i North American Infrastructure Fund.
2025-07 - EC Waste acquired ARB Waste Services in Puerto Rico.

oneSentenceDescription: Regional Rail LLC is a leading operator of short line freight railroads and rail-related businesses across North America.
principalLocationOfOperations: Pennsylvania; Florida; Illinois; Indiana; Ohio; Minnesota; Saskatchewan; Alberta
ownershipDetails: Firm: 3i Group | Fund: 3i North American Infrastructure Fund | Ownership Interest: 100%
companyOverview: Regional Rail owns and operates short-line freight railroads and related rail services across the United States and Canada. Its customer base spans industrial, agricultural, chemicals, aggregates, food, and logistics end markets that depend on local freight connectivity and transload services. The company follows an asset-heavy operating model built around owned and leased track, rail-served industrial infrastructure, and operating railroad franchises. Public disclosures describe a network of more than 875 route miles, 17 railroad operations, and approximately 65,000 annual carloads as of 2023. The footprint expanded materially after 2019 through acquisitions in Florida, the Midwest, Ohio, Indiana, and western Canada. 3i agreed to acquire 100% of Regional Rail in 2019 as its North American rail infrastructure platform and remains the disclosed owner.
historicalMilestones: 2007-04 - Regional Rail was formed and acquired East Penn Railroad as its first operating railroad.
2019-04 - 3i agreed to acquire 100% of Regional Rail.
2019-10 - Acquired Pinsly Railroad Company's Florida operations.
2022-10 - Expanded into the Midwest and western Canada through multiple railroad acquisitions.
2023-01 - Acquired Indiana Eastern Railroad and Ohio South Central Railroad.
2024-07 - Completed the acquisition of Cincinnati Eastern Railroad.
2025-06 - Acquired Minnesota Commercial Railway.

oneSentenceDescription: Smarte Carte is the world's leading provider of self-service luggage carts, electronic lockers, commercial strollers, and massage chairs, operating in over 3,400 locations across six continents.
principalLocationOfOperations: Minnesota; United States; United Kingdom; Europe
ownershipDetails: Firm: 3i Group | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Smarte Carte provides self-service luggage carts, electronic lockers, strollers, mobility services, and related passenger services in airports and other high-traffic venues. Its customer base is concentrated in travel, leisure, and public-venue operators that outsource essential passenger equipment and related services. The company combines an asset-heavy fleet of owned equipment with contracted concession and service arrangements across airports and other locations. Public materials describe more than 140,000 owned units and operations in more than 3,400 locations worldwide. The business has broadened its service offering through acquisitions and contract wins, including Aviation Mobility in 2018, Heathrow trolley operations in 2024, and Lost and Found Software in 2026. 3i agreed to invest alongside management in Smarte Carte in October 2017, and public disclosures do not state the current ownership split.
historicalMilestones: 1970 - Smarte Carte was founded in Minnesota.
2017-10 - 3i agreed to invest alongside management in Smarte Carte.
2018-04 - Acquired Aviation Mobility and completed a $225 million refinancing.
2019 - Expanded its ancillary passenger-services platform through additional add-on activity.
2024-06 - Won the Heathrow Airport luggage trolley contract, expanding its European operations.
2026-02 - Acquired Lost and Found Software.

oneSentenceDescription: Alpha Generation (AlphaGen) is one of the largest independent power infrastructure platforms in the United States, managing approximately 14,000 megawatts of generation capacity across 23 natural gas- and oil-fired facilities in six states spanning four RTO markets.
profileSubsector: Thermal generation infrastructure and independent power production
principalLocationOfOperations: California; Connecticut; Maryland; New Jersey; New York; Ohio
ownershipDetails: Firm: ArcLight Capital Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Abu Dhabi Investment Authority subsidiary | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: AlphaGen manages and operates a large portfolio of gas- and oil-fired generation assets in major U.S. power markets. Its customer base is primarily wholesale power, capacity, and ancillary-services markets across PJM, NYISO, ISO-NE, and CAISO rather than retail end users. The platform follows an asset-heavy model built around dispatchable generating facilities and associated development, repowering, and grid-upgrade opportunities. Public disclosures describe approximately 13 to 14 gigawatts of capacity across 23 to 24 facilities in six states and nearly $2 billion of annual revenue at formation. The portfolio is concentrated in strategic markets where reliability, peaking capacity, and time-to-power have gained importance as electricity demand has risen. ArcLight created AlphaGen in January 2024, and ADIA agreed a $500 million minority investment in January 2025; public disclosures indicate ArcLight remains the controlling sponsor while current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2024-01-10 - ArcLight created AlphaGen to manage its U.S. power infrastructure portfolio.
2024-01-16 - AlphaGen announced its executive leadership team.
2024-10-23 - AlphaGen completed $3.7 billion of inaugural corporate financing.
2025-01-13 - ADIA agreed a $500 million minority investment in AlphaGen.
2025-05-07 - AlphaGen projects were selected by PJM for expedited interconnection.
2025-10-02 - CPP Investments announced a $1.0 billion investment in AlphaGen.

ownershipVehicle: ADIA Infrastructure Department (Direct Co-Investment with APG & CalSTRS)
oneSentenceDescription: Arevon Energy is a leading U.S.
principalLocationOfOperations: Arizona; California; Texas; Nevada; multi-state U.S. operations
ownershipDetails: Firm: APG Asset Management | Fund: APG-managed pension client capital | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: California State Teachers' Retirement System | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Abu Dhabi Investment Authority | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Arevon develops, finances, builds, owns, and operates utility-scale solar generation and battery storage projects in the United States. Its customers are primarily utilities, community-choice aggregators, and other offtakers that procure power and capacity under long-term project contracts. The company follows an asset-heavy independent power producer model that combines development, construction, ownership, and long-term operation of contracted renewable assets. As of 2025 public disclosures described more than 5.3 gigawatts of operating solar and storage capacity across 17 states, roughly 1 gigawatt under construction, and more than $10 billion of operating assets. The platform was formed in 2021 from Capital Dynamics' U.S. clean energy infrastructure team and the Arevon asset-management business and has continued to scale through project financings and new project completions. Arevon was formed in August 2021 with backing from APG, CalSTRS, and a wholly owned subsidiary of ADIA, and current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2021-08 - Arevon Energy was formed from Capital Dynamics' U.S. clean energy infrastructure team and Arevon Asset Management.
2021-08 - APG, CalSTRS, and ADIA-backed investors were announced as the owners of the new platform.
2025-07 - Arevon announced a $600 million credit facility at the corporate level.
2025-08 - Reached full operations at the Eland solar-plus-storage project.
2025-09 - Reported more than $10 billion of operating assets and 5.3 GW of operating capacity.

ownershipVehicle: ADIA Infrastructure Department (24.9% Direct Stake via Consortium)
oneSentenceDescription: Chicago Parking Meters operates the third-largest metered parking system in the United States under a 75-year concession with the City of Chicago, managing approximately 36,000 parking meter spaces and generating over $160 million in annual revenue.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Morgan Stanley Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Allianz Capital Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Abu Dhabi Investment Authority | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Chicago Parking Meters operates and maintains the City of Chicago's on-street metered parking system under a long-dated public-private concession. Its customer base is the daily parking demand of residents, commuters, businesses, and visitors using approximately 36,000 on-street parking spaces across Chicago. The business follows a concession-based infrastructure model in which revenues are tied to meter collections while operating obligations include maintenance, payment systems, and contractual compliance with the city. Public sources describe it as the third-largest metered parking system in the United States and one of the first large-scale U.S. parking-meter concessions. The concession closed in 2009 for 75 years and the system has since been modernized with pay boxes, mobile payments, and ongoing operating and maintenance requirements. Current public materials identify Morgan Stanley Infrastructure Partners, Allianz Capital Partners, and ADIA as owners, although current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed in primary public sources.
historicalMilestones: 2008-12 - Chicago Parking Meters, LLC was selected as concessionaire for the Chicago metered parking system.
2009-02 - The 75-year concession with the City of Chicago closed for $1.151 billion.
2013 - The city and concessionaire amended portions of the operating relationship following early implementation disputes.
2020 - The concession was affected by pandemic-related meter suspensions and subsequent settlement discussions.
2026 - Chicago officials stated the city would not pursue a buyback of the concession in the near term.

ownershipVehicle: NATFinCo / NATCo II (10% Indirect Passive Economic Interest via Brookfield)
oneSentenceDescription: FirstEnergy Transmission is a holding company of regulated electric transmission entities including ATSI, MAIT, and TrAILCo, serving millions of customers across Ohio, Pennsylvania, West Virginia, Maryland, New Jersey, and New York.
principalLocationOfOperations: Ohio; Pennsylvania; West Virginia; Maryland; New Jersey; New York
ownershipDetails: Firm: FirstEnergy Corp. | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 50.1%
Firm: Brookfield Super-Core Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Brookfield Super-Core Infrastructure Partners | Ownership Interest: 49.9%
companyOverview: FirstEnergy Transmission is the holding platform for FirstEnergy's regulated transmission businesses, including ATSI, MAIT, and TrAILCo. Its customers are transmission users served through regional grid tariffs, with revenues derived from regulated rate frameworks rather than merchant power sales. The business operates an asset-heavy utility model centered on long-lived transmission networks and related grid-investment programs across the Mid-Atlantic and Midwest. Public disclosures describe a system of roughly 24,000 transmission miles spanning multiple states within PJM and adjacent markets. Brookfield first acquired a 19.9% minority stake in 2022 and increased its position through a second transaction that closed in 2024, while FirstEnergy remained the operator and controlling owner. ADIA's exposure is publicly described as an indirect interest through Brookfield's infrastructure vehicle rather than as a separately disclosed direct stake in FET.
historicalMilestones: 2011 - FirstEnergy formed FirstEnergy Transmission to house its transmission businesses.
2021-11 - FirstEnergy agreed to sell a 19.9% interest in FET to Brookfield.
2022-06 - Brookfield closed the acquisition of the initial 19.9% minority stake.
2023-02 - FirstEnergy announced the sale of an additional 30.0% interest to Brookfield.
2024-03-25 - FirstEnergy closed the second transaction, increasing Brookfield's ownership to 49.9%.

oneSentenceDescription: Landmark Dividend is a global leader in the acquisition, development, and management of real estate and infrastructure assets focused on digital infrastructure, wireless communications, outdoor advertising, and renewable power generation, managing over 5,000 assets.
profileSubsector: Ground leases and digital real estate
ownershipDetails: Firm: DigitalBridge-sponsored investment vehicles | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 60%
Firm: Abu Dhabi Investment Authority | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 40%
companyOverview: Landmark Dividend acquires, develops, and manages real-estate interests and infrastructure tied to wireless communications, data centers, outdoor advertising, and renewable-power sites. Its customer base includes tower operators, data-center users, enterprise customers, municipalities, faith-based organizations, and other landowners and infrastructure counterparties. The company follows an asset-heavy model focused on long-duration ground leases, digital real estate interests, and related infrastructure ownership structures. Public materials describe a portfolio of more than 5,000 assets and a business model centered on acquiring and managing mission-critical site interests. The platform has been part of DigitalBridge's digital infrastructure ecosystem and remains active across wireless, data-center, and adjacent infrastructure verticals. ADIA completed the acquisition of a 40% stake in Landmark in April 2024 alongside DigitalBridge-sponsored vehicles, with DigitalBridge retaining the balance.
historicalMilestones: 2002 - Landmark Dividend was founded.
2023-11 - DigitalBridge announced the sale of a 40% stake in Landmark to ADIA.
2024-04 - ADIA completed the acquisition of its 40% stake alongside DigitalBridge-sponsored vehicles.
2024 - Landmark continued to operate as a DigitalBridge portfolio company with ADIA as minority co-owner.

oneSentenceDescription: Sempra Infrastructure Partners develops, builds, operates, and invests in energy infrastructure critical to the North American energy transition, including LNG export facilities, clean power generation, carbon capture, and natural gas transportation networks, with an enterprise value exceeding $31 billion.
profileSubsector: LNG export, gas infrastructure, and low-carbon energy infrastructure
ownershipDetails: Firm: Sempra | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 70%
Firm: KKR | Fund: KKR-managed investment vehicles | Ownership Interest: 20%
Firm: Abu Dhabi Investment Authority | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 10%
companyOverview: Sempra Infrastructure develops and operates LNG, energy networks, and lower-carbon infrastructure across North America. Its customer base includes utilities, LNG buyers, industrial counterparties, and other energy-market participants, and its operating model combines long-lived, asset-heavy infrastructure with a mix of contracted services and commodity-linked activity. The platform is headquartered in Houston and its principal assets and development projects are concentrated in Texas, Louisiana, and Mexico. Public disclosures describe the business as one of North America's larger private energy infrastructure platforms, anchored by LNG export, pipeline, storage, and related assets. Sempra completed the sale of a 30% non-controlling interest in Sempra Infrastructure to KKR and ADIA on June 1, 2022, leaving Sempra with a 70% controlling stake. In September 2025, Sempra announced an agreement that would change the ownership structure, but the company still described the 70%/20%/10% structure as current pending that future closing.
historicalMilestones: 2021-09 - Sempra announced the sale of a 20% non-controlling interest in Sempra Infrastructure to KKR.
2021-12 - Sempra announced the sale of an additional 10% non-controlling interest to ADIA.
2022-06-01 - Sempra completed the sale of the 30% combined non-controlling interest, establishing the current 70% Sempra, 20% KKR, and 10% ADIA ownership structure.
2022 - Sempra Infrastructure issued its inaugural standalone sustainability report following the transaction close.
2025-09-23 - Sempra announced an agreement to sell an additional 45% equity interest in Sempra Infrastructure to KKR and CPP Investments, subject to closing.

oneSentenceDescription: LBJ Infrastructure Group operates the LBJ Express, a $2.7 billion, 13.3-mile managed-lanes highway along I-635 in Dallas, Texas, featuring six dynamically tolled TEXpress Lanes under a 52-year concession with TxDOT expiring in 2061.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Cintra | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Meridiam | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: APG Asset Management | Fund: APG-managed pension client capital | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: LBJ Infrastructure Group is the concession company for the LBJ Express managed-lanes project on I-635 and I-35E in Dallas County, Texas. Its customers are passenger and commercial vehicle users seeking priced congestion-relief lanes in one of the Dallas-Fort Worth region's busiest highway corridors. The project follows a long-term toll-concession model under which the private partner finances, operates, and maintains the managed lanes while TxDOT retains ownership of the underlying public right of way. Public materials describe a $2.6 to $2.7 billion project covering about 13 miles with six managed lanes and associated frontage-road and interchange improvements. The project reached full opening in 2015 and later public updates have described average daily traffic of roughly 250,000 vehicles on the corridor. The current project consortium is publicly described as including Cintra, Meridiam, and APG, while current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2009-09-04 - Commercial close was reached for the LBJ Express concession.
2011-01-18 - Construction began on the LBJ managed-lanes project.
2015-09 - LBJ Express opened to traffic ahead of the contractual schedule.
2025-09 - The consortium marked the tenth anniversary of the LBJ Express opening.

oneSentenceDescription: Astoria Energy comprises two adjacent 615 MW dual-fuel combined-cycle gas turbine facilities in Astoria, Queens, New York, totaling approximately 1,230 MW, providing critical baseload electricity and system stability to New York City.
ownershipDetails: Firm: APG-led investor consortium | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Other consortium investors | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Astoria Energy comprises two adjacent combined-cycle gas-fired generation facilities in Queens that supply dispatchable power to the New York City market. Its customers are wholesale power-market participants and, in the case of Astoria Energy II, the New York Power Authority under a long-term tolling arrangement. The assets operate under a mixed model in which Astoria Energy I sells output on a merchant basis into NYISO while Astoria Energy II benefits from contracted revenues. Public disclosures describe the two facilities at a combined roughly 1.2 gigawatts of capacity and position them as important sources of reliable power in New York City. The plants are asset-heavy thermal generation infrastructure located in Astoria, one of the densest load pockets in the United States. An APG-led consortium agreed to acquire 100% of Astoria Energy I and 55% of Astoria Energy II in January 2020 and closed the transaction in July 2020.
historicalMilestones: 2006 - Astoria Energy I reached commercial operation.
2011 - Astoria Energy II entered service under a long-term arrangement with NYPA.
2020-01 - An APG-led consortium agreed to acquire stakes in Astoria Energy I and II.
2020-07 - The investor consortium closed the acquisition of 100% of Astoria Energy I and 55% of Astoria Energy II.

oneSentenceDescription: Conterra Networks is one of the largest independent fiber infrastructure providers in the United States, operating over 14,000 route miles delivering high-capacity fiber and wireless services to enterprise, carrier, education, and government customers across 21 states.
principalLocationOfOperations: North Carolina; multi-state operations across the Southeast, South Central, and Western United States
ownershipDetails: Firm: Fiera Infrastructure | Fund: EICLP & EISCSp | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: APG | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Senior management team | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Conterra Networks provides middle-mile and last-mile fiber and fixed wireless broadband services and designs, deploys, and operates customized networks, with a stated focus on underserved areas. Its customer base includes enterprise, carrier, education, government, and data-center users. The business is asset-heavy, owning and operating a fiber network that Fiera Infrastructure described at 11,600 route miles across 21 states and that the company later described at 15,000 miles across 22 states. Public materials place the operating footprint across the Southeast, South Central, and Western United States, with headquarters in Charlotte, North Carolina. Fiera Infrastructure first acquired an indirect equity interest in 2019 through EICLP and EISCSp, and APG joined Fiera in a later transaction that closed in 2021. Public disclosures also note significant participation by the senior management team, while current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.

oneSentenceDescription: Cross-Sound Cable operates a 330 MW HVDC submarine transmission system spanning 24 miles beneath Long Island Sound, connecting the electric grids of New England and Long Island, with capacity 100% contracted to the Long Island Power Authority.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Argo Infrastructure Partners-managed vehicle | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Cross-Sound Cable owns and operates a high-voltage direct-current submarine transmission link between Connecticut and Long Island. Its customer base is the regional power system, with transmission capacity sold under contractual arrangements that support interregional power transfers and grid reliability. The asset uses a contracted infrastructure model rather than retail utility service, with revenues linked to the availability and use of transmission capacity. Public materials describe a 24-mile, 330-megawatt HVDC cable connecting the New England and Long Island electric grids. The project has operated since the early 2000s and remains a strategic interconnector between ISO New England and New York load centers. AIA Energy, an Argo-managed vehicle backed by institutional investors including APG and CalSTRS, acquired Cross-Sound Cable in 2015, although current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2002 - Construction began on the Cross-Sound Cable system.
2002 - Cross-Sound Cable entered commercial operation.
2015-08 - AIA Energy, managed by Argo Infrastructure Partners, acquired Cross-Sound Cable.
2026-03 - Cross-Sound Cable announced a redevelopment program to expand Northeast grid connectivity.

oneSentenceDescription: Doral Renewables is a leading U.S.
ownershipDetails: Firm: APG Asset Management | Fund: ABP | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Doral Group | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Doral Renewables develops, owns, and operates utility-scale solar and storage projects in the United States. Its customer base consists of utilities, corporate offtakers, and wholesale power markets that contract for power, renewable attributes, and grid services. The business follows an asset-heavy independent power producer model with a large development pipeline and direct ownership of utility-scale projects. Public disclosures describe a development portfolio of more than 13 gigawatts across 18 states, anchored by the multi-phase Mammoth Solar development in Indiana. The company combines project development, financing, construction, and long-term ownership, with a focus on utility-scale and agrivoltaic projects. The company operates a regulated utility model in which revenues are derived from state-approved rates and long-term grid-investment programs rather than merchant power markets. oneSentenceDescription: Gemini is the largest co-located solar-plus-battery-energy-storage project operating in the United States, located in the Mojave Desert near Las Vegas.

companyOverview: Gemini is a utility-scale solar and battery storage project located in Clark County, Nevada. Its customer base is the Nevada power market and contracted offtakers that purchase output and capacity from one of the largest co-located solar-plus-storage assets in the country. The project follows an asset-heavy contracted operating model centered on long-lived generation and storage infrastructure. Public disclosures describe approximately 690 megawatts of solar capacity paired with 380 megawatts of battery storage, with the project representing one of the largest solar-plus-storage installations in the United States. Its customer base includes utility, commercial, military, hospitality, and residential users across the islands. companyOverview: Hudson Transmission owns and operates a transmission interconnector linking New Jersey and Manhattan. The project operates a contracted transmission model rather than a traditional rate-regulated utility model. Public materials describe a 660-megawatt underground and underwater link that entered service in 2013 and includes associated transmission upgrades in New Jersey. Public materials describe a $3.7 billion, 22.5-mile managed-lanes project delivered under a 50-year agreement with the Virginia Department of Transportation. companyOverview: LBJ Express is the trade name of the managed-lanes concession on I-635 and I-35E in Dallas County, Texas. Its users are commuters, freight carriers, and other motorists seeking priced travel-time reliability through the Dallas-Fort Worth metroplex. The project operates under a long-term toll-concession structure with dynamic pricing, operations and maintenance obligations, and handback requirements at the end of the term. Public disclosures describe a roughly 13-mile corridor with six managed lanes and a total project cost of about $2.6 to $2.7 billion. The road fully opened in 2015 and has since become one of the larger managed-lanes projects in North America. Public sources describe Phase I as a $2.12 billion project that opened in 2014 and later I-35W phases that added more than 10 miles of managed lanes and additional corridor upgrades. oneSentenceDescription: Chicago Parking Meters LLC operates a 75-year concession to manage approximately 36,000 metered parking spaces across the City of Chicago.

companyOverview: Chicago Parking Meters operates and maintains the City of Chicago's on-street metered parking system under a long-dated public-private concession. Its customer base is the daily parking demand of residents, commuters, businesses, and visitors using approximately 36,000 on-street parking spaces across Chicago. The business follows a concession-based infrastructure model in which revenues are tied to meter collections while operating obligations include maintenance, payment systems, and contractual compliance with the city. Public sources describe it as the third-largest metered parking system in the United States and one of the first large-scale U.S. parking-meter concessions. The concession closed in 2009 for 75 years and the system has since been modernized with pay boxes, mobile payments, and ongoing operating and maintenance requirements. Current public materials identify Morgan Stanley Infrastructure Partners, Allianz Capital Partners, and ADIA as owners, although current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed in primary public sources.

Its operating model is asset-heavy and infrastructure-like, with revenues tied to essential transport and storage capabilities rather than commodity production. Ancala acquired the portfolio from Hexion in December 2025 and established Valentra as the operating platform. Reviewed public sources do not disclose Ancala's ownership percentage or the detailed financing structure.
historicalMilestones: 2025 - Ancala appointed industry leadership to support the carve-out and future governance of the platform.
2025-12 - Ancala acquired Hexion's U.S. Gulf Coast formalin infrastructure business and formed Valentra.
2025-12 - Financing for the acquisition was arranged and disclosed by transaction advisers.

oneSentenceDescription: Empire Access is a regional fiber-to-the-premise broadband provider serving upstate New York and northern Pennsylvania, with a network of over 1,280 fiber route miles servicing more than 96,000 addresses.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Antin Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Empire Access provides fiber-to-the-home broadband and communications services in New York and Pennsylvania. Its customer base includes residential broadband subscribers, business users, and communications customers served over owned last-mile fiber infrastructure. The company follows an asset-heavy network expansion model with recurring subscription revenues rather than a wholesale-only carrier model. Antin completed its investment in Empire Access and North Penn in 2023, and public transaction materials described roughly 96,000 addresses served and approximately 29,000 customers at signing. Reviewed public sources do not disclose Antin's ownership percentage.
historicalMilestones: 1896 - Empire Telephone Company was founded in Prattsburgh, New York.
2022 - Antin announced its investment in Empire Access and North Penn.
2023-01 - Antin completed the acquisition of Empire Access and North Penn.
2023-2025 - Empire Access continued fiber expansion across its New York and Pennsylvania footprint.

oneSentenceDescription: FirstLight Fiber is a major fiber-optic network provider operating across the northeastern United States, with a network spanning over 25,000 miles of high-capacity fiber across New York, New Hampshire, Vermont, Massachusetts, Maine, and Pennsylvania.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Antin Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: FirstLight Fiber owns and operates fiber communications infrastructure across the northeastern United States. Its customers include enterprises, carriers, wireless operators, schools, healthcare organizations, and public-sector users served through an asset-heavy regional fiber platform. Public company materials describe a network of more than 14,000 route miles and service to thousands of on-net and near-net locations. Antin acquired the company from Oak Hill in 2018 as its first U.S. investment and has continued to support network growth through acquisitions and organic expansion. Reviewed public sources do not disclose Antin's current ownership percentage.
historicalMilestones: 1999 - Tech Valley Communications, a predecessor to FirstLight, was founded in Albany, New York.
2010s - The business expanded through acquisitions and rebranding into FirstLight Fiber.
2018-07 - Antin acquired FirstLight from Oak Hill Capital Partners.
2020s - FirstLight continued expanding its Northeast fiber network and on-net building base.

oneSentenceDescription: GTL Leasing is the leading lessor of high-pressure gaseous hydrogen transportation and storage equipment in North America, providing short- and long-term operating leases for trailers and cylinders used to transport and store hydrogen and other industrial gases.
profileSubsector: Gas and hydrogen transport equipment leasing
ownershipDetails: Firm: Antin Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Antin NextGen Fund I | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: GTL Leasing leases transportation and storage equipment used in industrial gas and hydrogen supply chains. Its customer base includes gas distributors, industrial users, and emerging hydrogen market participants that require high-pressure gaseous trailers, cryogenic equipment, and related storage assets. The business follows an asset-heavy leasing model with contracted equipment deployments rather than commodity exposure. Antin announced its investment through NextGen Fund I in 2024 and described GTL as a North American lessor of hydrogen midstream equipment. Reviewed public sources do not disclose Antin's ownership percentage.
historicalMilestones: 2013 - GTL Leasing was founded.
2024-06 - Antin agreed to invest in GTL Leasing through NextGen Fund I.
2024 - Public transaction materials highlighted GTL's position in hydrogen and industrial gas equipment leasing.

oneSentenceDescription: Lake State Railway Company is a Michigan-based regional freight railroad operating approximately 375 miles of track spanning eastern Michigan's Lower Peninsula, handling more than 60,000 carloads annually for over 60 customers.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Antin Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Lake State Railway Company operates a regional freight rail network in Michigan's Lower Peninsula. Its customer base includes industrial and bulk commodity shippers that depend on short line and regional rail connectivity, transload services, and interchange access. The business follows an asset-heavy rail operating model built around owned or leased track infrastructure and associated freight services. Antin made a strategic investment in the company in 2022, and public materials describe a network of roughly 375 route miles handling tens of thousands of annual carloads. Reviewed public sources do not disclose Antin's ownership percentage.
historicalMilestones: 1992 - Lake State Railway was formed following the acquisition of lines from Detroit & Mackinac Railway.
2022-03 - Antin completed a strategic investment in Lake State Railway.
2024 - Public transaction materials described annual volumes of approximately 60,000 carloads.

oneSentenceDescription: Origis Energy is a leading vertically integrated U.S.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Antin Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Brookfield Asset Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Origis Energy develops, owns, and operates utility-scale solar and energy storage projects in the United States. Its customers are utilities, corporates, and wholesale power counterparties served through an asset-heavy model spanning development, construction, ownership, and operation. Public company materials describe roughly 1 GW operating, about 3 GW in construction or construction-ready stages, and a development pipeline of approximately 25 GW. Antin became majority shareholder in 2021, and in 2025 Brookfield and Antin announced a further strategic investment in the platform. Reviewed public sources do not disclose the current ownership percentages of Antin and Brookfield.
historicalMilestones: 2008 - Origis Energy was founded.
2021-10 - Antin announced it would become majority shareholder in Origis Energy.
2022 - Antin completed its investment and supported further platform expansion.
2025-01 - Origis announced a strategic investment of more than $1 billion from Brookfield and Antin.

oneSentenceDescription: Vicinity Energy is the largest owner and operator of district energy systems in the United States, operating 19 systems across 12 cities delivering steam, hot water, and chilled water to over 260 million square feet of building space.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Antin Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Vicinity Energy owns and operates district energy systems serving buildings in major U.S. urban markets. Its customer base includes commercial real estate, healthcare, education, and government users that buy steam, hot water, chilled water, and related thermal services. The company follows an asset-heavy utility model with recurring contracted or utility-like revenues from central energy plants and distribution networks. Antin acquired Veolia North America's U.S. district energy portfolio in late 2019 and launched the Vicinity Energy brand in 2020. Public company materials describe one of the largest district energy portfolios in the country, with systems across multiple cities.
historicalMilestones: 2019-12 - Antin completed the acquisition of Veolia North America's U.S. district energy assets.
2020-01 - Vicinity Energy launched as the new operating brand.
2020s - The company expanded decarbonization and electrification initiatives across its district energy systems.

oneSentenceDescription: Vigor Marine Group is a leading American shipbuilding, ship repair, and marine services company operating shipyard and fabrication facilities across Seattle, Portland, San Diego, and Norfolk, with six drydocks and 29 berths serving U.S.
ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Vigor Marine Group provides shipbuilding, ship repair, and marine fabrication services in the Pacific Northwest and Alaska. Its customer base includes defense, commercial maritime, and industrial users, and the business relies on an asset-heavy operating model built around shipyards, dry docks, and specialized fabrication facilities. Public materials describe a multi-yard platform assembled through acquisitions and capital investment over two decades. Antin announced an agreement to acquire the company from Lone Star Funds in February 2026, but the transaction was still subject to regulatory approvals and expected to close later in 2026 in reviewed public materials. Because a closing announcement was not identified in the reviewed public sources, the current ownership structure is treated as not publicly disclosed for this profile.
historicalMilestones: 1995 - Frank Foti acquired the Cascade General shipyard and founded Vigor.
2000s-2010s - The company expanded through acquisitions and consolidation of shipyard operations under the Vigor brand.
2026-02 - Antin announced an agreement to acquire Vigor Marine Group from Lone Star Funds.
2026 - The announced sale remained subject to regulatory approvals in reviewed public materials.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Argo Infrastructure Partners-managed funds | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: The Pearl/Ruby portfolio is a distributed commercial and industrial solar portfolio in the United States. The assets serve commercial, industrial, and institutional hosts under long-term power purchase agreements and therefore operate as an asset-heavy contracted generation platform. Public transaction materials described the portfolio at approximately 114 MW across 134 sites in 12 states at the time of Argo's 2019 investment. Reviewed public disclosures tie the investment to funds managed by Argo Infrastructure Partners, while Apollo later announced an agreement to acquire Argo at the management-company level. Public sources do not disclose the current project-level ownership split or confirm a separate Apollo fund vehicle directly owning the asset.
historicalMilestones: 2019-02 - Argo invested in the 114 MW Pearl/Ruby commercial and industrial solar portfolio.
2019 - Public transaction materials described the portfolio across 134 sites in 12 states under long-term PPAs.
2021 - Argo portfolio disclosures continued to identify Pearl/Ruby as part of its renewable holdings.
2025 - Apollo announced an agreement to acquire Argo Infrastructure Partners at the manager level.

ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Arthur Kill Terminal is a heavy-lift marine terminal project under development on Staten Island, New York for offshore wind staging and assembly. The facility is intended to serve offshore wind developers and marine logistics users through an asset-heavy port infrastructure model rather than a traditional shipping terminal concession. Public project materials describe a roughly 32.5-acre site with marine improvements designed to handle large turbine components. Apollo-managed funds announced in 2021 that they had secured the exclusive right to invest in the project alongside Atlantic Offshore Terminals, but reviewed public materials do not disclose the current finalized ownership structure. The project continued through permitting and development work, although a previously awarded federal port grant was later withdrawn in 2025.
historicalMilestones: 2021-07 - Apollo-managed funds announced an agreement for the exclusive right to invest in Arthur Kill Terminal.
2022 - The project was awarded federal PIDP funding for port development work.
2024 - Federal and local permitting materials were published for the offshore wind terminal project.
2025-08 - The U.S. Department of Transportation terminated the previously awarded federal grant for the project.

oneSentenceDescription: Brightspeed is a fiber-first broadband provider serving approximately 6.5 million homes and businesses across 20 states in the Midwest, Southeast, and Mid-Atlantic regions.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Apollo Global Management | Fund: Apollo-managed funds | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Mubadala Investment Company | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Brightspeed owns and operates wireline telecommunications and fiber broadband infrastructure in the United States. Its customers include residential broadband subscribers, businesses, and wholesale communications counterparties, and the business is investing capital into a largely asset-heavy fiber upgrade program across a legacy local-exchange footprint. Public disclosures describe operations across 20 states and a long-term plan to expand fiber coverage materially within that footprint. The company was formed in connection with Apollo-managed funds' acquisition of Lumen's incumbent local exchange carrier business and launched the Brightspeed brand in 2021 ahead of the transaction close. Mubadala announced a $500 million minority investment in Brightspeed in 2023, but reviewed public materials do not disclose the current ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: 2021-08 - Apollo announced an agreement to acquire Lumen's incumbent local exchange carrier business.
2021-11 - Apollo and Brightspeed unveiled the Brightspeed brand.
2022-10 - Apollo completed the acquisition from Lumen and Brightspeed began operating the business.
2023-05 - Mubadala announced a $500 million minority investment in Brightspeed.
2025 - Brightspeed continued its multi-year fiber expansion program across its 20-state footprint.

ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Broad Reach Power develops and operates renewable generation and battery storage projects in the United States. Its end markets are utilities and wholesale power counterparties, and the business follows an asset-heavy development and ownership model across solar, wind, and storage. Apollo acquired a 50% stake in the company in 2021 alongside the existing EnCap-led shareholder group. In 2023, the shareholders agreed to sell Broad Reach's battery storage business to ENGIE, while public transaction materials stated that the Mountain West solar, wind, and storage portfolio was excluded from that sale. Reviewed public sources do not disclose the current ownership structure of the retained Mountain West portfolio after the 2023 transaction.
historicalMilestones: 2019 - Broad Reach Power was founded with backing from EnCap Energy Transition.
2021-11 - Apollo agreed to acquire a 50% stake in Broad Reach Power.
2022 - The company continued to build out its U.S. storage and renewables pipeline.
2023-08 - The shareholder group agreed to sell the battery storage business to ENGIE while excluding the Mountain West portfolio from the sale.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Apollo Global Management | Fund: Apollo-managed funds | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Bullrock Energy Ventures | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Bullrock Energy Ventures is a community solar development platform focused on New York and New England. The platform serves community solar subscribers and utility counterparties through an asset-heavy development and ownership model for distributed generation projects. Apollo and Bullrock announced a new joint venture in 2025 with up to $220 million of Apollo capital committed to a portfolio of community solar assets. Public disclosures state that $100 million of the commitment is earmarked to advance Bullrock's nearly 500 MW pipeline of renewable energy assets. Reviewed public sources do not disclose the ownership percentages within the joint venture.
historicalMilestones: 2023 - Bullrock expanded its renewables platform and investment management capabilities.
2024 - Bullrock Energy Ventures launched its first fund.
2025-04 - Apollo and Bullrock announced a $220 million community solar joint venture.
2025 - The announced partnership targeted Bullrock's nearly 500 MW pipeline in New York and New England.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Apollo Global Management | Fund: Apollo-managed funds | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Caledonia Generating owns an 801 MW combined-cycle natural gas power plant near Steens in Lowndes County, Mississippi. The asset serves the wholesale power market and follows an asset-heavy merchant generation model rather than a regulated utility model. Public power-sector sources identify the facility as a three-unit combined-cycle plant that entered service in the early 2000s. Reviewed public regulatory and financing materials tie the asset to Apollo-managed funds, including joint regulatory filings and later public utility commission references. Detailed current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed in the reviewed free sources.
historicalMilestones: 2003 - Caledonia entered operation as a combined-cycle natural gas plant in Mississippi.
2018 - Public regulatory filings linked Apollo-managed funds to the plant's ownership structure.
2021 - Fitch rated Caledonia senior secured notes used in the plant's financing structure.
2025 - Public utility commission materials continued to identify Apollo-managed funds in connection with Caledonia ownership.

portfolioCompany: Capital Power US Natural Gas JV
profileCompanyName: Capital Power U.S. Natural Gas JV

Firm: Capital Power | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: The Capital Power U.S. Natural Gas JV is an investment partnership formed to acquire and operate U.S. natural gas-fired generation assets. Its target customer base is the wholesale power market, and the platform is intended to follow an asset-heavy merchant generation ownership model. Capital Power disclosed in late 2025 that Apollo-managed funds and Capital Power had signed an MOU to pursue acquisitions together with up to $3 billion of committed equity. Public disclosures indicate Apollo may provide up to $2.25 billion of that commitment and Capital Power up to $750 million, with Capital Power acting as operator for acquired assets. Reviewed public sources do not disclose a finalized ownership split because the platform was announced as a new investment partnership rather than as a completed portfolio acquisition.
historicalMilestones: 2025-12 - Capital Power and Apollo-managed funds signed an MOU to form a U.S. natural gas generation investment partnership.
2025-12 - The parties disclosed up to $3 billion of equity commitments for future acquisitions.
2025-12 - Capital Power was identified as operator for assets to be acquired by the partnership.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Argo Infrastructure Partners-managed vehicle | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Carville Energy Center is a gas-fired combined-cycle generating asset in St. Gabriel, Louisiana. The plant serves the wholesale power market and operates through an asset-heavy merchant generation model rather than rate-regulated utility service. Public transaction materials describe the facility at approximately 516 MW. LS Power agreed in 2019 to sell Carville and the Oneta plant to an investment vehicle managed by Argo Infrastructure Partners, and reviewed public materials continue to link the asset to Argo-managed ownership. Detailed current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed in the reviewed sources.
historicalMilestones: 2003 - Carville Energy Center entered operation in Louisiana.
2014 - LS Power acquired the plant as part of a six-facility generation acquisition.
2019-09 - LS Power announced the sale of Carville and Oneta to an Argo-managed investment vehicle.
