oneSentenceDescription: Corning Natural Gas Corporation is a regulated natural gas distribution utility serving approximately 15,000 residential, commercial, and industrial customers in the Southern Tier and Finger Lakes regions of upstate New York.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Argo Infrastructure Partners-managed funds | Fund: ACP Crotona | Ownership Interest: 100% of common and preferred stock
companyOverview: Corning Natural Gas is a regulated local gas distribution utility with affiliated operations that also include Leatherstocking Gas and Pike County Light & Power. Its customer base includes residential, commercial, and industrial utility customers in New York and northeastern Pennsylvania served under regulated tariff structures. The business follows an asset-heavy utility model built around pipeline networks, metering assets, and regulated customer relationships. Argo, through ACP Crotona, completed the acquisition of 100% of Corning's common and preferred stock in July 2022 after regulatory approval. Reviewed public materials do not disclose the ownership split within Argo's managed fund structure beyond that transaction.
historicalMilestones: 1904 - Corning Natural Gas was incorporated.
2014 - Corning acquired Pike County Light & Power.
2016 - Leatherstocking Gas expanded the platform into additional New York service territories.
2022-07-06 - ACP Crotona, managed by Argo, completed the acquisition of 100% of Corning's common and preferred stock.

oneSentenceDescription: Cross-Sound Cable Company owns and operates a 330 MW high-voltage direct current (HVDC) submarine transmission cable connecting New England (New Haven, CT) to Long Island (Shoreham, NY) across Long Island Sound.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Argo Infrastructure Partners-managed vehicle | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Cross-Sound Cable owns and operates a high-voltage direct-current submarine transmission link between Connecticut and Long Island. Its customer base is the regional power system, with transmission capacity sold under contractual arrangements that support interregional power transfers and grid reliability. The asset uses a contracted infrastructure model rather than retail utility service, with revenues linked to the availability and use of transmission capacity. Public materials describe a 24-mile, 330-megawatt HVDC cable connecting the New England and Long Island electric grids, and Argo acquired the asset through AIA Energy in 2015. Reviewed public sources do not disclose the detailed current ownership percentages within the vehicle.
historicalMilestones: 2002 - Construction began on the Cross-Sound Cable system.
2002 - Cross-Sound Cable entered commercial operation.
2015-08 - AIA Energy, managed by Argo Infrastructure Partners, acquired Cross-Sound Cable.
2026-03 - Cross-Sound Cable announced a redevelopment program to expand Northeast grid connectivity.

oneSentenceDescription: Duquesne Light Company is a regulated electric transmission and distribution utility serving approximately 600,000 customers in the Pittsburgh metropolitan area of southwestern Pennsylvania.
ownershipDetails: Firm: AIA Montana LLC | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 25.2%
Firm: Not publicly disclosed. | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 74.8%
companyOverview: Duquesne Light Company is a regulated electric transmission and distribution utility serving the Pittsburgh region. Its customer base includes residential, commercial, and industrial users across southwestern Pennsylvania. The company operates a regulated utility model in which revenues are derived from state-approved rates and long-term grid-investment programs rather than merchant power markets. Public disclosures describe service to more than 600,000 customers supported by a network of thousands of miles of transmission and distribution lines, and ESG materials identify AIA Montana as a significant shareholder managed by Argo. AIA Montana acquired its 25.2% stake in 2017, while the balance of the ownership structure is not fully disclosed in the reviewed free public sources.
historicalMilestones: 1912 - Duquesne Light Company was formed through the consolidation of predecessor electric companies.
2017 - AIA Montana acquired a 25.2% stake in DQE Holdings from IFM Global Infrastructure Fund.
2020 - Duquesne Light completed a private placement bond financing and debt refinancing.
2023 - The company continued execution of its long-term grid-modernization and resilience programs.

oneSentenceDescription: Eagle Creek Renewable Energy is the largest owner-operator of small hydroelectric power plants in the United States, with a portfolio of approximately 90 facilities generating over 400 MW of nameplate capacity across multiple states.
ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Eagle Creek Renewable Energy owns and operates a large portfolio of hydroelectric facilities across the United States. Its customer base includes utilities, power market counterparties, and communities served through an asset-heavy renewable generation model centered on owned hydro plants and related water infrastructure. Public company materials describe approximately 85 hydroelectric facilities across 18 states totaling nearly 700 MW. Apollo announced in October 2025 that Apollo-managed funds had agreed to acquire the company, with closing targeted for the first quarter of 2026, but a public completion notice was not identified in the reviewed sources. Accordingly, the current ownership structure is treated as not publicly disclosed for this profile.
historicalMilestones: 2009 - Eagle Creek Renewable Energy was formed.
2018-11 - Ontario Power Generation completed its acquisition of Eagle Creek.
2019-2024 - Eagle Creek continued to expand and operate its U.S. hydro portfolio.
2025-10 - Apollo-managed funds announced an agreement to acquire Eagle Creek, subject to closing conditions.

ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: FirstDigital Telecom provides fiber-based internet, data, voice, and unified communications services in the Intermountain West. Its customer base includes enterprise, multifamily, and other business users served over owned and managed fiber infrastructure. The company follows an asset-heavy communications model built around fiber networks, lit buildings, and managed telecom services rather than a consumer-only broadband approach. Apollo announced in 2021 that Apollo Infrastructure Funds would invest $200 million of preferred equity to support FirstDigital's acquisition of Veracity Networks and broader expansion. Because the disclosed Apollo investment was preferred equity rather than a publicly disclosed common equity acquisition, the current ownership structure is treated as not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2000 - Wesley McDougal founded FirstDigital.
2021-07 - Apollo announced a $200 million preferred equity investment in FirstDigital.
2021 - The investment supported FirstDigital's acquisition of Veracity Networks.
2025 - Company materials described more than 2,000 fiber miles and 10,000 lit buildings.

ownershipDetails: Firm: LAZ Parking | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: FleetLogix provides managed labor, staffing, and fleet management services for transportation and mobility customers. Its customer base includes fleet operators, rental car agencies, and logistics users that outsource dispatch, labor, and operational support functions. The business is more service-oriented than asset-heavy, but it operates within transportation infrastructure workflows that require national operating coverage and technology support. LAZ Parking acquired a majority interest in FleetLogix in April 2025, while Argo had previously announced a long-term investment in LAZ in 2021. Reviewed public sources do not disclose the current detailed ownership split at FleetLogix.
historicalMilestones: Founding date - Not publicly disclosed in reviewed primary sources.
2021-12 - LAZ Parking announced a long-term investment from Argo Infrastructure Partners.
2025-04-22 - LAZ Parking acquired a majority interest in FleetLogix.
2025 - FleetLogix began operating as "FleetLogix Powered by LAZ."

profileSubsector: Truck parking and industrial outdoor storage operations
ownershipDetails: Firm: LAZ Parking | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Freight Ninja manages truck, trailer, container, and fleet parking locations across the United States. Its customer base includes trucking companies, intermodal operators, and fleet owners that require secure parking and industrial outdoor storage solutions. The operating model combines site management and logistics services with leased or managed yards, making it more operating-infrastructure oriented than a pure real estate owner. LAZ Parking acquired a majority interest in the company in May 2025, while public Freight Ninja materials describe a national network of managed locations. Reviewed public sources do not disclose the detailed ownership split following the transaction.
historicalMilestones: Founding date - Not publicly disclosed in reviewed primary sources.
2021-12 - LAZ Parking announced a long-term investment from Argo Infrastructure Partners.
2025-05-06 - LAZ Parking acquired a majority interest in Freight Ninja.
2025-2026 - Company materials described a growing national network of truck parking and IOS locations.

oneSentenceDescription: GFL Environmental is the fourth-largest diversified environmental services company in North America, providing solid waste management, liquid waste management, and soil remediation services across Canada and the United States.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Apollo Global Management | Fund: Apollo-managed funds | Ownership Interest: 28%
Firm: BC Partners | Fund: BC-managed funds | Ownership Interest: 28%
Firm: GFL Environmental | Fund: Retained equity interest | Ownership Interest: 44%
companyOverview: GFL Environmental Services provides industrial and environmental services across North America. Its customer base includes industrial facilities, commercial customers, and municipal or infrastructure users that require liquid waste management, remediation, and related environmental services. The business combines route-based service operations with treatment, disposal, and field-service capabilities, making it more asset- and operations-intensive than an advisory model. GFL announced in January 2025 that Apollo-managed funds and BC-managed funds would acquire the business, and the transaction closed in March 2025 with GFL retaining a 44% equity interest. Public transaction materials describe the business as a large-scale platform with more than C$500 million of Adjusted EBITDA.
historicalMilestones: 2007 - GFL was founded in Ontario.
2025-01-07 - GFL announced the sale of its Environmental Services business to Apollo-managed funds and BC-managed funds.
2025-03-03 - GFL completed the sale at an C$8.0 billion enterprise value.
2025-03 - The post-closing ownership structure was disclosed as 28% Apollo, 28% BC Partners, and 44% retained by GFL.

oneSentenceDescription: Great Bay Renewables is a specialty renewable energy company that provides royalty-based financing to wind and solar project developers across North America.
profileSubsector: Renewable energy royalties and project finance
ownershipDetails: Firm: Apollo Global Management | Fund: Apollo-managed funds | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Altius Renewable Royalties | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Great Bay Renewables provides royalty capital and other structured financing solutions to renewable energy developers and operators. Its customers are primarily wind, solar, storage, and related energy infrastructure developers seeking capital that does not require traditional project-level equity dilution. The business is comparatively asset-light at the corporate level because it holds royalty and financing interests rather than directly operating generation assets. Apollo and Altius announced a joint venture in 2020 to accelerate Great Bay's growth, and Apollo disclosed the opportunity to acquire up to a 50% stake in the platform. Reviewed public sources do not disclose the current final ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: 2017 - Great Bay Renewables was formed.
2020-10 - Apollo and Altius announced a joint venture to accelerate Great Bay's growth.
2020 - Apollo disclosed the opportunity to acquire up to a 50% stake in Great Bay.
2020s - Great Bay expanded its renewable royalties and flexible financing activities across North America.

oneSentenceDescription: Hawaiʻi Gas (formerly The Gas Company, LLC) is the sole regulated gas utility serving the State of Hawaiʻi, delivering synthetic natural gas (SNG), liquefied petroleum gas (LPG), and liquefied natural gas (LNG) to approximately 36,000 residential, commercial, and industrial customers across Oahu, Maui, Kauai, and the Big Island.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Argo Infrastructure Partners-managed affiliate | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Hawaiʻi Gas is the state's only franchised full-service gas utility and also the largest propane distributor in Hawaiʻi. Its customer base includes utility, commercial, military, hospitality, and residential users across the islands. The business operates an asset-heavy utility model with synthetic natural gas distribution on Oʻahu and delivered gas services across the major Hawaiian islands. Public disclosures describe a roughly 1,100-mile pipeline system on Oʻahu and a statewide propane distribution footprint, and Argo completed the acquisition of the business from Macquarie in July 2022 after regulatory approval. Reviewed public sources do not disclose the detailed ownership split within Argo's managed vehicle.
historicalMilestones: 1904 - Honolulu Gas Company was founded.
2021-06 - Macquarie announced the sale of Hawaiʻi Gas to Argo Infrastructure Partners.
2022-07 - Argo completed the acquisition after Hawaiʻi PUC approval.
2025-06 - Hawaiʻi Gas announced the acquisition of UGI's propane-air assets on Maui and Hawaiʻi Island.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Argo Infrastructure Partners-managed vehicle | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Hudson Transmission owns and operates a transmission interconnector linking New Jersey and Manhattan. Its customer base is the regional power market, with capacity contracted to support transfers between PJM and New York City load zones. The project operates a contracted transmission model rather than a traditional rate-regulated utility model. Public materials describe a 660-megawatt underground and underwater link that entered service in 2013, and Argo acquired the asset in 2018 through a managed investment vehicle. Reviewed public sources do not disclose the detailed current ownership percentages within the vehicle.
historicalMilestones: 2011-05 - Construction began on the Hudson Transmission project.
2013-06 - Hudson Transmission entered commercial service.
2018-09 - Argo signed to acquire Hudson Transmission from Ares EIF and Starwood Energy.

companyOverview: INDIGO Park Canada provides parking management and urban mobility services across Canada. Its customer base includes municipalities, airports, healthcare facilities, commercial property owners, and parking users served through operating contracts and technology-enabled parking platforms. The business follows an operating-infrastructure model built around managed parking sites, digital customer interfaces, and urban mobility services rather than an ownership-only real estate model. LAZ Parking acquired a 60% stake in the company in July 2025, while INDIGO Group retained the remaining 40%, and public company materials describe more than 315,000 parking stalls across 79 cities. ownershipDetails: Firm: Apollo Global Management | Fund: Apollo Infrastructure Opps. Fund II | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Johnson Controls | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: IonicBlue is an energy-as-a-service provider that develops, finances, and manages building-efficiency and decarbonization projects for commercial, institutional, and public-sector customers. The business packages energy upgrades such as HVAC, controls, lighting, and related infrastructure into contracted service arrangements so customers can avoid large upfront capital expenditures. Its operating footprint spans the United States and Canada, and public materials describe the platform as a joint venture combining Johnson Controls' technical capabilities with Apollo's capital. Johnson Controls stated in 2023 that IonicBlue was founded in 2021 and had received up to C$100 million of additional capital from the Canada Infrastructure Bank to support Canadian projects. The platform was formed in 2021 through a joint venture between Johnson Controls and Apollo infrastructure funds, while public sources do not disclose the current ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: Aug 2021: Johnson Controls and Apollo announced the formation of a joint venture to pursue performance-based energy efficiency and smart-building services in the United States and Canada.
2021: IonicBlue was established as the operating platform for the new energy-as-a-service venture.
May 2023: The Canada Infrastructure Bank committed up to C$100 million to support IonicBlue projects in Canada.
2023: Johnson Controls publicly identified IonicBlue as a joint venture between Johnson Controls and Apollo funds focused on decarbonization and efficiency projects.

oneSentenceDescription: LAZ Parking is one of the largest parking management companies in the United States, operating approximately 3,000 parking locations across over 400 cities with combined capacity of over 1.5 million parking spaces.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Apollo Global Management | Fund: Argo Infrastructure Partners (Apollo) | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: LAZ Parking provides parking management, valet, shuttle, enforcement, and related mobility services for airports, municipalities, hospitals, universities, and commercial real estate owners. The company operates primarily through an asset-light contract model, although affiliated investment vehicles also pursue selected parking asset acquisitions and concessions. LAZ states that it manages more than 3,200 locations representing over 1.5 million parking spaces in more than 400 cities across the United States and Canada. Its scale makes it one of the larger parking-operations platforms in North America, with customers spanning public, institutional, and private property owners. LAZ announced in late 2021 that Argo Infrastructure Partners, now part of Apollo's infrastructure platform, made a long-term preferred investment, while public sources do not disclose the resulting ownership percentage.
historicalMilestones: 1981: LAZ Parking was founded in Hartford, Connecticut.
2007: VINCI Park acquired a 50% partnership stake in LAZ Parking, providing capital for expansion.
2009: LAZ, through its VINCI affiliation, acquired Ultimate Parking and expanded its contract footprint in Boston, Providence, and San Diego.
Dec 2021: LAZ announced a long-term preferred investment from Argo Infrastructure Partners.
Jul 2025: LAZ announced the acquisition of a 60% majority stake in INDIGO Park Canada.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Apollo Global Management | Fund: Argo Infrastructure Partners (Apollo) | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Leatherstocking Gas Company is a regulated natural gas utility that extends service into underserved areas of New York and Pennsylvania. It serves residential, commercial, and small industrial customers through an asset-heavy local distribution model built around owned pipeline infrastructure and utility franchises. Company history materials state that the business began as a 50/50 partnership in 2010 in New York and 2011 in Pennsylvania, and that it began serving its first Pennsylvania customer in 2013. Public utility materials describe Leatherstocking as the newest public utility in Pennsylvania in more than 40 years, with franchise territory in Susquehanna and Bradford counties and adjacent service territory in New York. The utility sits within the Corning and Argo ownership chain, but public sources do not disclose the current ownership percentages of the operating company.
historicalMilestones: Nov 2010: Leatherstocking Gas Company was formed as a joint venture between Corning Natural Gas Holding Corporation and Mirabito Regulated Industries in New York.
2011: The partnership expanded its utility development effort into Pennsylvania.
2013: Leatherstocking began serving its first customer in Susquehanna County, Pennsylvania.
Jan 2020: Corning Natural Gas announced that it had bought out Mirabito's interest in the Pennsylvania Leatherstocking utility operations.
Jun 2023: The Pennsylvania Public Utility Commission approved a rate settlement for Leatherstocking's Pennsylvania natural gas service territory.

oneSentenceDescription: Modern Aviation is one of North America's largest independent fixed base operator (FBO) networks, providing aviation fueling, hangar, and ground handling services to business, general, commercial, cargo, and military aircraft at 19 locations across the U.S.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Apollo Global Management | Fund: Apollo Infrastructure Opps. Fund III | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Tiger Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Modern Aviation operates fixed-base operations that provide fueling, hangarage, ground handling, and related services to business, general aviation, cargo, commercial, and military aircraft. The business serves airport operators, aircraft owners, charter operators, and corporate flight departments through an asset-heavy model combining long-term airport leases with recurring service revenue. Company materials describe a multi-airport footprint across the United States and Puerto Rico, and Apollo's 2023 transaction announcement referred to 16 locations. The platform was established in 2018 and has expanded through acquisitions and site additions since launch. Apollo announced in November 2023 that its infrastructure funds had acquired a majority stake in Modern Aviation, while Tiger Infrastructure Partners and management reinvested and public sources do not disclose the resulting ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: 2018: Modern Aviation was founded as an FBO platform focused on business and general aviation infrastructure.
2018: The company completed its first acquisition with the purchase of Landmark Aviation's facilities at Pittsburgh International Airport.
2020: Modern Aviation acquired FBO operations at San Juan, Puerto Rico, broadening its network beyond the continental United States.
Nov 2023: Apollo infrastructure funds acquired a majority stake in Modern Aviation, with Tiger Infrastructure Partners and management reinvesting alongside Apollo.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Apollo Global Management | Fund: Apollo Funds | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: NextEra Energy Partners 2.5 GW JV is a portfolio-level investment tied to utility-scale wind, solar, and battery storage assets in the United States. The portfolio sells power through long-term contracted structures, making it an asset-heavy renewable generation platform with limited merchant exposure relative to fully uncontracted assets. NextEra Energy Partners stated in 2021 that the transaction covered approximately 2,520 MW of wind and solar assets plus about 115 MW of battery storage across 13 facilities, including entry into three new states. The disclosed transaction supported NextEra Energy Partners' acquisition of a 50% interest in the portfolio from affiliates of NextEra Energy Resources. Apollo funds announced an $824 million investment in October 2021 to support that acquisition, but public sources do not disclose the current ownership percentages within the relevant investment vehicles.
historicalMilestones: Oct 2021: NextEra Energy Partners announced an agreement to acquire a 50% interest in an approximately 2.5 GW renewable portfolio from affiliates of NextEra Energy Resources.
Oct 2021: Apollo funds announced an $824 million investment to support NextEra Energy Partners' portfolio acquisition.
2021: Public disclosures described the portfolio as 13 wind and solar facilities totaling approximately 2,520 MW, plus roughly 115 MW of battery storage.
2021: The transaction expanded NextEra Energy Partners into three additional U.S. states through the acquired portfolio.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Apollo Global Management | Fund: Great Bay Renewables (Apollo) | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Nokomis Energy develops and owns distributed solar projects connected to local distribution grids in the United States. Its end markets include community solar programs, local power purchasers, and other distributed-energy counterparties, and the business follows an asset-heavy development-to-own model. Public materials tied to Great Bay Renewables' 2024 investment describe a 77-project solar portfolio across seven U.S. states, with activity focused on distributed generation markets. The disclosed transaction was structured as growth capital to support construction and expansion rather than as a publicly detailed sale of control. Great Bay Renewables announced a $30 million investment in August 2024, and Great Bay is jointly controlled by Altius Renewable Royalties and Apollo-affiliated funds, while public sources do not disclose Nokomis's broader ownership split.
historicalMilestones: 2024: Public disclosures identified Nokomis Energy as a distributed solar platform with a 77-project portfolio across seven U.S. states.
Aug 2024: Great Bay Renewables announced a $30 million investment in Nokomis Energy.
2024: Great Bay stated that the investment would support the continued build-out of Nokomis's solar portfolio.
2024: Public materials linked Great Bay's sponsorship to the Altius Renewable Royalties and Apollo-affiliated joint-venture structure.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Apollo Global Management | Fund: Argo Infrastructure Partners (Apollo) | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Oneta Energy Center is a natural gas-fired combined-cycle generation asset in Oklahoma that sells capacity and energy into wholesale power markets and contracted utility arrangements. The project is an asset-heavy thermal generation facility serving the Southwest Power Pool region rather than retail end users. Public materials describe the plant as a 1,127 MW facility near Broken Arrow, Oklahoma, and Missouri municipal disclosure documents reference contracted capacity from the plant beginning in 2020. LS Power announced in August 2019 that it had agreed to sell Oneta and the Carville project to Argo Infrastructure Partners. Public sources reviewed do not disclose a separate project-company founding year or the current ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: Aug 2019: LS Power announced the sale of Oneta Energy Center and the Carville project to Argo Infrastructure Partners.
Jun 2020: Missouri Joint Municipal Electric Utility Commission documents show contracted capacity from Oneta beginning on June 1, 2020.
2024: Municipal disclosure materials continued to identify Oneta as a source of contracted capacity for member utilities.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Apollo Global Management | Fund: Argo Infrastructure Partners (Apollo) | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Pike County Light & Power is a regulated electric and natural gas utility serving communities in northeastern Pennsylvania. The utility provides local distribution service to residential, commercial, and other customers through an asset-heavy model based on owned and operated utility infrastructure. Company materials state that it was incorporated on May 17, 1910 and continues to operate from Milford with defined electric and gas service territories in Pike County. The business is a local franchise utility rather than a merchant power or unregulated energy-services platform. Public filings show that the utility sits alongside Leatherstocking Gas in a regional regulated-utility ownership chain associated with Argo Infrastructure Partners, while public sources do not disclose the current operating-company ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: May 17, 1910: Pike County Light & Power Company was incorporated in Pennsylvania.
2010s: The company continued operating as a local electric utility while adding and expanding regulated natural gas service in its territory.
Nov 2025: Pennsylvania PUC filings documented a change-of-control application involving Pike County Light & Power and Argo-affiliated entities.
2025: Public filings continued to identify Pike as a regulated utility serving communities in Pike County, Pennsylvania.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Apollo Global Management | Fund: Argo Infrastructure Partners (Apollo) | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Pueblo Airport Generating Station is a natural gas-fired combined-cycle power plant in Pueblo County, Colorado. The asset serves wholesale and utility power demand through an asset-heavy generation model rather than through retail utility operations. Black Hills disclosures describe the station as a 440 MW facility that commenced operations on January 1, 2012 and is jointly owned by Black Hills Colorado Electric and Black Hills Colorado IPP. Publicly available project materials identify the plant as a substantial thermal generation asset in southern Colorado, but they do not clearly identify a separate project-company founding year. Public sources reviewed do not disclose the current fund-level ownership structure represented in this workbook.
historicalMilestones: Jan 1, 2012: Black Hills disclosures identify Pueblo Airport Generating Station as commencing operations.
2015: Graycor publicized work on the facility's Phase II expansion program.
2024: Public project databases continued to identify the station as a 440 MW operating gas-fired facility in Pueblo County, Colorado.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Apollo Global Management | Fund: Argo Infrastructure Partners (Apollo) | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Brookfield Asset Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Smoky Mountain Hydro is a portfolio of four hydroelectric facilities in eastern Tennessee and western North Carolina. The assets sell wholesale renewable electricity through an asset-heavy generation model centered on long-lived hydro infrastructure and contracted offtake. Public statements around the portfolio's 2024 transaction with TVA describe 377 MW of installed capacity and average annual generation of about 1.4 million MWh. TVA announced in November 2024 that it had signed a 10-year power purchase agreement beginning in 2025 with Argo and Brookfield for the facilities. Public sources do not disclose a separate portfolio-company founding year or the ownership percentages between the sponsors.
historicalMilestones: Nov 2024: TVA announced a 10-year power purchase agreement with Argo and Brookfield for the Smoky Mountain Hydro facilities.
2025: The TVA offtake agreement was scheduled to begin for the four-facility hydro portfolio.
2024: Public disclosures described the portfolio as 377 MW of installed hydro capacity producing about 1.4 million MWh annually on average.

oneSentenceDescription: Stream Data Centers is a leading purpose-built data center platform focused on hyperscale and enterprise colocation facilities across major U.S.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Apollo Global Management | Fund: Apollo Funds | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Stream Data Centers develops, leases, and operates wholesale and build-to-suit data center capacity for large enterprise and hyperscale customers. The business follows an asset-heavy model centered on powered-shell development, turnkey delivery, and long-duration leasing rather than retail colocation sold cabinet by cabinet. Company materials state that the platform was founded in 1999 and now has more than 4 GW in active development, with over 90% of current inventory leased to Fortune 100 customers. Its footprint is concentrated in major U.S. data center markets and supported by in-house development, construction, and operating capabilities. Apollo announced in August 2025 and completed in November 2025 the acquisition of a majority stake in Stream, while management retained a significant stake and public sources do not disclose the final ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: 1999: Stream Data Centers was founded.
2025: Company materials described more than 4 GW of active development and over 90% of inventory leased to Fortune 100 customers.
Aug 2025: Apollo announced an agreement to acquire a majority stake in Stream Data Centers.
Nov 2025: Apollo completed the acquisition of a majority stake in Stream Data Centers.

oneSentenceDescription: Summit Ridge Energy is the largest owner and operator of community solar assets in the United States, with approximately 1.5 GW of operating and contracted capacity across over 200 projects in states including Maryland, Illinois, Maine, and Massachusetts.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Apollo Global Management | Fund: Apollo Sustainable Investing | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Summit Ridge Energy develops, owns, and operates distributed generation and community solar assets in the United States. Its customers include commercial offtakers, community solar subscribers, utilities, and other power purchasers, and the business follows an asset-heavy, contracted model with development, financing, and long-term operating capabilities. Company materials state that Summit Ridge has more than 2 GW of solar projects operating or in development, has financed more than 200 facilities, and has raised over $5 billion of project capital since launch. The platform operates nationally, with public disclosures around Apollo's 2025 investment highlighting Illinois commercial solar assets. Apollo announced in April 2025 that its funds had committed up to $400 million to a new commercial solar partnership with Summit Ridge, while public sources do not disclose the broader platform-level ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: 2017: Summit Ridge Energy was launched.
Apr 2025: Apollo funds committed up to $400 million for a new commercial solar partnership with Summit Ridge.
Sep 2025: Summit Ridge and Bank of America closed a $305 million credit facility for a solar portfolio.
Jan 2026: Summit Ridge announced a $290 million facility with MUFG and stated that it had raised more than $5 billion for over 200 solar facilities since launch.

oneSentenceDescription: The State Group is a leading North American provider of electrical and infrastructure services specializing in power generation, transmission, distribution, and industrial electrical construction.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Apollo Global Management | Fund: Apollo Clean Transition Equity | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Blue Wolf Capital Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: The State Group provides multi-trade industrial and specialty services across electrical, mechanical, automation, and robotics disciplines. Its customers are industrial, utility, commercial, and institutional operators that outsource complex installation, maintenance, and modernization work, making the platform more services-led than asset-owning. Apollo's transaction announcement described the company as a North American business with operations across Canada and the United States and a long operating history dating to 1961. The company's operating model is based on labor, project execution, and technical field capabilities rather than contracted merchant infrastructure revenue. Apollo announced in November 2024 that its funds had acquired a majority stake in The State Group, while Blue Wolf retained a minority stake and public sources do not disclose the ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: 1961: The State Group was founded.
2016: Blue Wolf Capital Partners invested in The State Group.
Nov 2024: Apollo funds acquired a majority stake in The State Group.
Nov 2024: Blue Wolf announced that it would retain a minority stake following Apollo's investment.

oneSentenceDescription: TierPoint is a leading national provider of colocation, cloud, and managed IT services operating approximately 40 data centers across more than 20 U.S.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Apollo Global Management | Fund: Argo Infrastructure Partners (Apollo) | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: TierPoint operates a U.S. data center and managed-services platform offering colocation, cloud, connectivity, disaster recovery, and security services. The company serves enterprise, public-sector, and mid-market customers through an asset-heavy network of data centers supplemented by recurring managed-service revenue. TierPoint states that it operates more than 40 data centers in over 20 markets, maintains coast-to-coast fiber access, and serves more than 3,000 customers. The platform therefore combines physical digital infrastructure with service-based IT offerings rather than relying on wholesale development alone. TierPoint announced in February 2022 that Argo Infrastructure Partners had closed a $500 million investment that made Argo its largest single equity investor, while public sources do not disclose the full ownership split.
historicalMilestones: 2010: TierPoint was formed.
Feb 2022: TierPoint announced the closing of a $500 million investment from Argo Infrastructure Partners.
2022: The company stated that Argo became TierPoint's largest single equity investor through the transaction.
Dec 2025: Argo announced an increase in its ownership and capital commitment to TierPoint.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Apollo Global Management | Fund: Apollo Clean Transition Equity | Ownership Interest: 50%
Firm: TotalEnergies | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 50%
companyOverview: TotalEnergies Texas Solar & BESS is a Texas portfolio of utility-scale solar generation and battery storage assets in the ERCOT market. The assets serve wholesale power demand through an asset-heavy generation model, with TotalEnergies retaining operating responsibility after the announced transaction. Public disclosures describe the portfolio as three solar projects totaling about 1.7 GW together with two battery systems totaling about 300 MW, for roughly 2 GW in aggregate. The portfolio is concentrated in Texas and is positioned within ERCOT rather than a regulated utility rate base. Apollo announced in December 2024 that its funds would acquire a 50% stake from TotalEnergies, with TotalEnergies retaining the other 50% and continuing to operate the portfolio.
historicalMilestones: Dec 2024: Apollo announced an agreement to acquire a 50% stake in TotalEnergies' approximately 2 GW Texas solar and battery storage portfolio.
Dec 2024: TotalEnergies stated that it would retain a 50% stake and continue operating the portfolio.
2024: Public disclosures described the portfolio as three solar projects totaling about 1.7 GW and two battery systems totaling about 300 MW.

oneSentenceDescription: US Water Systems is a platform of water and wastewater concession assets owned by Argo Infrastructure Partners (Apollo affiliate), providing essential water utility services to municipalities through long-term public-private partnership agreements.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Apollo Global Management | Fund: Argo Infrastructure Partners (Apollo) | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: US Water Systems is a platform of municipal water and wastewater concession assets in the northeastern United States. Its customers are municipalities and utility users, and the operating model is asset-heavy and contract-based, with revenue supported by long-duration concession arrangements rather than uncontracted merchant demand. Public materials linked to the platform identify a 40-year Bayonne, New Jersey concession awarded in 2012 and a Middletown, Pennsylvania concession awarded in 2019. The platform therefore combines essential-service utility operations with public-private partnership structures and concession obligations. Public sources reviewed do not fully disclose the ownership structure within the Argo and Apollo investment chain.
historicalMilestones: 2012: Bayonne, New Jersey awarded a 40-year water and wastewater concession to the platform.
2019: Middletown Borough selected a private concession arrangement for water and wastewater operations.
2019: Public reporting connected the Middletown concession to the same US Water Systems platform.

oneSentenceDescription: US Wind is an offshore wind development company advancing the MarWin project, a large-scale offshore wind farm located in a federal lease area approximately 12 nautical miles off the coast of Ocean City, Maryland.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Apollo Global Management | Fund: Apollo Infrastructure Opps. Fund II | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Renexia | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: US Wind develops offshore wind generation projects off the coast of Maryland. The platform serves utility and wholesale power markets through an asset-heavy development model focused on federal leasehold rights, permitting, interconnection, and long-term offtake arrangements. Public materials describe an 80,000-acre federal lease area and a development program capable of supporting roughly 1,500 MW to 1,800 MW of generation. The company has been active since 2011 and has advanced both state-level offtake awards and federal permitting milestones for its Maryland offshore projects. Apollo announced in 2020 that its infrastructure funds had made a strategic investment in US Wind, whose controlling shareholder is associated with Renexia, while public sources do not disclose the ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: 2011: US Wind was founded.
2014: US Wind acquired federal offshore wind lease rights off Maryland.
Dec 2020: Apollo infrastructure funds announced a strategic investment in US Wind.
2021: Maryland regulators selected additional offshore wind output associated with the company's development program.
2024: Federal approval was announced for the project's Construction and Operations Plan.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Apollo Global Management | Fund: Apollo Funds | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Valor Compute Infrastructure is an investment vehicle created to acquire and lease data center compute infrastructure used in AI workloads. The platform serves large-scale digital infrastructure demand through an asset-heavy leasing model rather than through conventional retail colocation or managed hosting services. Apollo stated in January 2026 that VCI was part of a $5.4 billion transaction to acquire and lease NVIDIA GB200 GPU infrastructure to an xAI subsidiary under a triple-net structure. The disclosed scale makes the platform a specialized owner-lessor of high-value compute equipment tied to next-generation data center demand. Apollo announced that it led a $3.5 billion capital solution for VCI in January 2026, while public sources do not disclose the current ownership percentages within the vehicle.
historicalMilestones: Jan 2026: Apollo announced a $3.5 billion capital solution for Valor Compute Infrastructure.
Jan 2026: Public disclosures described a $5.4 billion transaction to acquire and lease NVIDIA GB200 compute infrastructure to an xAI subsidiary.
2026: The transaction was structured as a triple-net lease arrangement tied to AI compute infrastructure.

oneSentenceDescription: Centric Fiber is a leading fiber-to-the-home (FTTH) broadband infrastructure provider serving master-planned communities, multi-family developments, and mixed-use projects in Texas, Florida, and Arizona.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ara Partners | Fund: Ara Infrastructure Fund I | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Centric Fiber develops and operates fiber-to-the-home networks serving residential communities and mixed-use developments. The business follows an asset-heavy digital infrastructure model based on network construction, long-lived fiber assets, and recurring subscription revenue. Ara described the company in 2025 as a fast-growing North American FTTH platform with operations in Texas, Florida, and Arizona and a focus on master-planned communities, multifamily, and mixed-use developments. Centric Fiber therefore targets localized broadband markets where network ownership and service quality can support durable recurring cash flow. Ara announced in November 2025 that it had acquired a majority controlling stake in Centric Fiber, while public sources do not disclose the final ownership percentage.
historicalMilestones: 2020: Centric Fiber was established as an FTTH platform.
Nov 2025: Ara Partners announced the acquisition of a majority controlling stake in Centric Fiber.
2025: Ara stated that the company was operating in Texas, Florida, and Arizona and serving master-planned communities, multifamily, and mixed-use developments.

oneSentenceDescription: Gate City Power consists of thermal generation assets across NYISO and ISO-NE totaling approximately 2.2 GW of natural gas-fired capacity.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ara Partners | Fund: Ara Energy | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Gate City Power is a portfolio of thermal generation assets in the NYISO and ISO New England markets. The business serves wholesale power demand through an asset-heavy generation model focused on dispatchable thermal capacity rather than regulated utility service. Ara stated in March 2026 that the portfolio comprised roughly 2.2 GW of thermal generation capacity across the two markets. Because the transaction was publicly described as a portfolio acquisition, reviewed sources do not disclose a separate founding year for the portfolio company. Ara Energy announced in March 2026 that it had secured rights to acquire Gate City Power from HF Capital, while public sources reviewed do not disclose a closing date or the post-close ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: Mar 2026: Ara Energy announced an agreement to acquire Gate City Power from HF Capital as part of an approximately $875 million transaction.
Mar 2026: Public disclosures described Gate City Power as owning about 2.2 GW of thermal generation capacity across NYISO and ISO New England.
2026: The announced transaction was framed as part of Ara Energy's strategy to optimize and decarbonize critical energy infrastructure over time.

oneSentenceDescription: Gate City Renewable Fuels comprises U.S.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ara Partners | Fund: Ara Energy | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Gate City Renewable Fuels is a portfolio of U.S. ethanol production assets. The business serves fuel blending and broader renewable fuels end markets through an asset-heavy industrial production model rather than through contracted utility-style revenues. Ara stated in March 2026 that the portfolio represented approximately 400 million gallons per year of ethanol production capacity. Because the publicly described transaction concerned a portfolio acquisition, reviewed sources do not disclose a separate founding year for the portfolio company. Ara Energy announced in March 2026 that it had secured rights to acquire Gate City Renewable Fuels from HF Capital, while public sources reviewed do not disclose a closing date or the post-close ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: Mar 2026: Ara Energy announced an agreement to acquire Gate City Renewable Fuels from HF Capital as part of an approximately $875 million transaction.
Mar 2026: Public disclosures described the renewable fuels portfolio as representing about 400 million gallons per year of ethanol production capacity.
2026: The announced transaction positioned the assets within Ara Energy's decarbonization and conventional-energy transition strategy.

oneSentenceDescription: Lincoln Terminal Holdings is a leading renewable fuel logistics and infrastructure provider in the Southeast and Mid-Atlantic United States.
principalLocationOfOperations: Alabama, Georgia, North Carolina & South Carolina
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ara Partners | Fund: Ara Infrastructure Fund I | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Lincoln Terminal Holdings provides rail transloading, storage, and logistics infrastructure for renewable fuels and related products. Its customers include fuel producers, distributors, and other supply-chain participants, and the business follows an asset-heavy terminaling model based on owned and operated infrastructure. Ara stated in 2022 that Lincoln operated five transloading and storage facilities across four southeastern U.S. states and described the business as a renewable fuel logistics provider in the Southeast and Mid-Atlantic. The platform is therefore tied to physical fuel movement and storage rather than commodity trading alone. Ara announced in November 2022 that it had acquired a majority ownership interest in Lincoln Terminal Holdings, while public sources do not disclose the exact ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: Nov 2022: Ara Partners acquired a majority ownership interest in Lincoln Terminal Holdings.
2022: Ara described Lincoln as the first investment for its infrastructure strategy.
2022: Public disclosures stated that Lincoln operated five rail transloading and storage facilities across four states.
2022: Ara also committed additional capital to expand Lincoln's renewable fuel infrastructure footprint.

oneSentenceDescription: USD Clean Fuels (USDCF) is a developer of large-scale multi-modal logistics centers for clean transportation fuels including biodiesel, renewable diesel, sustainable aviation fuel (SAF), and ethanol.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ara Partners | Fund: Ara Infrastructure Fund I | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: USD Clean Fuels develops and operates logistics infrastructure for renewable fuel feedstocks, biofuels, and related products. The business serves participants across the clean-fuels value chain through an asset-heavy model based on terminals, storage, handling infrastructure, and commercial logistics capabilities. Company materials describe a strategy spanning feedstocks, production, blending, and distribution, with assets such as the West Colton terminal supporting physical product flows. The platform is therefore positioned as infrastructure supporting renewable fuels supply chains rather than as a pure merchant producer. Ara announced in January 2024 that it had acquired a majority interest in USD Clean Fuels, while public sources do not disclose the resulting ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: Jan 2024: Ara Partners announced the acquisition of a majority interest in USD Clean Fuels.
2024: Ara described USD Clean Fuels as a logistics infrastructure platform spanning renewable fuel feedstocks, biofuels, and distribution.
2024: Company materials highlighted West Colton as a strategic terminal asset within the platform.

oneSentenceDescription: Advanced Power is a leading power infrastructure developer founded in 2000 with a pipeline of 12+ GW of conventional and renewable projects and 10+ GWh of energy storage.
ownershipDetails: Firm: ArcLight Capital Partners | Fund: ArcLight Capital Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Advanced Power develops, constructs, and manages large-scale power generation and storage projects. Its end markets are wholesale electricity systems, utilities, and capacity markets, and the business follows an asset-heavy development model centered on permitting, engineering, interconnection, procurement, and project financing. ArcLight stated in 2025 that Advanced Power had developed more than 6 GW historically and had a pipeline of more than 12 GW of new thermal and renewable projects plus more than 10 GWh of storage. The company operates from U.S. offices including Boston and Houston and functions as a development platform rather than a single-asset owner. ArcLight announced in July 2025 that it had acquired Advanced Power and committed $1 billion of equity to support further development, while public sources do not disclose the resulting ownership percentage.
historicalMilestones: 2000: Advanced Power was founded.
Jul 2025: ArcLight announced the acquisition of Advanced Power and a $1 billion equity commitment for new power infrastructure development.
2025: ArcLight disclosed that the platform had developed more than 6 GW of power projects historically.
2025: Public materials described a development pipeline exceeding 12 GW of new generation and more than 10 GWh of storage.

oneSentenceDescription: Alpha Generation (AlphaGen) is a strategic management platform overseeing one of the largest domestic power portfolios in the U.S.
ownershipDetails: Firm: ArcLight Capital Partners | Fund: ArcLight Energy Partners Fund VII | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Alpha Generation is ArcLight's management platform for a large portfolio of power generation assets in the United States. The platform serves wholesale power markets through strategic management and oversight of existing thermal generation assets rather than through greenfield development alone. ArcLight stated in 2024 that AlphaGen was formed to oversee about 14 GW of power infrastructure across four U.S. competitive power markets and a portfolio generating nearly $2 billion of annual revenue. Its role is therefore operational and portfolio-oriented, covering commercial, strategic, and asset-management functions across multiple generation subsidiaries. ArcLight announced the creation of AlphaGen in January 2024 as part of the management structure for ArcLight Energy Partners Fund VII, while public sources do not disclose the internal ownership split within the platform.
historicalMilestones: Jan 2024: ArcLight announced the formation of Alpha Generation.
2024: ArcLight stated that AlphaGen would oversee approximately 14 GW of power infrastructure across four U.S. markets.
2024: Public disclosures also described the managed portfolio as generating nearly $2 billion of annual revenue.

oneSentenceDescription: Catalyst Old River Hydroelectric owns and operates the 192 MW Sidney A.
ownershipDetails: Firm: ArcLight Capital Partners | Fund: ArcLight Capital Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Catalyst Old River Hydroelectric owns the Sidney A. Murray Jr. Hydroelectric Station on the Mississippi River near Vidalia, Louisiana. The asset serves utility and municipal offtakers through an asset-heavy hydro generation model supported by long-duration contractual arrangements. Public financing materials describe the facility as a 192 MW hydro plant with power sales to an Entergy subsidiary and the Town of Vidalia through contracts extending to 2031. The project is a long-life essential power asset rather than a diversified development platform, and reviewed sources do not disclose a separate project-company founding year. ArcLight has identified Catalyst Old River as a current investment, while public sources do not disclose the current ownership percentages at the asset level.
historicalMilestones: 1990: The Sidney A. Murray Jr. Hydroelectric Station entered commercial operation.
2011: Prudential financing materials referenced ArcLight's acquisition of the hydro investment.
Jul 2021: ArcLight announced the closing of a continuation fund that contemporaneously acquired a 25% interest in the 192 MW Sidney Murray hydro project from an older ArcLight fund.
2021: Prudential Private Capital invested $60 million in a refinancing tied to the hydro asset and its holding structure.

oneSentenceDescription: Eastern Generation owns and operates electric generating facilities totaling approximately 3,000 MW and manages an additional 6,000 MW portfolio.
ownershipDetails: Firm: ArcLight Capital Partners | Fund: AlphaGen / ArcLight | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Eastern Generation is a power generation platform focused on thermal assets in the northeastern United States. It serves wholesale electricity and capacity markets through an asset-heavy operating model built around gas-fired and dual-fuel generation plants. ArcLight stated in December 2015 that Eastern Generation was a newly formed portfolio company that acquired seven power plants totaling 4.8 GW from affiliates of Tenaska. The platform's footprint is concentrated in markets such as New York and Connecticut and later became part of ArcLight's broader AlphaGen management structure. Eastern Generation was formed in 2015 under ArcLight Energy Partners Fund VI, while public sources do not disclose the current ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: Dec 2015: Eastern Generation, a newly formed ArcLight portfolio company, acquired seven power plants totaling 4.8 GW from affiliates of Tenaska.
2016: Regulatory filings addressed the indirect transfer of control of lightly regulated generation facilities involving Eastern Generation.
2024: ArcLight's AlphaGen platform was formed to provide strategic oversight across Eastern Generation and related power assets.

oneSentenceDescription: Elevate Renewables is a national battery energy storage developer focused on co-locating BESS at existing large generation facilities owned by ArcLight.
ownershipDetails: Firm: ArcLight Capital Partners | Fund: ArcLight Energy Partners Fund VII | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Elevate Renewables develops and owns battery energy storage projects in the United States. The platform serves wholesale power markets and grid reliability needs through an asset-heavy development model focused on interconnection, site control, permitting, and long-duration storage operation. ArcLight stated in early 2023 that it had committed $150 million to Elevate and that the initial brownfield pipeline exceeded 5 GW across more than 20 sites. The company's strategy emphasizes storage development at or near existing power infrastructure rather than stand-alone greenfield land banking alone. ArcLight announced the capital commitment to Elevate in January 2023, while public sources do not disclose the platform's ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: 2022: Elevate Renewables was formed as ArcLight's battery storage platform.
Jan 2023: ArcLight announced a $150 million commitment to Elevate Renewables.
2023: Public materials described an initial brownfield storage pipeline exceeding 5 GW across more than 20 sites.
Jun 2024: Elevate and ArcLight announced a large battery storage project at Arthur Kill in New York City.

oneSentenceDescription: Generation Bridge is a 4.9 GW portfolio of 8 low-carbon power facilities serving large metro demand centers in PJM, NYISO, and ISONE markets.
ownershipDetails: Firm: ArcLight Capital Partners | Fund: AlphaGen / ArcLight | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Generation Bridge is a portfolio of dispatchable thermal generation assets in major U.S. competitive power markets. The business serves wholesale electricity and capacity demand through an asset-heavy operating model rather than through regulated utility distribution. ArcLight stated in 2021 that the portfolio comprised eight power generation facilities totaling 4.9 GW across markets including California, New York, and New England. The portfolio later came under ArcLight's AlphaGen management platform as part of a broader thermal generation strategy. ArcLight announced in December 2021 that it had closed the acquisition of Generation Bridge from NRG Energy for $760 million, while public sources do not disclose the current ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: Dec 2021: ArcLight closed the acquisition of the 4.9 GW Generation Bridge portfolio from NRG Energy for $760 million.
2021: Public disclosures described the portfolio as eight power generation facilities across California, New York, and New England.
2024: Generation Bridge was identified as part of ArcLight's AlphaGen-managed power portfolio.

oneSentenceDescription: The Gulf Coast Express Pipeline (GCX) is a 500-mile natural gas pipeline with ~2 Bcf/d capacity transporting gas from the Permian Basin to Gulf Coast markets.
ownershipDetails: Firm: ArcLight Capital Partners | Fund: AL GCX Holdings LLC (Fund VIII) | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Kinder Morgan | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Gulf Coast Express Pipeline is a large-diameter natural gas transmission system moving Permian Basin gas to Gulf Coast markets. The asset serves producers, processors, and downstream market participants through an asset-heavy, fee-based midstream model rather than commodity-owning merchant activity. Public materials describe GCX as an approximately 500-mile pipeline with about 2 Bcf per day of capacity connecting Permian supply to Agua Dulce in Texas under long-term committed contracts. The system is operated by Kinder Morgan and represents strategic takeaway infrastructure for Permian gas volumes. ArcLight announced in February 2025 that it had completed the acquisition of a 25% equity interest in GCX from an affiliate of Phillips 66 and that, going forward, the pipeline would be jointly owned by subsidiaries of Kinder Morgan and ArcLight.
historicalMilestones: Dec 2017: Kinder Morgan, DCP Midstream, and Targa announced a final investment decision for the Gulf Coast Express Pipeline project.
Sep 2019: Gulf Coast Express Pipeline entered service as a major Permian takeaway project.
Dec 2024: Phillips 66 announced an agreement to sell its 25% non-operated equity interest in GCX to an ArcLight affiliate.
Feb 2025: ArcLight announced completion of the $865 million acquisition and stated that GCX would be jointly owned by subsidiaries of Kinder Morgan and ArcLight.
2024-2026: Kinder Morgan advanced a compression-based expansion project to increase GCX deliveries to South Texas markets.

oneSentenceDescription: The Haynesville Gas Gathering System is a natural gas gathering system in the Haynesville shale play with nominal capacity of up to 1.2 Bcf/d.
ownershipDetails: Firm: ArcLight Capital Partners | Fund: ArcLight / Cutlass Energy | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Haynesville Gas Gathering System is a natural gas gathering network serving production in the Haynesville shale. The system operates under an asset-heavy midstream model focused on field gathering, compression, and transportation rather than commodity ownership. ArcLight stated in 2022 that the network was located in Caddo Parish, Louisiana and Harrison County, Texas and had capacity of up to 1.2 Bcf per day. The asset therefore functions as critical upstream infrastructure linked to one of the largest U.S. gas-producing basins. ArcLight and Cutlass Energy announced in March 2022 that they were acquiring the system through Saber Midstream, while public sources do not disclose separate ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: Mar 2022: ArcLight and Cutlass Energy announced the acquisition of the Haynesville Gas Gathering System through Saber Midstream.
2022: Public disclosures described the system as having up to 1.2 Bcf per day of gathering capacity.
2022: The assets were identified as being located in Caddo Parish, Louisiana and Harrison County, Texas.

oneSentenceDescription: Infinigen Renewables is a renewable energy developer, operator, and IPP and the leading solar platform in Puerto Rico with over 100 MW of fleet capacity and 1+ GW development pipeline.
ownershipDetails: Firm: ArcLight Capital Partners | Fund: ArcLight Energy Partners Fund VII | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Infinigen Renewables is a solar and storage platform focused on Puerto Rico. The business serves the island's power market through an asset-heavy model combining operating renewable assets, new development, and storage integration. ArcLight stated at launch that the platform included what it described as the largest solar and storage operating fleet in Puerto Rico and a controlled pipeline approaching 1 GW of additional projects. Later public disclosures included the acquisition of Sonnedix's Puerto Rico solar portfolio, extending the platform's operating base. ArcLight launched Infinigen in December 2021 with a $400 million capital commitment through ArcLight Energy Partners Fund VII, while public sources do not disclose the detailed ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: Dec 2021: ArcLight launched Infinigen Renewables with a $400 million capital commitment.
2021: ArcLight described Infinigen as the largest solar and storage operating platform in Puerto Rico at launch.
2023: Sonnedix agreed to sell its Puerto Rico solar operations to Infinigen and ArcLight.

oneSentenceDescription: Inspiration Mobility is a sustainable infrastructure platform focused on enabling the EV transition, providing fleet management, charging-as-a-service, and electrification finance to commercial fleets.
ownershipDetails: Firm: ArcLight Capital Partners | Fund: ArcLight Energy Partners Fund VII | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Inspiration Mobility Group provides electric-vehicle infrastructure and fleet electrification solutions for commercial fleet operators. The platform serves customers that need vehicles, charging, and operating support, and its model combines service delivery with ownership and deployment of charging assets. At launch, the company announced a fleet partnership with Revel and an initial $200 million capital commitment from ArcLight, and later stated that it had grown to more than 60 employees with thousands of EVs on the road. Its footprint is U.S.-focused and tied to commercial fleet use cases rather than passenger retail charging alone. ArcLight backed the launch in November 2021 and Macquarie Asset Management and Ferrovial later provided additional capital in 2024, while public sources do not disclose the detailed ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: Nov 2021: Inspiration Mobility launched with a $200 million capital commitment from ArcLight and a fleet partnership with Revel.
2021: The company entered the market as a purpose-built EV solutions platform for commercial fleets.
Sep 2024: Inspiration announced $215 million of additional capital from Macquarie Asset Management and Ferrovial.

oneSentenceDescription: Invenergy AMPCI Thermal Power (IATP) is a portfolio of 11 highly efficient combined-cycle power infrastructure assets totaling 5.4 GW across seven markets in the U.S.
ownershipDetails: Firm: ArcLight Capital Partners | Fund: ArcLight Capital Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Invenergy AMPCI Thermal Power is a portfolio of dispatchable thermal generation assets operated by Invenergy. The business serves competitive wholesale power markets through an asset-heavy portfolio of generation plants rather than through regulated distribution operations. ArcLight stated in March 2026 that the portfolio comprised 11 assets totaling 5.4 GW across North America. The announcement described the transaction as an acquisition of InfraBridge's 50% stake, indicating a continuing co-ownership and operating role for Invenergy after closing. ArcLight announced the transaction in March 2026, but public sources reviewed do not disclose a separate platform founding year or the post-close ownership percentages beyond the acquired 50% interest.
historicalMilestones: Mar 2026: ArcLight announced an agreement to acquire InfraBridge's 50% stake in the Invenergy AMPCI Thermal Power portfolio.
2026: Public disclosures described the portfolio as 11 dispatchable thermal assets totaling 5.4 GW across North America.
2026: The transaction announcement indicated that Invenergy would remain involved as operator and continuing owner.

oneSentenceDescription: Kleen Energy Systems is a 620 MW natural gas-fired combined-cycle power plant in Middletown, Connecticut, one of New England's newest and most efficient plants.
ownershipDetails: Firm: ArcLight Capital Partners | Fund: ArcLight Capital Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Kleen Energy Systems owns a combined-cycle natural gas power plant in Middletown, Connecticut. The asset serves wholesale electricity markets through an asset-heavy merchant generation model. Public disclosures describe the facility as a 620 MW plant, making it a sizeable thermal generation asset in New England. ArcLight's documented investment history reflects a staged ownership build-up rather than a single initial transaction. ArcLight announced in November 2023 that it had completed the acquisition of a preferred and minority common interest from Sojitz and in April 2025 announced a further agreement to acquire Osaka Gas and Kyuden common equity interests.
historicalMilestones: 2010: Kleen Energy's Middletown plant entered commercial operation following development and construction.
Nov 2023: ArcLight completed the acquisition of a preferred and minority common interest in Kleen Energy Systems from Sojitz.
Apr 2025: ArcLight announced an agreement to acquire Osaka Gas and Kyuden common equity interests in Kleen Energy Systems.

oneSentenceDescription: Lordstown Energy Center is a 940 MW combined-cycle natural gas-fueled facility in Lordstown, Ohio, providing power to approximately 850,000 homes and businesses via PJM Interconnection.
ownershipDetails: Firm: ArcLight Capital Partners | Fund: AlphaGen / ArcLight | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Lordstown Energy Center is a combined-cycle natural gas power plant in Ohio. The asset serves wholesale electricity markets through an asset-heavy thermal generation model rather than through regulated utility operations. Public disclosures describe the plant as a 940 MW facility in Trumbull County near the village of Lordstown. The plant is part of ArcLight's broader power infrastructure portfolio and later falls under the AlphaGen management umbrella. ArcLight announced in February 2024 that an affiliate would acquire Lordstown Energy Center from Macquarie Infrastructure Partners III, while public sources do not disclose the final ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: 2018: Lordstown Energy Center entered commercial operation as a large combined-cycle gas plant in Ohio.
Feb 2024: An ArcLight affiliate announced an agreement to acquire Lordstown Energy Center from Macquarie Infrastructure Partners III.
2024: Lordstown was identified within the broader AlphaGen-managed power portfolio.

oneSentenceDescription: Middletown Energy Center is a 483 MW combined-cycle natural gas power plant in Butler County, Ohio, one of the newest CCGT plants in PJM.
ownershipDetails: Firm: ArcLight Capital Partners | Fund: Parkway Generation / ArcLight | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Middletown Energy Center is a natural gas-fired combined-cycle power plant in Butler County, Ohio. The asset serves wholesale electricity markets through an asset-heavy thermal generation model. Public materials identify the facility as a highly efficient plant of roughly 475 MW to 484 MW, with ribbon-cutting and operating disclosures in 2018. Its revenue profile is tied to wholesale power and capacity markets rather than regulated customer rates. ArcLight announced in July 2025 that it had entered into agreements to acquire 100% of the economic interests in Middletown Energy Center, while public sources do not disclose a later closing statement within the materials reviewed.
historicalMilestones: May 2018: Public materials marked the ribbon cutting for Middletown Energy Center.
Jul 2025: ArcLight announced agreements to acquire 100% of the economic interests in Middletown Energy Center.
Jul 2025: Axium Infrastructure and its equity partners separately announced the sale of the plant to ArcLight.

portfolioCompany: Natural Gas Pipeline Co. of America
oneSentenceDescription: Natural Gas Pipeline Company of America (NGPL) is one of the largest interstate pipeline systems in the U.S., spanning nine states with ~9,100 miles of pipeline, 1M+ compression horsepower, and 288 Bcf of working natural gas storage.
profileCompanyName: Natural Gas Pipeline Co. of America
ownershipDetails: Firm: ArcLight Capital Partners | Fund: ArcLight Capital Partners | Ownership Interest: 62.5%
Firm: Kinder Morgan | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 37.5%
companyOverview: Natural Gas Pipeline Company of America is a large interstate natural gas transmission and storage system. The asset serves producers, utilities, marketers, and industrial users through an asset-heavy, predominantly fee-based transportation and storage model. ArcLight stated in 2025 that NGPL includes about 9,100 miles of pipeline, more than one million horsepower of compression, and approximately 288 Bcf of working gas storage. The scale of the system makes it one of the larger gas infrastructure platforms in North America. ArcLight announced in May 2025 that, after acquiring Brookfield's remaining 25% interest, it would become NGPL's largest owner with a 62.5% economic interest alongside operator Kinder Morgan's 37.5% interest.
historicalMilestones: Jun 2023: Public deal reporting described ArcLight acquiring an incremental 12.5% stake in NGPL.
May 2025: ArcLight announced the acquisition of Brookfield's remaining 25% interest in NGPL.
May 2025: ArcLight stated that it would become the largest owner of NGPL with a 62.5% economic interest.
2025: Public disclosures identified Kinder Morgan as operator and 37.5% economic owner.

oneSentenceDescription: Naugatuck Avenue Storage is a battery energy storage project in Connecticut, part of the state's push toward its 1,000 MW by 2030 energy storage goal.
ownershipDetails: Firm: ArcLight Capital Partners | Fund: ArcLight Capital Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Naugatuck Avenue Storage is a battery energy storage project in Connecticut. The project is intended to serve grid reliability needs through an asset-heavy storage model tied to interconnection rights, permitting, and dispatchable capacity rather than retail end users. Publicly available interconnection and project-tracking materials identify the project as a battery facility in New Haven County with planned capacity of roughly 205 MW. Reviewed public sources do not disclose a separate founding year for the project company, and sponsor-level ownership information is limited. The project appears in ArcLight-related portfolio materials, but public sources reviewed do not disclose the current ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: 2021: Interconnection materials show the project in the ISO New England queue process.
2024: Public reporting on Connecticut procurement activity referenced Naugatuck Avenue as a battery storage project.
2026: Project-tracking databases continued to list Naugatuck Avenue Storage as a planned battery facility in Connecticut.

oneSentenceDescription: Parkway Generation is a 4.8 GW portfolio of nine modern, highly efficient natural gas-fired power generation facilities in New Jersey and Maryland acquired from PSEG for $1.37 billion.
ownershipDetails: Firm: ArcLight Capital Partners | Fund: AlphaGen / ArcLight | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Parkway Generation is a portfolio of modern natural gas-fired power plants in the northeastern United States. The business serves wholesale electricity and capacity markets through an asset-heavy merchant generation model. ArcLight stated in 2022 that Parkway included nine modern gas-fired facilities totaling approximately 4.8 GW in New Jersey and Maryland. The portfolio later became part of ArcLight's AlphaGen-managed power platform, reflecting a centralized management approach across multiple thermal assets. ArcLight announced in February 2022 that Parkway Generation had closed the acquisition of the portfolio from PSEG, while public sources do not disclose the detailed ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: Feb 2022: Parkway Generation closed the acquisition of a 4.8 GW portfolio of nine gas-fired plants from PSEG.
2022: Public disclosures located the portfolio in New Jersey and Maryland.
2024: Parkway Generation was identified as part of ArcLight's AlphaGen-managed power platform.

oneSentenceDescription: Phoenix Renewables is a portfolio of operating renewable energy assets under ArcLight Fund VIII, managed through the SkyVest Renewables platform by ArcLight Renewable Services.
ownershipDetails: Firm: ArcLight Capital Partners | Fund: ArcLight Infra Partners Fund VIII | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Phoenix Renewables is an operating portfolio of renewable generation assets managed within ArcLight's renewables services platform. The assets serve wholesale power markets through an asset-heavy operating model focused on existing wind and solar infrastructure rather than early-stage development. ArcLight's portfolio-services materials identify Phoenix Renewables as one of the initial ArcLight Fund VIII portfolio investments managed through SkyVest Renewables. The platform is therefore tied to operating renewable asset management and commercialization rather than a standalone equipment or services business. ArcLight's renewable-services platform disclosed the management structure in 2024, while public sources reviewed do not disclose Phoenix's separate founding year or ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: Jul 2024: ArcLight launched SkyVest Renewables and disclosed Phoenix Renewables as one of the initial Fund VIII portfolios it would manage.
2024: ArcLight framed Phoenix Renewables as part of an operating-focused renewables initiative.
2025: ArcLight investment materials continued to identify Phoenix Renewables as a current investment.

oneSentenceDescription: Prospect Power Storage is a 150 MW/600 MWh battery storage project in Rockingham County, Virginia near Data Center Alley, the largest standalone BESS in PJM.
ownershipDetails: Firm: ArcLight Capital Partners | Fund: Elevate Renewables / ArcLight | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Prospect Power is a standalone battery storage project in Rockingham County, Virginia. The asset is designed to serve grid reliability and wholesale market needs through an asset-heavy storage model rather than retail electricity supply. Public disclosures describe the project as a 150 MW and 600 MWh facility near Northern Virginia's Data Center Alley, with operations expected in 2026. The project is positioned within PJM and reflects the growing role of storage assets in balancing high-load power markets.
