Elevate Renewables announced in January 2026 that it had acquired Prospect Power from Swift Current Energy, while public sources do not disclose the detailed ownership percentages within ArcLight's platform.
historicalMilestones: Jan 2026: Elevate Renewables announced the acquisition of Prospect Power from Swift Current Energy.
2026: Public disclosures described the project as a 150 MW and 600 MWh battery storage asset in Rockingham County, Virginia.
2026: The project was reported to be under construction with expected operations in mid-2026.

oneSentenceDescription: REC Solar is a leading developer, owner, and operator of distributed solar, batteries, and fuel cell assets across 29 U.S.
ownershipDetails: Firm: ArcLight Capital Partners | Fund: ArcLight Energy Partners Fund VII | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: REC Solar develops, installs, owns, and operates distributed solar, storage, fuel cell, and microgrid systems for commercial and public-sector customers. The platform combines development, engineering, construction, financing, and operations services, so its model blends project execution income with long-lived distributed energy assets. Company materials state that REC Solar was founded in 1997 and now has a national record spanning more than 700 projects across 29 states. Its customer base includes businesses, public agencies, and institutional buyers seeking on-site or behind-the-meter energy infrastructure. Duke Energy completed the sale of its commercial distributed generation portfolio, including REC Solar, to ArcLight in October 2023, while public sources do not disclose the detailed post-closing ownership structure within ArcLight's fund.
historicalMilestones: 1997: REC Solar was founded in California.
2005: REC Solar was acquired by Mainstream Energy and continued to operate under the REC Solar brand.
Oct 2023: Duke Energy completed the sale of its commercial distributed generation portfolio, including REC Solar, to ArcLight.
2025: REC Solar stated that it had delivered more than 700 projects across 29 states.

oneSentenceDescription: Sequitur Renewables is a wind energy platform focused on acquiring mature operational wind farms and executing value-add operational, commercial, and financial strategies.
ownershipDetails: Firm: ArcLight Capital Partners | Fund: ArcLight Capital Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Sequitur Renewables is an operating renewable-energy platform built around wind assets and related optimization opportunities. The business serves wholesale power markets through an asset-heavy operating model focused on existing projects rather than early-stage development alone. ArcLight launched the platform in 2022 with the acquisition of a 185 MW wind portfolio in PJM spanning Pennsylvania and West Virginia, and later expanded it with an operating California wind farm in Altamont Pass. The platform is therefore oriented toward ownership, operation, and commercial optimization of renewable assets across multiple markets. ArcLight established Sequitur in 2022 and public sources do not disclose the detailed ownership percentages within the platform.
historicalMilestones: 2022: ArcLight established Sequitur Renewables through the acquisition of a 185 MW wind portfolio in PJM.
May 2023: Sequitur announced the acquisition of an operating California wind farm in Altamont Pass.
2023: The California acquisition expanded Sequitur beyond its original PJM wind portfolio.

oneSentenceDescription: SkyVest Renewables is an operating-focused renewables platform backed by ArcLight with an initial $500 million capital commitment, targeting utility-scale wind and solar assets in North America.
ownershipDetails: Firm: ArcLight Capital Partners | Fund: ArcLight Renewable Services | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: SkyVest Renewables is ArcLight's operating platform for managing and optimizing renewable power assets. The platform supports wholesale renewable generation portfolios through an operating and commercialization model rather than through equipment manufacturing or standalone advisory services. ArcLight announced at launch that SkyVest would be backed by an initial $500 million capital commitment and that it was simultaneously acquiring a 160 MW operating wind farm in Texas as its inaugural transaction. Portfolio-services materials show SkyVest managing Phoenix Renewables and Thunderbird Renewables for ArcLight Fund VIII. ArcLight launched SkyVest in July 2024, while public sources do not disclose the detailed ownership percentages within the operating platform.
historicalMilestones: Jul 2024: ArcLight launched SkyVest Renewables with an initial $500 million capital commitment.
Jul 2024: SkyVest concurrently closed on the acquisition of a 160 MW operating wind farm in Texas.
2024: ArcLight portfolio-services materials identified SkyVest as the manager of Phoenix Renewables and Thunderbird Renewables for Fund VIII.

oneSentenceDescription: Takanock is a provider of innovative digital and power infrastructure solutions addressing the power shortage limiting data center development.
ownershipDetails: Firm: ArcLight Capital Partners | Fund: ArcLight Capital | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: DigitalBridge | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Takanock develops and finances digital and power infrastructure solutions for data centers in power-constrained markets. The platform serves hyperscale and other large data center demand through an asset-heavy model tied to power delivery, infrastructure deployment, and site enablement rather than retail colocation. Public disclosures state that Takanock secured $500 million of capital from ArcLight and DigitalBridge in June 2025 and identified the company as being based in Traverse City, Michigan. The business is designed to address the power and infrastructure bottlenecks associated with rapid data center expansion. ArcLight and DigitalBridge announced the capital commitment in June 2025, while public sources do not disclose the ownership percentages within Takanock.
historicalMilestones: Jun 2025: Takanock announced a $500 million capital commitment from ArcLight and DigitalBridge.
2025: Public disclosures described the company as a provider of digital and power infrastructure solutions for data centers.
2025: The transaction positioned Takanock to pursue deployments in power-constrained data center markets.

oneSentenceDescription: Third Coast Infrastructure is the premier privately owned U.S.
ownershipDetails: Firm: ArcLight Capital Partners | Fund: ArcLight Capital Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Third Coast Infrastructure owns and operates midstream assets serving Gulf of Mexico and coastal hydrocarbon production systems. Its customers are offshore producers and related midstream counterparties, and the business follows an asset-heavy model based on gas transmission and gathering, crude pipelines, gas processing, and NGL infrastructure. The platform emerged from ArcLight's take-private of American Midstream and now includes infrastructure tied to deepwater Gulf production and regional processing networks. Its revenue model is infrastructure-based rather than upstream reserve ownership. ArcLight took American Midstream private in 2019 and the business subsequently operated under the Third Coast structure, while public sources do not disclose the current ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: Jul 2019: ArcLight completed the take-private of American Midstream Partners, which later became part of the Third Coast platform.
Jul 2021: ArcLight announced a continuation fund that included Third Coast infrastructure assets.
Jun 2021: Third Coast Midstream completed the sale of a significant minority interest in a subsidiary infrastructure holding company.
Jan 2025: Third Coast Super Holdings announced a $25 million loan upsize and repricing at the infrastructure subsidiary level.

oneSentenceDescription: Thunderbird Renewables is a portfolio of operating renewable energy assets under ArcLight Fund VIII, managed through the SkyVest Renewables platform by ArcLight Renewable Services.
ownershipDetails: Firm: ArcLight Capital Partners | Fund: ArcLight Infra Partners Fund VIII | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Thunderbird Renewables is an operating portfolio of renewable generation assets managed within ArcLight's renewables services platform. The assets serve wholesale power markets through an asset-heavy operating model focused on commercial optimization of existing renewable infrastructure. ArcLight's portfolio-services materials identify Thunderbird Renewables as one of the initial ArcLight Fund VIII portfolio investments managed through SkyVest Renewables. The platform is therefore structured around ownership and management of operating renewable assets rather than equipment supply or greenfield development alone. ArcLight disclosed the management structure in 2024, while public sources reviewed do not disclose Thunderbird's separate founding year or detailed ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: Jul 2024: ArcLight launched SkyVest Renewables and disclosed Thunderbird Renewables as one of the initial Fund VIII portfolios it would manage.
2024: ArcLight framed Thunderbird Renewables as part of an operating-focused renewables initiative.
2025: ArcLight investment materials continued to identify Thunderbird Renewables as a current investment.

oneSentenceDescription: Two Rivers Storage is a battery energy storage project developed as part of ArcLight's broader strategy of deploying BESS at legacy thermal generation sites.
ownershipDetails: Firm: ArcLight Capital Partners | Fund: ArcLight Capital Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Two Rivers Storage is a standalone battery energy storage project associated with the Bergen Generating Station site in New Jersey. The project is intended to serve grid reliability and wholesale market needs through an asset-heavy storage model rather than retail customer supply. Public materials tied to the New Jersey Board of Public Utilities identify the project, also referred to as the Garden State Reliability Project, as a 150 MW and 600 MWh battery facility in Ridgefield. Reviewed public sources do not disclose a separate founding year for the project company, although they do connect the project to ArcLight's Elevate platform. New Jersey BPU and company materials in March 2026 publicly advanced the project, while public sources do not disclose the detailed project-level ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: Mar 2026: New Jersey BPU selected Elevate's Garden State Reliability Project, also known as Two Rivers Storage, under the state energy storage program.
2026: Public materials described the project as a 150 MW and 600 MWh battery facility at the Bergen Generating Station site in Ridgefield, New Jersey.
2026: The project was advanced as part of ArcLight and Elevate's broader battery-storage build-out in high-demand power markets.

oneSentenceDescription: Zeem Solutions is a zero-emission fleet-as-a-service provider offering electric trucks, vans, and shuttle buses for a flat monthly fee including vehicle lease, charging, maintenance, and insurance.
ownershipDetails: Firm: ArcLight Capital Partners | Fund: ArcLight Capital Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Zeem Solutions provides electric-vehicle fleet charging infrastructure and fleet-as-a-service solutions. Its customers are commercial fleet operators, and the business combines charging depots, fleet support services, and related operating infrastructure in an asset-heavy model. Public materials identify Inglewood, California as a key operating site and describe the company as focused on accelerating electrification for medium- and heavy-duty fleets. The platform is therefore aligned with fleet transition infrastructure rather than with consumer charging networks. Zeem announced in July 2022 that it had secured a $50 million capital investment from ArcLight affiliates and a strategic partnership with LAZ Parking, while public sources do not disclose the detailed ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: 2020: Zeem Solutions was founded.
Jul 2022: Zeem announced a $50 million capital investment from ArcLight affiliates.
Jul 2022: Zeem also announced a strategic partnership with LAZ Parking.
2020s: The company developed charging and fleet infrastructure centered on commercial EV deployments in Southern California.

oneSentenceDescription: AFCO is a leader in on-airport facility investment, development, financing, leasing, and management, operating over 3.5 million sqft of airport infrastructure across 29 properties at 15 airport locations in the US and UK.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ardian | Fund: Ardian Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: AFCO develops, finances, leases, and manages on-airport cargo and other aviation facilities. Its customers include airlines, freight forwarders, ground handlers, and airport users that require specialized airport-adjacent infrastructure. The business follows an asset-heavy model built around long-duration aviation real estate and operating platforms at multiple airports. Public company materials state that AFCO manages more than 3.5 million square feet across 29 properties at 15 airport locations in North America and the United Kingdom. AFCO was founded in 1992, Goldman Sachs Alternatives acquired the company in 2018, and Ardian announced in February 2025 that it had signed an agreement to acquire Goldman Sachs Alternatives' stake.
historicalMilestones: 1992: AFCO was established.
2000: AFCO sold a majority interest in 16 facilities to AMB.
2018: Goldman Sachs Alternatives acquired AFCO.
February 2025: Ardian signed an agreement to acquire Goldman Sachs Alternatives' stake in AFCO.

ownershipVehicle: Ardian Americas Infrastructure Fund V (AAIF V)
oneSentenceDescription: CampusParc is the first and largest university parking concession in the U.S., managing over 38,000 parking spaces at The Ohio State University under a 50-year concession agreement.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ardian | Fund: Ardian Americas Infrastructure Fund V (AAIF V) | Ownership Interest: 100%
companyOverview: CampusParc is the concessionaire that manages, operates, and maintains the parking system at The Ohio State University. Its customer base includes students, faculty, staff, visitors, and users of the Wexner Medical Center campus. The business operates under a long-term concession model in which parking revenues support operations, maintenance, and capital obligations across the system. Public disclosures describe more than 37,000 parking spaces across garages, surface lots, and metered spaces under a 50-year concession in Columbus, Ohio. CampusParc was formed in 2012, originally by QIC and LAZ Parking, and Ardian acquired a 100 percent stake from funds managed by QIC in June 2024 through AAIF V.
historicalMilestones: June 2012: Ohio State executed the 50-year parking lease and concession agreement with CampusParc LP.
2012: CampusParc was formed by QIC and LAZ Parking to serve as the concessionaire for the university parking system.
2012: The concession became the first long-term lease and concession for a North American university parking system.
June 2024: Ardian acquired a 100 percent stake in CampusParc from funds managed by QIC.

ownershipVehicle: Ardian Americas Infrastructure Fund V (AAIF V)
oneSentenceDescription: Clermont is a Canadian parking joint venture between Ardian and Indigo Group formed to invest in parking assets, concessions, and long-term leases across Canada.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ardian | Fund: Ardian Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Indigo Group | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Clermont is a Canadian parking investment platform formed by Ardian and Indigo Group. The platform is intended to acquire and manage parking assets, concessions, and long-term leases serving municipalities, institutions, and commercial customers across Canada. Its operating model combines long-duration parking rights with Indigo's operating capabilities and local market coverage. Public launch materials describe Clermont as a dedicated national vehicle for further parking investments in Canada, with Indigo continuing to manage operations. Clermont was created in March 2024 by Ardian and Indigo Group as a new joint venture, and the detailed ownership split has not been publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: March 2024: Ardian and Indigo Group announced the creation of Clermont.
March 2024: Clermont was launched to invest in parking assets, concessions, and long-term leases across Canada.
March 2024: Indigo Group said it would continue to manage parking operations for the platform.

portfolioCompany: Dauntless Energy (incl. Skyline Renewables & 1370 Clean Energy)
ownershipVehicle: Ardian Americas Infrastructure Fund IV (AAIF IV)
oneSentenceDescription: Dauntless Energy is Ardian's exclusive North American energy asset manager and IPP building a low-carbon generation portfolio across solar, wind, and efficient natural gas.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ardian | Fund: Ardian Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Dauntless Energy is Ardian's North American energy asset manager and independent power platform. The company develops, acquires, and manages wind, solar, storage, and efficient gas-fired generation assets serving wholesale power markets and contracted offtakers. Its operating model is asset-heavy and combines portfolio management, development, and operating oversight across multiple generation vehicles. Public materials identify Skyline Renewables, launched in 2018, and 1370 Clean Energy, launched in 2025, as key portfolio vehicles managed by Dauntless in the United States. The platform traces back to Ardian's 2018 launch of Skyline Renewables with TPH and was rebranded as Dauntless Energy, while ownership details for the current management platform have not been publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: March 2018: Ardian and TPH launched Skyline Renewables and announced the acquisition of a 60 MW wind project.
2018: Skyline Renewables was established as Ardian's United States renewables platform.
2024: The North American energy asset manager began operating under the Dauntless Energy brand.
2025: Dauntless added 1370 Clean Energy as a growth vehicle for clean energy investments.

portfolioCompany: Maple Leaf (Enel X Battery Storage JV)
oneSentenceDescription: Maple Leaf is a joint venture between Ardian (80%) and Enel X (20%) managing a battery storage portfolio of ten asset locations throughout Ontario, Canada with ~30 MW of combined capacity.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ardian | Fund: Ardian Infrastructure / AAIF IV | Ownership Interest: 80%
Firm: Enel X | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 20%
companyOverview: Maple Leaf is the battery storage joint venture formed by Ardian and Enel X in Canada. The platform provides grid services through utility-scale battery assets in Ontario and serves the provincial power system. Its operating model is asset-heavy and based on contracted and market-based storage revenues from a portfolio of operating sites. At launch, the venture comprised ten battery storage sites in Ontario with about 30 MW of aggregate capacity. Ardian and Enel X launched Maple Leaf in September 2020 with an 80 percent and 20 percent ownership split, respectively.
historicalMilestones: September 2020: Enel X and Ardian launched Maple Leaf as a battery storage partnership in Canada.
September 2020: The venture was launched with ten Ontario battery storage sites totaling about 30 MW.
September 2020: Ardian took an 80 percent stake and Enel X retained 20 percent.

ownershipVehicle: Ardian Americas Infrastructure Fund V (AAIF V)
oneSentenceDescription: Unison Infrastructure is a telecom and renewables infrastructure investment firm that purchases cell site ground leases, rooftop communication sites, and fiber-based site leases.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ardian | Fund: Ardian Americas Infrastructure Fund V (AAIF V) | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Unison Site Management acquires and manages wireless infrastructure lease interests, including cell site ground leases, rooftop communication sites, and fiber-related site leases. Its customers are tower companies, wireless carriers, landlords, and infrastructure operators that rely on long-dated communications site rights. The business follows an asset-heavy cash flow model based on acquiring leasehold interests and collecting contracted rent streams over time. Public materials state that Unison was founded in 2003 and expanded from the United States into Europe after building a large portfolio of communications site interests. Ardian made its initial investment in Unison in 2017 and later cited the company as the first telecommunications investment for AAIF V when it closed the fund in 2023.
historicalMilestones: 2003: Unison Site Management was founded by Dewey Shay.
2017: Ardian made a strategic investment in Unison.
2023: Ardian highlighted Unison as the first telecommunications investment in AAIF V at the fund's close.
2023: Ardian stated that Unison had expanded from the United States into Europe.

oneSentenceDescription: Ada Infrastructure is Ares Management's global data center platform with nine in-flight campuses across the US, Latin America, Japan, and the UK totaling over 1 GW of IT capacity.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ares Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Ada Infrastructure develops and operates hyperscale and enterprise data center campuses. Its customer base includes cloud, artificial intelligence, and enterprise users that require large-scale powered land, development, and operating support. The business follows an asset-heavy development model centered on campus origination, design, construction management, and long-term operation of data center infrastructure. Public company materials describe nine in-flight campuses across the United States, Latin America, Japan, and the United Kingdom with more than 1 GW of planned IT capacity. Ares acquired the underlying platform from GCP International and has continued to expand it, including a 314-acre site in Spotsylvania County announced in December 2025, while detailed fund ownership has not been publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2023: Ares acquired the data center platform from GCP International.
2024: Ares said Ada Infrastructure formed part of its vertically integrated data center development strategy.
December 2025: Ada announced the acquisition of a 314-acre site in Spotsylvania County, Virginia for a planned 200 MW first phase.
2025: Public company materials described nine in-flight campuses totaling more than 1 GW of planned IT capacity.

oneSentenceDescription: Apex Clean Energy is a leading U.S.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ares Management | Fund: Ares Infrastructure Opportunities | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Apex Clean Energy develops, constructs, owns, and operates utility-scale wind, solar, and storage projects. Its customers include utilities, corporations, and wholesale market participants that purchase renewable electricity, renewable energy credits, and capacity from completed projects. The company combines development and operating activities, so its model includes both development-stage risk and long-term ownership of selected operating assets. Public disclosures describe Apex as an established United States renewables platform founded in 2009 and headquartered in Charlottesville, Virginia. Ares Management closed its acquisition of a majority stake in November 2021 and Apex later refinanced the business in 2023 as part of a broader transition toward an independent power producer model.
historicalMilestones: 2009: Apex Clean Energy was founded.
November 2021: Ares Management and Apex closed the acquisition of a majority stake in the company.
2023: Apex announced a refinancing tied to its transition toward an independent power producer model.

oneSentenceDescription: Atlas Crane Service is a full-service crane rental provider offering turnkey maintenance, repair, and overhaul solutions primarily serving the wind industry with over 250 customers.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ares Management | Fund: Ares Infrastructure Opportunities | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Atlas Crane Service provides crane rental and maintenance, repair, and overhaul services focused primarily on wind energy equipment and related industrial lifts. Its customers include wind project owners, operators, and service providers that require heavy-lift equipment and field service support. The business combines an asset-heavy crane fleet with service labor and project execution capabilities, giving it exposure to recurring maintenance work as well as new assignments. Ares' acquisition announcement described Atlas as serving more than 250 customers from its headquarters in Aurora, Illinois. Atlas was founded in 2009, and Ares Management acquired a controlling interest in August 2022 through its Infrastructure Opportunities strategy.
historicalMilestones: 2009: Atlas Crane Service was founded.
August 2022: Ares Management acquired a controlling interest in Atlas Crane Service.
2022: Ares said Atlas served more than 250 customers at the time of acquisition.

oneSentenceDescription: Birdsboro Power is a 488 MW natural gas-fired combined-cycle power plant in Berks County, Pennsylvania serving the PJM market.
ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Birdsboro Power LLC owns the Birdsboro combined-cycle natural gas power plant in Berks County, Pennsylvania. The plant serves the PJM market and is designed to supply efficient thermal generation to regional power customers. It is an asset-heavy operating power plant, and public sources identify it as a 488 MW facility that entered commercial operation in 2019. Public disclosures from the original sponsor group showed Ares EIF, Tokyo Gas America, Sojitz, and Kyushu Electric as investors, and a 2025 announcement stated that Strategic Value Partners acquired a stake in the project. Because the full current cap table and ownership percentages have not been publicly disclosed, the detailed current ownership structure is not publicly available.
historicalMilestones: April 2017: Tokyo Gas America acquired a 33.33 percent interest in Birdsboro from a fund managed by Ares EIF.
December 2017: Kyushu Electric announced the acquisition of an indirect 11.1 percent stake in the project from Sojitz Group.
May 2019: The 488 MW Birdsboro plant commenced commercial operation.
May 2025: Strategic Value Partners announced the acquisition of a stake in Birdsboro Power.

oneSentenceDescription: Bluepeak is a leading fiber broadband provider serving residential and commercial customers across the Great Plains region including North Dakota, South Dakota, Oklahoma, Wyoming, and Minnesota.
principalLocationOfOperations: South Dakota, North Dakota, Oklahoma, Wyoming, and Minnesota
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ares Management | Fund: Ares Infrastructure Opportunities | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Bluepeak provides fiber broadband services to residential and commercial customers in secondary and underserved markets in the Great Plains region. Its operating model combines owned fiber infrastructure with recurring subscription revenue from internet and related communications services. The company is asset-heavy because it finances, builds, and operates last-mile fiber networks across its footprint. At the time of Ares' investment announcement, Bluepeak said it had expanded across five states since 2021 and added access to more than 140,000 new homes. Ares Management announced a strategic investment in February 2024 to support further network expansion, while detailed ownership percentages were not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2021: Bluepeak began operating as a regional fiber broadband platform.
2021 to 2024: Bluepeak expanded across five states and added access to more than 140,000 new homes.
February 2024: Ares Management announced a strategic investment in Bluepeak.

oneSentenceDescription: Burnham RNG is a wastewater and agricultural waste to renewable natural gas (RNG) platform that transforms organic waste into RNG, clean water, and soil additives.
principalLocationOfOperations: Washington; multi-state United States project pipeline
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ares Management | Fund: Ares Infrastructure Opportunities | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Burnham RNG develops, owns, and operates projects that convert wastewater and agricultural waste into renewable natural gas and related byproducts. Its customers include utilities, gas buyers, and waste generators seeking renewable gas supply and organic waste management solutions. The business follows an asset-heavy model centered on anaerobic digestion, gas upgrading, and associated environmental credit generation. Public materials identify the Pasco Resource Recovery Center in Washington and a pipeline of additional biogas projects as part of the platform. Ares Management acquired Burnham RNG from Edge Natural Resources in December 2023, and detailed ownership percentages were not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2021: Burnham RNG was established as a renewable natural gas platform.
December 2023: Ares Management acquired Burnham RNG from Edge Natural Resources.
2023: Public disclosures identified the Pasco Resource Recovery Center and a broader project pipeline as part of the platform.

oneSentenceDescription: Current Trucking is a fleet electrification solutions company providing trucks-as-a-service for Class 3-8 electric vehicles including EV truck procurement, charging infrastructure, and O&M.
principalLocationOfOperations: New Jersey; multi-state United States operations
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ares Management | Fund: Ares Infrastructure Opportunities | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Current Trucking provides fleet electrification services for commercial vehicle operators through a trucks-as-a-service model. Its customers include logistics, distribution, and commercial fleet operators that need electric trucks, charging infrastructure, and operating support. The business combines vehicle procurement, charging deployment, and operations and maintenance, giving it a mixed asset and service profile with contracted customer relationships. Public company materials describe a focus on Class 3 through Class 8 electric fleets and a commitment of up to $250 million from Ares to fund growth. Ares Management acquired a controlling interest in February 2023, and the detailed percentage ownership was not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2021: Current Trucking was founded.
February 2023: Ares Management acquired a controlling interest in Current Trucking and committed up to $250 million of capital.
2023: Current continued to market its trucks-as-a-service model for Class 3 through Class 8 electric fleets.

oneSentenceDescription: DSD Renewables is a renewable energy platform deploying distributed solar, storage, and EV charging for commercial, industrial, and municipal partners.
profileSubsector: Distributed solar, storage, and EV charging
principalLocationOfOperations: New York; multi-state United States portfolio
ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: DSD Renewables develops, finances, builds, owns, and operates distributed solar, storage, and electric vehicle charging projects. Its customers include commercial, industrial, municipal, and nonprofit organizations seeking on-site and distributed clean energy solutions. The platform follows an asset-heavy operating model with long-term project ownership and financing layered alongside development and construction capabilities. Public disclosures state that DSD was created in 2019 from GE's distributed solar business and has continued to expand through financing and strategic capital raises. Ares Management provided a $200 million preferred equity investment in March 2022, Cox Enterprises made a $250 million strategic investment in November 2023, and the detailed current ownership structure has not been publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2019: DSD Renewables was formed from GE's distributed solar business with backing from BlackRock.
March 2022: DSD secured a $200 million preferred equity investment from Ares Management.
November 2023: DSD received a $250 million strategic investment from Cox Enterprises.
August 2025: DSD secured $238 million of long-term financing for a 233 MW solar portfolio across 12 states and the District of Columbia.

oneSentenceDescription: A portfolio of U.S.
profileSubsector: Operating wind, solar, and storage portfolio
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ares Management | Fund: Ares Infrastructure Opportunities | Ownership Interest: 49%
Firm: EDP Renováveis, S.A. | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 51%
companyOverview: This portfolio comprises operating United States wind, solar, and battery storage assets developed by EDPR. The assets serve wholesale power markets and contracted counterparties, and EDPR said the portfolio is supported by long-term power purchase agreements with an average remaining life of about 18 years. The operating model is asset-heavy and based on cash flows from a diversified group of utility-scale generation projects. EDPR stated in 2025 that the portfolio included ten assets totaling 1,632 MW across four United States power markets, including solar, wind, and storage capacity. EDPR closed the asset rotation in October 2025 by selling a 49 percent common equity stake to a fund managed by Ares Infrastructure Opportunities while retaining 51 percent and continuing to manage the assets.
historicalMilestones: October 2025: EDPR closed the sale of a 49 percent common equity stake in a 1,632 MW United States renewables portfolio to a fund managed by Ares.
October 2025: EDPR stated that the portfolio comprised ten operating assets across four United States power markets.
October 2025: EDPR retained 51 percent ownership and continued as manager of the portfolio.

oneSentenceDescription: A portfolio of U.S.
profileCompanyName: ENGIE United States renewables partnership portfolio
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ares Management | Fund: Ares Infrastructure Opportunities | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: ENGIE North America Inc. | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: This portfolio represents the operating wind and solar assets held in the United States renewables partnership between ENGIE and Ares. The assets sell power under long-term arrangements and into wholesale markets, with ENGIE continuing to operate and manage the facilities. The business is asset-heavy and structured around operating utility-scale renewable generation with long-duration cash flow characteristics. Public announcements in 2025 and 2026 described the addition of 730 MW of operating solar and wind assets in ERCOT to an existing partnership portfolio. ENGIE said it retained a controlling share in the assets while expanding the partnership with Ares, but the precise current ownership percentages were not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2020: ENGIE and Ares formed a United States renewables partnership.
October 2025: ENGIE announced an expansion of the partnership through the contribution of additional operating assets.
January 2026: ENGIE and Ares said the partnership had added 730 MW of operating solar and wind assets in ERCOT.
January 2026: ENGIE said it retained a controlling share in the expanded portfolio.

oneSentenceDescription: Meade Pipeline owns ~40% of the Central Penn Line, a FERC-regulated 180-mile natural gas pipeline transporting gas from the Marcellus/Utica Shale to demand centers via Williams' Transco system with ~2.3 Bcf/d gross capacity.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ares Management | Fund: Ares Infrastructure Opportunities | Ownership Interest: 100%
companyOverview: Meade Pipeline Co. LLC owns an approximately 40 percent interest in the Central Penn Line, a FERC-regulated interstate natural gas pipeline system. The asset moves gas from the Marcellus and Utica production regions to downstream demand centers through Williams' Transco system. Its operating model is asset-heavy and based on contracted transportation infrastructure rather than commodity production. Ares said the Central Penn Line is about 180 miles long and has gross capacity of about 2.3 Bcf per day. Ares Management announced the acquisition of Meade from XPLR Infrastructure in September 2025, giving Ares full ownership of the entity.
historicalMilestones: 2018: Meade Pipeline acquired its ownership interest in the Central Penn Line as part of the original infrastructure buildout.
September 2025: Ares Management announced the acquisition of Meade Pipeline from XPLR Infrastructure.
September 2025: Ares said the asset represented an approximately 40 percent interest in the 180-mile Central Penn Line.

oneSentenceDescription: Northampton Generating Company operates a power generation facility in Northampton County, Pennsylvania.
ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Northampton Generating Company operates a waste coal-fired generating facility in Northampton County, Pennsylvania. The plant converts anthracite mining refuse into electricity and also provides process steam to an adjacent recycled paper mill. Its model is asset-heavy and based on an operating thermal generation asset with site-specific fuel sourcing and industrial integration. Public sources describe the facility as approximately 134 MW and note that it commenced commercial operation in 1995. The facility has been associated with Ares through its power infrastructure strategy, but the detailed current ownership structure is not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 1995: Northampton Generating Plant commenced commercial operation.
1995: The facility was developed to convert anthracite mining refuse into energy and provide steam to an adjacent paper mill.
2014: Ares acquired EIF Management, which had managed power infrastructure investments associated with the asset.

oneSentenceDescription: Prime Data Centers is an international provider of hyperscale and wholesale data centers with a 4 GW power roadmap across top-tier U.S.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ares Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Macquarie Capital | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Data Realty Group | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Prime Data Centers develops and operates hyperscale and wholesale data center infrastructure. Its customers are cloud, hyperscale, and large enterprise users that require high-density capacity in major United States markets. The platform follows an asset-heavy development and ownership model built around secured power, land, and long-term data center operations. Public company materials describe a multi-gigawatt development roadmap across top-tier markets, with campuses in places such as Dallas and Northern Virginia. Prime announced a strategic partnership and equity investment from Macquarie Capital in 2021, and a 2025 European Commission filing identified Prime as jointly controlled by Ares, Macquarie, and Data Realty Group.
historicalMilestones: 2017: Prime Data Centers was founded.
June 2021: Prime announced a strategic partnership and equity investment from Macquarie Capital.
2025: A European Commission filing described Prime as jointly controlled by Ares, Macquarie, and Data Realty Group.

oneSentenceDescription: RENEW Energy Partners provides energy-efficiency-as-a-service solutions, funding and managing building retrofits including lighting, steam, building automation, and distributed power generation.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ares Management | Fund: Ares Infrastructure Opportunities | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: RENEW Energy Partners develops and finances energy efficiency and distributed energy projects for commercial and institutional buildings. Its customers include building owners and operators seeking to reduce energy costs through lighting, controls, steam, and on-site energy upgrades. The business uses an energy-as-a-service model in which it funds, installs, and manages retrofit projects rather than selling equipment only. Public materials describe RENEW as a national platform founded in 2013 and headquartered in Boston. RENEW announced a partnership with Ares in August 2020 to acquire and fund energy efficiency infrastructure projects, and detailed ownership percentages were not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2013: RENEW Energy Partners was founded.
August 2020: RENEW and Ares announced a partnership to provide energy efficiency infrastructure projects to customers.
2021 to 2025: RENEW continued to market the energy-as-a-service model for building retrofits and distributed energy projects.

oneSentenceDescription: SB Energy is a leading Climate Infrastructure and Technology Platform backed by SoftBank Group and Ares Management, developing, constructing, and operating some of the largest renewable projects in the U.S.
principalLocationOfOperations: California and Texas; multi-state United States portfolio
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ares Management | Fund: Ares Infrastructure Opportunities | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: SoftBank Group Corp. | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: SB Energy develops, constructs, and operates utility-scale solar and energy storage projects in the United States. Its customers include utilities, corporate offtakers, and grid markets that purchase renewable power, capacity, and related products from large operating projects. The platform is asset-heavy and combines late-stage development, project financing, construction execution, and long-term ownership of operating assets. Public disclosures describe SB Energy as a United States climate infrastructure platform founded in 2019 and backed by SoftBank. Ares led a strategic equity investment of up to $600 million in March 2022, and SB Energy subsequently raised large project financings while the detailed current ownership split remained undisclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2019: SB Energy was established as a United States renewables platform.
March 2022: Ares led a strategic equity investment of up to $600 million in SB Energy.
November 2023: SB Energy announced a $2.4 billion financing for four large solar projects.

oneSentenceDescription: Sagepoint Energy is a leading renewable waste-to-energy platform combining Dynamic Renewables, BC Organics, and National Organics.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ares Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: NorthWestern Energy affiliate | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Sagepoint Energy is a renewable waste-to-energy platform focused on converting organic waste streams into renewable natural gas and related products. Its customers include farms, food processors, municipalities, and energy counterparties that need waste management and low-carbon fuel solutions. The platform is asset-heavy and combines anaerobic digestion, landfill gas, and organics processing infrastructure with long-term operating capabilities. The company was launched in 2025 through the combination of Dynamic Renewables, BC Organics, and National Organics, and it later announced the acquisition of two landfill gas-to-electric facilities. Ares holds a majority position in Sagepoint, an unregulated affiliate of NorthWestern made a minority investment at launch, and the detailed percentages have not been publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: March 2025: Dynamic Renewables, BC Organics, and National Organics combined to form Sagepoint Energy.
March 2025: Ares was disclosed as the majority owner and an unregulated affiliate of NorthWestern made a minority investment.
March 2025: Sagepoint announced the acquisition of two landfill gas-to-electric facilities in Wisconsin and Indiana.

oneSentenceDescription: Spruance Operating Services manages power generation assets in Richmond, Virginia.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ares Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Spruance Operating Services LLC manages the Spruance gas-fired power asset in Richmond, Virginia. The facility serves regional electricity markets and related wholesale counterparties in the PJM area. It is an asset-heavy operating power plant, and public power plant databases describe the site as approximately 114.8 MW. Public sources identify the asset as part of the broader power infrastructure activities associated with Ares' energy investing platform, while detailed contracting terms are not publicly disclosed. Ares became associated with Spruance through its 2014 acquisition of EIF Management, and the detailed current ownership structure of the asset has not been publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2014: Ares acquired EIF Management, the infrastructure manager associated with Spruance in legacy portfolio materials.
January 2025: Virginia DEQ issued a Title V permit action for Spruance Operating Services LLC.
2025: Public power plant databases continued to identify Spruance as an operating gas-fired generation asset in Richmond.

oneSentenceDescription: Tango Holdings is a solar energy portfolio under Ares Management's Infrastructure Opportunities strategy, contributing to the firm's growing clean energy infrastructure platform across the United States.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ares Management | Fund: Ares Infrastructure Opportunities | Ownership Interest: 80%
Firm: Savion, LLC | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 20%
companyOverview: Tango Holdings LLC is the holding vehicle for a portfolio of operating and near-complete utility-scale solar projects in the United States. The assets sell electricity into regional power markets and under offtake arrangements, and Savion continues to manage the projects while Shell Renewable Asset Management oversees asset management. Its model is asset-heavy and based on ownership of operating renewable generation assets rather than solely development-stage projects. At launch, the joint venture included 496 MW across projects in Ohio, Kentucky, Oklahoma, and Indiana. Ares established the joint venture with Savion in July 2025 with an 80 percent and 20 percent ownership split, respectively.
historicalMilestones: July 2025: Ares and Savion established Tango Holdings LLC as a joint venture for United States solar investments.
July 2025: The initial portfolio included 496 MW across projects in Ohio, Kentucky, Oklahoma, and Indiana.
July 2025: Ares took an 80 percent stake and Savion retained 20 percent.

oneSentenceDescription: Underline Infrastructure is a community fiber platform that designs, finances, constructs, and operates open access fiber-optic networks.
ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Underline Infrastructure designs, finances, builds, and operates open access fiber networks. Its customers are internet service providers and end users in residential and commercial communities that need broadband access over wholesale and retail-enabled fiber systems. The business is asset-heavy because it owns and operates last-mile fiber infrastructure while using an open access model to support multiple service providers. Public materials cite initial markets including Colorado Springs and Fountain in Colorado and Salinas in California. Ares Management announced a strategic investment in September 2022 as its first digital infrastructure investment in North America, and the detailed current ownership structure has not been publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2021: Underline Infrastructure was founded.
September 2022: Ares Management announced a strategic investment in Underline.
2022: Underline identified Colorado Springs, Fountain, and Salinas among its initial markets.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Argo Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Argo managed funds | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Astro Solar is a portfolio of operating commercial and industrial distributed solar assets in the United States. The assets serve commercial hosts and offtakers through long-term on-site solar arrangements rather than utility-scale generation sales. Its model is asset-heavy and based on ownership of distributed operating projects with contracted cash flows. Public transaction materials described the portfolio sold to Argo in 2021 as 129 MW of operating commercial and industrial solar projects across seven states. Argo acquired the portfolio from CarVal Investors and CleanCapital in September 2021, and detailed current fund-level ownership information has not been publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: September 2021: Argo acquired a 129 MW commercial and industrial solar portfolio from CarVal Investors and CleanCapital.
2021: Public materials stated that the operating portfolio spanned seven states.
2021: The acquired assets were identified as distributed solar projects serving commercial and industrial sites.

ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: This asset is the long-term water and wastewater concession serving Bayonne, New Jersey. The concession serves municipal water and wastewater customers and is responsible for operating, maintaining, and upgrading the local system. Its model is asset-heavy and concession-based, with regulated-style customer revenues supporting operations and committed capital investment over the contract term. Public case studies describe a 40-year concession that delivered an upfront payment to the city and required substantial long-term system investment. The concession was originally awarded in 2012 to a KKR and United Water consortium, Argo acquired KKR's interests in 2017, and the detailed current ownership structure has not been publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2012: Bayonne approved the long-term water and wastewater concession.
2012: KKR and United Water agreed to a 40-year concession and upfront payment to the city.
2017: Argo acquired KKR's interests in the Bayonne concession.
January 2025: Global Water Intelligence reported that the concession was part of Argo's agreed sale to Apollo.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Argo Infrastructure Partners | Fund: AIA Energy North America LLC | Ownership Interest: 49.9%
Firm: Black Hills Corp. | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 50.1%
companyOverview: Black Hills Colorado IPP owns the independent power plant portion of the co-located Pueblo Airport generating facilities in Colorado. The plant serves Black Hills' regulated utility under a long-term tolling arrangement and provides dispatchable gas-fired generation in southern Colorado. It is an asset-heavy operating power plant, and public filings identify the asset as a 200 MW combined-cycle facility that commenced operation in 2012. Black Hills has stated that the project remains contracted to provide capacity and energy through 2031. AIA Energy North America, a platform managed by Argo Infrastructure Partners, acquired a 49.9 percent interest in 2016 while Black Hills retained the majority ownership and operating role.
historicalMilestones: January 2012: The 200 MW Black Hills Colorado IPP facility commenced commercial operation.
February 2016: Black Hills announced the sale of a 49.9 percent member equity interest in the plant to AIA Energy North America.
December 2031: Black Hills has disclosed that the tolling agreement for the plant runs through the end of 2031.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Argo Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Argo managed funds | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Carville Energy Center is an operating combined-cycle natural gas power plant in St. Gabriel, Louisiana. The facility serves wholesale electricity markets and power counterparties in the Gulf region. Its model is asset-heavy and based on ownership of a large thermal generation plant, while detailed current commercial arrangements are not publicly disclosed. Public transaction materials described the asset sold to Argo in 2019 as a 516 MW plant, while more recent power databases report a somewhat higher nameplate figure. LS Power agreed to sell Carville to an Argo-managed investment vehicle in August 2019, and Argo subsequently took ownership of the asset.
historicalMilestones: August 2019: LS Power announced the sale of Carville Energy Center to an Argo-managed investment vehicle.
2019: Public transaction materials described Carville as a 516 MW natural gas-fired combined-cycle facility.
2024 to 2026: Power plant databases continued to list Carville as an operating gas-fired generation asset in St. Gabriel, Louisiana.

profileSubsector: Regulated natural gas and electric distribution
ownershipDetails: Firm: Argo Infrastructure Partners | Fund: ACP Crotona Corp. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Corning Natural Gas Holding Corp. owns regulated gas and electric utility operations serving communities in New York and Pennsylvania. Its customer base consists of residential, commercial, and industrial utility users served through Corning Natural Gas, Leatherstocking Gas, and Pike County Light and Power. The business is asset-heavy and regulated, with earnings supported by distribution networks, utility tariffs, and customer service obligations. Public company history states that the system includes about 425 miles of gas mains and serves roughly 15,000 customers through Corning Natural Gas alone. The utility traces its origins to 1904, Corning acquired Pike in 2016, and Argo acquired the holding company through ACP Crotona in July 2022.
historicalMilestones: 1904: The predecessor company began as Crystal City Gas Company.
2010: Corning formed Leatherstocking Gas in partnership with Mirabito.
August 2016: Corning acquired Pike County Light and Power from Orange and Rockland.
July 2022: Argo acquired Corning Natural Gas Holding Corp. through ACP Crotona.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Argo Infrastructure Partners | Fund: AIA Energy North America LLC | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Cross-Sound Cable owns and operates a high-voltage direct current transmission link between Connecticut and Long Island. The asset provides interregional transfer capability between New England and New York and serves utilities, power marketers, and grid users that benefit from transmission capacity. Its operating model is asset-heavy and based on regulated or contracted transmission infrastructure rather than merchant generation. Public company materials describe the project as a 330 MW, 24-mile submarine cable under Long Island Sound. Cross-Sound entered service in 2002, Argo acquired the asset in 2015 through AIA Energy North America, and the company announced a system redevelopment process in March 2026.
historicalMilestones: 2002: Cross-Sound Cable entered service as a 330 MW transmission interconnector between Connecticut and Long Island.
2015: Argo acquired Cross-Sound Cable through AIA Energy North America.
March 2026: Cross-Sound Cable announced a redevelopment process to upgrade the system.

ownershipDetails: Firm: GIC | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 44.4%
Firm: Manulife Investment Management / PGGM Infrastructure Fund | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 30.4%
Firm: APG Americas / CalSTRS / Argo Infrastructure Partners | Fund: AIA Montana LLC | Ownership Interest: 25.2%
companyOverview: Duquesne Light Company is the regulated electric transmission and distribution utility serving the Pittsburgh area. Its customers are residential, commercial, and industrial users across southwestern Pennsylvania that rely on the utility for electricity delivery rather than competitive generation supply. The business is asset-heavy and regulated, with earnings supported by a large distribution and transmission network and approved utility tariffs. Public disclosures state that the utility serves more than 600,000 customers and operates thousands of miles of electric infrastructure. Current ownership is publicly disclosed on the company's investor page, with GIC holding 44.4 percent, Manulife and PGGM holding 30.4 percent together, and APG Americas, CalSTRS, and Argo holding 25.2 percent through AIA Montana.
historicalMilestones: 1912: Duquesne Light Company was incorporated.
2011: GIC became an investor in DQE Holdings.
2016: Manulife Investment Management and PGGM acquired a combined 30.4 percent stake.
2017: APG Americas, CalSTRS, and Argo acquired a combined 25.2 percent stake through AIA Montana.

ownershipDetails: Firm: LAZ Parking | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: FleetLogix provides fleet management, staffing, transportation, and related operational services for mobility and transportation customers. Its customers include rental car operators, fleet owners, and transportation providers that outsource labor-intensive fleet handling and support activities. The business is primarily service-based, although it is embedded in transportation operations that require recurring, site-based staffing and process management. Public company materials describe a nationwide operating footprint and identify the corporate office in San Diego, California. LAZ Parking announced in April 2025 that it had acquired a majority interest in FleetLogix, and the detailed post-transaction ownership percentages were not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2025: FleetLogix continued to market nationwide fleet management and staffing services.
April 2025: LAZ Parking announced the acquisition of a majority interest in FleetLogix.
2025: FleetLogix remained headquartered in San Diego, California.

profileSubsector: Truck parking and industrial outdoor storage
ownershipDetails: Firm: LAZ Parking | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Freight Ninja develops, leases, and operates truck parking and industrial outdoor storage locations for freight and logistics users. Its customers include trucking fleets, owner-operators, brokers, and industrial users that need secure parking and trailer or equipment storage. The business is asset-heavy because it relies on leased and managed sites that generate recurring parking and storage revenue. Public company materials describe more than 65 facilities nationwide, including over 300 acres and about 10,000 parking spaces across the portfolio. LAZ Parking announced in May 2025 that it had acquired a majority interest in Freight Ninja, and the detailed ownership split was not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2025: Freight Ninja publicly described a nationwide network of more than 65 truck parking facilities.
May 2025: LAZ Parking announced the acquisition of a majority interest in Freight Ninja.
2025: Public company materials cited over 300 acres and about 10,000 parking spaces across the platform.

profileSubsector: Regulated gas utility and propane distribution
ownershipDetails: Firm: Argo Infrastructure Partners | Fund: AMF Hawaiʻi Investment Holdings | Ownership Interest: 100%
companyOverview: Hawaiʻi Gas is the only government-franchised full-service gas utility in the State of Hawaii and also operates a large propane distribution business. Its customers include residential, commercial, industrial, and hospitality users across the islands that rely on synthetic natural gas, propane, and related energy services. The company is asset-heavy and regulated in its utility operations, with additional logistics and storage infrastructure supporting propane distribution. Public company materials state that Hawaiʻi Gas serves nearly 34,000 non-utility propane customers in addition to its regulated utility customer base. Argo announced the acquisition in 2021, completed the purchase in July 2022 after regulatory approval, and later disclosed that Hawaiʻi Gas is wholly owned by funds managed by Argo.
historicalMilestones: 1904: The predecessor Honolulu Gas Company was established.
June 2021: Hawaiʻi Gas announced that it would be acquired by Argo Infrastructure Partners.
July 2022: The acquisition of Hawaiʻi Gas by Argo-managed funds closed following regulatory approval.
June 2025: Hawaiʻi Gas said both Hawaiʻi Gas and Isle Gas were wholly owned by funds managed by Argo.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Argo Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Argo managed funds | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Hudson Transmission Project is a high-voltage direct current transmission link connecting New Jersey and New York City. The asset provides transmission capacity between the PJM and New York markets and serves grid users that benefit from interregional power transfers. Its operating model is asset-heavy and based on transmission infrastructure rather than generation ownership. Public company materials describe the project as a 660 MW underground and underwater transmission system that began construction in 2011 and was completed in 2013. Argo acquired the asset from the Starwood Energy and Ares-led owners in 2018, and detailed current fund-level ownership percentages have not been publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: May 2011: Construction began on the Hudson Transmission Project.
June 2013: The 660 MW transmission line was completed.
September 2018: Starwood Energy and Ares agreed to sell Hudson Transmission to Argo.

principalLocationOfOperations: Ontario, Quebec, Alberta, and British Columbia
ownershipDetails: Firm: LAZ Parking | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 60%
Firm: Indigo Group | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 40%
companyOverview: Indigo Park Canada provides parking management, concession, and mobility services across Canada. Its customers include municipalities, healthcare systems, universities, airports, and commercial property owners that outsource parking operations and concession management. The operating model is service-oriented but also includes long-term lease and concession rights that create infrastructure-like cash flows. Public company materials describe a national platform operating across major Canadian provinces under the Indigo brand. LAZ Parking acquired a 60 percent majority stake from Indigo Group in July 2025, while Indigo retained a 40 percent interest.
historicalMilestones: July 2025: LAZ Parking acquired a 60 percent majority stake in Indigo Park Canada from Indigo Group.
July 2025: Indigo Group retained a 40 percent ownership interest in the Canadian business.
March 2026: Indigo Group's annual results confirmed the June 2025 disposal of the 60 percent stake.

ownershipDetails: Firm: LAZ Parking | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: International Parking Management provides parking management services in the Seattle market. Its customers include commercial and hospitality properties, event venues, and off-airport parking users that require outsourced parking operations and local market expertise. The business is primarily service-based and cash-flow driven, with site-level operating contracts rather than ownership of large physical infrastructure portfolios. LAZ said at acquisition that the company had approximately 74 employees and managed about $40 million of annual parking revenues. IPM traces its origins to 1985, the business was purchased by Mark and Tracy Sundberg in 2006, and LAZ acquired the company in April 2024.
historicalMilestones: 1985: The business that became IPM was founded as Premier Parking.
2006: Mark and Tracy Sundberg purchased the company.
April 2024: LAZ Parking acquired International Parking Management.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Argo Infrastructure Partners | Fund: AMF Hawaiʻi Investment Holdings | Ownership Interest: 100%
companyOverview: Isle Gas is the holding company for propane gas storage and supply assets in Hawaii acquired from AmeriGas. The business supports retail and wholesale propane customers in Hawaii through storage, supply, and logistics infrastructure. Its model is asset-heavy and based on energy logistics and storage rather than regulated utility rate base. Public company materials state that the business is serviced through Hawaiʻi Gas and sits alongside that utility within Argo's Hawaii energy platform. UGI announced the sale in June 2025, Hawaiʻi Gas later said Isle Gas is wholly owned by funds managed by Argo, and detailed intermediate holding structures were not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: June 2025: UGI announced an agreement to sell its Hawaii propane storage and supply assets to Isle Gas.
June 2025: Hawaiʻi Gas said the acquired assets would be held through Isle Gas.
June 2025: Hawaiʻi Gas disclosed that Isle Gas is wholly owned by funds managed by Argo.

principalLocationOfOperations: Connecticut; nationwide United States and Canada operations
ownershipDetails: Firm: Argo Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Argo managed funds | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: LAZ Parking provides parking management, transportation, and mobility services across the United States and Canada. Its customers include airports, municipalities, healthcare systems, universities, hospitality properties, and commercial real estate owners that outsource parking and ground transportation operations. The business is primarily service-based but also has exposure to long-term concessions, leases, and mobility platforms that create recurring infrastructure-like cash flows. Public company materials describe LAZ as a large parking operator in North America and note a series of add-on acquisitions in 2024 and 2025. LAZ was founded in 1981, Argo announced a long-term investment in the company at the start of 2022, and LAZ has since expanded through acquisitions including IPM, FleetLogix, Freight Ninja, and Indigo Park Canada.
historicalMilestones: 1981: LAZ Parking was founded in Hartford, Connecticut.
January 2022: LAZ announced a long-term investment from Argo Infrastructure Partners.
April 2024: LAZ acquired International Parking Management.
April 2025: LAZ acquired a majority interest in FleetLogix.
May 2025: LAZ acquired a majority interest in Freight Ninja.
July 2025: LAZ acquired a 60 percent majority stake in Indigo Park Canada.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Argo Infrastructure Partners | Fund: ACP Crotona Corp. (via Corning Natural Gas Holding Corp.) | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Leatherstocking Gas Company is a regulated natural gas distribution utility operating in Pennsylvania. Its customers are local residential and commercial gas users served through a franchised distribution network. The business is asset-heavy and regulated, with earnings tied to utility infrastructure and approved tariffs rather than commodity production. Public company materials state that the utility serves about 500 customers through roughly 21 miles of gas pipeline. Leatherstocking was formed in 2010 through a partnership involving Corning, Corning acquired the remaining Pennsylvania interest in 2020, and Argo became the indirect owner when it acquired Corning in 2022.
historicalMilestones: 2010: Corning partnered with Mirabito to form Leatherstocking Gas.
January 2020: Corning acquired the remaining 50 percent interest in Leatherstocking Gas Company of Pennsylvania.
July 2022: Argo acquired Corning Natural Gas Holding Corp., becoming the indirect owner of Leatherstocking.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Argo Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: SUEZ Water Environmental Services Inc. | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Middletown Water Joint Venture LLC is the concessionaire for the water and wastewater system serving Middletown, Pennsylvania. The concession serves municipal utility customers and is responsible for operating, maintaining, and upgrading the local water and sewer assets. Its model is asset-heavy and concession-based, with customer revenues supporting operations and capital investment under a long-term agreement. Public project materials describe six groundwater sources, 36 miles of water main, one wastewater treatment plant, and 25 miles of sewer main under the concession. The concession agreement was executed in 2014, court filings later identified the joint venture as comprising SUEZ and Argo, and the detailed current ownership percentages have not been publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: September 2014: Middletown executed the concession and lease agreement with Middletown Water Joint Venture LLC.
2017: Argo acquired KKR's interest in the Middletown concession.
2018: Court filings identified the joint venture as comprising SUEZ Water Environmental Services and Argo Infrastructure Partners.
January 2025: Global Water Intelligence reported that the concession was part of Argo's agreed sale to Apollo.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Argo Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Argo managed funds | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Mobile Energy LLC owns the Hog Bayou gas-fired power plant in Mobile, Alabama. The facility supplies power to wholesale counterparties, and public local business materials state that it furnishes electric power to Calpine Energy Services as needed. Its operating model is asset-heavy and based on ownership of a dispatchable thermal generation asset. Power plant databases identify Hog Bayou as an operating natural gas facility with about 280 MW of capacity and an initial operation date in July 2001. The asset has been associated with Argo's power generation portfolio, but detailed current ownership percentages and contracting terms have not been publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: July 2001: Hog Bayou began operations according to public power plant databases.
2019 to 2025: Argo portfolio materials associated Mobile Energy with its operating power generation assets.
2025 to 2026: Public databases continued to identify the plant as an operating 280 MW gas-fired facility in Mobile, Alabama.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Argo Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Argo managed funds | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Oneta Power owns and operates the Oneta Energy Center in Broken Arrow, Oklahoma. The facility serves wholesale electricity markets and industrial and utility power users through a large operating gas-fired generation asset. Its model is asset-heavy and based on ownership of a combined-cycle plant with thermal generation infrastructure on site. Public company materials describe the plant as approximately 1,150 MW, while LS Power's sale announcement in 2019 described the asset as 1,127 MW. LS Power agreed to sell Oneta to an Argo-managed investment vehicle in August 2019, and Argo subsequently added the asset to its power generation portfolio.
historicalMilestones: August 2019: LS Power announced the sale of Oneta Energy Center to an Argo-managed investment vehicle.
2021: Oneta Power began using a public website identifying the facility in Broken Arrow, Oklahoma.
2025 to 2026: Public sources continued to identify Oneta as an operating gas-fired plant with capacity above 1,100 MW.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Argo Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Argo managed funds | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Pearl / Ruby Solar is an operating distributed solar portfolio in the United States. The assets serve commercial and institutional hosts through long-term power purchase agreements tied to on-site or distributed generation projects. Its model is asset-heavy and based on ownership of operating commercial and industrial solar installations with contracted revenue streams. Transaction materials from 2019 described the portfolio as 114 MW across 134 sites in 12 states. Argo announced its investment in the portfolio in February 2019, and detailed current fund-level ownership information has not been publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: February 2019: Argo invested in a 114 MW distributed solar portfolio later referenced as Pearl / Ruby Solar.
2019: Transaction materials described 134 operating sites across 12 states.
2021: SEC transaction materials continued to identify the Pearl / Ruby Solar portfolio within Argo's holdings.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Argo Infrastructure Partners | Fund: ACP Crotona Corp. (via Corning Natural Gas Holding Corp.) | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Pike County Light and Power Company is a regulated utility serving electric and gas customers in northeastern Pennsylvania. Its customers are residential and commercial users served through local distribution infrastructure. The business is asset-heavy and regulated, with revenue tied to electric and gas delivery service under approved utility tariffs. Public company materials state that PCLP serves about 5,200 electric customers and about 1,400 gas customers through more than 100 miles of overhead electric lines and about 20 miles of gas pipeline. The utility was incorporated in 1910, Corning acquired it from Orange and Rockland in 2016, and Argo became the indirect owner when it acquired Corning in 2022.
historicalMilestones: May 1910: Pike County Light and Power Company was incorporated.
August 2016: Corning acquired Pike County Light and Power from Orange and Rockland.
