Firm: Brookfield Renewable | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: This portfolio comprises the Cheoah, Calderwood, Chilhowee, and Santeetlah hydroelectric facilities in the Smoky Mountain region. The assets sell renewable electricity under long-term arrangements, including a power purchase agreement with TVA. Its model is asset-heavy and based on ownership of operating hydroelectric generation facilities with long useful lives. Public financing materials in 2025 described the assets as being held in Smoky Mountain Holdings LLC, a joint venture between Argo and Brookfield. The portfolio was refinanced in August 2025 through a $435 million secured notes transaction, while detailed ownership percentages were not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: November 2024: TVA signed a 10-year power purchase agreement with the Smoky Mountain hydro facilities owned by Argo and Brookfield.
August 2025: Smoky Mountain Holdings LLC closed a $435 million secured notes financing.
2025: Financing materials identified the portfolio as a joint venture between Argo and Brookfield.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Argo Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Argo managed funds | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Thule Energy Storage owns and develops distributed thermal energy storage assets commercialized under the Ice Bear brand. Its customers are utilities and commercial building owners that use behind-the-meter storage to reduce peak cooling load and shift electricity demand. The business is asset-heavy and based on deployment and ownership of distributed storage hardware rather than a pure software model. Public materials show that Argo provided long-term funding to Ice Energy in 2018 and that ACP Thule Investments later supported development of the platform's assets. The business emerged from the restructuring of Ice Energy's operations, and the detailed current ownership percentages have not been publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: June 2018: Ice Energy announced a long-term funding agreement with Argo Infrastructure Partners.
2019: Ice Energy filed for Chapter 7 bankruptcy and Thule Energy Storage acquired customer assets and services.
2020: CPUC filings identified ACP Thule Investments LLC, managed by Argo, as the parent of the operating platform.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Argo Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Argo managed funds | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: TierPoint provides colocation, cloud, connectivity, and managed information technology services from a national data center platform. Its customers are enterprises, channel partners, and public-sector users that need outsourced data center capacity and hybrid infrastructure services. The business is asset-heavy because it owns and operates data center facilities while also selling recurring managed services. Public company materials state that TierPoint operates more than 40 data centers in 20 United States markets. Argo made a $500 million investment in February 2022, increased its ownership and capital commitment in December 2025, and has said total capital formation under its ownership now exceeds $3 billion.
historicalMilestones: 2010: TierPoint was formed.
February 2022: TierPoint closed a $500 million investment from Argo Infrastructure Partners.
December 2025: Argo increased its ownership and capital commitment to TierPoint.
December 2025: Argo said total capital formation under its ownership of TierPoint exceeded $3 billion.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Argo Infrastructure Partners | Fund: AMF Hawaiʻi Investment Holdings | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Waihonu Solar Farm is an operating solar generation asset on Oahu. The project supports Hawaii's clean energy supply and forms part of the broader energy platform associated with Hawaiʻi Gas. Its model is asset-heavy and based on ownership of an operating solar plant rather than development-stage activity. Hawaiʻi Gas describes Waihonu as one of Oahu's larger active solar farms with a combined output of 6.5 MW from 26,232 solar modules on about 12 acres in Mililani. The project was dedicated in 2016, and Argo acquired it indirectly as part of the 2022 purchase of Hawaiʻi Gas and related smaller energy businesses.
historicalMilestones: August 2016: Waihonu Solar and Sheep Farm was dedicated in Mililani.
June 2021: Public reporting on Argo's acquisition of Hawaiʻi Gas stated that the transaction also included Waihonu Solar Farm.
July 2022: Argo completed the acquisition of Hawaiʻi Gas and related smaller energy businesses, including Waihonu.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Astatine Investment Partners | Fund: Astatine Investment Partners (Funds) | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: BTR rents refuse trucks and related fleet equipment used by municipal and private waste collection operators across North America. Its customer base includes municipalities, large waste companies, and independent haulers that need replacement vehicles, seasonal fleet capacity, or outsourced fleet-management support. The business is asset-heavy because it owns and manages the underlying truck fleet, and its revenues are generated through rental, fleet-program, and related service arrangements rather than commodity exposure. Its operating footprint spans the United States and Canada, and company materials describe the platform as serving essential-services customers across North America. BTR was founded in 2002 and was recapitalized by Astatine Investment Partners in 2021, with the current ownership split not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2002: Big Truck Rental was founded to provide refuse-truck rental services in North America.
2018: Trivest Growth Investment Fund I made an investment in Big Truck Rental.
2021-10-18: Astatine Investment Partners recapitalized Big Truck Rental.
2025-04-25: Big Truck Rental announced a rebrand to BTR.
2025-04-28: BTR announced the appointment of Zach Martin as chief executive officer.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Astatine Investment Partners | Fund: Astatine Investment Partners (Funds) | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Everfast Fiber Networks provides fiber-based internet, voice, and video services in the Kansas City metropolitan area. Its customer base includes residential households, small and mid-sized businesses, and carrier or enterprise users that require local network access in the market. The operating model is network-based and asset-heavy because the company owns and operates a single-market fiber platform rather than functioning purely as a reseller, and its revenues are tied to subscription and connectivity services. Public materials identify the business as headquartered in Lenexa, Kansas, focused on Kansas City, and serving neighborhoods on both the Kansas and Missouri sides of the metro area; the company states it has served the market since 1999. Astatine Investment Partners acquired the Kansas City assets of Consolidated Communications in 2022 to establish Everfast as an independent platform, and the current ownership split is not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 1999: The operating business that now trades as Everfast began serving the Kansas City market.
2022-12: Astatine Investment Partners acquired the Kansas City assets of Consolidated Communications and established Everfast Fiber Networks as a standalone platform.
2024-07-12: Everfast Fiber Networks announced the appointment of Scott Randall as chief executive officer.
2025: Everfast continued expanding fiber-internet, business-connectivity, and managed Wi-Fi offerings in the Kansas City metro area.

Firm: Energy Transfer LP | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 51%
companyOverview: Maurepas Pipeline is a natural-gas transportation asset in southern Louisiana. The pipeline serves an upstream and industrial end market through contracted transportation service, including long-term arrangements with a Shell affiliate disclosed at the time of Astatine's investment. Its operating model is asset-heavy and contracted because the asset generates infrastructure cash flows from pipeline transportation rather than merchant commodity trading. The system operates in Louisiana, where it forms part of the regional gas-transport network serving Gulf Coast demand centers. Astatine, then operating under the Alinda name, acquired a 49% interest in 2018, and the remaining interest is held by Energy Transfer.
historicalMilestones: 2018-08-20: Alinda Capital Partners, now Astatine Investment Partners, announced the acquisition of a 49% interest in Maurepas Pipeline from SemGroup for $350 million.
2018: Following the transaction, Maurepas became a joint venture between Energy Transfer and Astatine.
2018: Public transaction materials described the pipeline as supported by long-term contracted transportation arrangements with a Shell affiliate.
2022: Alinda Capital Partners rebranded as Astatine Investment Partners.

oneSentenceDescription: McKeil Marine is one of Canada's largest marine transportation and towing companies, operating a diversified fleet of tugs, barges, cargo vessels, and specialized marine equipment across the Great Lakes, St.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Astatine Investment Partners | Fund: Astatine Investment Partners (Funds) | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: McKeil Marine provides marine transportation and project logistics services on the Great Lakes, St. Lawrence Seaway, East Coast, and Canadian Arctic. Its customer base includes industrial shippers moving dry bulk, liquid bulk, project cargo, and other specialized freight that requires inland and coastal marine capacity. The business is asset-heavy because it operates and manages owned or controlled vessels, tugs, and barges rather than functioning as a pure broker, and its revenues are driven by freight and marine-service contracts. Astatine describes the company as operating 13 dry- and liquid-bulk vessels and the largest in-class fleet of tugs and barges on the Great Lakes, with principal operations in Ontario and broader service coverage across eastern Canada and adjacent U.S. routes. McKeil was founded in 1956, and Astatine Investment Partners agreed to acquire the company in 2023; the current ownership split is not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 1956: McKeil Marine was founded in Ontario.
2023-11-03: Astatine Investment Partners announced an agreement to acquire McKeil Marine.
2023: At announcement, Astatine described McKeil as operating 13 dry- and liquid-bulk vessels and the largest in-class fleet of tugs and barges on the Great Lakes.
2024: McKeil continued operating marine transportation and project logistics services across the Great Lakes, St. Lawrence, East Coast, and Arctic corridors.

oneSentenceDescription: PECO Pallet is the largest provider of pallet rental services (pallet pooling) in North America, operating a fleet of approximately 340 million wood block pallets used by consumer packaged goods manufacturers, retailers, and food & beverage companies throughout the supply chain.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Astatine Investment Partners | Fund: Astatine Investment Partners (Funds) | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Universities Superannuation Scheme | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: PECO Pallet operates a pooled wood-block pallet platform serving food, beverage, grocery, and consumer-products supply chains across North America. Its customers are manufacturers, distributors, and retailers that use pooled pallets to move goods through distribution networks and recover, sort, and reuse those assets after each trip. The business is asset-heavy because it owns the pallet pool and depot network economics are tied to recurring rental turns, recovery, and repair activity rather than one-time pallet sales alone. Company materials describe a North American footprint spanning the United States, Canada, and Mexico, with more than 1,200 manufacturing, recovery, sort, and depot locations and roughly 24 million pallets in circulation. PECO traces its operating history to 1997, and Astatine's predecessor Alinda agreed with USS to acquire the company in 2020; the current ownership split is not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 1997: PECO Pallet began operations in the rental-pallet market.
2020-10-05: Alinda Capital Partners and USS announced an agreement to acquire PECO Pallet.
2020-10-09: The acquisition of PECO Pallet by Alinda and USS closed.
2022: PECO marked its 25th year in business and reported a pool of roughly 22 million pallets.
2025: Company materials described more than 90 depots and approximately 24 million pallets in circulation across North America.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Astatine Investment Partners | Fund: Astatine Investment Partners (Funds) | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Twin Parking Holdings owns parking assets in the Minneapolis central business district. Its end market is urban mobility and commercial real estate, serving commuters, visitors, and businesses that rely on structured parking in the downtown core. The operating model is asset-heavy because revenues are generated from owned garages and related parking operations rather than from an advisory or management-only model. Astatine identifies the platform as consisting of three parking garages in Minneapolis, Minnesota. Astatine Investment Partners is the disclosed sponsor of the business, but the acquisition date and the current ownership split are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: Not publicly disclosed.

ownershipDetails: Firm: IFM Investors-affiliated funds | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Buckeye Partners owns and operates liquid-energy midstream assets, including pipeline, terminal, and marine logistics infrastructure. Its customer base includes refiners, marketers, traders, and other energy counterparties that use the system for storage, transportation, blending, and related logistics services. The operating model is asset-heavy and largely infrastructure-based because cash flows are tied to pipeline transportation, terminal throughput, storage, and marine-services activity rather than direct commodity-price exposure alone. Public company materials describe more than 5,000 miles of domestic pipeline, more than 130 liquid-petroleum terminals, and approximately 125 million barrels of tank capacity across key U.S. and international markets. Buckeye was founded in 1886 and was acquired by entities affiliated with IFM Investors in 2019; the current ownership split is not publicly disclosed.

2019-05-11: Buckeye agreed to be acquired by entities affiliated with IFM Investors.

oneSentenceDescription: The Chesapeake Expressway (also known as the Chesapeake Bay Bridge-Tunnel) and related toll road concessions in the Hampton Roads region of Virginia represent critical transportation infrastructure linking the Norfolk-Virginia Beach metropolitan area.

companyOverview: Chesapeake is the ownership vehicle for Transurban's Greater Washington managed-lane toll-road assets and related development projects. The network serves passenger and commercial traffic in the Washington metropolitan area through the 495, 95, and 395 Express Lanes and associated expansions. Its operating model combines operational toll-road cash flows with development activity, and revenues are generated by dynamically priced managed lanes under long-dated concession arrangements. AustralianSuper describes the asset as comprising three operational toll roads and two developments in the Greater Washington, D.C. area, while Transurban's 2020 partnership materials covered the 495, 95, and 395 Express Lanes portfolio. AustralianSuper acquired its 25% stake in 2020 alongside CPP Investments and UniSuper, with Transurban retaining 50% and continuing to operate the network.

ownershipDetails: Firm: AustralianSuper | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: DigitalBridge | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: DataBank provides colocation, interconnection, and managed infrastructure services for enterprise, cloud, and artificial-intelligence workloads. Its customers include enterprises, hyperscale users, and network counterparties that require data-center capacity, connectivity, and related managed services across multiple U.S. markets. The operating model is asset-heavy because the business owns and operates data-center facilities and critical power and cooling infrastructure, with revenues generated through long-term capacity, colocation, and service contracts. AustralianSuper describes the platform as one of the larger North American data-center operators, and public disclosures in 2024 referenced more than 65 data centers across 27 key markets and a build-out plan exceeding 850 MW of additional capacity. DataBank was founded in 2005, DigitalBridge has been a long-term sponsor since 2016, and AustralianSuper became a significant minority owner through the 2024 equity raise.
historicalMilestones: 2005: DataBank was founded in Dallas, Texas.
2016: DigitalBridge, then Colony Capital, acquired DataBank and began expanding the platform nationally.
2018: DataBank acquired C7 Data Centers.
2019: DataBank acquired zColo, materially expanding its market footprint.
2024-10-15: DataBank announced an approximately $2.0 billion equity raise led by AustralianSuper, including a $1.5 billion commitment from the fund.

ownershipVehicle: Mid Risk Portfolio (Real Assets / Direct Investment)
oneSentenceDescription: Generate Capital is a sustainable infrastructure investment and operating platform that builds, owns, operates, and finances distributed and clean energy infrastructure assets across the United States.
principalLocationOfOperations: California; New York; nationwide United States
ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Generate Capital is an investment and operating platform focused on distributed and sustainable infrastructure assets. Its end markets include solar, storage, electric mobility, water, waste, and other infrastructure categories that serve commercial, industrial, municipal, and community customers. The model is capital-intensive because Generate finances, owns, and operates physical infrastructure assets and portfolios, with cash flows derived from long-term contracted project revenues and operating performance. Public company materials describe a nationwide U.S. footprint and a portfolio that has grown to more than 2,000 operating assets with deployed capital above $8 billion. Generate was founded in 2014, and AustralianSuper participated in the company's 2021 capital raise, but the current ownership structure and percentages are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2014: Generate Capital was founded.
2017: Generate raised $200 million of equity capital to support platform expansion.
2020: Generate acquired Clean Energy Collective, adding community-solar capability.
2021: Generate announced a $2 billion capital raise backed by AustralianSuper, QIC, and other institutional investors.
2023: Public company materials indicated the platform had grown to more than 2,000 operating assets and more than $8 billion of deployed capital.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Axium Infrastructure | Fund: Axium Managed Funds | Ownership Interest: 49%
Firm: Constellation Renewables Partners, LLC | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 51%
companyOverview: 527 Renewables Holdings holds a diversified U.S. portfolio of wind and solar generation assets. The portfolio sells electricity under long-term power purchase arrangements to utility and corporate counterparties, with Axium identifying Exelon Generation as the principal counterparty at the time of its disclosure. Its operating model is asset-heavy and largely contracted because revenues are generated by physical renewable assets under long-term offtake agreements, with additional value tied to recontracting and repowering opportunities. Axium's portfolio materials describe 28 operating assets totaling 1,415 MW across 12 states in the United States. Axium acquired a 49% interest in the portfolio in 2022 from Manulife Investment Management, while Constellation retained the balance.
historicalMilestones: 2022-02-02: Axium completed the acquisition of a 49% equity interest in Constellation Renewables Partners from Manulife Investment Management.
2022: Axium described the portfolio as comprising 24 wind assets and 4 solar assets totaling 1,415 MW.
2022-2023: Axium disclosed repowering plans for one 70 MW wind asset in Maryland within the portfolio.
2024-2025: The portfolio continued operating as a diversified contracted renewable platform across 12 U.S. states.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Axium Infrastructure | Fund: Axium Managed Funds | Ownership Interest: 92.5%
Firm: AgeCare | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 7.5%
companyOverview: AgeCare Facilities Portfolio is a Canadian long-term-care platform comprising facilities in Ontario, Alberta, and British Columbia. The homes serve senior residents and their families, with revenues supported by provincially regulated and funded long-term-care and continuing-care frameworks plus resident co-payments. The operating model is asset-heavy and regulated because the portfolio owns physical facilities while AgeCare operates the homes under provincial licensing, funding, and care standards. Axium's portfolio materials describe a 92.5% interest in an Ontario portfolio of 16 facilities and 2,418 beds and a separate 92.5% interest in the Aster and Bloom portfolios in Alberta and British Columbia totaling 26 facilities and 4,114 beds. Axium partnered with AgeCare in 2020 in Alberta and currently owns the majority interest in the disclosed portfolios, with AgeCare retaining the balance and operating the assets.
historicalMilestones: 2020-01-08: Axium and AgeCare announced a partnership covering five continuing-care facilities in Alberta totaling 1,402 beds.
2020s: Axium's portfolio disclosures expanded to include the Aster and Bloom portfolios in Alberta and British Columbia.
2020s: Axium's portfolio disclosures also described a 16-facility, 2,418-bed long-term-care portfolio in Ontario operated by AgeCare.
2025: Axium continued to report majority ownership positions in AgeCare-operated long-term-care portfolios across Ontario, Alberta, and British Columbia.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Fiera Axium Infrastructure Canada L.P. | Fund: Fiera Axium Infrastructure Canada L.P. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Anthony Henday Drive Southeast is a public-private partnership road concession forming the southeast leg of Edmonton's ring road. The asset serves passenger and freight traffic in the Edmonton metropolitan area and its economic value is tied to a strategic urban bypass corridor rather than a local access road alone. Its operating model is asset-heavy and concession-based, with cash flows supported by a long-term agreement with the Province of Alberta rather than direct merchant exposure. Axium's portfolio materials describe an 11-kilometre roadway that reached commercial operation in October 2007 and remains under concession through 2037. A Fiera Axium-led consortium acquired the concession in 2010 from Macquarie's vehicle, and the current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2007-10: Anthony Henday Drive Southeast reached commercial operation.
2010-12-02: A Fiera Axium-led consortium acquired the concession from Macquarie Essential Assets Partnership.
2011-03-31: Fiera Axium Infrastructure Canada announced the closing of its investment in the project through its Canadian fund.
2037: The concession is scheduled to expire, according to Axium's portfolio disclosures.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Axium Infrastructure | Fund: Axium Managed Funds | Ownership Interest: 75%
Firm: Revera Inc. | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 25%
companyOverview: Arbour Heights is a long-term-care home in Kingston, Ontario. The facility serves senior residents and families and operates within Ontario's regulated long-term-care funding and licensing framework. Its model is asset-heavy and regulated because it derives cash flows from a physical care facility operated under provincial standards rather than from a variable market-pricing model. Axium and Revera described the home as a 174-bed, A-class long-term-care property and the first external acquisition completed by their broader Canadian long-term-care joint venture. The home was added to the Revera-Axium joint venture in 2019, and the venture's disclosed ownership split is 75% Axium and 25% Revera.
historicalMilestones: 2017-10-19: Axium announced a joint-venture agreement with Revera covering 32 long-term-care homes.
2018-04-30: The Revera-Axium long-term-care joint venture closed, with Axium owning 75% and Revera retaining 25%.
2019-07-03: The joint venture acquired Arbour Heights, a 174-bed long-term-care home in Kingston.
2019: Axium and Revera described Arbour Heights as the first external acquisition for the joint venture.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Axium Infrastructure | Fund: Axium Managed Funds | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Aspire at West Campus is a purpose-built graduate student housing asset at the University of Iowa in Iowa City. The property serves graduate students and related campus demand through on-campus apartment-style housing. Its operating model is asset-heavy and concession-based because the project is operated under a long-term ground lease and operating agreement with the university, with rental revenues derived from student occupancy. Axium's disclosures describe 521 apartments totaling 862 beds and a 50-year term with a 10-year sponsor extension option, making the asset the only on-campus graduate housing facility at the university at the time of acquisition. Axium acquired the project from Balfour Beatty Campus Solutions in 2019, and the current ownership split is not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2019-10-03: Axium acquired Aspire at West Campus from Balfour Beatty Campus Solutions.
2019: Axium disclosed that the asset comprised 521 apartments and 862 beds under a 50-year agreement with the University of Iowa.
2020s: Aspire continued operating as the university's on-campus graduate student housing facility.
2030s: The sponsor extension option can extend the original concession term by an additional 10 years.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Axium Infrastructure | Fund: Axium Managed Funds | Ownership Interest: 100%
companyOverview: Aurora Solar Portfolio is a portfolio of operating solar facilities in Ontario. The assets sell electricity under long-term contracted arrangements in Ontario's renewable-power market, giving the portfolio a utility-style renewable-generation revenue profile. Its operating model is asset-heavy and contracted because the investment is backed by physical solar plants rather than a development-only platform or merchant trading strategy. Axium has disclosed that the portfolio comprises nine operating solar facilities totaling 101 MWdc in Ontario. Axium acquired an initial 50% interest from Mitsubishi Corporation in 2019 and bought Osaka Gas's remaining 50% in 2020, bringing its ownership to 100%.
historicalMilestones: 2019-02-28: Axium acquired an initial 50% equity interest in the 101 MWdc Aurora Solar portfolio from Mitsubishi Corporation.
2020-07-16: Axium acquired Osaka Gas's remaining 50% interest, bringing ownership to 100%.
2020: Axium described the portfolio as comprising nine operating solar facilities in Ontario.
2020s: Aurora Solar continued operating as an Ontario contracted solar portfolio under Axium ownership.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Axium Infrastructure | Fund: Axium Managed Funds | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Autopistas Metropolitanas de Puerto Rico, or Metropistas, is the concessionaire for PR-22 and PR-5 in Puerto Rico. The roads serve commuters, freight movement, and business traffic in the island's northern corridor, linking San Juan with western and metropolitan markets. The operating model is asset-heavy and concession-based because revenues are generated primarily from toll collections under a long-term public-private partnership with the Puerto Rico Highways and Transportation Authority. Axium's portfolio materials describe a 50-year concession that began in 2011 for PR-22 and PR-5, with PR-22 and PR-5 acting as essential transport links for residents and businesses. Axium discloses an ownership interest in Metropistas, but the acquisition date and current ownership split are not publicly disclosed in the sources reviewed.
historicalMilestones: 2011: The concession for PR-22 and PR-5 commenced under the Puerto Rico public-private partnership structure.
2016-04-19: The PRHTA and Metropistas amended the concession agreement, according to compliance-report disclosures.
2017: Metropistas implemented additional tolling infrastructure and lane changes on PR-22 and PR-5.
2025: Public compliance disclosures continued to describe Metropistas as responsible for operating, rehabilitating, preserving, and improving PR-22 and PR-5.

principalLocationOfOperations: Massachusetts; Maine; New York; New Jersey; Maryland
ownershipDetails: Firm: Axium Infrastructure | Fund: Axium Managed Funds | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: BlueWave is a renewable-development platform focused on distributed-generation solar, community solar, and utility-scale storage in the northeastern and mid-Atlantic United States. Its customers and end markets include utilities, community-solar subscribers, and wholesale and retail electricity markets where storage and distributed generation can be monetized under state programs and contracted frameworks. The model combines development-stage and operating renewable infrastructure, so returns depend on originating, building, and operating projects rather than on a merchant trading strategy alone. Axium's portfolio materials describe a development pipeline of more than 1.3 GW across more than 140 projects in five states. Axium acquired BlueWave in 2022 to expand its U.S. solar and storage development platform, and the current ownership split is not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2022-05-12: Axium acquired BlueWave to accelerate growth in solar and energy-storage development.
2022: Axium disclosed that BlueWave's pipeline exceeded 1.3 GW across more than 140 projects in five states.
2023-2025: BlueWave continued developing community-solar, DG solar, and storage assets in the U.S. Northeast and Mid-Atlantic.

Firm: Recurrent Energy | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 20%
companyOverview: Crimson Storage is a standalone battery-storage asset in Southern California. The project serves the California grid by providing capacity and energy-shifting services backed by utility offtake contracts. Its operating model is asset-heavy and predominantly contracted because the asset earns revenues through long-term tolling and capacity arrangements, with limited merchant exposure on the contracted portion. Axium's disclosures describe a 350 MW / 1,400 MWh project with 200 MW under a 14-year-and-10-month tolling agreement and 150 MW under a 15-year capacity contract, and the project entered operation in 2022. Axium acquired the majority interest during development and, when operations were announced in October 2022, Axium held 80% while developer Recurrent Energy retained 20%.
historicalMilestones: 2021-08: Construction was scheduled to begin on Crimson Storage, according to Axium portfolio disclosures.
2022-08: Commercial operation was targeted for August 2022 in Axium's portfolio materials.
2022-10-18: Axium and Recurrent Energy announced that Crimson Storage was in operation.
2022-10-18: Public disclosures stated that a fund managed by Axium owned 80% of the project and Recurrent Energy retained 20%.

companyOverview: Edwards Sanborn 1A is a co-located solar and battery-storage facility in Kern County, California. The project serves utility, municipal-utility, and corporate offtakers through contracted renewable-power and storage arrangements. Its operating model is asset-heavy and contracted because value is derived from a physical generation-and-storage complex supported by long-term offtake agreements rather than from a merchant development strategy alone. Axium disclosed that phase 1A includes 397 MWac of solar capacity and 1,505 MWh of battery storage. Axium acquired an 80% interest from Terra-Gen in October 2022, and Terra-Gen retained the remaining 20%.

companyOverview: Axium Great Plains Wind LLC holds a diversified wind portfolio in the United States. The assets sell power to utility and corporate offtakers under long-term agreements, with a portion of capacity subject to recontracting over time. Its model is asset-heavy because returns are driven by operating wind farms and their contracted cash flows rather than by a development-fee model. Axium portfolio materials describe 1,062 MW across seven wind assets in three states, with about 65% of capacity under long-term offtake agreements. An Axium-led consortium acquired a 49% interest from EDP Renewables in 2016, while EDP Renewables retained 51%.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Fiera Axium Infrastructure Canada L.P. | Fund: Fiera Axium Infrastructure Canada L.P. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: The CHUM Research Centre, or CRCHUM, is a healthcare public-private partnership asset in Montreal. The project serves the Centre hospitalier de l'Université de Montréal modernization program and supports hospital and research functions for the public healthcare system. Its operating model is concession-based and asset-heavy because the partnership finances and supports the physical infrastructure under a long-term public contract rather than relying on demand-based market revenues. Axium identified CRCHUM as the first phase of the CHUM modernization project and one of the early assets in its Canadian infrastructure fund. Fiera Axium reached financial close on phase 1 in 2010, and the current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2010-05-26: Fiera Axium achieved financial close on phase 1 of the CHUM modernization project.
2011-02-18: Fiera Axium cited CRCHUM as one of the first assets in its Canadian infrastructure fund.
2010s: CRCHUM proceeded as the initial phase of the CHUM modernization program in Montreal.

companyOverview: CPV Three Rivers Energy Center is a large combined-cycle natural-gas power project in Grundy County, Illinois. The plant serves the PJM market and regional load by supplying dispatchable generation capacity to utility and wholesale power customers. Its model is asset-heavy and market-linked because the project is backed by a physical power plant with revenues derived from energy and capacity markets and related fuel and operating arrangements. Axium's transaction materials described the project at financial close in 2020 as a roughly 1,250 MW facility, and portfolio materials later referenced approximately 1,258 MW. Axium joined Competitive Power Ventures, GE Energy Financial Services, Osaka Gas USA, and Harrison Street at financial close in 2020, and the current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.

companyOverview: Cascade Power Project is a combined-cycle natural-gas generation asset in Alberta. The project serves the Alberta power market with dispatchable generation capacity and is positioned near gas supply and high-voltage transmission infrastructure. Its model is asset-heavy and market-based because revenues depend on physical plant operations and power-market participation, while a substantial portion of gas supply is supported by long-term netback agreements. Axium's disclosures described the plant as a 900 MW project located about 200 kilometres west of Edmonton. Axium partnered with OPTrust, DIF Capital Partners, Kineticor Resource Corp., and Macquarie Capital at financing close in 2020, and the current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.

companyOverview: Cedar Point II is an operating wind-generation project in Ontario. The asset sells all of its output under a long-term feed-in-tariff power purchase agreement, giving the project a contracted renewable-generation profile. Its model is asset-heavy and contracted because returns are tied to a physical wind facility and the long-dated offtake agreement with the provincial market counterparty. Axium's transaction announcement described the project as a 100 MW operational wind asset with 100% of power sold to the Independent Electricity System Operator under a 20-year FiT PPA. The project supplies renewable power under long-term contracted arrangements, with Axium and Avangrid describing Salt River Project as the offtake counterparty for the Arizona wind-and-solar portfolio acquired in 2019. companyOverview: Georgetown Energy Partners is the long-term concession vehicle for the comprehensive energy partnership serving Georgetown University in Washington, D.C. companyOverview: K2 Wind Facility is a 270 MW wind farm in Goderich, Ontario and is one of the larger operating wind projects in the province. All output is sold under a 20-year power purchase agreement with the Independent Electricity System Operator, which makes the project fully contracted rather than merchant. The asset is capital intensive and operates as a single utility-scale generation facility within Axium’s Canadian renewable portfolio. Public disclosures show that a consortium including Axium, Alberta Teachers’ Retirement Fund Board, and Manulife first acquired a one-third interest in 2016 and then acquired the remaining two-thirds from Pattern Energy and Capital Power in 2018. The current ownership percentages among the consortium members are not publicly disclosed.

The facility sells capacity and energy into the regional power market and forms part of a portfolio of U.S. gas-fired generation assets acquired by Axium from Capital Dynamics. companyOverview: Longwood Medical Area Energy System, also known as MATEP, is a district energy and microgrid platform serving major hospitals, research institutions, and medical schools in Boston’s Longwood area. The system provides electricity, steam, and chilled water under long-term utility contracts to five hospitals and the Harvard Institutes of Medicine, supporting mission-critical healthcare and research operations. Its operating model is asset-heavy and contracted, with physical generation and thermal infrastructure underpinning long-duration service agreements through 2051. Public materials describe the platform as serving 74 buildings across roughly 12 million square feet and supporting institutions with more than 2,000 beds and substantial inpatient and outpatient volumes. The project’s footprint is concentrated in Amherst, Massachusetts and serves the university’s student housing market rather than open-market multifamily demand. companyOverview: PUC Transmission LP is a regulated transmission development platform in Sault Ste. Marie, Ontario. The company is developing a 230 kV transmission line and transformer station intended to connect new industrial and regional loads, including Algoma Steel’s electric arc furnace project. Its operating model is asset-heavy and regulated, with cost recovery and allowed returns governed by Ontario’s utility regulatory framework rather than merchant power exposure. Public materials describe the project as approximately 10 kilometers of double-circuit line and a new 230 kV station, with commercial operation expected by the end of 2027. PUC Transmission was incorporated in 2021, and Axium acquired an 80% equity interest in 2024 while the Public Utility Commission of Sault Ste. Marie retained 20%.

2020s: GCT continued to operate Deltaport and Vanterm in British Columbia.

ownershipDetails: Firm: British Columbia Investment Management Corporation (BCI) | Fund: Infrastructure & Renewable Resources | Ownership Interest: 28.7%
companyOverview: InTransit BC is the concessionaire responsible for the Canada Line rapid transit system in Metro Vancouver. The system serves urban commuters, airport passengers, and local transit users through a long-term public-private partnership with TransLink. Its operating model is asset-heavy and concession-based, with value tied to long-lived rail infrastructure and contractual payments rather than merchant transportation demand alone. Public sources describe the Canada Line as an 18-kilometre system with 16 stations linking downtown Vancouver, Richmond, and Vancouver International Airport. Operations are concentrated in British Columbia under a 35-year concession that runs to 2040. BCI's pooled-fund disclosures report a 28.7% interest in InTransit BC, while full public disclosure of the remaining ownership group is limited.
historicalMilestones: 2009-08: TransLink's public reporting indicates the Canada Line concession commenced in August 2009.
2009: The Canada Line entered service between downtown Vancouver, Richmond, and Vancouver International Airport.
2022: BCI pooled-fund financial statements disclosed a 28.7% interest in InTransit BC.
2040-07: Public TransLink materials state that the concession runs to July 2040.

principalLocationOfOperations: California, Texas, Illinois, Tennessee, Florida, and Pennsylvania
ownershipDetails: Firm: British Columbia Investment Management Corporation (BCI) | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Paine Schwartz Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Monterey Mushrooms is a producer and marketer of fresh mushrooms and related products for grocery, foodservice, and packaged-food customers. The business operates a vertically integrated model that includes growing, harvesting, packaging, and distribution rather than a purely branded or distribution-only approach. It is asset-heavy because output depends on controlled-environment growing facilities, composting capacity, distribution infrastructure, and labor-intensive operations. Company materials state that Monterey Mushrooms produces roughly 250 million pounds of mushrooms annually, employs around 4,000 people, and operates across multiple US states and Mexico. Its principal US operations include California, Texas, Illinois, Tennessee, Florida, and Pennsylvania. A 2025 European Commission filing stated that BCI and Paine Schwartz Partners were acquiring joint control of Monterey Mushrooms Holdings, and public materials do not disclose the partners' ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: 1971: Company history materials indicate that Monterey Mushrooms was founded in 1971.
2021: Company materials referenced a 50-year operating history.
2025: A European Commission filing stated that BCI and Paine Schwartz Partners were acquiring joint control of Monterey Mushrooms Holdings.
2025: Public company materials continued to describe national operations and approximately 250 million pounds of annual mushroom production.

ownershipDetails: Firm: British Columbia Investment Management Corporation (BCI) | Fund: Infrastructure & Renewable Resources | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: PSP Investments | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Mosaic Forest Management is a timberlands management company overseeing large private forestlands and associated public tenures on Vancouver Island and coastal British Columbia. The platform serves timber, lumber, and broader forest-products markets through harvesting, stewardship, and land-management activities. Its operating model is asset-heavy because it manages extensive timberlands where value is tied to land quality, biological growth, harvest planning, and log sales. Company materials describe Mosaic as the manager for the TimberWest and Island Timberlands ownership interests and note a supply chain supporting roughly 2,000 employees and contractors. Operations are concentrated in British Columbia, particularly on Vancouver Island and the province's coast. Mosaic launched in 2018, and later public reporting indicated that the underlying ownership of the managed timberlands rests with BCI and PSP Investments.
historicalMilestones: 2018-11: Mosaic Forest Management launched as the management platform for TimberWest and Island Timberlands.
2018: Mosaic began managing extensive private forest lands and public tenures in coastal British Columbia.
2024: Supply-chain reporting described a network of roughly 2,000 employees and contractors and more than 700 vendors.
2024: Public materials continued to identify BCI and PSP as the ultimate owners of the underlying timberland interests.

ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Nexus Water Group is a regulated water and wastewater utility platform formed through the combination of Corix Infrastructure's US regulated water and wastewater business and SouthWest Water Company. The platform serves residential, commercial, and municipal utility customers and is structured around regulated networks rather than uncontracted commodity exposure. Its operating model is asset-heavy because cash flows are tied to owned distribution, collection, and treatment infrastructure across numerous local systems. Public launch materials described a multi-state footprint, and later company announcements referenced assets in at least eight states that were subject to a 2025 sale agreement with American Water. Operations are centered in multiple US states, with the company headquartered in Sugar Land, Texas. The merger that created Nexus closed on April 1, 2024, but the current ownership structure and ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2024-04-01: Corix Infrastructure's U.S. regulated water and wastewater business and SouthWest Water completed their merger to create Nexus Water Group.
2024-04-02: Nexus Water Group formally announced the closing of the merger.
2025-05-19: Nexus announced a definitive agreement to sell operations and assets in eight states to American Water.
2026-02-25: California Water Service Group announced an agreement to purchase Nexus systems in Nevada and Oregon.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Brookfield | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 33.3%
Firm: British Columbia Investment Management Corporation (BCI) | Fund: Infrastructure & Renewable Resources | Ownership Interest: 33.3%
Firm: Norges Bank Investment Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 33.3%
companyOverview: Northview Energy is a renewable-power platform announced by Brookfield, BCI, and Norges Bank Investment Management in March 2026. The platform is intended to own contracted utility-scale solar and wind assets that sell power under long-term agreements to investment-grade counterparties. Its operating model is asset-heavy and contracted, with value tied to owned generation assets and long-duration power purchase agreements rather than merchant development alone. The seed portfolio comprises 22 operating solar and wind assets totaling approximately 2.3 GW across six US power markets, with an average remaining contract term of roughly 16 years according to the launch announcement. Operations are concentrated in the United States at launch, with a framework agreement to pursue additional renewable acquisitions from Brookfield-managed portfolios in the United States and Canada. Brookfield, BCI, and Norges Bank Investment Management announced equal ownership of the platform, while public disclosures indicated that the formal launch remained subject to approvals and customary closing conditions as of March 2026.
historicalMilestones: 2026-03-03: Brookfield, BCI, and Norges Bank Investment Management announced the launch of Northview Energy.
2026-03-03: The partners disclosed a 2.3 GW seed portfolio of 22 contracted operating renewable assets across six U.S. power markets.
2026-03-03: The partners announced equal ownership among Brookfield, BCI, and Norges Bank Investment Management.
2026-Q2: Public launch materials stated that formal launch was expected in the second quarter of 2026, subject to approvals and closing conditions.

companyOverview: Puget Sound Energy is a regulated electric and natural gas utility serving customers in Washington State. Its customer base is predominantly residential, commercial, and industrial, and company materials describe it as the largest utility in Washington. The business is asset-heavy and rate-regulated, with earnings tied to utility networks and generation resources rather than a merchant-only power model. Operations are concentrated in Washington, where public filings reference approximately 1.2 million electric customers and 800,000 natural gas customers. The company was formed in 1997 through the merger that created the current utility structure, although its predecessor history extends much further back. Public investor materials disclose a consortium ownership structure that includes BCI alongside Macquarie, Ontario Teachers', OMERS, AIMCo, and PGGM.
historicalMilestones: 1997: Puget Sound Energy was formed through the merger of Puget Sound Power & Light and Washington Energy Company.
2009: Puget Energy was acquired by a long-term investor consortium.

ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Teays River Investments is a permanent-capital agricultural investment platform focused on assets and businesses across the food and agriculture value chain. Its activities span farmland, agribusiness, and related operating investments serving agricultural production and food distribution markets. The model is asset-heavy because returns depend on owned agricultural assets and operating businesses rather than fee-only advisory revenue. Public sources identify Carmel, Indiana as the company's headquarters and indicate a multi-state US operating footprint. The company announced in 2025 that it had been founded in 2008 and continues to operate as a long-duration investment platform. BCI has referenced Teays River Investments in public materials about its renewable-resources partnerships, but current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2008: Teays River Investments was founded.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Basalt Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: EnviroSpark designs, installs, operates, and services electric-vehicle charging systems for commercial and institutional customers. Its customer base includes multifamily properties, workplaces, fleets, retailers, municipalities, and other site hosts adopting EV charging infrastructure. The business combines project development, installation, software-enabled operations, and ongoing service, giving it a hybrid model with both equipment deployment and contracted operating revenue. Company and sponsor materials state that EnviroSpark was founded in 2014, is based in Atlanta, and has deployed more than 10,000 chargers. Its footprint spans multiple US states, with Georgia serving as the company's primary operating base. Basalt Infrastructure Partners announced a $50 million investment in the company in 2024, and public sources do not disclose the resulting ownership percentage.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Basalt Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Basalt III | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Fatbeam is a fiber-network operator providing connectivity, transport, and related services to commercial, education, government, carrier, and residential customers. The business is infrastructure-led and asset-heavy because it owns and operates the underlying fiber network rather than acting solely as a reseller. Company materials describe a network footprint across eight western states and more than 150 cities, while sponsor materials previously referenced 1,250 route miles at the time of Basalt's acquisition. Its operating geography includes Washington, Oregon, Montana, Arizona, New Mexico, Idaho, Wyoming, and Nevada. The company was established in 2010 and has expanded its network reach through organic buildout and acquisitions. Basalt acquired Fatbeam from SDC Capital Partners and the company's founders in 2023, and public sources do not disclose the resulting ownership percentage.
historicalMilestones: 2010: Basalt portfolio materials identify Fatbeam's founding year as 2010.
2023-08-14: Basalt Infrastructure Partners announced the acquisition of Fatbeam from SDC Capital Partners and the company's founders.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Basalt Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Fortbrand leases aviation ground-support and airfield-maintenance equipment used by airports, airlines, and air-service providers. The business is asset-heavy because it owns the underlying equipment fleet and earns revenue through long-lived leasing and related service arrangements. Sponsor materials described more than 1,200 pieces of equipment at the time of Basalt's acquisition, giving the company meaningful scale in a specialized transportation niche. Operations are based in Plainview, New York and serve customers across the United States and Canada. Public sources do not disclose the company's founding year. Basalt Infrastructure Partners acquired Fortbrand from Wincove Private Holdings in 2023, and the company subsequently expanded through the acquisition of Xced in 2025.
historicalMilestones: 2023-09-07: Basalt Infrastructure Partners announced the acquisition of Fortbrand from Wincove Private Holdings.

companyOverview: Go Lime provides residential equipment rental, service, and installation offerings centered on water heaters, HVAC systems, and related home-comfort products. Its customer base is primarily homeowners in Ontario using recurring-rental and service arrangements rather than one-time equipment purchases. The business is asset-heavy because it finances and manages installed residential equipment fleets that generate recurring contracted cash flows. Company materials state that Go Lime has served more than 150,000 customers across Ontario since 2011. Its operating footprint is concentrated in Ontario, and Basalt's transaction also incorporated the assets of Simply Green Home Services into the platform. DC Advisory announced in 2025 that Basalt completed the parallel acquisition and subsequent merger of Go Lime and Simply Green, while public sources do not disclose the resulting ownership percentage.

companyOverview: Habitat Solar is a residential solar platform formed to own and operate distributed solar assets serving homeowners in the United States. The platform's revenue profile is tied to long-term residential solar arrangements rather than merchant wholesale electricity exposure. Its model is asset-heavy because returns depend on the performance of owned distributed-generation systems deployed at customer sites. Public Basalt materials describe Habitat as a partnership with IGS Solar, an established residential solar provider. Operations span multiple US states where residential distributed solar deployment supports the platform's installed base. Basalt formed Habitat in partnership with IGS Solar around 2020, and public sources do not disclose founding-year treatment or partner ownership percentages for the platform.

companyOverview: Helios Power is a distributed solar platform launched to build and own US solar projects. The platform serves commercial and utility off-takers through distributed generation assets rather than a development-only advisory model. Its operating model is asset-heavy and contracted because value is tied to owned project cash flows and long-term customer agreements. Basalt and Soltage announced the platform in 2019 with an initial target of deploying 200 MW of solar capacity in the United States. Operations span multiple US states through portfolios of distributed and community-scale solar projects. The platform was launched by Soltage and Basalt in 2019, and public sources do not disclose the partners' ownership percentages.

companyOverview: OnSite Partners develops, owns, and operates behind-the-meter power infrastructure for commercial, industrial, municipal, and institutional customers. Its customer base includes energy-intensive facilities such as data centers, schools, municipalities, and other organizations seeking resiliency, sustainability, and cost-management solutions. The operating model is asset-heavy and contracted because revenues are tied to owned distributed-energy assets operating under long-term customer arrangements. Company materials describe an operating portfolio of more than 90 projects, more than 200 MW of installed capacity, and assets across 21 US states. The portfolio spans solar, battery storage, peaking engines, substations, and fuel cells, giving the platform a diversified distributed-energy mix. Basalt acquired the business from American Electric Power in 2024, and the platform continued expanding through acquisitions such as One Power Company in 2026.

companyOverview: Skyway Towers develops, owns, and operates communications towers for wireless carriers and related connectivity customers. Its revenue model is infrastructure-based and contracted, with value driven by lease-up of multi-tenant tower sites rather than consumer retail subscriptions. The platform is asset-heavy because it owns the underlying tower infrastructure and supports long-lived lease cash flows. Transaction materials at the time of Basalt's acquisition described a portfolio of more than 400 tower structures across the United States. Operations are geographically diversified across multiple US markets rather than concentrated in a single state. Basalt III acquired Skyway Towers from Tinicum in 2022, and public sources do not disclose the resulting ownership percentage.

Its operating model is asset-heavy and logistics-intensive because it relies on compression, transportation, and related delivery infrastructure rather than a pure marketing model. companyOverview: ClearCurrent is a utility operations platform focused on water and wastewater systems. The business provides services to utility customers and communities through infrastructure operations rather than a consumer-branded retail model. Its operating model is asset- and operations-intensive because performance depends on utility-system management, regulatory compliance, and field execution. Bernhard Capital Partners' public materials describe the platform as serving customers in North Carolina and Indiana. The company is headquartered in Raleigh, North Carolina and is positioned within the water-utility services segment. Bernhard Capital Partners invested in ClearCurrent in 2021 through BCP Infrastructure Fund I, and public sources do not disclose the firm's ownership percentage.

companyOverview: Delta Utilities is a regulated natural gas distribution utility operating in Louisiana and Mississippi. Its customer base is primarily residential, commercial, and industrial gas users served through regulated local distribution networks. The business is asset-heavy and rate-regulated because earnings depend on owned pipeline and distribution infrastructure within utility service territories. Public company materials indicate that the platform now serves about 600,000 customers and employs more than 800 people following two major acquisitions in 2025. Its operations are concentrated in Louisiana and Mississippi, with corporate headquarters in New Orleans. Bernhard Capital Partners backed the platform and completed the acquisition of CenterPoint Energy's Louisiana and Mississippi gas utilities in April 2025, followed by the acquisition of Entergy's gas businesses in New Orleans and East Baton Rouge Parish in July 2025.

The business blends equipment deployment, project financing, and software-enabled energy management rather than operating as a pure installer only. Public materials identify Chandler, Arizona as the company's headquarters and indicate that installations are active in multiple states. Scale disclosures in sponsor and company materials emphasize a national offering footprint, but public sources do not fully disclose all operating metrics. Bernhard Capital Partners first invested in Elevation through a Series B financing in 2022 and added a $20 million convertible note investment in 2023.

companyOverview: National Water Infrastructure is a private wastewater utility platform serving residential communities and local customers in Louisiana. Its business model is regulated and infrastructure-based, with performance tied to owned wastewater collection and treatment systems rather than merchant exposure. Public testimony filed in 2025 described the company as serving more than 20,000 customers and as one of the largest private wastewater utility operators in Louisiana.
