BlackRock Alternatives announced the acquisition of a majority interest in 2023, while the founder remained one of the largest individual shareholders and exact ownership percentages were not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2018: Environmental 360 Solutions was founded.
2023-02-01: Environmental 360 Solutions announced the closing of BlackRock Alternatives' acquisition of a majority interest.
2023: The founder remained one of the largest individual shareholders following the transaction.
2025: Company materials described a workforce of more than 2,200 and service to more than 750,000 customers.

ownershipDetails: Firm: BlackRock | Fund: Evergreen Infrastructure Fund | Ownership Interest: 100%
companyOverview: This portfolio comprises operating US solar and solar-plus-storage assets acquired from Excelsior Energy Capital. The assets sell power under contracted structures and therefore have an operating model tied to long-lived renewable generation infrastructure rather than a development-only pipeline. Its revenue profile is asset-heavy and contracted because cash flows depend on the operating performance of the underlying projects and customer agreements. Public transaction materials described a 38-project portfolio totaling 89 MW across multiple US states. The assets form part of BlackRock's Evergreen Infrastructure strategy focused on energy transition and energy security investments. BlackRock's Evergreen Infrastructure fund acquired the portfolio in March 2024, and Excelsior announced that it had sold the sub-portfolio to BlackRock.
historicalMilestones: 2024-03-12: Excelsior Energy Capital announced the sale of a sub-portfolio of solar and solar-plus-storage assets to BlackRock.
2024-03: Public transaction materials described the portfolio as 38 projects totaling 89 MW.
2024: Natural Power announced support for BlackRock's acquisition of the operating portfolio.

ownershipDetails: Firm: AT&T | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: BlackRock | Fund: Diversified Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Gigapower is a wholesale open-access fiber broadband platform formed by AT&T and BlackRock. The business builds, owns, and operates fiber networks that internet service providers can use to serve end customers, rather than selling only through a single captive retail brand. Its operating model is asset-heavy because value is tied to owned fiber infrastructure and recurring wholesale network revenues. AT&T's launch materials described plans to deploy multi-gig fiber in markets beyond AT&T's traditional footprint, and later reporting referenced operations across multiple states. The company launched in 2023 and has been building an open-access broadband footprint in the United States. AT&T and BlackRock announced the joint venture in 2022 and launched Gigapower in 2023, but public sources do not disclose the partners' percentage ownership.
historicalMilestones: 2022-12-23: AT&T and BlackRock announced plans to form the Gigapower joint venture.
2023-05-11: AT&T announced the launch of Gigapower.
2025: Public reporting referenced Gigapower's expansion across multiple U.S. states and communities.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Capital Power | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 50%
Firm: BlackRock | Fund: Diversified Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: 50%
companyOverview: Harquahala Generation Company owns a large combined-cycle natural gas power plant in Arizona. The asset serves wholesale power needs through dispatchable generation and benefits from a contracted tolling structure rather than full merchant exposure. Its operating model is asset-heavy because value is tied to a single large thermal generation facility and its long-term operating agreements. Capital Power's transaction materials describe the plant as a 1,092 MW facility in Maricopa County with a tolling agreement extending through 2031. Operations are concentrated in Arizona as a single-asset generation business. Capital Power completed the acquisition in February 2024 through a 50-50 partnership with BlackRock's Diversified Infrastructure business.
historicalMilestones: 2004: Capital Power materials indicate that Harquahala reached commercial operation in 2004.
2023-11-14: Capital Power announced the strategic acquisition of Harquahala and La Paloma through a 50-50 partnership with BlackRock.
2024-02-16: Capital Power completed the acquisition of the 1,092 MW Harquahala facility.
2031: Capital Power materials reference a tolling agreement running through 2031.

ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Indian Energy develops and integrates microgrids, renewable generation, energy storage, and related distributed-energy systems. Its customer base includes tribal communities, commercial users, and institutional clients seeking resilient on-site power infrastructure. The business combines development, engineering, and systems integration with ownership participation in selected projects rather than a pure equipment-sales model. Company materials identify a headquarters in Southern California and state that the company is 100% Native American-owned, with a development pipeline that includes utility-scale renewable generation and long-duration storage. Operations are concentrated in California, with broader project activity in tribal and distributed-energy markets. BlackRock Impact Opportunities identified Indian Energy as a portfolio investment made in 2023, but public sources do not disclose the fund's ownership percentage or the company's full current capitalization.
historicalMilestones: 2009: Company materials identify Indian Energy's founding year as 2009.
2023: BlackRock Impact Opportunities identified Indian Energy as a portfolio investment.
2024-08-20: Indian Energy announced financial close on the Viejas Enterprise microgrid project.
2024: Company materials described a development pipeline including 4 GW of renewable generation and 6 GWh of storage.

ownershipDetails: Firm: BlackRock | Fund: Diversified Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: 100%
companyOverview: Jupiter Power develops, owns, and operates utility-scale battery energy storage projects in the United States. Its counterparties include utilities, load-serving entities, and power-market participants that contract for dispatchable storage capacity and ancillary services. The operating model is asset-heavy because the company owns large-scale battery systems and monetizes them through contracted and market-based storage revenues. Company materials describe an operating and development pipeline exceeding 11,000 MW, making it one of the larger dedicated storage platforms in the US market. Its footprint spans multiple US power markets rather than a single-region buildout. BlackRock acquired Jupiter Power from EnCap Investments in 2022, and public transaction materials described the company as having been sold to BlackRock.
historicalMilestones: 2017: Company materials identify Jupiter Power's founding year as 2017.
2022-05-10: EnCap Investments announced the sale of Jupiter Power to BlackRock.
2024: Jupiter announced new battery storage projects totaling 652 MWh.
2025-01-28: Jupiter announced a $500 million upsized corporate credit facility to support expansion.

profileSubsector: Commercial and industrial solar and battery storage
ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Lighthouse is a US commercial and industrial solar and battery storage platform. The platform's customers are commercial and industrial off-takers using distributed renewable generation and storage assets. Its model is asset-heavy and contracted because project returns depend on owned operating systems and underlying customer agreements. BlackRock's Evergreen Infrastructure fundraising materials described Lighthouse as an operating portfolio spanning six US states. Operations are therefore geographically diversified within the United States rather than concentrated in a single region. BlackRock disclosed in November 2023 that the Evergreen Infrastructure fund had signed definitive documentation to acquire Lighthouse, but public sources reviewed here do not disclose a completed closing or final ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: 2023-11-15: BlackRock stated that its Evergreen Infrastructure fund had signed definitive documentation to acquire Lighthouse.
2023-11: BlackRock described Lighthouse as a U.S. C&I solar and battery storage platform with an operating portfolio across six states.
2023-2024: Public fundraising materials continued to reference Lighthouse as an early Evergreen Infrastructure investment.

ownershipDetails: Firm: BlackRock | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 100%
companyOverview: National Renewable Solutions is an early-stage wind and solar project development company. Its business centers on site origination, permitting, interconnection, land control, and development of utility-scale renewable projects for eventual construction or sale. The model is development-led rather than contracted utility ownership, but it remains capital-intensive because value depends on building a pipeline of viable renewable projects. Company materials identify Minnetonka, Minnesota as headquarters and describe activity across multiple US renewable markets. The company was founded in 2011 and has built a portfolio spanning wind, solar, and energy-storage opportunities. BlackRock acquired the company in 2021, and National Renewable Solutions announced that 100% of its shares had been purchased by BlackRock.
historicalMilestones: 2011: Company materials identify National Renewable Solutions' founding year as 2011.
2021-07-06: National Renewable Solutions announced that BlackRock had acquired 100% of the company.
2020s: The company continued to advance an early-stage wind and solar development pipeline under BlackRock ownership.

ownershipDetails: Firm: BlackRock | Fund: Diversified Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Morgan Stanley Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Portland Natural Gas Transmission System is a FERC-regulated natural gas transmission pipeline serving New England and Atlantic Canada markets. Its customers include gas shippers, utilities, and power-market participants that rely on pipeline transportation capacity into the northeastern United States. The business is asset-heavy and contracted because value is tied to a long-lived interstate pipeline system and transportation agreements rather than commodity ownership. Company materials describe a 295-mile pipeline with approximately 460,000 dekatherms per day of capacity. Its principal operating corridor runs through New Hampshire, Maine, and Massachusetts, with connections into Québec and New Brunswick. TC Energy announced the sale of PNGTS in March 2024 to BlackRock's Diversified Infrastructure business and Morgan Stanley Infrastructure Partners, and public sources do not disclose the buyers' respective ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: 1999: Company materials identify PNGTS's in-service date as 1999.
2024-03-04: TC Energy announced the sale of PNGTS to BlackRock's Diversified Infrastructure business and Morgan Stanley Infrastructure Partners.
2024: Transaction materials valued the sale at approximately $1.14 billion including assumption of notes.
2024-2025: PNGTS continued operating as a FERC-regulated pipeline serving New England and Atlantic Canada.

principalLocationOfOperations: United States and Canada, with international operations
ownershipDetails: Firm: Canadian Solar | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: BlackRock | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 20%
companyOverview: Recurrent Energy develops, owns, and operates utility-scale solar and energy storage projects on a global basis. Its counterparties include utilities, corporate off-takers, and other long-term buyers of renewable-power output and capacity. The business is asset-heavy and contracted because value is tied to owned project portfolios, development pipelines, and long-term power sales. Company materials in 2026 referenced approximately 12 GW of solar projects and 6.2 GWh of storage completed, with a much larger solar and storage development pipeline. Its operating footprint includes the United States and Canada as well as international markets. BlackRock committed $500 million to the company in 2024 through a convertible investment that, on closing, represented 20% of Recurrent Energy's fully diluted shares, while Canadian Solar stated that it retained majority ownership.
historicalMilestones: 2006: Company materials identify Recurrent Energy's founding year as 2006.
2024-01-08: Recurrent Energy announced a $500 million capital commitment from BlackRock.
2024-10-01: Recurrent Energy announced the closing of BlackRock's $500 million investment.
2024: Recurrent stated that BlackRock's investment represented 20% of fully diluted shares and that Canadian Solar retained majority ownership.

ownershipDetails: Firm: 1PointFive / Occidental | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: BlackRock | Fund: Diversified Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: STRATOS is a direct-air-capture project being developed in Ector County, Texas. The facility is intended to remove carbon dioxide directly from the atmosphere and serve carbon-management and sequestration markets rather than conventional power demand. Its operating model is asset-heavy and project-based because value depends on a large-scale carbon-removal facility, associated sequestration infrastructure, and contracted carbon-management demand. 1PointFive's public materials state that the project is designed to capture up to 500,000 tonnes of carbon dioxide per year. Operations are concentrated in Texas as a single flagship carbon-removal facility. Occidental and BlackRock announced a joint venture for the project in 2023, but public sources do not disclose the final ownership percentages between 1PointFive and BlackRock clients.
historicalMilestones: 2023-08-15: Occidental and BlackRock announced a joint venture to develop STRATOS.
2023-09-22: 1PointFive held the groundbreaking ceremony for the facility.
2025-04-03: Occidental and 1PointFive announced receipt of Class VI permits for STRATOS.
2025: 1PointFive stated that the project remained on track to start commercial operations in 2025.

principalLocationOfOperations: Northeast United States and national project pipeline
ownershipDetails: Firm: BlackRock | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Vanguard Renewables develops and operates anaerobic digestion and renewable natural gas projects that convert agricultural and food waste into energy products. Its customer base includes farms, food processors, waste suppliers, and energy off-takers seeking organics recycling and renewable gas supply. The business is asset-heavy because it develops, owns, and operates digestion and gas-upgrading infrastructure tied to long-term feedstock and energy arrangements. Company materials describe 17 organics-to-renewable-energy facilities and annual production capacity of more than 440 GWh of renewable electricity equivalent and 1.5 Bcf of RNG. Its installed operating base is concentrated in the Northeast, with a broader national project pipeline. BlackRock Real Assets announced the acquisition of Vanguard Renewables in 2022, and later company materials described BlackRock as the majority shareholder while public sources did not disclose the exact percentage.
historicalMilestones: 2014: Company materials identify Vanguard Renewables' founding year as 2014.
2022-07-20: Vanguard Renewables announced its acquisition by BlackRock Real Assets.
2024-03-12: Vanguard and TotalEnergies announced an equally owned joint venture for future U.S. farm-based RNG projects.
2024: Company materials continued to describe BlackRock as Vanguard's majority shareholder.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Royal Vopak | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 50%
Firm: BlackRock | Fund: Global Energy & Power Infrastructure Fund | Ownership Interest: 50%
companyOverview: Vopak Industrial Infrastructure Americas is an industrial-terminal platform on the US Gulf Coast. The assets provide storage, logistics, and industrial infrastructure services to a large chemical customer base rather than directly serving consumer end markets. Its operating model is asset-heavy and contracted because value depends on terminal infrastructure and long-term service agreements. Public transaction materials describe three terminals acquired from Dow in Plaquemine, Saint Charles, and Freeport, giving the platform exposure to both Louisiana and Texas. Operations are concentrated on the US Gulf Coast, where the assets are integrated with Dow's industrial footprint. Royal Vopak and BlackRock's Global Energy & Power Infrastructure Fund formed the 50-50 venture in 2020 and completed the acquisition of the terminals the same year.
historicalMilestones: 2020-04-23: Vopak and BlackRock's GEPIF announced the acquisition of three industrial terminals from Dow on the U.S. Gulf Coast.
2020-11-02: Vopak and BlackRock announced completion of the acquisition.
2020: The partners established a 50-50 industrial infrastructure joint venture covering terminals in Louisiana and Texas.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Blackstone | Fund: Blackstone Energy Transition Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Accel International manufactures high-performance electrical conductors and related wire products for industrial end markets. Its customer base includes aerospace, medical electronics, telecommunications, computing, industrial, and oil and gas equipment manufacturers. The business is asset-heavy because it operates vertically integrated manufacturing facilities and depends on specialized production capacity rather than outsourced fabrication alone. Company materials describe three operating factories in Connecticut and Indiana with nearly 500,000 square feet of manufacturing space and reference expansion activity in Florida. Operations are therefore concentrated in Connecticut, Indiana, and Florida within the United States. Blackstone Energy Transition Partners is identified in Blackstone's public portfolio biographies as an investor in Accel International, but public sources reviewed here do not disclose the investment date, exact ownership percentage, or full ownership structure.
historicalMilestones: 2021: Company materials referenced expansion of a Florida manufacturing facility.
2025-2026: Blackstone executive biographies identified Accel International as a current Blackstone Energy Transition Partners investment.
2026: Company materials continued to describe vertically integrated manufacturing operations in Connecticut, Indiana, and Florida.

ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Advanced Cooling Technologies designs and manufactures thermal management, heat transfer, and energy efficiency products used in data centers, defense, aerospace, electronics, HVAC, and industrial applications. The company sells engineered components and systems to original equipment manufacturers, end users, and government-related customers that require temperature control and power-density management. Its operating model is asset-heavy, with in-house engineering and manufacturing supporting both standard products and custom applications. Public materials identify Pennsylvania as its operating base, while its end markets extend across the United States. Blackstone Energy Transition Partners announced an agreement in March 2026 to acquire a majority stake, but post-closing ownership percentages were not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2003: Advanced Cooling Technologies was founded.
2012: The company acquired Q-Dot Technology to add advanced heat-spreader capabilities.
2015: ACT acquired PCM Products to expand into phase change material and thermal storage applications.
2026-03-11: Blackstone Energy Transition Partners announced an agreement to acquire a majority stake in ACT.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Blackstone | Fund: Blackstone Energy Transition Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Alliance Technical Group provides emissions testing, stack testing, laboratory analysis, leak detection, and environmental compliance services to utilities, industrial facilities, and other regulated operators. Its customer base spans power generation, manufacturing, chemicals, waste, and other end markets that require environmental permitting, monitoring, and reporting support. The business operates as a field-service and lab network, combining technicians, mobile laboratories, and acquired specialist service lines. Public disclosures state that the company has more than 110 locations across the United States and Canada and operates more than 60 mobile laboratories. Blackstone announced the acquisition of Alliance Technical Group in January 2026, and detailed ownership percentages were not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2000: Alliance Technical Group was founded.
2022: Alliance acquired Oxford Biomedical Technologies to expand into source emissions and analytical capabilities.
2025: Alliance acquired Earth Analytical Sciences, Mid-Continent Testing Laboratories, and Element One to broaden laboratory and compliance services.
2026-01-06: Blackstone Energy Transition Partners announced the acquisition of Alliance Technical Group.

ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Arlington Industries manufactures electrical fittings, boxes, cable management products, and related accessories used in residential, commercial, industrial, and data center installations. Its products are sold into electrical distribution channels and support contractors, OEMs, and end users involved in power, communications, and building systems projects. The business operates as a manufacturing platform with a substantial catalog of proprietary components and a long-established domestic production base in Pennsylvania. Public company materials describe a multi-decade operating history and broad product penetration across electrical end markets in North America. Blackstone announced an agreement to acquire Arlington Industries in January 2026, but current and post-closing ownership percentages were not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 1949: Arlington Industries was founded.
1956: Current family ownership began when the company was acquired by the Shapiro family.
1990s-2020s: Arlington expanded its electrical fittings, boxes, and cable management product portfolio across commercial and industrial applications.
2026-01-26: Blackstone announced an agreement to acquire Arlington Industries.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Blackstone | Fund: Blackstone Infrastructure Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Atlantic Power Transmission develops transmission infrastructure intended to connect offshore wind generation to onshore demand centers on the US East Coast. Its counterparties are expected to include utilities, grid operators, state agencies, developers, and other participants in offshore generation and transmission procurement processes. The business follows a project-development model in which capital is deployed into permitting, stakeholder engagement, design, and construction planning ahead of long-term operation of regulated or contracted assets. Public materials identify New Jersey as a principal operating focus, including the company's bid activity and workforce development commitments in the state. Blackstone Infrastructure launched and funded the platform, and detailed ownership percentages have not been publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2021: Atlantic Power Transmission emerged as a Blackstone Infrastructure-backed transmission development platform focused on East Coast offshore wind interconnection.
2021-09-09: APT announced a bid for New Jersey's offshore wind transmission project.
2022-11-16: APT announced a $50 million commitment to workforce development in New Jersey.
2023-2025: The platform continued advancing transmission development efforts tied to offshore wind buildout in the Northeast.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Blackstone | Fund: Blackstone Energy Transition Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Aypa Power develops, owns, and operates utility-scale energy storage and hybrid renewable projects in North America. Its customers are primarily utilities, corporate offtakers, and grid participants that need dispatchable capacity, ancillary services, and renewable integration support. The platform follows a development-and-ownership model, with projects contracted through power purchase agreements, tolling agreements, or other structured revenue arrangements rather than pure merchant exposure alone. Public disclosures describe a project pipeline of more than 22 gigawatts and note that more than 30 projects were operating or under construction by early 2026. Blackstone acquired the business in 2020 and has continued to fund its expansion, while detailed ownership percentages were not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2017: The business was founded as NRStor C&I.
2018: The company's first battery storage project entered service.
2020: Blackstone acquired the company and it was rebranded as Aypa Power.
2026-02-04: Aypa announced a $1.5 billion portfolio debt financing to support battery storage development.

oneSentenceDescription: Cheniere Energy Partners (NYSE American: CQP) is the largest producer and exporter of liquefied natural gas (LNG) in the United States and among the largest globally, operating the Sabine Pass LNG terminal in Cameron Parish, Louisiana.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Blackstone Inc. | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Brookfield Asset Management Inc. | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Cheniere Energy Partners owns the Sabine Pass LNG terminal and related pipeline infrastructure and sells liquefaction capacity and LNG under long-term commercial arrangements. Its customer base includes global utilities, energy marketers, and industrial buyers seeking contracted LNG supply from the US Gulf Coast. The business is asset-heavy and largely contracted, with cash flow supported by long-term sale and purchase agreements and fixed-fee capacity arrangements. Public filings describe six operating liquefaction trains at Sabine Pass with aggregate nominal production capacity of about 30 mtpa, making it one of the largest US LNG export platforms. Cheniere Energy remains the sponsor and manager, while public filings indicate that the remaining non-controlling limited partner interests are held by Blackstone, Brookfield, and public unitholders, with detailed percentages not publicly disclosed in the cited sources.
historicalMilestones: 1996: Cheniere Energy was founded.
2005: Sabine Pass received federal authorization for LNG import terminal development.
2012: Cheniere Partners closed a major credit facility for Sabine Pass liquefaction development.
2016: Sabine Pass Train 1 achieved substantial completion and commenced LNG exports.
2022: Sabine Pass Train 6 was completed, bringing the terminal to six operating liquefaction trains.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Blackstone | Fund: Blackstone Energy Transition Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Enverus provides software, data, and analytics used by oil and gas operators, utilities, investors, traders, and service providers across energy and infrastructure markets. Its products support subsurface interpretation, land and asset analysis, power market intelligence, emissions tracking, and commercial decision-making. The business operates as a subscription software and data platform rather than an asset-owning utility or midstream operator. Public disclosures cite more than 6,000 customers, a presence in 50 countries, and a workforce of more than 1,500 employees. Blackstone announced an agreement to acquire Enverus from Hellman & Friedman and Genstar in August 2025, and detailed ownership percentages were not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 1999: The business was founded as Drillinginfo.
2021: The company rebranded as Enverus to reflect a broader software and analytics offering across energy markets.
2023-2025: Enverus expanded its power and energy transition data offerings alongside its upstream software platform.
2025-08-06: Blackstone announced an agreement to acquire Enverus from Hellman & Friedman and Genstar.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Blackstone | Fund: Blackstone Energy Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Geosyntec Consultants is an environmental engineering and consulting firm that advises public- and private-sector clients on natural resources, civil infrastructure, remediation, water, and energy-transition projects. Its clients include industrial companies, utilities, developers, municipalities, and infrastructure owners that require technical design, permitting, and environmental risk management support. The business follows a professional-services model built around engineers, scientists, and consultants rather than ownership of regulated or contracted infrastructure assets. Public materials identify Boca Raton, Florida as a key operating center and describe a broad footprint across the United States and selected international markets. Blackstone Energy Partners made a majority investment in 2022, and the detailed ownership split has not been publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 1983: Geosyntec Consultants was founded.
2010s: The firm expanded from environmental remediation into broader water, coastal, geotechnical, and civil infrastructure consulting services.
2022-05-10: Geosyntec announced a majority investment from Blackstone Energy Partners.
2022-2025: The company used the new capital base to support acquisitions, recruiting, and geographic expansion.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Blackstone | Fund: Blackstone Energy Transition Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Hill Top Energy Center is a combined-cycle natural gas power plant in western Pennsylvania. The facility supplies electricity into the PJM market and is positioned near large commercial and data center power demand in the region. Its operating model is asset-heavy and tied to wholesale power generation, with value driven by plant efficiency, dispatch profile, and local market conditions. Public disclosures describe Hill Top as a 620 megawatt plant and one of the newer and more efficient combined-cycle facilities in Pennsylvania. Blackstone announced an agreement to acquire the asset from Ardian in September 2025, and detailed ownership percentages were not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2021: Hill Top Energy Center entered commercial operation in western Pennsylvania.
2025-09-15: Blackstone announced an agreement to acquire Hill Top Energy Center from Ardian.
2025: The asset was highlighted by Blackstone as part of its strategy to invest in dispatchable generation supporting rising data center demand.
2026: Hill Top remained part of Blackstone's regional power-generation buildout alongside new greenfield generation initiatives.

oneSentenceDescription: Hotwire Communications is a fiber-first broadband, video, and voice services provider focused on multi-dwelling unit (MDU) and planned community markets, primarily in Florida, the Mid-Atlantic, and other high-growth Southeastern U.S.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Brookfield Infrastructure | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Hotwire Communications provides fiber-based broadband, video, voice, and managed services to residential communities, multifamily properties, and commercial customers. Its primary end markets include homeowner associations, master-planned communities, condominiums, and other multi-dwelling environments that value bundled connectivity and service support. The business combines network ownership with long-term service relationships, making it an asset-heavy digital infrastructure platform with recurring subscription revenue. Brookfield has publicly described the company as serving more than 300,000 customers and having an addressable base of more than 12 million HOA households. Blackstone invested in Hotwire in 2021 in partnership with the founders, and Brookfield Infrastructure completed its acquisition of the company in September 2025.
historicalMilestones: 2000: Hotwire Communications was founded in South Florida.
2021-04-05: Blackstone announced an investment in Hotwire in partnership with the founders.
2025-09-03: Brookfield Infrastructure completed its acquisition of Hotwire Communications.
2025: Brookfield identified Hotwire as a scaled residential fiber platform serving more than 300,000 customers.

oneSentenceDescription: Invenergy is one of the largest privately held clean energy companies in North America, having developed over 31 GW of wind, solar, natural gas, and energy storage projects since inception.
profileSubsector: Wind, solar, storage and power generation development
ownershipDetails: Firm: CDPQ | Fund: Not applicable. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Invenergy management | Fund: Not applicable. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Blackstone Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Invenergy develops, owns, and operates wind, solar, storage, and natural gas generation projects, along with transmission and other power infrastructure. Its customers include utilities, corporates, public agencies, and other counterparties that procure electricity, capacity, or clean energy attributes through long-term contracts and market arrangements. The platform combines development, construction oversight, and asset ownership, giving it both development fees and long-duration operating exposure. Public materials describe more than 31 gigawatts of developed projects and a broad operating footprint across North America and selected international markets. Blackstone Infrastructure invested approximately $3 billion in Invenergy Renewables in 2022, while CDPQ and Invenergy management remained majority owners.
historicalMilestones: 2001: Invenergy was founded in Chicago.
2010s: The company expanded from wind development into solar, storage, natural gas generation, and transmission.
2022-01-07: Invenergy announced an approximately $3 billion investment from Blackstone Infrastructure Partners in Invenergy Renewables.
2023-06-21: Invenergy announced a $1 billion follow-on investment from Blackstone Infrastructure Partners.

profileSubsector: Power generation development and asset management
principalLocationOfOperations: New Jersey and multiple U.S. states
ownershipDetails: Firm: Blackstone | Fund: Blackstone Energy Transition Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Kindle Energy develops, acquires, manages, and operates power generation assets in North America. Its customer and counterparties include utilities, cooperatives, municipalities, commercial and industrial buyers, and wholesale power markets. The business operates as an asset-heavy generation platform with responsibilities spanning investment, operations, fuel strategy, and commercial management. Public materials state that the team has managed more than 70 gigawatts of generation historically and that the operating platform had roughly 11 gigawatts of capacity by early 2026. Blackstone has backed Kindle as its North American power platform, and detailed ownership percentages have not been publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2015: Kindle Energy was formed.
2018-2021: Kindle built out a portfolio of gas-fired power assets and asset-management capabilities in North America.
2022: Construction began on the Magnolia Power Generating Station in Louisiana with Kindle as the development platform.
2026-02-25: Magnolia entered commercial operation, highlighting Kindle's role within Blackstone's generation strategy.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Blackstone | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Kinetik Holdings is an integrated midstream company focused on gas gathering, processing, treating, compression, water handling, and transportation in the Delaware Basin. Its customers are upstream producers and downstream market participants that need wellhead services, residue transportation, and access to takeaway infrastructure. The business is asset-heavy and largely supported by fee-based midstream arrangements tied to basin throughput rather than pure commodity ownership. Public company materials describe Kinetik as one of the largest publicly traded integrated midstream operators in the Delaware Basin. The company was created through the 2022 combination of Altus Midstream and EagleClaw, a legacy Blackstone-backed platform, and Blackstone remains a significant shareholder through related holding entities.
historicalMilestones: 2017: Blackstone-backed EagleClaw grew as a Delaware Basin midstream platform.
2021-12-01: Altus Midstream and EagleClaw announced an all-stock business combination.
2022-02-15: Altus shareholders approved the combination with BCP Raptor HoldCo.
2022: The combined company began operating as Kinetik Holdings.

profileSubsector: AI power campus and power interconnection development
ownershipDetails: Firm: Blackstone | Fund: Blackstone Energy Transition Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Lancium develops energy and digital infrastructure campuses designed to supply large-scale computing and AI workloads with grid-connected power. Its customers and counterparties include hyperscale data center developers, generation and transmission providers, and large industrial power users. The business follows a development model centered on interconnection rights, site control, transmission planning, and energy optimization rather than traditional colocation operations alone. Public materials describe multiple gigawatt-scale campuses in Texas, including the Abilene Clean Campus with a 1.2 gigawatt interconnection. Blackstone-backed funds invested in Lancium in 2024, and detailed ownership percentages were not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2017: Lancium was founded.
2024-07-18: Lancium and Crusoe announced the Abilene Clean Campus project.
2024: Blackstone-backed funds invested in Lancium to support large-scale power campus development.
2025-10-16: Lancium announced $600 million of debt financing tied to its development program.

profileSubsector: Building systems engineering and energy services
ownershipDetails: Firm: Blackstone | Fund: Blackstone Energy Transition Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Legence is a building systems platform providing engineering, design, installation, commissioning, and maintenance services for HVAC, controls, electrical, and energy efficiency projects. Its end markets include data centers, healthcare, life sciences, education, commercial buildings, and other mission-critical facilities. The company operates through a collection of regional brands, combining recurring service revenue with project-based engineering and construction work. Public materials describe a national footprint built from the former Therma Holdings platform and a focus on energy efficiency and building-performance modernization. Blackstone acquired Therma Holdings in 2022, and the platform was rebranded as Legence later that year.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Brookfield Renewable | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Deriva Energy develops, owns, and operates wind, solar, and battery storage projects in the United States. Its customers include utilities, corporates, and other offtakers that procure renewable electricity and related environmental attributes through long-term contracts. The business is asset-heavy and contract-oriented, with value supported by operating generation assets and a continuing development pipeline. Public company materials describe approximately 5,900 megawatts of operating and under-construction projects across more than 80 facilities in 16 states. Brookfield acquired Duke Energy Renewables in 2023 and rebranded the business as Deriva Energy later that year.

companyOverview: Duke Energy Florida is a regulated electric utility serving retail customers in Florida. Its customer base consists of residential, commercial, and industrial users served under state-regulated tariffs and capital programs. The business is asset-heavy and rate-based, with earnings driven by investment in generation, transmission, distribution, and storm-hardening infrastructure. Duke Energy announced a staged capital partnership with Brookfield in 2025 to support Duke Energy Florida's enlarged capital plan. The initial closing occurred in March 2026, at which point Brookfield held a 9.2% non-controlling interest and Duke Energy retained 90.8%, with additional phases expected to increase Brookfield's stake over time.

profileSubsector: Residential energy infrastructure and home services
ownershipDetails: Firm: Brookfield Infrastructure | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Enercare provides residential water heaters, HVAC equipment, and related home services under rental, service, and protection-plan arrangements. Its customer base is primarily residential households in Canada, with a smaller presence in selected US markets through service businesses. The platform combines owned-in-home equipment with recurring service contracts, producing infrastructure-like cash flow supported by a large installed base. Public disclosures at the time of Brookfield's acquisition emphasized scale in water heater rentals, HVAC servicing, and home protection plans. Brookfield Infrastructure and its institutional partners acquired Enercare in 2018.

companyOverview: Entropy develops carbon capture technology and project solutions for industrial emitters, with an initial focus on gas processing and related applications. Its counterparties include energy producers, industrial operators, and public-sector capital providers seeking lower-cost carbon capture deployment and carbon credit exposure. The company combines technology development with project participation, making its model a mix of intellectual property, project development, and structured infrastructure investment. Public materials identify Brookfield Renewable's strategic capital commitment and later support from Canada Growth Fund as key financing milestones. Brookfield Renewable announced a C$300 million strategic investment agreement with Entropy in 2022, while detailed current ownership percentages were not publicly disclosed in the cited sources.

companyOverview: Evolugen is Brookfield Renewable's Canadian operating platform for hydroelectric, wind, solar, and storage assets. companyOverview: FirstEnergy Transmission owns and develops electric transmission assets across a multi-state Mid-Atlantic and Midwest footprint. Its revenues are derived from regulated transmission rate frameworks rather than merchant power generation or commodity exposure. The platform is asset-heavy and focused on long-duration grid investment, with earnings linked to planned transmission capex and authorized returns. Brookfield has described the business as a transmission-focused platform spanning close to 13,000 miles of high-voltage lines. FirstEnergy sold a 19.9% stake to Brookfield in 2021 and an additional 30% stake in 2023, leaving FirstEnergy with 50.1% ownership and Brookfield with 49.9%.

companyOverview: The GATX and Brookfield joint venture owns a large railcar leasing fleet acquired from Wells Fargo. Its customers are rail shippers and lessees that require freight cars under operating lease arrangements across industrial and commodity end markets. The platform is asset-heavy and lease-based, with cash flow supported by railcar utilization, lease rates, and fleet management. Public disclosures state that the January 2026 transaction included approximately 105,000 railcars in the joint venture, while Brookfield separately acquired a finance lease portfolio of roughly 22,000 railcars and about 400 locomotives. GATX manages the operating lease fleet for the joint venture, and the parties did not publicly disclose the ownership split.

companyOverview: Genesee & Wyoming owns and operates short line and regional freight railroads. Its customers are industrial shippers, transload users, ports, and logistics providers that move bulk, agricultural, consumer, and industrial products by rail. The business is asset-heavy because it depends on owned and leased rail lines, locomotives, freight cars, terminals, and related infrastructure, with revenue generated through transportation and ancillary rail services. Company and transaction materials describe a network of more than 100 railroads, about 8,000 employees, roughly 3,000 customers, and operations spanning 41 US states and four Canadian provinces. Its principal North American operating footprint is in the United States and Canada, with additional international operations outside the region. Brookfield Infrastructure and GIC completed the take-private acquisition of Genesee & Wyoming in December 2019, and public sources do not disclose their current ownership percentages.

companyOverview: Geronimo Power develops, owns, and operates utility-scale solar, wind, and battery storage projects in the United States. profileCompanyName: Holtwood and Safe Harbor Hydroelectric Facilities

companyOverview: Holtwood and Safe Harbor are hydroelectric facilities on the lower Susquehanna River in Pennsylvania. Their output serves wholesale power markets and contracted clean-power buyers rather than retail utility customers directly. The assets are asset-heavy and long-lived because cash flow depends on regulated water rights, dam and generation infrastructure, and power sales from installed hydro capacity. Brookfield materials identify Holtwood and Safe Harbor as the first facilities covered under the company's 2025 hydro framework agreement with Google, and those materials cite combined capacity of roughly 670 megawatts. The principal operating footprint is in Pennsylvania. Brookfield Renewable acquired the remaining interest in Safe Harbor in 2014 and continues to own US hydro assets that include these facilities, although public sources reviewed do not disclose the current ownership percentages for the combined row.

companyOverview: HomeServe North America markets home-repair memberships, service-line plans, and emergency repair solutions for residential customers. Its principal operating footprint spans both countries, with a large installed base tied to utility-partner channels. companyOverview: Intel Arizona Joint Venture is the semiconductor-fabrication investment vehicle formed for new manufacturing capacity at Intel's Ocotillo campus in Chandler, Arizona. principalLocationOfOperations: More than 20 US states and the District of Columbia
ownershipDetails: Firm: Brookfield Asset Management | Fund: Brookfield Global Transition Fund | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Standard Solar develops, finances, owns, and operates distributed-generation and community-solar projects in the United States. Its customers include commercial, public-sector, community-solar, and utility counterparties that use or purchase electricity from contracted solar projects. The business is asset-heavy and contracted because value is tied to long-lived operating solar assets and development capabilities rather than equipment distribution. Company materials state that Standard Solar has operated since 2004 and has more than 500 megawatts owned, operated, and under construction across more than 20 states and the District of Columbia. Its principal operating footprint is in the United States. Brookfield announced the acquisition of Standard Solar in September 2022 through its transition strategy and committed additional growth capital alongside the transaction.
historicalMilestones: 2004: Company materials identify 2004 as the founding year.
2008: Standard Solar completed a major commercial rooftop project for the US Department of Energy.
2022-09-29: Brookfield announced the acquisition of Standard Solar and Scout Clean Energy and committed up to $2 billion of investment.
2024: Standard Solar marked 20 years of operations.
2025-2026: Company materials described more than 500 MW owned, operated, and under construction across more than 20 states and the District of Columbia.

principalLocationOfOperations: Multiple US states and Canadian provinces
ownershipDetails: Firm: Brookfield Renewable Partners | Fund: Brookfield Renewable Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: TerraForm Power owns and operates a portfolio of wind, solar, and storage assets. Its counterparties include utilities, municipalities, and other power buyers that contract for renewable-electricity output under long-term arrangements. The business is asset-heavy and largely contracted because cash flow depends on operating renewable-generation assets rather than equipment sales or development fees. Brookfield described TerraForm Power as a large listed renewable-power platform when it first assumed sponsorship in 2017 and later pursued full ownership through a merger completed in 2020. Its principal North American footprint is in the United States and Canada, with operations beyond the region as well. Brookfield first acquired a controlling stake and sponsorship position in 2017 and completed the take-private merger in July 2020.
historicalMilestones: 2014: TerraForm Power was formed as a publicly traded renewable-power company.
2017-03-07: Brookfield announced that it would acquire a controlling stake and assume sponsorship of TerraForm Power.
2020-01-13: Brookfield Renewable and TerraForm Power announced a definitive merger agreement.
2020-07-31: TerraForm Power closed its merger with Brookfield Renewable and was taken private.
2022-2026: Brookfield continued to report TerraForm as part of its global renewable-power portfolio.

ownershipDetails: Firm: NextEra Energy Transmission, LLC | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Trans Bay Cable owns and operates a high-voltage direct-current transmission link between Pittsburg and San Francisco, California. Its function is to provide transmission capacity into the San Francisco area rather than to generate power or provide retail utility service directly. The business is asset-heavy because value is tied to a long-lived submarine and underground transmission cable system and related transmission rights. Current company materials describe the system as a 53-mile cable that supplies approximately 40% of the electricity used daily in San Francisco and surrounding areas. Its principal operating footprint is in California. Public ownership records reviewed identify Trans Bay Cable as a subsidiary of NextEra Energy Transmission, which completed its acquisition of the asset in July 2019.
historicalMilestones: 2005: CAISO selected the Trans Bay Cable project as the transmission solution for the San Francisco area.
2010: The cable entered commercial service.
2019-07-16: NextEra Energy Transmission announced completion of its acquisition of Trans Bay Cable from SteelRiver Infrastructure Fund North America affiliates.
2024-2025: Company materials continued to describe the asset as a 53-mile HVDC transmission system serving the San Francisco market.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Brookfield Renewable Partners | Fund: Brookfield Renewable Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Urban Grid develops utility-scale solar and energy-storage projects in the United States. Its customers are utilities, cooperatives, and corporate off-takers that contract for power and capacity from projects that the company originates and advances through development. The business is development-led and asset-heavy because value is tied to land positions, interconnection rights, permitting, and the eventual construction and ownership of utility-scale projects. Brookfield announced in 2022 that the acquisition added more than 20,000 megawatts of development pipeline across 12 states. Its principal operating footprint is in the United States. Brookfield Renewable acquired Urban Grid in January 2022 and has since used it as one of its US development platforms.
historicalMilestones: 2010s: Urban Grid built a US utility-scale renewable development business.
2022-01-26: Brookfield Renewable announced the acquisition of Urban Grid.
2022: Brookfield stated that Urban Grid added more than 20,000 MW of development pipeline across 12 states.
2026-03-03: Brookfield identified Urban Grid assets as part of the seed portfolio for Northview Energy.

principalLocationOfOperations: Pennsylvania and multiple US and Canadian locations
ownershipDetails: Firm: Brookfield Renewable Partners and institutional partners | Fund: Brookfield Global Transition Fund I | Ownership Interest: 51%
Firm: Cameco Corporation | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 49%
companyOverview: Westinghouse Electric Company provides nuclear technology, fuel, equipment, and services to utilities and other nuclear-industry customers. Its customer base includes operators of existing nuclear fleets as well as buyers of reactor technology, outage services, maintenance, and fuel-related products. The business is asset- and technology-intensive because value depends on installed equipment, engineering capability, long-duration service relationships, and nuclear intellectual property. Public transaction materials describe Westinghouse as one of the largest nuclear-services businesses globally. Its operations span the United States and Canada as well as other international nuclear markets. Brookfield and Cameco completed the acquisition in November 2023, with Brookfield holding 51% through Brookfield Global Transition Fund I and associated institutional partners and Cameco holding 49%.
historicalMilestones: 1886: Westinghouse traces its origins to the original Westinghouse company founded in 1886.
2022-10-11: Cameco and Brookfield announced a strategic partnership to acquire Westinghouse.
2023-11-07: Cameco and Brookfield completed the acquisition of Westinghouse.
2023-11-07: Public materials stated that Brookfield would own 51% and Cameco would own 49%.
2024-2026: Westinghouse continued operating as a nuclear-technology and services platform under the new ownership structure.

ownershipDetails: Firm: CBRE Investment Management | Fund: CBRE Private Infrastructure Funds | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Accelerate Investment Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Accelerate Infrastructure Opportunities acquires and manages infrastructure ground leases in the United States. Its customers are infrastructure asset owners and operators that seek long-duration real-estate capital under transportation, utility, and communications sites rather than conventional corporate finance. The business model is long-duration and contract-based because value is tied to leased land interests and recurring rent streams. CBRE Investment Management reported in 2024 that the partnership had assembled a portfolio of more than 200 properties across 38 states since December 2022. Its principal operating footprint is in the United States. Accelerate and CBRE IM launched the partnership in 2023 to build a dedicated infrastructure ground-lease platform.
historicalMilestones: 2022-12: CBRE IM later reported that the platform began assembling assets in December 2022.
2023-03-30: Accelerate and CBRE IM announced the launch of their partnership to acquire infrastructure ground leases.
2024-10-29: CBRE IM announced that Accelerate Infrastructure Opportunities had raised $630 million from CBRE IM.
2024-10-29: CBRE IM stated that the platform had acquired more than 200 properties across 38 states.

ownershipDetails: Firm: CBRE Investment Management | Fund: CBRE Private Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: CitySwitch owns, develops, and operates wireless towers and specialized communications infrastructure in the United States. Its customers are wireless carriers, public-safety users, and other tenants that lease tower and site access rights for communications coverage and capacity. The business is asset-heavy and contracted because revenue is tied to long-duration site leases and tower tenancy rather than one-time equipment sales. Company materials state that the platform manages digital infrastructure deployment along major railroad rights of way and other specialized locations, and public descriptions cite access along extensive rail corridors. Its principal operating footprint is in the United States. CBRE Investment Management made a strategic acquisition and follow-on commitment in 2021 as part of its private infrastructure strategy.
historicalMilestones: 2004: Public company descriptions identify 2004 as CitySwitch's founding year.
2021: CBRE Investment Management made a strategic acquisition and follow-on investment in CitySwitch.
2025: CitySwitch completed a refinancing that included an inaugural investment-grade rated private placement, according to CBRE IM.
2025-2026: CitySwitch continued developing and operating communications infrastructure throughout the United States.

ownershipDetails: Firm: CBRE Investment Management | Fund: CBRE Private Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: ClearGen Holdings finances and owns distributed clean-energy projects in the United States. Its customers include project developers, commercial counterparties, and host-site users that require capital for solar, storage, EV charging, and related distributed infrastructure. The business is infrastructure-backed and contracted because it provides capital against long-duration project cash flows rather than selling hardware alone. CBRE IM stated in 2025 that ClearGen had financed more than 250 projects across 14 states. Its principal operating footprint is in the United States. CBRE IM announced the acquisition of ClearGen in August 2025 to expand its clean-energy infrastructure strategy.
historicalMilestones: 2020: CBRE IM stated that ClearGen was founded in 2020.
2020-2025: ClearGen built a distributed clean-energy financing platform across multiple US states.
2025-08-21: CBRE IM announced the acquisition of ClearGen Holdings.
2025-08-21: CBRE IM stated that ClearGen had financed more than 250 projects across 14 states.

ownershipDetails: Firm: ENGIE North America | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: CBRE Investment Management | Fund: CBRE Private Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: ENGIE Battery Storage Portfolio is a US stand-alone battery storage portfolio. Its revenues are tied to power-market services and contracted or structured storage arrangements in ERCOT and CAISO rather than to conventional generation output. The business is asset-heavy because value depends on owned battery installations and related operating systems. ENGIE and CBRE IM announced in 2025 that the partnership covered 2.4 gigawatts of battery storage assets in Texas and California, and ENGIE stated that it would retain the controlling share and continue to operate and manage the assets. Its principal operating footprint is in Texas and California. The partnership was announced in May 2025, and the parties did not disclose detailed ownership percentages in the public materials reviewed.
historicalMilestones: 2025-05-15: ENGIE and CBRE IM announced a partnership covering a 2.4 GW portfolio of battery storage assets in Texas and California.
2025-05-15: ENGIE stated that it would retain the controlling share and continue to operate and manage the portfolio.
2025: Public materials described the portfolio as one of the larger US stand-alone battery storage platforms.
2025-2026: The assets continued operating in ERCOT and CAISO under the new partnership structure.

profileSubsector: Zero-emission truck charging and fleet infrastructure
ownershipDetails: Firm: CBRE Investment Management | Fund: CBRE Private Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Homecoming Capital | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Forum Mobility develops and operates charging depots and related infrastructure for zero-emission heavy-duty trucking. Its customers are drayage fleets and logistics operators serving the California port complex and related freight corridors. The business is asset-heavy because it depends on truck-charging depots, power interconnections, and fleet infrastructure rather than software-only services. In 2023 the company announced a $400 million joint venture with CBRE IM and Homecoming Capital alongside a $15 million Series A financing. Its principal operating footprint is in California. CBRE IM became part of the ownership group through the 2023 joint venture, while public materials reviewed do not disclose the investors' respective ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: 2023: Forum Mobility announced a $400 million joint venture with CBRE IM and Homecoming Capital.
2023: The company also announced a $15 million Series A financing.
2024-2025: Forum continued building charging infrastructure for zero-emission trucking around California freight hubs.
2025-2026: The platform remained focused on heavy-duty charging and fleet electrification infrastructure.

ownershipDetails: Firm: CBRE Investment Management | Fund: CBRE Private Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Gateway Fiber builds and operates fiber-to-the-home broadband networks in the United States. Its customers are residential and small-business subscribers that purchase high-speed internet access over newly built fiber infrastructure. The business is asset-heavy because value depends on network construction, customer take-up, and recurring broadband subscriptions. Company materials state that Gateway Fiber was founded in 2019 and is based in Wright City, Missouri, while later transaction materials describe an expanded footprint after combining with other fiber platforms. Its principal operating footprint is in the United States. CBRE IM acquired Gateway Fiber in 2022 and announced a strategic merger of Gateway Fiber and WANRack in 2025.
historicalMilestones: 2019: Gateway Fiber was founded in Wright City, Missouri.
2022: CBRE Investment Management agreed to acquire Gateway Fiber.
2025: CBRE IM announced a strategic merger of Gateway Fiber and WANRack.
2025: The combined platform was described as serving communities across 25 states.

profileSubsector: Sustainable infrastructure investment and operating platform
ownershipDetails: Firm: CBRE Caledon | Fund: CBRE Caledon | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Generate Capital finances, owns, and operates sustainable infrastructure across clean energy, transportation, water, waste, agriculture, and digital infrastructure. Its customers and partners are project developers, technology companies, and asset operators that need long-duration capital and operating support rather than short-term project finance only. The business is infrastructure-backed because it invests directly in hard assets and long-lived contracted projects. Company materials state that Generate was founded in 2014 and has built thousands of infrastructure projects and investments globally. Its principal operating base is in the United States. CBRE Caledon participated in Generate's institutional equity base, and public materials reviewed do not disclose CBRE's current ownership percentage.
historicalMilestones: 2014-12-04: Generate Capital launched as a dedicated resource-efficiency and sustainable-infrastructure finance company.
2021-07-19: Generate announced a $2 billion equity raise from institutional investors.
2022-2025: Company materials described Generate as an investment and operating platform with thousands of projects and investments globally.
2025-2026: Generate continued financing and operating sustainable infrastructure across multiple sectors.

profileCompanyName: Vantage Data Centers Stabilized North America Portfolio
ownershipDetails: Firm: CBRE Caledon | Fund: CBRE Caledon | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: DigitalBridge | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Vantage Data Centers Stabilized North America Portfolio is a portfolio of operating data centers leased to hyperscale and enterprise customers. Its customers are cloud, technology, and large enterprise users that contract for powered shell, wholesale colocation, and related data-center capacity. The business is asset-heavy and contracted because value depends on owned campuses, critical-power infrastructure, and long-term customer leases. Transaction materials described the portfolio as 12 stabilized North American data centers totaling more than 1.4 million square feet and about 150 megawatts of IT capacity. Its principal operating footprint includes California, Washington, and Québec. Public transaction materials identified CBRE Caledon as part of the investor group acquiring an approximately 80% stake in the stabilized portfolio, while individual investors' ownership percentages were not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2020: Vantage and Colony Capital announced a strategic partnership involving a stabilized North American portfolio.
2020: Transaction materials described the portfolio as 12 stabilized data centers totaling more than 1.4 million square feet and about 150 MW of IT capacity.
2020: Public filings identified CBRE Caledon as one of the investors in the acquiring vehicle.
2024-2026: The stabilized portfolio continued operating in major North American data-center markets.

ownershipDetails: Firm: CBRE Investment Management | Fund: CBRE Private Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: WANRack management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: WANRack builds and operates fiber networks for schools, municipalities, enterprises, and other institutional users, and it has expanded into broader community broadband through acquisitions including KwiKom. Its customers buy lit fiber, dark fiber, and related connectivity services under recurring service arrangements rather than one-time equipment purchases. The business is asset-heavy because value depends on network construction, long-term contracts, and recurring bandwidth revenues. Company materials describe a continental US footprint and more than a decade of operating history, with a significant expansion in Kansas and other markets after the KwiKom transaction. Its principal operating footprint is in the United States. CBRE Caledon completed an investment in WANRack in 2021 and company materials later stated that management retained an equity stake.
historicalMilestones: 2021: CBRE Caledon completed an investment in WANRack.
2023: WANRack expanded through the KwiKom acquisition.
2025: CBRE IM announced the strategic merger of WANRack and Gateway Fiber.

ownershipDetails: Firm: CDPQ | Fund: CDPQ Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: 50%
Firm: Transurban | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 50%
companyOverview: A25 Concession operates a tolled road and bridge link serving the Montréal area. Its users are passenger and commercial vehicle customers that pay tolls for travel on the Autoroute 25 corridor and associated bridge infrastructure. The business is asset-heavy and concession-based because value depends on a long-lived toll asset, traffic demand, and contractual concession rights. CDPQ described the asset as a 7.2-kilometer network that includes a toll bridge and road infrastructure in Québec. Its principal operating footprint is in Québec. CDPQ acquired a 50% stake from Transurban in 2023, leaving the asset owned jointly by CDPQ and Transurban.
historicalMilestones: 2011: The A25 toll bridge and road concession entered service in the Montréal market.
2023-02-06: CDPQ announced an agreement to acquire 50% of the A25 concession from Transurban.
2023-02-28: Transaction advisers reported closing of the sale to CDPQ.
2023-2026: A25 continued operating as a tolled urban road and bridge asset in Québec.

ownershipDetails: Firm: CDPQ | Fund: CDPQ Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: 30%
Firm: The AES Corporation | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: AES Indiana is a regulated electric utility serving customers in the Indianapolis area. Its customer base consists of retail electricity users served through a regulated distribution and generation system rather than through competitive merchant power arrangements. The business is asset-heavy and rate-regulated because value is tied to utility infrastructure and approved investment recovery. AES filings state that CDPQ owns direct and indirect interests in IPALCO totaling approximately 30%. Its principal operating footprint is in Indiana. CDPQ established its utility partnership with AES through AES Indiana and later used the same model for AES Ohio, with AES remaining the operating sponsor.
historicalMilestones: 2001: AES acquired Indianapolis Power & Light, the predecessor utility business.
2021-2024: AES Indiana advanced a multiyear regulated investment program in the Indianapolis market.
2024-09-17: AES and CDPQ described AES Ohio as an expansion of their existing partnership at AES Indiana.
2026-03-20: AES's annual report stated that CDPQ owned direct and indirect interests in IPALCO totaling approximately 30%.

ownershipDetails: Firm: CDPQ | Fund: CDPQ Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: 30%
Firm: The AES Corporation | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: AES Ohio is a regulated electric utility serving customers in the Dayton region. Its customer base consists of regulated retail electricity users connected to local transmission and distribution infrastructure rather than competitive merchant-power buyers. The business is asset-heavy and rate-regulated because earnings depend on utility capital investment and authorized recovery mechanisms. AES stated in 2026 that CDPQ began owning an aggregate indirect equity interest of approximately 30% in AES Ohio in April 2025. Its principal operating footprint is in Ohio. AES and CDPQ announced the transaction in September 2024 as an extension of their utility partnership model, with AES remaining the operator.
historicalMilestones: 2024-09-17: AES and CDPQ announced an agreement for CDPQ to acquire a 30% indirect equity interest in AES Ohio.
2025-04: AES later disclosed that CDPQ began owning an aggregate indirect equity interest of approximately 30% in AES Ohio in April 2025.
2025-2026: AES Ohio continued operating as the regulated utility serving the Dayton market.
2026-03-20: AES reiterated the 30% CDPQ ownership position in its 2025 annual report.

ownershipDetails: Firm: CDPQ Infra | Fund: CDPQ Infra | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Alto is the high-speed rail project selected to connect Toronto and Québec City. Its counterparties are public-sector sponsors and future rail users rather than conventional utility or commodity customers. The business is development-stage and infrastructure-heavy because value depends on route design, civil works, rolling stock, and long-term concession or project-delivery arrangements. Public materials describe the corridor at roughly 1,000 kilometers across Ontario and Québec. Its principal operating footprint is the Toronto-Québec City corridor. CDPQ Infra leads the Cadence consortium selected to develop the project, and public materials reviewed do not disclose detailed ownership percentages for consortium participants.
historicalMilestones: 2025: Public authorities selected the Cadence consortium, led by CDPQ Infra, to develop the Toronto-Québec City high-speed rail project.
2025-2026: Project materials described a corridor of roughly 1,000 kilometers.
2026: Alto branding was adopted for the high-speed rail project.
2026: CDPQ Infra continued overseeing project development through the Cadence consortium.

ownershipDetails: Firm: CDPQ | Fund: CDPQ Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: 45%
Firm: DP World | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 55%
companyOverview: DP World Canada operates marine terminals and related logistics infrastructure in Canada. Its customers include ocean carriers, importers, exporters, and supply-chain intermediaries that require terminal, stevedoring, and logistics services. The business is asset-heavy because value depends on port terminals, marine logistics assets, and throughput volumes. Public descriptions of the platform cite operations in Vancouver, Nanaimo, Prince Rupert, and Saint John. Its principal operating footprint is in British Columbia and New Brunswick. DP World and CDPQ have disclosed a 55/45 ownership split in their ports-and-terminals investment platform, which includes Canadian terminal assets operated by DP World Canada.
historicalMilestones: 2016: DP World Canada expanded its Canadian footprint through the acquisition of Fraser Surrey Docks.
2019-2021: Public reporting identified CDPQ as holding a 45% stake in DP World Canada.

Firm: AtkinsRéalis | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 33.3%

companyOverview: InTransit BC is the concessionaire for the Canada Line rapid transit system in Metro Vancouver. Its customers are transit users and the public-sector transit authority that relies on the concessionaire for operations and maintenance performance. The business is asset-heavy and concession-based because value depends on a long-term contract tied to a rail system rather than on merchant demand alone. CDPQ materials describe the Canada Line as a 19.5-kilometer public-transit line under a 35-year concession. Its principal operating footprint is in British Columbia. CDPQ acquired a 33.3% ownership interest in InTransit BC in 2016, leaving the asset jointly owned with the other original equity partners.

2016: CDPQ announced the acquisition of a 33.3% ownership interest in InTransit BC.
2016: Public materials identified the remaining ownership interests as held by the other two equity partners.

ownershipDetails: Firm: CDPQ | Fund: CDPQ Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: 15%
Firm: IFM Global Infrastructure Fund | Fund: IFM Global Infrastructure Fund | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Indiana Toll Road operates the tolled east-west highway corridor across northern Indiana. Its users are passenger and freight vehicles that pay tolls for access to a major interstate transportation route. The business is asset-heavy and concession-based because value depends on a long-lived roadway asset, traffic demand, and concession rights rather than contracted utility-style revenues. Company materials describe the corridor as 157 miles in length. Its principal operating footprint is in Indiana. CDPQ acquired a 15% interest from IFM Investors in 2021, while IFM Global Infrastructure Fund retained a majority position that was described publicly as more than 70% but not specified exactly in the materials reviewed.

oneSentenceDescription: Innergex Renewable Energy is a leading Canadian independent renewable power producer operating a diversified portfolio of approximately 4.2 GW of net installed capacity across hydroelectric, wind, solar, and battery storage assets in Canada, the United States, France, and Chile.
profileSubsector: Hydro, wind, solar, and energy storage
ownershipDetails: Firm: CDPQ | Fund: CDPQ Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Innergex Renewable Energy develops, owns, and operates hydroelectric, wind, solar, and storage assets. Its counterparties include utilities, public entities, and corporate off-takers that buy renewable electricity and related capacity under long-term arrangements. The business is asset-heavy and largely contracted because cash flow depends on operating renewable assets and power-purchase agreements. Company materials in 2026 describe 89 facilities in operation and 4,411 megawatts of gross installed capacity across Canada, the United States, France, and Chile. Its principal North American operating footprint is in Canada and the United States. Innergex entered into a definitive agreement with CDPQ in February 2025 and announced completion of the acquisition by La Caisse in July 2025, while public materials reviewed do not disclose the ownership percentages of the broader post-closing investor group.

ownershipDetails: Firm: CDPQ | Fund: CDPQ Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
