Its customers include U.S. government agencies and other counterparties that require ocean transport, liner shipping, vessel management, stevedoring, and related logistics services. The operating model is asset-heavy and contracted because it depends on vessel operations, technical management, and logistics capabilities supported by charter and service arrangements. Public transaction materials describe a fleet of nine vessels operating under long-term U.S. government time charters and related commercial contracts, with headquarters in Annapolis, Maryland. Institutional investors advised by J.P. Morgan Asset Management acquired Bold Ocean from NOVA Infrastructure in July 2024, while financial terms and the detailed ownership split were not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2020: NOVA Infrastructure partnered with founders to build the Bold Ocean platform.
2024-07-31: NOVA announced the sale of Bold Ocean to institutional investors advised by J.P. Morgan Asset Management.
2024-08-05: Maritime trade press described Bold Ocean as operating nine vessels under long-term U.S. government charters.

ownershipDetails: Firm: J.P. Morgan Asset Management | Fund: Infrastructure Investments Fund (IIF) | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: El Paso Electric Company is a regulated electric utility providing generation, transmission, and distribution services in west Texas and southern New Mexico. Its customer base includes residential, commercial, industrial, and public-sector electricity users across a 10,000 square mile service territory. The operating model is asset-heavy and regulated because the business owns utility infrastructure and earns returns primarily through regulated utility frameworks rather than merchant power sales alone. Company materials state that it serves about 465,000 customers from Hatch, New Mexico to Van Horn, Texas and traces its history back to 1901. Infrastructure Investments Fund, advised by J.P. Morgan Asset Management, completed the acquisition of El Paso Electric in July 2020, while the current ownership percentages were not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 1901: El Paso Electric traces its history to 1901.
2020-02-24: El Paso Electric shareholders approved the sale to Infrastructure Investments Fund.
2020-07-29: El Paso Electric announced the closing of the acquisition by Infrastructure Investments Fund.

ownershipDetails: Firm: J.P. Morgan Asset Management | Fund: Infrastructure Investments Fund (IIF) | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Enstor Gas owns and operates underground natural gas storage and related transmission infrastructure in the United States. Its customers include utilities, marketers, pipeline operators, industrial users, LNG-linked counterparties, and other shippers seeking storage and deliverability services. The operating model is asset-heavy and fee-based because the business relies on storage caverns, reservoirs, pipeline interconnects, and contracted commercial arrangements rather than commodity production. Public disclosures state that Enstor operates six active underground storage facilities in Alabama, Mississippi, Texas, and New Mexico with more than 110 Bcf of working gas capacity, about 179 miles of transmission pipeline, and 39 interconnects. Infrastructure Investments Fund, advised by J.P. Morgan Asset Management, acquired Enstor from ArcLight in May 2022, and the detailed ownership percentages were not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2022-05-11: Enstor announced its acquisition by Infrastructure Investments Fund from ArcLight.
2022-05-11: Enstor disclosed six active underground storage facilities in four states with more than 110 Bcf of working gas capacity.
2025-08-19: Enstor announced an agreement to acquire Black Bear Transmission as a downstream expansion of its platform.

ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Nexus Water Group is a regulated water and wastewater utility platform formed through the combination of Corix Infrastructure's US regulated water and wastewater business and SouthWest Water Company. The platform serves residential, commercial, and municipal utility customers and is structured around regulated networks rather than uncontracted commodity exposure. Its operating model is asset-heavy because cash flows are tied to owned distribution, collection, and treatment infrastructure across numerous local systems. Public launch materials described a multi-state footprint, and later company announcements referenced assets in at least eight states that were subject to a 2025 sale agreement with American Water. Operations are centered in multiple US states, with the company headquartered in Sugar Land, Texas. The merger that created Nexus closed on April 1, 2024, but the current ownership structure and ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2024-04-01: Corix Infrastructure's U.S. regulated water and wastewater business and SouthWest Water completed their merger to create Nexus Water Group.
2024-04-02: Nexus Water Group formally announced the closing of the merger.
2025-05-19: Nexus announced a definitive agreement to sell operations and assets in eight states to American Water.
2026-02-25: California Water Service Group announced an agreement to purchase Nexus systems in Nevada and Oregon.

ownershipDetails: Firm: J.P. Morgan Asset Management | Fund: Infrastructure Investments Fund (IIF) | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Nieuport Aviation owns and operates the passenger terminal at Billy Bishop Toronto City Airport. Its users are airline passengers and airport tenants that depend on the terminal for airside access, passenger processing, and commercial services. The operating model is asset-heavy and concession-based because value is derived from long-term airport terminal rights, passenger traffic, and associated infrastructure revenues. Company materials state that Billy Bishop serves more than 2.8 million passengers annually and that Nieuport invested more than $50 million in the terminal during its first four years of ownership. A consortium acquired the terminal from Porter Aviation Holdings in January 2015, and institutional investors advised by J.P. Morgan Asset Management agreed in 2019 to consolidate ownership by purchasing the interests of the other consortium partners.
historicalMilestones: 2015-01: A consortium acquired the passenger terminal at Billy Bishop Toronto City Airport from Porter Aviation Holdings.
2019-01-25: Nieuport announced that institutional investors advised by J.P. Morgan Asset Management had agreed to consolidate ownership.
2021-04-13: Nieuport announced completion of a terminal upgrade program including additional gate capacity.

ownershipDetails: Firm: J.P. Morgan Asset Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Onward Energy owns and operates a diversified portfolio of wind, solar, and natural gas generation assets in the United States. Its customers and end markets include utilities, load-serving entities, and wholesale power markets that require reliable thermal capacity and renewable generation. The operating model is asset-heavy because the company owns and manages a fleet of operating plants with a mix of contracted and market-based revenue exposure rather than only developing projects for sale. Current company materials describe 57 generation projects in 22 states and more than 6.7 GW of operating capacity following the 2025 Griffith acquisition. Onward Energy was formed in 2021 through the combination of Novatus Energy and Southwest Generation, and company materials state that it is majority-owned by institutional investors advised by J.P. Morgan Asset Management.
historicalMilestones: 2021-01-12: Onward Energy was formed through the combination of Novatus Energy and Southwest Generation.
2025-01-06: Onward Energy completed the acquisition of Griffith Power Plant.
2025: Company materials stated that Onward had 57 projects in 22 states and that it was majority-owned by investors advised by J.P. Morgan Asset Management.

ownershipDetails: Firm: J.P. Morgan Asset Management | Fund: Infrastructure Investments Fund (IIF) | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: South Jersey Industries is an energy infrastructure holding company whose principal businesses include regulated natural gas utilities in New Jersey. Its customer base includes residential, commercial, and industrial gas utility users served by South Jersey Gas and Elizabethtown Gas. The operating model is asset-heavy and regulated because earnings are primarily tied to regulated distribution and transmission networks rather than merchant commodity trading. Public company materials describe more than 700,000 utility customers and roughly 10,000 miles of distribution and transmission pipeline across its gas utility operations. SJI traces its roots to 1910, and Infrastructure Investments Fund, advised by J.P. Morgan Asset Management, completed the acquisition of the company in February 2023 after announcing the transaction in 2022.
historicalMilestones: 1910: SJI traces its roots to Atlantic City Gas and Water Company.
2022-02-24: South Jersey Industries entered into an agreement to be acquired by Infrastructure Investments Fund.
2023-02-01: Infrastructure Investments Fund completed the acquisition of South Jersey Industries.

ownershipDetails: Firm: J.P. Morgan Asset Management | Fund: Infrastructure Investments Fund (IIF) | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Summit Utilities owns and operates regulated natural gas distribution utilities in the central United States. Its customer base consists primarily of residential, commercial, and industrial gas users served through local distribution systems in Arkansas, Oklahoma, Texas, and Missouri. The operating model is asset-heavy and regulated, with earnings supported by state-approved utility rates and ongoing investment in pipeline, safety, and customer-service infrastructure. Public company materials indicate that Summit serves about 623,000 customers and operates more than 22,000 miles of pipeline across its utility territories. Reviewed public materials describe the company as owned by institutional investors advised by J.P. Morgan Asset Management, and KeyBanc disclosed that Summit was a portfolio company of J.P. Morgan Infrastructure Investments Fund when it acquired additional gas distribution assets in 2022.
historicalMilestones: 2022-01-13: KeyBanc disclosed Summit Utilities' acquisition of Arkansas and Oklahoma gas distribution assets from CenterPoint Energy as a portfolio company of J.P. Morgan Infrastructure Investments Fund.
2022: Summit's sustainability report described the integration of acquired Arkansas and Oklahoma utility assets into the broader Summit platform.
2024: Summit reported serving approximately 623,000 customers and operating more than 22,000 miles of pipeline across its utility territories.

profileSubsector: Offshore midstream pipelines, processing, and floating production
principalLocationOfOperations: Louisiana; Mississippi; Offshore Gulf of Mexico
ownershipDetails: Firm: J.P. Morgan Asset Management | Fund: Infrastructure Investments Fund (IIF) | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Third Coast Infrastructure owns and operates offshore-focused midstream assets serving Gulf of Mexico oil and natural gas production. Its customers are upstream producers that require gathering, transportation, processing, and related infrastructure services to move hydrocarbons to market. The operating model is asset-heavy and fee-based, built around pipelines, processing assets, and floating production infrastructure rather than an asset-light services platform. Public materials describe operations concentrated along the Gulf Coast and in deepwater Gulf of Mexico producing regions, including natural gas and crude pipelines, gas processing assets, NGL infrastructure, and a floating production system. Reviewed public materials indicate that J.P. Morgan Asset Management's infrastructure team has investment responsibilities for Third Coast, while the precise current ownership percentage was not publicly disclosed in the reviewed sources.
historicalMilestones: 2021-06-07: Third Coast announced the sale of an approximately 50% interest in Lighthouse Super Holdings to an affiliate of a global infrastructure investment vehicle.
2021: Third Coast disclosed that Lighthouse held the company's offshore-focused midstream assets and a 50% interest in King's Quay Infrastructure.
2024: Third Coast's public website continued to describe an offshore-focused midstream platform spanning pipelines, processing assets, and floating production infrastructure in the Gulf.

oneSentenceDescription: AEP Transmission Company is a subsidiary of American Electric Power focused on developing, owning, and operating high-voltage transmission infrastructure across the United States.
ownershipDetails: Firm: KKR | Fund: KKR Core Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: PSP Investments | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: AEP Transmission Company develops, owns, and operates high-voltage electric transmission infrastructure through transmission-only utility subsidiaries. Its end markets are electric load-serving utilities, wholesale power networks, and end users that depend on reliable grid expansion and interconnection capacity. The operating model is asset-heavy and regulated, with returns tied to transmission rate base growth and FERC-regulated utility frameworks rather than merchant power sales. Public disclosures for the 2025 transaction describe the investment as covering AEP's Ohio and Indiana Michigan transmission companies, which support AEP's large utility footprint and capital plan. AEP announced in January 2025, and closed in June 2025, a 19.9% equity investment in these transmission companies by KKR and PSP Investments through a joint strategic partnership.
historicalMilestones: 2012: AEP Transmission Company was formed as a transmission-focused utility platform within American Electric Power.
2025-01-09: AEP announced a definitive agreement for KKR and PSP Investments to acquire a 19.9% equity interest in its Ohio and Indiana Michigan transmission companies.
2025-06-05: AEP, KKR, and PSP Investments announced closing of the $2.82 billion transmission investment partnership.

oneSentenceDescription: Altavair is a global aircraft leasing platform that acquires, manages, and leases commercial aircraft to airlines worldwide.
principalLocationOfOperations: Global operations; Dublin, Ireland; Seattle, Washington
ownershipDetails: Firm: KKR | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Altavair is an aircraft leasing and aviation finance platform that acquires, manages, finances, and leases commercial aircraft to airlines and other aviation counterparties globally. Its customers include passenger and cargo airlines, lessors, and aviation market participants seeking fleet capacity, structured leasing solutions, and asset management support. The operating model is asset-heavy and contracted, with capital deployed into aircraft and financing structures that generate lease and related income over the life of the assets. Public company materials state that the platform manages more than 200 aircraft with portfolio value above $10 billion across the global leased aircraft market. KKR formed a strategic partnership with Altavair in 2018 and Altavair announced in January 2026 that KKR had agreed to increase its ownership stake in the platform.
historicalMilestones: 2014: Altavair was founded.
2018: Altavair formed a long-term strategic partnership with KKR.
2026-01-19: Altavair announced that KKR had agreed to increase its ownership stake in the platform.

oneSentenceDescription: Atlantic Aviation is one of the largest fixed-base operator (FBO) networks in the United States, providing fueling, hangar, and ground handling services at approximately 100 airports.
ownershipDetails: Firm: KKR | Fund: KKR Global Infrastructure Investors IV | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Atlantic Aviation operates a national network of fixed-base operations that provide aircraft fueling, hangar, ramp, passenger, and ground-handling services. Its customer base is concentrated in business aviation, general aviation, and aircraft operators that require near-terminal service infrastructure at major U.S. airports. The operating model is asset-heavy, supported by airport leaseholds, facilities, hangars, and service operations that generate recurring aviation-services revenue. Public company materials describe more than 100 U.S. locations and a broad domestic footprint spanning business and private aviation markets. Macquarie Infrastructure Corporation agreed to sell Atlantic Aviation to KKR in 2021, and Atlantic later announced a combination with Ross Aviation backed by KKR and KSL Capital.
historicalMilestones: 2021-06-07: Macquarie Infrastructure Corporation and KKR announced an agreement for KKR to acquire Atlantic Aviation for $4.475 billion.
2021-11-16: Atlantic Aviation and Ross Aviation announced an agreement to combine their FBO networks with investment from KKR and KSL Capital.
2026: Atlantic continued to report a national FBO network with more than 100 U.S. locations.

oneSentenceDescription: Avantus (formerly 8minute Solar Energy) is a leading solar and energy storage developer in the United States with a development pipeline of over 18 GW.
principalLocationOfOperations: California; Arizona; Nevada; Western United States
ownershipDetails: Firm: KKR | Fund: KKR Global Climate Strategy | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: EIG | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Avantus develops utility-scale solar and battery storage projects in the United States. Its end markets include utilities, corporate offtakers, and wholesale power markets seeking new renewable generation and storage capacity. The operating model is development-led and asset-heavy, with value created through siting, permitting, financing, construction, and monetization or ownership of large power projects. Public materials describe a 30 GW-plus pipeline concentrated in the Western United States, with solar projects often paired with large storage components. KKR announced a majority investment in March 2024 and Avantus stated in July 2024 that KKR and EIG were the sole equity investors following the close.
historicalMilestones: 2009: The business that became Avantus was founded as 8minute Solar Energy.
2024-03-20: KKR announced an agreement to acquire a majority interest in Avantus.
2024-07-31: Avantus announced completion of the KKR transaction and stated that KKR and EIG were the sole equity investors.

oneSentenceDescription: CarbonCount Holdings 1 is a KKR platform company focused on investing in sustainable infrastructure assets that deliver measurable carbon reduction outcomes.
ownershipDetails: Firm: KKR | Fund: KKR Core Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc. | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: CarbonCount Holdings 1 is a sustainable infrastructure investment vehicle that provides capital to clean energy and climate-positive projects in the United States. Its end markets include developers and owners of renewable, efficiency, and other sustainable infrastructure assets that require long-duration capital solutions. The operating model is capital-intensive but portfolio based, deploying equity and leverage into infrastructure investments rather than running a single operating asset platform. Public disclosures state that the HASI-KKR partnership had closed nearly $3 billion of investment commitments across multiple sustainable infrastructure asset classes through November 2025 and had increased total investment capacity to nearly $5 billion. HASI and KKR formed CCH1 in 2024 and announced an additional $1 billion of capital commitments in December 2025.
historicalMilestones: 2024-05: CCH1 was formed as a strategic partnership between HASI and KKR to invest in sustainable infrastructure projects in the United States.
2025-06-09: CCH1 announced a $592 million senior unsecured notes issuance and disclosed increased investment capacity of $2.6 billion.
2025-12-15: HASI and KKR announced an additional $1 billion of capital commitments, bringing total investment capacity to nearly $5 billion.

oneSentenceDescription: Coastal GasLink is a 670-kilometer natural gas pipeline running from the Dawson Creek area in northeastern British Columbia to the LNG Canada export facility in Kitimat.
ownershipDetails: Firm: TC Energy | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 35%
Firm: KKR | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Alberta Investment Management Corporation (AIMCo) | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Coastal GasLink is a long-haul natural gas transmission pipeline connecting northeastern British Columbia supply to the LNG Canada export facility near Kitimat. Its end market is LNG export and related upstream gas production, with transportation services supporting feedgas delivery from the Western Canadian Sedimentary Basin. The operating model is asset-heavy and infrastructure based, with value derived from long-life pipeline transportation under contracted or utility-style tolling arrangements rather than asset-light services. Public materials describe an approximately 670-kilometre pipeline across northern British Columbia that entered commercial service and is positioned to support LNG Canada. TC Energy announced in 2019 that it would sell a combined 65% equity interest in the project to KKR and AIMCo while retaining 35%, and 2024 Coastal GasLink materials continued to describe the project as owned by affiliates of those three parties.
historicalMilestones: 2012: TC Energy was selected to design, build, own, and operate the Coastal GasLink pipeline.
2019-12-26: TC Energy announced the sale of a combined 65% equity interest in Coastal GasLink to KKR and AIMCo.
2024-06-13: Coastal GasLink announced completion of a major corporate bond offering and described the project as owned by affiliates of TC Energy, KKR, and AIMCo.

oneSentenceDescription: Cordia Energy, formerly Clearway Community Energy, is a district energy services provider that owns and operates thermal infrastructure systems serving commercial, institutional, and government buildings.
profileSubsector: District energy and on-site energy systems
principalLocationOfOperations: Massachusetts; Pennsylvania; Colorado; Arizona; Ohio; Maryland; New York
ownershipDetails: Firm: KKR | Fund: KKR Core Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Cordia develops, owns, and operates district energy, on-site energy, and energy-as-a-service systems for commercial, institutional, and public-sector customers. Its customers include campuses, hospitals, municipalities, federal users, and other large-load facilities seeking thermal, power, resiliency, and decarbonization solutions. The operating model is asset-heavy and long duration, with revenues supported by owned utility-like systems, service contracts, and long-term customer relationships. Public company materials describe a portfolio of district energy and distributed energy systems across multiple U.S. urban markets and emphasize long-term ownership and operation of the underlying infrastructure. KKR acquired the business from Clearway in 2022 for approximately $1.9 billion and the platform now operates under the Cordia brand.
historicalMilestones: 2022: KKR acquired Clearway Community Energy in a transaction valued at approximately $1.9 billion.
2022: The business was rebranded as Cordia and appointed Earl Collins as chief executive officer.
2024: Cordia's public materials described a broadened offering spanning district energy, distributed energy, and energy-as-a-service systems.

oneSentenceDescription: CyrusOne is a premier global data center developer and operator with over 50 facilities across North America and Europe, providing mission-critical colocation, hyperscale, and build-to-suit data center services.
ownershipDetails: Firm: KKR | Fund: KKR Global Infrastructure Investors | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Global Infrastructure Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: CyrusOne designs, builds, and operates data centers for hyperscale cloud, enterprise, and network customers. Its customers use the platform for mission-critical colocation, wholesale, and build-to-suit deployments that require large amounts of reliable power and connectivity. The operating model is asset-heavy and contracted, centered on long-lived data center campuses, utility power procurement, and multi-year customer commitments. Public company materials indicate that the business operates more than 60 data center facilities in nine countries and has more than 50 additional data centers in development. Funds managed by KKR and Global Infrastructure Partners completed the take-private acquisition of CyrusOne in March 2022.
historicalMilestones: 2001: CyrusOne was founded.
2022-03-25: KKR and Global Infrastructure Partners completed the $15 billion acquisition of CyrusOne.
2025: CyrusOne's public materials described more than 60 operational data centers and more than 50 data centers in development.

oneSentenceDescription: Depot Connect International is a leading provider of intermodal container depot services in North America, offering container storage, maintenance, repair, and modification services.
profileSubsector: Tank container depot and logistics infrastructure
ownershipDetails: Firm: KKR | Fund: KKR Global Infrastructure Investors IV | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Depot Connect International provides tank container cleaning, storage, maintenance, transportation, and related depot services. Its customers include chemical, industrial, and logistics users that rely on intermodal tank container infrastructure and ancillary maintenance services. The operating model combines owned and leased depot infrastructure with recurring service revenue from cleaning, storage, repair, transportation, and parts distribution activities. Public company materials describe an expanding network of more than 190 locations across the United States, Canada, and Europe. KKR acquired Boasso Global in 2022 and has used Depot Connect International as a platform for growth in the intermodal tank container and related logistics-services market.
historicalMilestones: 2021: Depot Connect International was formed as a growth platform in tank container services.
2022: DCI disclosed that Apax agreed to sell Boasso Global to KKR Global Infrastructure Investors IV.
2025: DCI's public materials described a network of more than 190 locations across six countries.

oneSentenceDescription: Metronet is a fiber-to-the-premise broadband provider delivering high-speed internet, TV, and phone services to residential and business customers across the Midwest and Southeast United States.
ownershipDetails: Firm: KKR | Fund: KKR Global Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: T-Mobile | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Metronet builds and operates fiber broadband networks that provide internet and related connectivity services to residential, business, and wholesale customers. Its end markets include households, enterprises, and service providers seeking fiber-to-the-premise access in underserved and expanding U.S. communities. The operating model is asset-heavy and infrastructure based, requiring substantial capital to construct and maintain local fiber networks while generating recurring subscription and wholesale revenues. Public company materials describe Metronet as one of the largest independent fiber broadband providers in the United States and note that it has been building and operating 100% fiber networks since 2005. T-Mobile and KKR completed a joint venture acquisition of Metronet in July 2025 and stated that the platform would continue operating fiber networks on a commercial and wholesale basis with T-Mobile Fiber leading residential marketing and customer experience.
historicalMilestones: 2005: Metronet began building and operating fiber networks in the United States.
2024-07-24: T-Mobile and KKR announced a joint venture to acquire Metronet.
2025-07-24: Metronet announced completion of the joint venture acquisition by T-Mobile and KKR.

oneSentenceDescription: Neon Renewables is a renewable energy platform that owns and operates a diversified portfolio of wind and solar generation assets across the United States.
profileSubsector: Contracted wind and solar generation portfolio
ownershipDetails: Firm: KKR | Fund: KKR Diversified Core Infrastructure Fund | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Neon Renewables is a KKR-backed vehicle associated with investments in contracted U.S. renewable energy assets. Its end markets are utility and other contracted offtakers purchasing wind and solar generation from operating projects. The operating model is asset-heavy and infrastructure based, with cash flows linked to ownership interests in renewable generation assets rather than an asset-light services business. Public filings related to KKR's transactions with NextEra Energy entities identify Neon as part of the investment structure used for a portfolio of contracted U.S. renewable energy assets, but detailed operating information on the standalone vehicle was not publicly disclosed in the reviewed sources. KKR's infrastructure team has publicly identified Neon Renewables as one of its portfolio investments, while additional details on current ownership percentages and portfolio composition were not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2020-11-02: NextEra Energy entities and KKR announced transactions covering portfolios of contracted U.S. renewable energy assets.
2020: Public transaction documentation identified Neon-related investment vehicles within the KKR acquisition structure.
2025: KKR's public personnel and portfolio materials continued to reference Neon Renewables as an infrastructure investment.

oneSentenceDescription: Nitrogen Renewables is a renewable energy platform focused on developing and operating wind energy projects across the United States.
profileSubsector: Wind and solar portfolio joint venture
ownershipDetails: Firm: KKR | Fund: KKR Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: NextEra Energy Partners | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Nitrogen Renewables is a KKR-related renewable energy holding structure associated with a portfolio of contracted wind and solar assets. Its end markets are utilities and other contracted power buyers purchasing generation from operating renewable projects. The operating model is asset-heavy, with returns supported by ownership of long-lived wind and solar assets and related contracted cash flows. Public information reviewed indicates that Nitrogen was part of a joint venture structure with NextEra Energy Partners covering seven wind projects and three solar projects, but more detailed public operating disclosures on the standalone vehicle were limited. KKR has publicly identified Nitrogen Renewables as one of its infrastructure investments, while precise current ownership percentages were not publicly disclosed in the reviewed sources.
historicalMilestones: 2019: Public transaction documents referenced Nitrogen-related entities within KKR's renewable investment structure.
2020-11-02: NextEra Energy entities and KKR announced transactions covering contracted renewable portfolios that included Nitrogen-related structures.
2025: KKR's public team materials continued to reference Nitrogen Renewables as an infrastructure investment.

oneSentenceDescription: Pembina Gas Infrastructure is a joint venture between Pembina Pipeline Corporation and KKR that owns and operates natural gas processing and fractionation infrastructure in western Canada.
profileSubsector: Natural gas processing, transportation, and NGL services
ownershipDetails: Firm: Pembina Pipeline Corporation | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 60%
Firm: KKR | Fund: KKR Global Infrastructure Funds | Ownership Interest: 40%
companyOverview: Pembina Gas Infrastructure is a Western Canadian gas processing and midstream platform providing natural gas and natural gas liquids processing, transportation, and related services. Its customers are upstream producers in the Montney and Duvernay regions that need processing and takeaway capacity for natural gas and liquids production. The operating model is asset-heavy and fee-based, supported by large processing and transportation infrastructure with long-life industrial use and recurring customer throughput. Public company materials describe combined processing capacity of 5 billion cubic feet per day and a footprint extending from central Alberta to northeast British Columbia. Pembina and KKR formed the joint venture in 2022, with Pembina retaining 60% ownership and operating control and KKR's global infrastructure funds holding the remaining 40%.
historicalMilestones: 2022-03-01: Pembina and KKR announced agreements to combine their Western Canadian processing assets into Pembina Gas Infrastructure.
2022: Pembina announced closing of the PGI transaction and disclosed the 60% Pembina and 40% KKR ownership split.
2025: Public materials continued to describe PGI as a major Western Canadian gas processing entity with 5 Bcf/d of capacity.

oneSentenceDescription: Port Arthur LNG is a large-scale LNG export facility under development in Port Arthur, Texas, with an initial planned capacity of approximately 13.5 MTPA.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Sempra Infrastructure | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 58%
Firm: KKR | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 42%
companyOverview: Port Arthur LNG is a liquefied natural gas export project under development on the U.S. Gulf Coast in Jefferson County, Texas. Its customer base consists of global LNG buyers, gas suppliers, and counterparties seeking long-term liquefaction capacity and U.S. Gulf Coast export access. The operating model is asset-heavy and project based, centered on large liquefaction trains, storage tanks, marine infrastructure, and long-term commercial contracts. Public project materials describe a four-train, two-phase facility with approximately 26 million tonnes per annum of nameplate capacity across Phases 1 and 2 and characterize the development as a $27 billion energy infrastructure investment. Sempra Infrastructure announced in September 2023 that it had completed the sale of a 42% indirect non-controlling interest in Port Arthur LNG Phase 1 to KKR after the project reached final investment decision.
historicalMilestones: 2023-03: Port Arthur LNG Phase 1 reached final investment decision.
2023-09-12: Sempra Infrastructure completed the sale of a 42% indirect non-controlling interest in Port Arthur LNG Phase 1 to KKR.
2025-09: Public materials described KKR's participation in an additional investment related to Port Arthur LNG Phase 2.

oneSentenceDescription: Sempra Infrastructure Partners is a platform subsidiary of Sempra focused on developing, building, and operating energy infrastructure in North America, including LNG export facilities, renewable generation, and cross-border energy networks.
profileSubsector: LNG export, gas infrastructure, and low-carbon energy infrastructure
ownershipDetails: Firm: Sempra | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 70%
Firm: KKR | Fund: KKR-managed investment vehicles | Ownership Interest: 20%
Firm: Abu Dhabi Investment Authority | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 10%
companyOverview: Sempra Infrastructure develops and operates LNG, energy networks, and lower-carbon infrastructure across North America. Its customer base includes utilities, LNG buyers, industrial counterparties, and other energy-market participants, and its end markets span export, gas transportation, and related energy services. The operating model is asset-heavy and long duration, combining large infrastructure assets with a mix of contracted services and commodity-linked activity. Public disclosures describe the business as one of North America's larger private energy infrastructure platforms, anchored by LNG export, pipeline, storage, and related assets in Texas, Louisiana, and Mexico. Sempra completed the sale of a 30% non-controlling interest in Sempra Infrastructure to KKR and ADIA in 2022, while a later September 2025 transaction announcement would further change ownership only after a future closing.
historicalMilestones: 2021-04-05: Sempra announced an agreement to sell a 20% equity interest in Sempra Infrastructure Partners to KKR.
2021-12: Sempra announced the sale of an additional 10% non-controlling interest to ADIA.
2022-06-01: Sempra completed the 30% combined sale, establishing the 70% Sempra, 20% KKR, and 10% ADIA ownership structure.
2025-09-23: Sempra announced a further transaction with a KKR-led consortium and CPP Investments, subject to closing.

oneSentenceDescription: Sol Systems is a national solar energy firm specializing in the development, financing, and management of distributed and community solar projects.
profileSubsector: Solar development, ownership, and energy-market services
ownershipDetails: Firm: KKR | Fund: KKR Global Infrastructure Investors III | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Sol Systems develops, finances, owns, and operates solar and storage projects in the United States. Its customers and counterparties include corporations, municipalities, utilities, and other organizations seeking clean power procurement, renewable energy credits, and project financing solutions. The operating model combines development, long-term ownership, environmental commodity management, and power marketing, making it more asset-heavy than a pure advisory platform. Public company materials state that Sol develops and finances renewable projects across the United States and has used KKR's capital commitment to scale utility-scale solar and storage deployment. KKR made a strategic minority investment in Sol Systems in 2021 and the companies announced a partnership with up to $1 billion of committed capital for project development and acquisition.
historicalMilestones: 2008: Sol Systems was founded.
2021-07-22: Sol Systems and KKR announced a strategic partnership with up to $1 billion of committed capital.
2025-07-16: Sol Systems announced a $675 million revolving construction financing facility to support its project pipeline.

oneSentenceDescription: The Parking Spot is the largest near-airport parking company in the United States, operating over 30 off-airport parking facilities at major airports.
ownershipDetails: Firm: KKR | Fund: KKR Global Infrastructure Strategy | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: The Parking Spot owns and operates off-airport parking facilities serving major U.S. airports. Its customers are air travelers who use self-park, valet, and shuttle services as part of airport ground-access demand. The operating model is asset-heavy and recurring, centered on long-term parking properties, customer reservations, shuttle fleets, and service operations located near major airports. Public company materials describe the business as the largest near-airport parking operator in the United States with a national network of facilities. KKR announced the acquisition of The Parking Spot in October 2024 and later highlighted the company as an infrastructure investment with an employee ownership program.
historicalMilestones: 1998: The Parking Spot was founded.
2024-10: KKR announced the acquisition of The Parking Spot from Green Courte Partners.
2025: KKR highlighted rollout of an employee ownership program at The Parking Spot.

oneSentenceDescription: The TotalEnergies US Solar Portfolio is a joint venture between KKR and TotalEnergies comprising a portfolio of operating and under-construction utility-scale solar assets across the United States.
ownershipDetails: Firm: TotalEnergies | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 50%
Firm: KKR | Fund: KKR Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: 50%
companyOverview: This joint venture holds a portfolio of contracted utility-scale solar assets in the United States. Its end markets are power buyers under long-term contracts and wholesale electricity systems supported by operating solar generation assets. The operating model is asset-heavy and contracted, with equity returns driven by ownership of operating renewable projects rather than a development-only platform. TotalEnergies announced in September 2025 that it would divest 50% of a 1.4 GW North American solar portfolio to insurance vehicles and funds managed by KKR, describing the assets as de-risked operating renewable infrastructure. The transaction positioned KKR as a 50% co-owner alongside TotalEnergies within the portfolio-level joint venture structure.
historicalMilestones: 2025-09-29: TotalEnergies announced the sale of a 50% interest in a 1.4 GW North American solar portfolio to KKR-managed vehicles.
2025: TotalEnergies described the portfolio as operating renewable assets that had reached commercial operation and been de-risked.
2025: The transaction established a 50/50 ownership structure between TotalEnergies and KKR across the portfolio.

profileSubsector: Distributed generation, community solar, and battery storage
ownershipDetails: Firm: Climate Adaptive Infrastructure | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Kimmeridge | Fund: Kimmeridge Carbon Solutions | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: 38 Degrees North develops, owns, and operates distributed generation, community solar, battery storage, and related sustainable infrastructure assets. Its end markets include communities, development partners, capital providers, utilities, and commercial counterparties seeking distributed clean-energy solutions. The operating model combines development and ownership of physical energy assets with flexible capital partnerships, making it more infrastructure oriented than a software-only platform. Public materials describe a focus on community solar, distributed generation, and storage, and a July 2025 funding announcement stated that the company had raised more than $230 million of corporate growth capital and acquired U.S. Light Energy's 250 MW-plus pipeline. Kimmeridge made a growth equity investment alongside Climate Adaptive Infrastructure in 2025.
historicalMilestones: 2025-07-17: 38 Degrees North announced more than $230 million of corporate growth capital and new equity investment from Climate Adaptive Infrastructure and Kimmeridge.
2025-07-17: The company also announced completion of the acquisition of U.S. Light Energy, including a pipeline of more than 250 MW of projects.
2025: Public materials continued to describe a strategy focused on distributed generation, community solar, and battery storage.

profileSubsector: Integrated natural gas and LNG platform
ownershipDetails: Firm: Kimmeridge | Fund: Kimmeridge Flagship Funds | Ownership Interest: 75.9%
Firm: Mubadala Energy | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 24.1%
companyOverview: Caturus is an integrated natural gas and LNG platform that combines upstream gas production with associated midstream and export infrastructure. Its end markets include LNG buyers, gas marketers, and downstream customers seeking low-emission U.S. natural gas supply linked to export capacity. The operating model is asset-heavy and integrated, spanning production, gathering and related infrastructure, and an LNG export project through Commonwealth LNG rather than a pure asset-light marketing model. Public materials state that the platform had crossed the 600 MMcfe per day threshold by August 2025 and targets 3 Bcfe per day over time, while also advancing the 9.5 mtpa Commonwealth LNG project. Kimmeridge rebranded SoTex HoldCo as Caturus in August 2025 when Mubadala Energy closed a strategic investment for a 24.1% equity stake.
historicalMilestones: 2024: Commonwealth LNG disclosed that 100% of its equity had been acquired by a wholly owned subsidiary of Kimmeridge Texas Gas LNG, LLC.
2025-08-07: Kimmeridge announced the rebranding of SoTex HoldCo as Caturus and the closing of Mubadala Energy's strategic investment.
2025-09-05: DOE change-of-control materials disclosed that Mubadala Energy owned 24.1% of Caturus and Kimmeridge retained 75.9%.

profileSubsector: Nature-based carbon removal and forest carbon projects
ownershipDetails: Firm: Kimmeridge | Fund: Kimmeridge Carbon Solutions | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Chestnut Carbon develops nature-based carbon removal projects focused on afforestation and improved forest management in the United States. Its customers are corporations and other organizations purchasing verified carbon removal or offset credits as part of climate and sustainability programs. The operating model is land and project intensive, built around long-dated forestry project development, ecological stewardship, measurement, and monetization of verified carbon credits. Public company materials state that Chestnut was founded by Kimmeridge in 2022 and combines forestry, carbon-market, and land-management expertise to develop U.S. forest carbon projects. Kimmeridge launched and capitalized the platform through its carbon solutions strategy and announced a strategic acquisition of Forest Carbon Works at launch.
historicalMilestones: 2022-03-18: Kimmeridge launched Chestnut Carbon and committed up to $200 million of capital to the platform.
2022-03-18: Chestnut announced the concurrent acquisition of Forest Carbon Works.
2022: Chestnut Carbon was founded by Kimmeridge.

profileSubsector: Community solar customer acquisition and clean-energy services
ownershipDetails: Firm: Kimmeridge | Fund: Kimmeridge Carbon Solutions | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Common Energy helps households, businesses, nonprofits, and institutions participate in community solar and related clean-energy programs. Its end markets are retail and organizational electricity users that want bill savings and renewable-energy participation without owning on-site generation. The operating model is more service and platform oriented than a pure asset-owning utility, connecting customers to community solar subscriptions and other clean-energy offerings across multiple states. Public company materials reference community solar enrollment, power purchase agreement support, and more than 10 billion kilowatt-hours of load analyses as part of the platform's customer offering. Reviewed public materials indicate Kimmeridge Carbon Solutions has invested in community-solar-related businesses, but detailed current ownership terms for Common Energy were not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2023-09-11: Kimmeridge Carbon Solutions announced a $15 million investment in U.S. Light Energy, another community-solar developer in its clean-energy investment strategy.
2025: Common Energy continued to market community solar and related clean-energy services across multiple U.S. states.
Not publicly disclosed.: Additional historical milestones for Common Energy were not publicly disclosed in the reviewed primary sources.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Kimmeridge | Fund: Kimmeridge Flagship Funds / Caturus | Ownership Interest: 75.9%
Firm: Mubadala Energy | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 24.1%
companyOverview: Commonwealth LNG is developing a liquefied natural gas export terminal near Cameron, Louisiana. Its end markets include international LNG buyers, gas suppliers, and counterparties seeking long-term Gulf Coast liquefaction and export capacity. The operating model is asset-heavy and project based, centered on liquefaction trains, storage, marine facilities, and long-term commercial agreements rather than an asset-light marketing platform. Public materials describe the project at 9.5 mtpa and note that it forms part of Caturus's integrated wellhead-to-water strategy linking U.S. natural gas production with LNG exports. Kimmeridge closed a follow-on control investment in Commonwealth LNG in 2024, and the parent platform was rebranded as Caturus in 2025 when Mubadala Energy acquired a 24.1% equity stake.
historicalMilestones: 2024-06-25: Kimmeridge announced a follow-on control investment in Commonwealth LNG.
2024-09-19: Commonwealth LNG and Kimmeridge Texas Gas announced heads of terms with Glencore for a natural gas and LNG partnership.
2025-08-07: Kimmeridge announced the rebranding of the parent platform to Caturus after closing Mubadala Energy's strategic equity investment.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Kimmeridge | Fund: Kimmeridge Carbon Solutions | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: LandGate provides data, analytics, and marketplace tools for renewable energy, natural gas, carbon, and infrastructure site selection and development. Its customers include developers, capital providers, landowners, and commercial counterparties evaluating infrastructure opportunities and land-resource economics. The operating model is software and data oriented rather than asset-heavy, generating value through proprietary datasets, analytics, and transaction or subscription-driven services. Public company materials describe solutions for site selection, origination, financing, and market analysis across renewables, data centers, energy storage, EV infrastructure, and natural gas. LandGate raised a Series B funding round in 2022 that included participation from Kimmeridge.
historicalMilestones: 2022-05-12: LandGate announced a $10 million Series B funding round that included participation from Kimmeridge.
2022-09-14: LandGate announced launch of PowerCapital for renewable-energy capital markets.
2025: Public materials continued to describe LandGate as a data and analytics platform serving renewable, data-center, storage, EV, and natural-gas infrastructure markets.

profileSubsector: Renewable asset operations and market optimization software
ownershipDetails: Firm: Kimmeridge | Fund: Kimmeridge Carbon Solutions | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: SYSO Technologies provides software and market operations services for renewable energy and battery storage assets. Its customers are asset owners, developers, and operators that need dispatch, revenue optimization, and day-to-day market participation support for renewable and storage fleets. The operating model is more software and services based than a traditional asset-owning utility, although the platform is directly tied to the performance of physical generation and storage infrastructure. Public company materials describe SYSO as a market operator of renewable energy and battery storage assets and stated in 2024 that the company had raised Series B funding led by Kimmeridge to expand gigawatts under management. Kimmeridge led SYSO's $14.5 million Series B round in 2024, and a 2026 extension increased the total Series B financing to $15.5 million.
historicalMilestones: 2024-08-08: SYSO announced a $14.5 million Series B financing led by Kimmeridge.
2024: Kimmeridge's public materials identified SYSO as a portfolio investment focused on operating renewable assets in power markets.
2026-03-04: SYSO announced a $7 million Series B extension, bringing the total round to $15.5 million.

principalLocationOfOperations: Virginia; Texas; Arizona; Illinois; Ohio; Oregon; Utah
ownershipDetails: Firm: Macquarie Asset Management | Fund: MIP IV / MIP V and related vehicles | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Mubadala Investment Company | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Aligned Data Centers develops and operates large-scale data centers for hyperscale, cloud, and enterprise customers. Its end markets require high-density powered capacity, low-latency connectivity, and build-to-scale deployments across major U.S. data center markets. The operating model is asset-heavy and contracted, centered on powered land, campuses, and long-term colocation or hyperscale leases. Public materials describe approximately 2.5 GW of capacity across operating and planned facilities and more than 40 data centers at full buildout across major U.S. markets. Macquarie acquired a majority stake in 2021, Mubadala announced a minority investment in 2023, and Macquarie announced a sale agreement in 2025 that remained subject to closing.
historicalMilestones: 2013: Aligned Data Centers was founded.
2021: Macquarie Asset Management acquired a majority stake in Aligned Data Centers.
2023-08-02: Mubadala announced a minority investment in Aligned.
2025-10-15: Macquarie announced an agreement to sell Aligned Data Centers, subject to closing.

profileCompanyName: APLD HPC TopCo 2 / Applied Digital HPC JV
profileSubsector: High-performance computing and AI data centers
ownershipDetails: Firm: Applied Digital | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 92.5%
Firm: Macquarie Asset Management | Fund: Macquarie-managed funds and investment vehicles | Ownership Interest: 7.5%
companyOverview: The Applied Digital HPC joint venture funds and develops high-performance computing data center infrastructure for artificial intelligence and other compute-intensive workloads. Its end markets include hyperscale and enterprise customers that need large, specialized compute campuses with substantial power and cooling capacity. The operating model is asset-heavy and project based, with capital deployed into land, buildings, power systems, and long-duration compute infrastructure. Macquarie and Applied Digital announced in January 2025 a partnership providing up to $5 billion of funding, and Applied Digital later disclosed that the relevant JV entity was owned 92.5% by Applied Digital and 7.5% by a Macquarie affiliate, alongside preferred equity. Public materials indicate the first investments are tied to the Ellendale HPC campus and future Applied Digital data center pipeline.
historicalMilestones: 2025-01-14: Macquarie and Applied Digital announced an HPC infrastructure partnership with up to $5 billion of funding capacity.
2025-10-07: Applied Digital and Macquarie announced the first funding milestone in the partnership.
2025-12-05: Applied Digital disclosed that the relevant JV entity was 92.5% owned by Applied Digital and 7.5% owned by a Macquarie affiliate, plus preferred equity.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Macquarie Asset Management | Fund: MIP IV | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Bluebird Fiber operates regional communication infrastructure and fiber networks in the U.S. Midwest. Its customers include enterprises, carriers, schools, government users, and other organizations requiring middle-mile and last-mile fiber connectivity. The operating model is asset-heavy and recurring, supported by owned and leased fiber routes, network equipment, and long-term connectivity contracts. Macquarie's portfolio materials describe Bluebird as a communications infrastructure provider in the Midwest, and public transaction materials indicate Macquarie acquired the platform in 2019 to expand its regional fiber footprint. Reviewed public sources did not disclose the detailed current ownership percentage held by Macquarie-managed funds.
historicalMilestones: 2019-01-15: Bluebird Network announced an agreement to be acquired by Macquarie Infrastructure Partners.
2019-09-03: Bluebird announced closing of the acquisition by Macquarie Infrastructure Partners.
2025: Macquarie continued to describe Bluebird as a Midwest communications infrastructure platform in its portfolio materials.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Macquarie Asset Management | Fund: MGETS | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Calibrant Energy develops, owns, and operates on-site energy solutions for large power users. Its customers include commercial and industrial facilities, data centers, and other large-load users seeking resilience, decarbonization, and energy-cost management solutions. The operating model is asset-heavy and service based, with revenues tied to long-term ownership and operation of distributed solar, storage, microgrid, and related energy infrastructure. Public company materials state that Calibrant was founded by Macquarie in 2020 and had expanded into Canada through a joint venture with Ardian by 2023. Reviewed public materials identify Macquarie Asset Management as the founding sponsor and backing institution for the platform.
historicalMilestones: 2020: Macquarie Asset Management founded Calibrant Energy.
2023: Calibrant's public history states that the company expanded into Canada through a joint venture with Ardian Infrastructure.
2025: Public materials continued to describe an on-site energy platform serving large power users in North America.

ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Ceres Terminals Jacksonville is a marine terminal operation at JAXPORT serving container and related port logistics activity. Its customers include shipping lines, cargo owners, trucking operators, and logistics providers moving containerized freight through Jacksonville. The operating model is asset-heavy and concession based, relying on terminal lease rights, yard and berth infrastructure, cargo-handling equipment, and throughput-linked operating revenue. Public materials reviewed indicate that Ceres signed a long-term lease and modernization program for the TraPac Jacksonville terminal in 2022, while Macquarie later announced the divestment of Ceres Terminals in 2023. Because the reviewed current ownership structure for the Jacksonville terminal after that broader divestment was not publicly disclosed in the reviewed primary sources, the current ownership details are listed as not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2022-02-14: JAXPORT approved a 20-year agreement with Ceres for the lease and modernization of the TraPac Jacksonville container terminal.
2023-06-28: Macquarie announced an agreement to divest Ceres Terminals.
2023-10-12: Macquarie announced completion of the sale of Ceres Terminals.

oneSentenceDescription: Cleco Corporate Holdings is a regulated electric utility holding company serving approximately 300,000 customers across Louisiana through its subsidiary Cleco Power LLC.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Macquarie Asset Management-led consortium | Fund: MIP III / MIRA | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Cleco Corporate Holdings is the parent of a regulated electric utility serving customers in Louisiana through Cleco Power. Its customer base includes residential, commercial, and industrial electricity users across central and southeastern Louisiana. The operating model is asset-heavy and regulated, with revenues derived from state-approved rates and utility capital investment rather than merchant power sales. Public company materials indicate service to roughly 300,000 customers and a system that includes generation, transmission, and distribution infrastructure across Louisiana. A Macquarie-led consortium took Cleco private in 2016, and public sources reviewed did not disclose the detailed current ownership percentages within that investor group.
historicalMilestones: 1935: Central Louisiana Electric Company was incorporated.
2016: A Macquarie-led consortium completed the take-private acquisition of Cleco.
2019: Cleco completed the Madison Solar Energy Center as its first utility-scale solar project.
2023: Cleco continued implementation of its integrated resource planning and grid investment programs.

profileSubsector: Solid waste collection, transfer, recycling, and disposal
ownershipDetails: Firm: Macquarie Asset Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Coastal Waste & Recycling provides municipal, residential, commercial, industrial, and recycling services across the southeastern United States. Its customers include municipalities, households, commercial establishments, and industrial waste generators that require recurring collection and post-collection services. The operating model is asset-heavy, supported by a network of collection routes, transfer stations, recycling facilities, portable restroom operations, and disposal infrastructure. Macquarie stated in 2023 that Coastal had 19 locations in Florida and Georgia at closing, while more recent company materials describe a footprint spanning Florida, Georgia, and South Carolina with more than 1,100 employees, more than 700 vehicles, and 25 facilities. Macquarie Asset Management completed an investment in the company in June 2023, and the detailed current ownership percentage was not publicly disclosed in the reviewed sources.
historicalMilestones: 2017: Coastal Waste & Recycling was founded by Brendon Pantano and partners.
2021: Company materials described the business as having completed 11 acquisitions during its early expansion period.
2023-06-13: Macquarie Asset Management announced completion of its investment in Coastal Waste & Recycling.

2026: Company materials describe operations across Florida, Georgia, and South Carolina with 25 facilities.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Macquarie Asset Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Cyrq Energy develops, owns, and operates geothermal power assets in the western United States. Its customers are utility and power-market counterparties that purchase renewable electricity and related generation output from geothermal facilities. The operating model is asset-heavy and infrastructure based, with long-lived generation assets and contracted or utility-linked power sales rather than an asset-light services model. Company materials state that the platform is headquartered in Salt Lake City and operates five geothermal facilities across Nevada, California, and Utah, with additional projects under development. Macquarie Asset Management identifies Cyrq Energy as a portfolio company, while the detailed current acquisition date, fund vehicle, and ownership percentage were not publicly disclosed in the reviewed sources.
historicalMilestones: 2014: Public project materials referenced operating geothermal assets that now form part of Cyrq Energy’s western U.S. portfolio.
2024: Cyrq Energy publicly described itself as a geothermal platform with operating assets and development projects in the western United States.

profileSubsector: Utility-scale solar and battery storage development and ownership
ownershipDetails: Firm: Macquarie Asset Management | Fund: Multiple Macquarie-managed funds | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: The D. E. Shaw group | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: DESRI develops, acquires, owns, and operates utility-scale solar and storage projects across the United States. Its customers include electric utilities, corporate offtakers, and other counterparties purchasing renewable generation or capacity from long-duration infrastructure assets. The operating model is asset-heavy and development oriented, combining project origination and construction with long-term ownership of operating renewable assets. Company materials state that DESRI has operated since 2010 and has built a diversified portfolio across 22 states with more than 11 GW of generation capacity. Macquarie Asset Management announced the closing of an investment in DESRI in January 2025 alongside the D. E. Shaw group, while detailed post-transaction ownership percentages were not publicly disclosed in the reviewed sources.

2024-12-17: DESRI announced a 400 MWac clean energy and storage portfolio with Meta, reflecting continued project expansion.
2025-01-13: DESRI and Macquarie Asset Management announced closing of Macquarie’s investment in the company.

profileSubsector: Industrial utilities, pipelines, and site infrastructure
ownershipDetails: Firm: Dow | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 51%
Firm: Macquarie Asset Management | Fund: Macquarie Infrastructure Partners VI | Ownership Interest: 49%
companyOverview: Diamond Infrastructure Solutions is an infrastructure platform serving industrial customers at Dow chemical sites on the U.S. Gulf Coast. Its customer base includes Dow and third-party industrial tenants that rely on utility systems, pipelines, and related site services to support manufacturing operations. The operating model is asset-heavy and contracted, built around ownership and operation of essential industrial infrastructure rather than merchant commodity exposure alone. Macquarie and Dow have stated that the platform serves five locations in Texas and Louisiana and was established to improve operational efficiency and support third-party customer growth. The partnership was announced in December 2024 with Macquarie acquiring an initial 40 percent stake, and Macquarie Infrastructure Partners VI exercised an option in September 2025 to increase that ownership stake to 49 percent.

2025-09-23: Macquarie Infrastructure Partners VI exercised its option to increase its equity stake from 40% to 49%.

Firm: APG Americas Infrastructure / CalSTRS / Argo Infrastructure Partners | Fund: AIA Montana LLC | Ownership Interest: 25.2%
companyOverview: Duquesne Light Company is a regulated electric distribution utility serving the Pittsburgh metropolitan area. Its customer base comprises households, businesses, and industrial users in southwestern Pennsylvania that rely on the utility for electricity delivery rather than competitive generation supply. The business is asset-heavy and regulated, with earnings supported by rate-based transmission and distribution infrastructure and state-approved tariffs. Company investor materials state that it serves roughly 600,000 customers in Allegheny and Beaver counties and publicly disclose the current ownership breakdown among GIC, Three Rivers Utility Holdings, and AIA Montana. The shareholder group evolved after the 2007 take-private transaction, with GIC becoming a major investor in 2011, Three Rivers Utility Holdings taking its current position in 2016, and AIA Montana acquiring its stake in 2017.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Macquarie Asset Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: DynaGrid provides utility services focused on maintaining, upgrading, and building transmission and substation infrastructure. Its customers are electric utilities and related infrastructure owners that outsource specialized field, construction, and maintenance work on grid assets. The operating model is service based but infrastructure-linked, with revenue derived from recurring and project-based work supporting regulated utility capital programs and reliability needs. Macquarie has stated that the business is headquartered in Dallas and primarily operates across the Sunbelt and the Midwest as one of the larger U.S. providers in its category. Macquarie Asset Management announced in February 2025 that it had acquired a majority stake in DynaGrid, while the exact ownership percentage was not publicly disclosed in the reviewed sources.
historicalMilestones: 2025-02-20: Macquarie Asset Management announced the acquisition of a majority stake in DynaGrid.
2025: Macquarie described DynaGrid as the first investment for a new strategy focused on infrastructure-related buyout opportunities.
2026: Public materials continue to describe DynaGrid as a Dallas-headquartered utility services provider focused on transmission and substations.

profileSubsector: Waste collection, recycling, and resource recovery
ownershipDetails: Firm: Macquarie Asset Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: GreenWaste provides collection, recycling, organics processing, and resource recovery services in California. Its customers include municipalities, households, businesses, and public agencies that require integrated waste collection and diversion services. The operating model is asset-heavy, supported by hauling fleets, material recovery facilities, organics processing assets, and related recycling infrastructure. Company and sponsor materials describe a Northern California platform with long operating history in recycling and waste diversion and an emphasis on high-diversion resource recovery. Macquarie Asset Management identifies GreenWaste as a portfolio company, while the reviewed public sources did not disclose the detailed current acquisition date, fund vehicle, or ownership percentage.

2025-11-04: GreenWaste announced the appointment of Kevin DiVincenzo as Chief Operating Officer.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Macquarie Asset Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: International Transportation Service operates a container terminal at the Port of Long Beach. Its customers include ocean carriers, trucking companies, rail operators, and cargo owners moving containerized freight through Southern California. The operating model is asset-heavy and concession based, relying on terminal infrastructure, cranes, rail connections, yard operations, and throughput-linked service revenues. Macquarie materials state that ITS is undergoing a redevelopment intended to increase terminal capacity by approximately 50 percent to around 1.5 million lifts per year. Macquarie Asset Management continues to identify ITS as a current port terminal investment, while the reviewed public sources did not disclose the detailed current ownership percentage.
historicalMilestones: 1971: ITS was founded and began operations in Long Beach.
1972: ITS moved into its completed terminal on Pier J in the Port of Long Beach.
2006: ITS marked its 35th anniversary and the anniversary of its on-dock double-stack rail operations.

profileSubsector: Waste diversion, recycling, and portable services
ownershipDetails: Firm: Macquarie Asset Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Goldman Sachs Asset Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: LRS provides waste collection, recycling, portable services, and related environmental services across the Midwest. Its customers include municipalities, residential customers, commercial businesses, and construction and industrial users that require integrated solid-waste and recycling solutions. The operating model is asset-heavy, supported by collection fleets, transfer and recycling facilities, and ancillary service equipment deployed across local markets. Company sustainability materials state that LRS was founded in 2013, and Macquarie describes it as a waste diversion, recycling, and portable services provider in the United States. Macquarie recapitalized the company in 2021 alongside Goldman Sachs Asset Management, while the reviewed sources did not disclose the precise current ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: 2013: LRS was founded.
2021-12-08: Macquarie and Goldman Sachs Asset Management recapitalized LRS to support continued expansion.
2022: LRS continued Midwest expansion through acquisitions including GHW Waste Services.
2024: LRS published sustainability materials highlighting continued investment in recycling and diversion infrastructure.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Macquarie Asset Management | Fund: Macquarie Infrastructure Partners IV | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Long Beach Container Terminal operates an automated marine container terminal at the Port of Long Beach. Its customers include shipping lines, trucking operators, rail providers, and beneficial cargo owners moving import and export freight through Southern California. The operating model is asset-heavy and concession based, relying on large-scale terminal infrastructure, automated equipment, and throughput-linked port operations. Company materials state that the current Pier E facility opened in 2016, and Macquarie describes it as an automated terminal that plays a significant role in the Los Angeles-Long Beach port complex. A Macquarie-managed fund acquired LBCT in 2019, and Macquarie continues to position the asset around operating performance and decarbonization initiatives including the terminal’s Net Zero 2030 plan.
historicalMilestones: 2016: The current Pier E facility opened at the Port of Long Beach.

2022: LBCT launched its Net Zero 2030 Climate Action Plan.
2025: Macquarie highlighted LBCT as a case study in port decarbonization and terminal modernization.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Macquarie Asset Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Maher Terminals operates a large multi-user marine container terminal in the Port of New York and New Jersey. Its customers include global ocean carriers, truckers, rail providers, and cargo owners serving one of the largest consumer markets in North America. The operating model is asset-heavy and throughput driven, supported by terminal concessions, berth and yard infrastructure, cargo-handling equipment, and associated intermodal connections. Company and sponsor materials describe Maher as one of the largest privately held multi-user container terminal operators in the world and the largest marine container terminal in the Port of New York and New Jersey. Macquarie Asset Management continues to identify Maher as a current transport portfolio asset, while the reviewed public sources did not disclose the detailed current ownership percentage or the original acquisition date.
