2020: Maher published its inaugural sustainability report.
2023-10-12: Macquarie stated that Maher remained one of its significant port terminal investments following the Ceres sale.
2024: Maher published updated sustainability reporting covering 2023 operations.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Macquarie Asset Management | Fund: Macquarie Infrastructure Partners V | Ownership Interest: 50%
Firm: Medical Properties Trust | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 50%
companyOverview: Medical Properties Partnership is a joint venture that owns eight Massachusetts general acute care hospitals. Its end market is healthcare infrastructure, with cash flows tied to leased hospital real estate operated by healthcare providers rather than direct clinical service delivery. The operating model is asset-heavy and contracted, centered on essential hospital properties with long-term rental income and infrastructure-like real estate characteristics. Macquarie and Medical Properties Trust stated in 2021 and 2022 that the transaction covered eight Steward-operated hospitals in Massachusetts at a portfolio value of approximately $1.78 billion. A Macquarie Infrastructure Partners V-controlled subsidiary completed the acquisition of a 50 percent interest in the portfolio in March 2022, leaving Medical Properties Trust with the remaining 50 percent stake.
historicalMilestones: 2021-09-01: Macquarie Infrastructure Partners V and Medical Properties Trust announced their partnership for eight Massachusetts hospitals.
2022-03-16: Medical Properties Trust announced completion of the hospital partnership with Macquarie.
2023-03-30: Medical Properties Trust reaffirmed the March 2022 50/50 partnership structure in shareholder communications.
2025: The partnership remained part of Medical Properties Trust’s disclosed capital allocation actions in company reporting.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Porter Aviation Holdings | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Macquarie Asset Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: YHU Infrastructure Partners is developing and operating the new passenger terminal at Montréal Saint-Hubert Airport. Its customers include Porter Airlines, Pascan Aviation, additional future air carriers, and airport passengers using the South Shore airport serving Greater Montréal. The operating model is asset-heavy and concession like, with revenues tied to terminal operations, passenger throughput, and associated airport infrastructure services. Macquarie and the terminal operator describe the project as a partnership between Porter Aviation Holdings and Macquarie Asset Management, with an eventual capacity of up to four million passengers per year. The partnership was announced in 2023, and March 2026 public statements said the terminal would open on June 15, 2026, while the reviewed materials did not disclose ownership percentages.
historicalMilestones: 2023-09-27: Porter and Macquarie announced their partnership to develop the passenger terminal at Montréal Saint-Hubert Airport.
2025: Macquarie listed YHU Infrastructure Partners as a current transport portfolio asset.
2026-03-24: The terminal operator announced that the new terminal would open on 2026-06-15.
2026-06-15: Public materials state that passenger operations are scheduled to begin at the new terminal.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Macquarie Asset Management | Fund: Macquarie Infrastructure Partners III | Ownership Interest: 90%
Firm: Kiewit Development Company | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 10%
companyOverview: NYNJ Link Partnership is the concessionaire for the Goethals Bridge replacement project connecting Staten Island and New Jersey. Its end market is essential transportation infrastructure, with economics based on availability payments tied to the design, construction, financing, operation, and maintenance of the bridge rather than toll collection by the concessionaire. The operating model is asset-heavy and long duration, centered on a public-private partnership for a critical bridge that serves commuter and freight traffic in the New York-New Jersey region. U.S. Department of Transportation materials describe the bridge corridor as serving about 80,000 vehicles per day, and Macquarie has identified the asset as part of its transportation portfolio. The Port Authority and NYNJ Link reached commercial close in 2013, and Macquarie later disclosed a current ownership split of 90 percent for Macquarie and 10 percent for Kiewit.
historicalMilestones: 2013-08: The Port Authority and NYNJ Link reached commercial close on the concession agreement.
2013-11-05: NYNJ Link executed its TIFIA credit agreement for the project financing package.
2014: Construction began on the replacement bridge.
2018-06: U.S. Department of Transportation reporting states that the project reached substantial completion.
2025: Macquarie disclosed a 90% / 10% ownership split between Macquarie and Kiewit.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Macquarie Asset Management | Fund: Macquarie Infrastructure Partners IV | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Netrality management team | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Netrality Data Centers operates interconnection-focused data centers and carrier hotels in the United States. Its customers include network carriers, cloud and connectivity providers, enterprise users, and other digital infrastructure participants that require colocation and dense interconnection environments. The operating model is asset-heavy and recurring revenue based, supported by owned facilities, meet-me rooms, colocation deployments, and network connectivity services. Macquarie describes the platform as an interconnection data center business in the U.S., and transaction coverage around the acquisition referenced facilities in Chicago, Kansas City, Houston, Philadelphia, and St. Louis. Macquarie Infrastructure Partners IV acquired Netrality in partnership with the company’s management team in August 2019, while detailed current ownership percentages were not publicly disclosed in the reviewed sources.
historicalMilestones: 2019-08-30: Macquarie Infrastructure Partners IV and the management team acquired Netrality Data Centers.
2019-09-04: Netrality announced the completed acquisition from funds managed by Abrams Capital Management.
2022: Macquarie identified Netrality as one of the U.S. data center platforms it was continuing to expand.
2026: Macquarie continued to identify Netrality as part of its global digital infrastructure portfolio.

profileSubsector: Fiber connectivity for multifamily and student housing
ownershipDetails: Firm: Macquarie Asset Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Pavlov Media provides fiber-based broadband and related connectivity services to multifamily and student housing properties. Its customers include property owners, managers, and residents, with a historical concentration in off-campus student housing and growing expansion into broader multifamily markets. The operating model combines recurring service revenue with asset-backed network deployment, using owned last-mile fiber networks and contracted property relationships. Macquarie stated in 2023 that Pavlov was founded in 1994 and served approximately 800 buildings with more than 275,000 beds across more than 150 markets in the United States and Canada at the time of investment. Macquarie Asset Management announced a preferred equity investment in March 2023 and the company later secured a $235 million debt facility in July 2025 to support further fiber expansion.
historicalMilestones: 1994: Pavlov Media was founded.
2023-03-28: Macquarie Asset Management announced a preferred equity investment in Pavlov Media.
2023: The investment was positioned to support fiber construction in existing and new markets and expansion into fiber-to-the-home initiatives.
2025-07-22: Pavlov Media announced a $235 million debt facility to accelerate network expansion and technology upgrades.

profileSubsector: Road-marking materials and engineered glass media
ownershipDetails: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Potters Industries manufactures glass beads, microspheres, and related engineered materials used in road markings, runway markings, and specialized industrial applications. Its end markets include transportation safety, infrastructure maintenance, and industrial customers that use reflective media and specialty materials in their operations. The operating model is asset-heavy and manufacturing based, supported by a network of production and logistics sites across North America and Europe. Macquarie stated in November 2025 that Potters operates 26 manufacturing and logistics sites across North America and Europe and has additional presence in South America and Asia, while company materials identify Malvern, Pennsylvania as its global headquarters. Macquarie Asset Management and co-investors including UniSuper and Partners Capital announced an agreement in November 2025 to acquire a majority stake from TJC, but the reviewed sources did not confirm a completed closing or disclose the current post-transaction ownership structure as of the review date.
historicalMilestones: 2021: Potters moved to a new joint corporate and R&D headquarters in Malvern, Pennsylvania.
2024: Potters acquired Franklin Paint and UCM according to company history materials.
2025: Potters opened a new plant in Wilson, North Carolina.
2025-11-11: Macquarie Asset Management-led consortium agreed to acquire a majority stake in Potters from TJC.
2026: The reviewed public sources did not confirm that the announced Macquarie-led transaction had closed.

oneSentenceDescription: Puget Sound Energy is the largest investor-owned utility in Washington State, providing electric service to approximately 1.2 million customers and natural gas service to approximately 900,000 customers across 10 counties in the Puget Sound region.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Macquarie Asset Management | Fund: MGIF | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Puget Sound Energy is a regulated electric and natural gas utility serving residential, commercial, and industrial customers in Washington. Public disclosures state that the company serves about 1.2 million electric customers and nearly 900,000 natural gas customers across 10 counties. The operating model is asset-heavy and predominantly regulated, supported by generation, transmission, and distribution assets with revenues recovered through approved tariffs. Company materials describe a 6,000-square-mile service area and a resource base that includes owned and contracted hydro, wind, solar, natural gas, and other power resources. PSE was taken private in 2009 by a Macquarie-led consortium, and Macquarie Asset Management later announced in 2022 that it and Ontario Teachers' had completed an acquisition of a stake in Puget Holdings; the current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 1997: Puget Sound Energy was formed through the merger of Puget Sound Power & Light and Washington Natural Gas.
2009-02: A Macquarie-led consortium completed the take-private acquisition of Puget Energy and PSE.
2021-07: Macquarie Asset Management and Ontario Teachers' announced an agreement to acquire a stake in Puget Holdings.
2022-01: Macquarie Asset Management and Ontario Teachers' announced completion of their stake acquisition in Puget Holdings.
2025: PSE published its 2024 sustainability and green bond reporting as it continued capital investment tied to Washington clean-energy requirements.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Macquarie Asset Management | Fund: MIP VI | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: SwyftFiber provides fiber broadband and related connectivity services to residential and business customers in rural communities in Louisiana, Arkansas, and Mississippi. Its customer base is primarily households and small businesses that require fixed broadband rather than mobile connectivity or wholesale-only services. The operating model is asset-heavy because the platform is funding and operating last-mile fiber network buildouts in its service territories. Public materials identify active service and expansion activity across the U.S. Gulf South, and Macquarie stated that its investment was intended to accelerate fiber deployment in existing and new markets. Macquarie Asset Management announced an agreement to invest in SwyftFiber in October 2023 and Houlihan Lokey stated that the transaction closed in February 2024; the exact ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2023-10-26: Macquarie Asset Management announced an agreement to invest in SwyftFiber.
2024-02-23: Houlihan Lokey stated that SwyftFiber received an equity investment from Macquarie and that the transaction closed.
2025: Macquarie identified SwyftFiber as an existing investment of Macquarie Infrastructure Partners VI.
2026: Company operating materials continued to market fiber service across Louisiana, Arkansas, and Mississippi.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Macquarie Asset Management | Fund: MIP VI | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Ocean Network Express | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: TraPac operates marine container terminals in Los Angeles and Oakland serving ocean carriers, truckers, rail providers, and cargo owners moving containerized freight through the U.S. West Coast. Its revenue model is infrastructure and throughput driven, with asset-heavy terminal operations supported by berth access, cranes, yard equipment, gate systems, and intermodal rail connectivity. Company materials highlight automated on-dock rail in Los Angeles and direct access to BNSF and Union Pacific rail connections in Oakland. Public sustainability materials describe TraPac as a two-terminal California platform that continues to invest in equipment modernization and emissions reduction. TraPac was established in 1985 by Mitsui O.S.K. Lines, and company and ONE disclosures state that Ocean Network Express completed the acquisition of a 51% stake in 2023 while Macquarie-managed funds remain an investor.
historicalMilestones: 1985: TraPac was established by Mitsui O.S.K. Lines to operate a container terminal in Los Angeles.
1991: TraPac expanded to Oakland with the opening of its terminal there.
2014: TraPac Los Angeles became the first U.S. West Coast terminal to automate vessel loading and unloading operations.
2023-11-06: Ocean Network Express announced completion of its acquisition of a 51% stake in TraPac.
2026-01-09: TraPac announced the arrival of new ship-to-shore cranes at its U.S. West Coast terminals.

profileSubsector: Waste-to-energy, collection, transfer, and recycling services
ownershipDetails: Firm: Macquarie Asset Management | Fund: Macquarie Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: WIN Waste Innovations provides waste collection, transfer, recycling, landfill, rail-haul, and waste-to-energy services for municipalities, residential customers, and commercial and industrial users. Its operating model is asset-heavy and vertically integrated, combining collection fleets with transfer, disposal, and energy-recovery infrastructure. Company materials state that WIN serves the Northeast and Ohio and operates a platform of more than 50 collection, transfer, and disposal assets. The business was formed by combining Wheelabrator with other Macquarie-owned waste businesses and continues to market curb-to-grid and resource-recovery solutions. Wheelabrator was acquired by Macquarie-managed funds in 2019 and the combined platform was rebranded as WIN Waste Innovations in 2021; the current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 1908: The Wheelabrator predecessor business was founded.
2019: Macquarie-managed funds acquired Wheelabrator from Energy Capital Partners.
2021-04-06: Wheelabrator and nine affiliated waste businesses were combined under the WIN Waste Innovations brand.
2023: WIN highlighted its platform of waste-to-energy, rail, and transfer assets in company press materials.
2025: WIN disclosed a platform of 53 collection, transfer, and disposal assets in public meeting materials.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Macquarie Asset Management | Fund: MIP III | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Ocean Network Express | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Yusen Terminals operates a marine cargo terminal at the Port of Los Angeles serving shipping lines, trucking companies, and other supply-chain customers moving transpacific container traffic. The operating model is asset-heavy and throughput based, supported by berth, yard, gate, and cargo-handling infrastructure under long-dated port operating arrangements. Company materials describe high-volume operations at berths 212 to 218 and continuing investment in zero-emission equipment and other terminal modernization initiatives. The platform is concentrated in Los Angeles and functions as a critical gateway asset for Southern California import and export flows. YTI began operations in 1991, and public announcements in 2023 stated that Ocean Network Express completed the acquisition of a 51% stake while Macquarie portfolio materials have continued to reference YTI as an investment.
historicalMilestones: 1991: Yusen Terminals began providing marine terminal services at the Port of Los Angeles.
2022-12-27: Ocean Network Express announced an agreement to acquire a 51% stake in YTI.
2023-11-06: Ocean Network Express announced completion of its 51% acquisition in YTI.
2024-06-27: YTI announced deployment of commercially available battery-electric top handlers.
2025: YTI published updates highlighting operational and sustainability milestones at the terminal.

profileSubsector: Incumbent fiber broadband and telecom network
ownershipDetails: Firm: Macquarie Asset Management | Fund: MIP V | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: altafiber provides fiber-based broadband, voice, video, enterprise connectivity, and related telecommunications services to residential and business customers in Greater Cincinnati and through Hawaiian Telcom in Hawaii. The operating model is asset-heavy because the platform owns and upgrades local access networks, transport infrastructure, and related communications assets across incumbent service territories. Company materials state that the business can deliver gigabit internet to more than 1 million addresses and that Hawaiian Telcom remains a major fiber communications provider in Hawaii. The platform expanded materially through the 2018 combination of Cincinnati Bell and Hawaiian Telcom and has continued to invest in fiber deployment after going private. Macquarie Infrastructure Partners V completed the take-private acquisition of Cincinnati Bell in 2021, and the company subsequently operated under the altafiber brand; the current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 1873: Cincinnati Bell, the predecessor business to altafiber, was founded.
2018-07-02: Cincinnati Bell completed its combination with Hawaiian Telcom.
2021-09-07: Cincinnati Bell announced completion of its acquisition by Macquarie Infrastructure Partners V.
2022: Hawaiian Telcom branded its 100% fiber internet service as Fioptics.
2022-03: Cincinnati Bell rebranded as altafiber.

principalLocationOfOperations: Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, and U.S. wind and solar markets
ownershipDetails: Firm: iCON Infrastructure | Fund: iCON Infrastructure Partners III | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Capstone Infrastructure develops, owns, and operates renewable and low-carbon power projects in North America. Its customers are primarily utilities and other offtakers that purchase output under long-term power purchase arrangements rather than merchant-only sales. The operating model is asset-heavy and contracted, with a portfolio of utility-scale generation assets and an active development pipeline. Company materials describe approximately 1.1 GW of installed capacity across 36 facilities and a head office in Toronto with operations across Canada and the United States. iCON Infrastructure announced an agreement to acquire Capstone in 2016, and the company remains a privately held North American power platform.
historicalMilestones: 2016-01-20: Capstone announced an agreement for iCON Infrastructure Partners III to acquire the company.
2016: Capstone became privately held following the iCON acquisition.
2024: Capstone continued to advance its North American renewable development pipeline.
2025-09-16: Capstone announced successful commissioning of the 192 MW Wild Rose 2 wind farm in Alberta.

principalLocationOfOperations: Florida, Spain, Italy, Caribbean, and U.S. Virgin Islands

Firm: Royal Caribbean Group | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 10%
companyOverview: Cruise Terminals International develops, owns, and operates cruise terminals and related port infrastructure. Its end markets are cruise lines, port authorities, and destination operators that require long-duration passenger terminal capacity and concession-backed infrastructure. The operating model is asset-heavy and concession based, with development, financing, and operating exposure to terminal projects in North America, Europe, and the Caribbean. Company materials state that the initial portfolio included Cruise Terminal A in Miami and terminal projects in Italy, Spain, and the Caribbean. CTI was formed in 2023 as a partnership between funds advised by iCON Infrastructure and Royal Caribbean Group and disclosed a 90% and 10% ownership split, respectively.
historicalMilestones: 2023: Cruise Terminals International was formed as a partnership between iCON Infrastructure and Royal Caribbean Group.
2023: CTI began with Cruise Terminal A in Miami and additional cruise terminal projects in Europe and the Caribbean.
2025-12-17: CTI signed a long-term lease for the Crown Bay district redevelopment in St. Thomas.

ownershipDetails: Firm: iCON Infrastructure | Fund: iCON Infrastructure Partners V | Ownership Interest: 57%
Firm: Dobson family | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Dobson Fiber provides fiber-based internet and communications services to residential, business, and carrier customers in Oklahoma and parts of Arkansas and Texas. Its operating model is asset-heavy because the business owns and expands regional fiber networks rather than relying on resale or purely service-based arrangements. Public materials tied to the iCON transaction described a network of more than 5,000 route miles, and more recent company materials reference continued expansion beyond that footprint. The platform focuses on underserved and regional markets where network ownership and local operations are central to service delivery. Dobson traces its roots to 1936, and iCON Infrastructure announced in 2022 that its fund would acquire a 57% stake to support a $700 million fiber deployment plan while the Dobson family retained significant ownership.
historicalMilestones: 1936: Dobson was founded by E.R. "Doc" Dobson in Oklahoma.
2021: Dobson announced its 85th anniversary.
2022-02-14: iCON Infrastructure announced a strategic partnership to acquire a 57% stake in Dobson Fiber.
2022: iCON stated that the investment would support a $700 million fiber deployment plan.
2024: Company and industry updates highlighted continued network expansion across Oklahoma, Arkansas, and Texas.

ownershipDetails: Firm: iCON Infrastructure | Fund: iCON Infrastructure Partners V | Ownership Interest: 40%
Firm: Railroad Development Corporation | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 60%
companyOverview: Iowa Interstate Railroad operates a regional freight railroad linking Iowa and Illinois with the broader North American rail network. Its customers include agricultural, industrial, and other freight shippers that require rail transportation and interline access across the Midwest. The operating model is asset-heavy and network based, supported by track, locomotives, rolling stock, yards, and interchange relationships rather than asset-light logistics management. The railroad serves a focused Midwestern corridor and remains a strategic regional freight platform for bulk and industrial traffic. Railroad Development Corporation announced in late 2020 that iCON Infrastructure Partners V would become a 40% partner in IAIS while RDC remained the majority owner.
historicalMilestones: 1984: Iowa Interstate Railroad began operations.
2020-12-31: RDC announced that iCON Infrastructure Partners V would become a 40% partner in IAIS.
2021: Industry publications reported completion of the iCON partnership with RDC remaining majority owner.
2026-02-19: IAIS released a documentary marking 40 years of operations and highlighting its development as a Midwestern freight corridor.

Firm: QSL management shareholders | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: QSL is a North American port and marine logistics platform providing terminal operations, stevedoring, transportation, warehousing, and related marine services. Its customers are importers, exporters, industrial cargo owners, vessel operators, and other supply-chain users that require multimodal cargo handling and port logistics solutions. The operating model is asset-heavy and service intensive, supported by terminal infrastructure, handling equipment, and marine and inland transport capabilities across its network. Company materials describe a North American terminal network with a strong base in Québec and additional operations in Canada and the United States. QSL traces its roots to 1978, and the company announced in 2024 that funds advised by iCON Infrastructure and CDPQ had joined QSL management shareholders as owners; the exact share split was not publicly disclosed.

2024-02-23: QSL announced that iCON Infrastructure and CDPQ had joined management as shareholders.

profileSubsector: Rural fiber broadband and telecom services
ownershipDetails: Firm: iCON Infrastructure | Fund: iCON Infrastructure Partners IV | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: TruVista provides fiber broadband, voice, video, security, and related communications services to residential and business customers in South Carolina and Georgia. Its customer base is concentrated in smaller communities where fixed-line communications infrastructure is an essential utility-like service. The operating model is asset-heavy because the platform owns and upgrades access networks and related communications assets across its service footprint. Company materials highlight fiber internet speeds of up to 5 Gig and continued regional expansion through acquisitions such as PlantTel. TruVista agreed to be acquired by iCON Infrastructure in 2019, and the business expanded further with the closing of the PlantTel acquisition in 2021; the current ownership percentage is not publicly disclosed.

2021-11-04: PlantTel agreed to be acquired by TruVista.

profileSubsector: Natural gas distribution and related services
principalLocationOfOperations: Ohio, Pennsylvania, Indiana, Virginia, and West Virginia
ownershipDetails: Firm: iCON Infrastructure | Fund: iCON Infrastructure Partners VI | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Utility Pipeline provides natural gas distribution and related services to residential, commercial, industrial, and government customers. Its end markets are primarily rural and suburban communities that rely on local gas distribution systems and associated service operations. The operating model is asset-heavy and infrastructure based, supported by pipeline and local distribution assets rather than pure commodity marketing. Public transaction materials describe a footprint across Ohio, Pennsylvania, Indiana, Virginia, and West Virginia. UPL announced in 2023 that interests held by BBH Capital Partners and Allegheny Financial Group would be transferred to iCON Infrastructure VI, and the company subsequently announced that the acquisition had closed in 2024.
historicalMilestones: 2023-11-10: UPL announced an agreement transferring prior private equity interests to iCON Infrastructure VI.

2024-09-03: UPL stated that it had been acquired by iCON Infrastructure VI.

profileSubsector: Residential equipment leasing for HVAC, water, and home electrification equipment
ownershipDetails: Firm: iCON Infrastructure | Fund: iCON Infrastructure Partners IV | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Vista Services provides lease-to-own and rental solutions for residential and property-related equipment such as HVAC systems, water heaters, water treatment equipment, and EV chargers. Its customer base includes homeowners, contractors, builders, and property managers that require financing and service support for home-comfort equipment. The operating model is contracted and service linked, with recurring payments tied to installed equipment sourced and maintained through contractor partners. Company materials describe Vista as a long-standing Ontario platform and identify it as one of the province's larger lease-to-own providers in its category. Vista states that it was founded in 2002, and iCON Infrastructure has publicly identified the business as part of its Canadian portfolio; the current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2002: Vista Services states that it was founded.
2020: iCON publicly referenced Vista Services as part of its infrastructure portfolio.

oneSentenceDescription: TERRANOVA is a hyperscale data center platform launched by Actis (now part of General Atlantic) in December 2025 to accelerate Latin America's digital expansion.
principalLocationOfOperations: Querétaro and Guanajuato, Mexico; São Paulo state, Brazil; Chile
ownershipDetails: Firm: Actis | Fund: Actis Digital Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: TERRANOVA is a Latin American hyperscale data center platform being developed for cloud, AI, and enterprise computing demand. Its end markets are hyperscale cloud providers and other large digital infrastructure users that require large sites with secured land and power. The operating model is asset-heavy and development led, with value creation tied to site control, power access, construction, and eventual long-term customer occupancy. Actis disclosed at launch that the platform had secured land and power capable of supporting up to 1 GW across projects in Mexico, Brazil, and Chile, with the first campus located in Querétaro. Actis launched TERRANOVA in December 2025 through its digital infrastructure strategy, and the current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2024-10: Actis completed its combination with General Atlantic.
2025-12-02: Actis launched TERRANOVA with a stated $1.5 billion investment commitment.
2026-01: TERRANOVA's first campus in Querétaro entered operation with initial IT capacity.

oneSentenceDescription: Valia Energia is the leading private power producer in Mexico, operating 3.2 GW of installed capacity across seven natural gas-fired combined-cycle plants in Tamaulipas, Coahuila, and the State of Mexico, plus a 58-kilometer gas pipeline.
profileSubsector: Independent power generation and gas transportation
principalLocationOfOperations: Tamaulipas, Coahuila, and State of Mexico
ownershipDetails: Firm: Actis | Fund: Actis Energy 5 | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Valia Energía is a Mexican independent power producer operating natural gas-fired combined-cycle generation assets and a related gas pipeline. Its customers are power purchasers in Mexico's electricity market and other users of reliable dispatchable generation rather than retail end users. The operating model is asset-heavy and infrastructure based, with cash flows tied to large-scale generating plants and associated fuel transportation assets. Actis and company materials state that the platform has 3.2 GW of installed capacity across seven plants and a 58-kilometer gas pipeline. Actis created the platform in 2022 through the acquisition of MT Falcon and expanded it in 2023 through the acquisition of EVM Energia; the current ownership percentage is not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2022-11: Actis completed the acquisition of MT Falcon and launched Valia Energía.
2023-07: Valia Energía acquired EVM Energia.
2023-10: Valia announced completion of EVM integration and a 3.2 GW operating platform.
2024-01: Valia completed a $530 million project bond financing.

ownershipVehicle: Ardian Americas Infrastructure Fund V (AAIF V)
oneSentenceDescription: MXT Holdings is a diversified telecommunications infrastructure platform in Mexico that develops, acquires, owns, and operates neutral-host communication infrastructure including cell towers and metro fiber.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ardian | Fund: Ardian Americas Infrastructure Fund V | Ownership Interest: 50%
Firm: Existing shareholders | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 50%
companyOverview: MXT Holdings develops, owns, and operates neutral-host telecommunications infrastructure in Mexico, including towers and metropolitan fiber assets. Its customers are mobile network operators and other communications users that require shared infrastructure rather than single-tenant proprietary networks. The operating model is asset-heavy and lease driven, with recurring revenue tied to long-lived telecom sites and network infrastructure. The platform is focused on Mexico and is positioned around neutral-host infrastructure that supports carrier densification and connectivity demand. Ardian announced in 2023 that it had acquired a 50% co-control equity interest in MXT Holdings, which it described as its first direct investment in Mexico.
historicalMilestones: 2015: MXT Holdings was founded.
2023-07: Ardian announced acquisition of a 50% co-control equity interest in MXT Holdings.
2023: Ardian described MXT as its first direct investment in Mexico.

Firm: Enel Green Power | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 20%
companyOverview: Enel Green Power Mexico develops and operates utility-scale renewable power assets in Mexico. Its end markets are wholesale power purchasers and contracted offtakers rather than retail electricity customers. The operating model is asset-heavy and project based, with cash flows tied to large solar and wind plants and related project structures. Public transaction materials state that CDPQ and CKD IM acquired an 80% stake in a holding company for approximately 1.7 GW of Mexican renewable projects while Enel retained the remaining interest. The CDPQ investment was announced in 2017, and Enel Green Power Mexico has continued to report operational and digitalization milestones for its renewable platform in the years since.

ownershipDetails: Firm: CDPQ | Fund: CDPQ Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: 49%
Firm: Empresas ICA | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 51%
companyOverview: ICA OVT is an operating platform for Mexican transport concessions, focused on toll road and tunnel assets. Its customers are passenger and freight users of concessioned road infrastructure rather than consumers purchasing transport services directly from a carrier. The operating model is asset-heavy and concession based, with revenues linked to toll collection and long-term operating rights. Public disclosures at formation stated that ICA transferred interests in four concession projects into the platform, including the Acapulco Tunnel, the Mayab Toll Road, the Rio Verde-Ciudad Valles Highway, and the La Piedad bypass. ICA and CDPQ completed the creation of ICA OVT in 2015 with a 51% and 49% ownership split, respectively.

2015: ICA transferred four operating transport concessions into ICA OVT.

2016: ICA public filings continued to describe the 51% ICA and 49% CDPQ ownership structure at formation.

principalLocationOfOperations: State of Mexico, Hidalgo, Tlaxcala, and Puebla
ownershipDetails: Firm: CPP Investments | Fund: Real Assets (Infrastructure) | Ownership Interest: 29%
Firm: Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 20%
Firm: IDEAL | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 51%
companyOverview: Arco Norte is a concession toll road that provides a bypass route around Greater Mexico City for freight and passenger traffic. Its customers are motorists and commercial transport users that require an alternative to more congested routes through the Mexico City metropolitan area. The operating model is asset-heavy and concession based, with cash flow generated primarily from toll revenue over the concession term. Public materials describe a 223-kilometer corridor linking major national highways and serving as a strategic logistics route in central Mexico. CPP Investments announced in 2016 that Arco Norte was its first infrastructure investment in Mexico, and subsequent partnership announcements indicated a 29% stake for CPPIB, 20% for Ontario Teachers', and the balance for IDEAL.
historicalMilestones: 2005: The federal government granted the concession for Arco Norte.
2009: The first phase of Arco Norte opened to traffic.
2011: The second phase to Atlacomulco opened.
2016-06-09: CPPIB announced its investment in Arco Norte as its first infrastructure investment in Mexico.
2018: Partnership disclosures stated that the ownership structure was consistent with Arco Norte at 29% CPPIB and 20% Ontario Teachers'.

portfolioCompany: IDEAL (Impulsora del Desarrollo y el Empleo en América Latina)
profileCompanyName: Impulsora del Desarrollo y el Empleo en América Latina, S.A.B. de C.V. (IDEAL)
ownershipDetails: Firm: CPP Investments | Fund: Real Assets (Infrastructure) | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Controlling shareholder and other investors | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: IDEAL is a Mexican infrastructure concessions platform with exposure to toll roads, logistics terminals, wastewater treatment plants, electronic toll collection, and operating services. Its end markets are users of transport and utility infrastructure and public-sector counterparties that rely on long-term concession-based assets. The operating model is asset-heavy and concession driven, with revenue generated from operating infrastructure assets and related services rather than pure development fees. CPP Investments and Ontario Teachers' described IDEAL in 2020 as a portfolio comprising 18 infrastructure concessions in Mexico, including 13 toll roads, three logistics terminals, and two wastewater treatment plants. CPP Investments and Ontario Teachers' completed an expansion of their Mexican infrastructure partnership through IDEAL in 2020, but the detailed current ownership split of IDEAL itself is not publicly disclosed in the source materials reviewed.
historicalMilestones: 2016: CPP Investments entered Mexican infrastructure through an IDEAL partnership linked to Arco Norte.
2018: CPP Investments and Ontario Teachers' expanded the partnership through Pacifico Sur.
2019-11: IDEAL, CPPIB, and Ontario Teachers' announced a broader Mexican infrastructure partnership expansion.
2020-04-16: CPP Investments and Ontario Teachers' announced completion of the expanded IDEAL partnership.

ownershipDetails: Firm: CPP Investments | Fund: Real Assets (Infrastructure) | Ownership Interest: 29%
Firm: Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 20%
Firm: IDEAL | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 51%
companyOverview: Pacifico Sur is a toll road concession connecting central and northwestern Mexico, including the corridor between Guadalajara and Tepic. Its customers are passenger vehicles and commercial transport operators using a strategic intercity highway link. The operating model is asset-heavy and concession based, with long-duration toll revenue supporting the asset rather than short-term project development fees. Public investment announcements describe a 309-kilometer toll road and related bypasses serving both freight and tourism traffic. CPP Investments announced its investment in Pacifico Sur in 2018 in partnership with IDEAL and Ontario Teachers', with a disclosed ownership structure of 29%, 51%, and 20%, respectively.
historicalMilestones: 2018-10-02: CPP Investments and Ontario Teachers' announced the Pacifico Sur toll road investment with IDEAL.
2018: CPPIB disclosed a 29% interest and Ontario Teachers' a 20% interest in the asset, with IDEAL retaining 51%.
2019-11: Partnership materials referenced Pacifico Sur as part of the broader Mexican infrastructure relationship with IDEAL.
2020-04: CPP Investments and Ontario Teachers' completed the broader IDEAL transaction while remaining partners in Pacifico Sur.

ownershipVehicle: CI GMF II / CI ETF I
ownershipDetails: Firm: Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners | Fund: CI Growth Markets Fund II / CI Energy Transition Fund I | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Helax Istmo is a development-stage project intended to produce green hydrogen and green maritime fuels in southern Mexico. Its target end markets are shipping and other industrial users seeking lower-carbon fuel supply rather than retail electricity customers. The operating model is asset-heavy and pre-development in nature, with value tied to renewable generation, fuel-processing infrastructure, port and logistics integration, and future contracted offtake. Public announcements describe the project as part of Mexico's Interoceanic Corridor strategy and note that it is located in Oaxaca and remains in pre-development. Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners announced the project in late 2023 through its Growth Markets Fund II and Energy Transition Fund I strategies, and the current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2023-12-22: CIP announced an MoU with Mexican authorities for the Helax Istmo green hydrogen project.
2024-05: CIP stated publicly that the project remained in pre-development.
2024: Industry coverage described Helax Istmo as part of the Interoceanic Corridor fuel and logistics strategy.
2024-12: Industry reports indicated a targeted operating timeframe around 2028, subject to development progress.

profileSubsector: Solar PV and battery storage development
ownershipDetails: Firm: Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners | Fund: CI Growth Markets Fund II | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Sunstone Power is a renewable development platform in Mexico focused on utility-scale solar and battery storage projects. Its end markets are grid offtakers and power purchasers that require new renewable generation and storage capacity rather than end-user retail services. The operating model is asset-heavy and development led, with project value dependent on permitting, interconnection, construction, and long-term contracted power sales. Recent sector reporting in Mexico identified Sunstone as the largest awarded private developer in a new permitting round, tied to projects in Campeche totaling more than 1 GW of solar capacity plus battery storage. The platform is backed by Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners through its Growth Markets strategy, and the current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2025: Sunstone Power was identified as a key CIP-backed renewable developer in Mexico.
2025-12: Sector reporting said Sunstone secured permits for the La Alegría and La Esperanza projects in Campeche.
2026: Reporting linked Sunstone's projects to a potential power purchase arrangement with CFE.
2028: Trade reporting has referenced expected operations timing around 2028 for Sunstone's Campeche projects, subject to construction progress.

profileSubsector: Wireless towers and edge digital infrastructure
ownershipDetails: Firm: DigitalBridge | Fund: DigitalBridge Equity | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Macquarie Asset Management | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Mexico Telecom Partners owns and operates wireless communications infrastructure in Mexico, with activities centered on towers and adjacent digital infrastructure. Its customers include major wireless carriers and other communications users that require shared infrastructure for coverage, densification, and network expansion. The operating model is asset-heavy and lease driven, supported by long-lived telecom sites and recurring tenancy revenue rather than consumer retail billing. DigitalBridge and company materials describe MTP as one of the largest private tower companies in Mexico, founded in 2014, with operations across the country and additional activity in edge data centers. MTP was created in 2014 with the backing of DigitalBridge and Macquarie, and the current ownership percentages between those shareholders are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2014: Mexico Telecom Partners was created from the merger of multiple companies.
2014: DigitalBridge and Macquarie were identified by the company as its shareholders at formation.
2023-06-19: MTP inaugurated a data center in Mexico City.

companyOverview: Red de Carreteras de Occidente operates concessioned toll roads and related mobility services in western and central Mexico. Its customers are passenger and freight users moving through one of the country's main industrial and logistics corridors. The operating model is asset-heavy and concession based, with cash flow generated from toll collection and associated road services over long asset lives. Recent company materials describe more than 1,100 kilometers of managed highways, while transaction materials at the time of the Abertis and GIC acquisition described the asset as one of Mexico's largest motorway operators. RCO was constituted in 2007, and Abertis and GIC completed the acquisition of 72.3% of the company in 2020, with GIC disclosed at 21% of the equity.

companyOverview: KIO Networks operates data centers and related digital infrastructure services for enterprise, cloud, and critical-industry customers. Its customers require colocation, interconnection, cloud-adjacent, and mission-critical digital infrastructure rather than retail telecom services. The operating model is asset-heavy and utilization driven, supported by owned data center campuses, carrier-neutral connectivity, and long-term customer contracts. I Squared Capital described KIO in 2021 as Mexico's largest data center operator with a broader regional footprint, and more recent company materials point to continued expansion in Querétaro and other markets. I Squared Capital completed its acquisition of KIO Networks in 2021, and the current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.

companyOverview: Aleatica develops and operates toll roads and other mobility infrastructure assets across Europe and Latin America. Its customers are road users and transport-system participants that depend on concessioned mobility infrastructure rather than passenger transport services sold directly by a carrier. The operating model is asset-heavy and concession based, with long-duration revenue linked to regulated or concession toll and mobility assets. IFM states that Aleatica's portfolio comprises 20 assets across seven countries and that the platform focuses exclusively on transportation infrastructure. IFM first invested in Aleatica in 2015 through a minority toll-road stake and, according to IFM's 2025 portfolio disclosures, now holds a 100% fund ownership stake.

oneSentenceDescription: Monterra Energy is a natural gas distribution and pipeline company operating in Mexico, providing gas transportation and distribution services to industrial, commercial, and residential customers.
ownershipDetails: Firm: KKR | Fund: KKR Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Monterra Energy is a Mexican natural gas transportation and distribution platform serving industrial, commercial, and other gas users. Its customer base is tied to essential fuel delivery for industrial operations and other end users that require physical gas infrastructure rather than merchant trading alone. The operating model is asset-heavy and midstream oriented, with value tied to pipeline and gas-delivery infrastructure. KKR has described the platform as part of its infrastructure activities in Mexico following the acquisition of Fermaca assets. The platform was established in 2018 after KKR acquired Fermaca natural gas assets in Mexico, and the current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.

2018: Monterra Energy began operating as a KKR-backed midstream business.

companyOverview: Supervía Poniente is an urban toll road in Mexico City linking the Santa Fe district with the Periférico corridor in the city's southwest. Its customers are passenger and commercial vehicle users that require faster road access into and out of one of Mexico City's main business districts. The operating model is asset-heavy and concession based, with toll revenue generated from a fixed transport corridor rather than from transport operations or rolling stock. The road comprises tunnels, elevated structures, and related urban highway infrastructure crossing complex topography in western Mexico City. The final section opened in 2013, and Macquarie Asset Management continues to identify Supervía as a portfolio transportation investment in Mexico; the current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.

Firm: Digital Realty | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 20%
companyOverview: This joint venture owns three stabilized hyperscale data center buildings in Northern Virginia. Its customers are investment-grade hyperscale users that require large-scale leased capacity in one of the deepest data center markets in the United States. The operating model is asset-heavy and leased, with Digital Realty continuing to manage day-to-day operations while the venture earns contracted data center cash flows. Digital Realty stated at signing that the portfolio represented about 104 MW of IT capacity. The venture was formed in July 2023 when TPG Real Estate agreed to acquire an 80% interest and Digital Realty retained a 20% interest.

companyOverview: Extenet designs, builds, owns, and operates distributed wireless and fiber connectivity infrastructure across North America. Its customers include wireless carriers, venue owners, enterprises, and public-sector users that require indoor and outdoor mobility infrastructure rather than retail broadband services. The operating model is asset-heavy and contract based, supported by small cells, distributed antenna systems, and associated fiber networks with long useful lives. Company materials describe the business as one of the largest privately held communications infrastructure providers in North America and list a headquarters in Frisco, Texas. Stonepeak and DigitalBridge led a recapitalization of ExteNet in 2015, Manulife announced a strategic investment in 2021, and the company rebranded as Extenet in 2022.

companyOverview: Cityside Networks provides fiber-to-the-premise broadband services to residential, commercial, and carrier customers in Southern California. Its customers are local households, businesses, and wholesale communications users that require fixed broadband and related connectivity services. The operating model is asset-heavy because the company is building and operating last-mile fiber networks in its markets. The business is geographically focused on Southern California and is positioned as a local fiber platform rather than a national reseller. Cityside announced a partnership with SDC Capital Partners in 2023, and the current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.

Its customer base is concentrated in residential and small-business markets that require fixed-line broadband and local communications services. The operating model is asset-heavy because the company owns and upgrades fiber access infrastructure across its operating territory. The platform is focused on rural and regional communities where broadband availability and reliability are key service differentiators. The FCC approved the indirect transfer of control of SLIC to an SDC acquisition vehicle in 2022, and the current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.

companyOverview: BIG Fiber builds and operates metro dark fiber infrastructure for data centers, hyperscalers, carriers, and enterprise customers. Its customers are network-intensive users that require high-capacity leased fiber rather than mass-market broadband service. The operating model is asset-heavy and lease based, with long-lived metro fiber infrastructure generating recurring network revenue. Company materials describe more than 320 route miles across the San Francisco Bay Area, Greater Atlanta, and Greater Portland markets. The platform was rebranded from Bandwidth IG to BIG Fiber in 2025, and the current ownership percentages held by SDC-managed funds are not publicly disclosed.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Manulife Investment Management | Fund: Manulife Infrastructure Fund I | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Duquesne Light is a regulated electric transmission and distribution utility serving homes and businesses in southwestern Pennsylvania. Its customer base is primarily regulated distribution customers in Allegheny and Beaver counties rather than merchant power buyers. The operating model is asset-heavy and regulated, with revenues linked to approved rates on poles, wires, substations, and related network infrastructure. Company materials state that the utility serves approximately 605,000 customers. Public materials tied to Three Rivers Utility Holdings state that Manulife Infrastructure Fund I participates in the ownership structure, but the detailed current shareholding attributable to Manulife is not publicly disclosed in the sources reviewed.

ownershipDetails: Firm: Manulife Investment Management | Fund: Manulife Infrastructure Fund II | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Landmark Properties develops, builds, and operates student housing and related residential communities in U.S. university markets. Its end markets are students, universities, and institutional capital partners seeking purpose-built residential infrastructure near campus demand centers. The operating model is asset-heavy and development oriented, with revenues linked to residential leasing and asset management rather than service-only contracts. Company materials state that Landmark has developed more than 120 residential projects totaling roughly 74,000 beds and over $15 billion of assets under management. Landmark was founded in 2004, and Manulife Investment Management announced a joint venture with the company in 2021 focused on student-housing development.
