The operating model is asset-heavy and site based, combining real estate, grid connections, charging equipment, and operational services into a long-duration infrastructure platform. Vision Ridge states that the company provides an end-to-end charging solution for fleet transportation and works with customers across vehicle classes in multiple U.S. markets. TeraWatt launched publicly in 2021 and announced a capital raise of more than $1 billion in 2022; the current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2021-05: TeraWatt launched publicly.
2022-09-13: TeraWatt announced a capital raise of more than $1 billion.
2025: Vision Ridge continued to list TeraWatt as an active investment in its sustainable real assets strategy.
2025: Vision Ridge board materials continued to reference TeraWatt as a fleet charging platform.

oneSentenceDescription: Swiss Life committed a minority equity stake in the JFK NTO project in July 2022.
profileCompanyName: The New Terminal One at JFK
ownershipDetails: Firm: Swiss Life Asset Managers | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: The New Terminal One is the privately financed redevelopment of JFK Airport's new international Terminal 1 on the airport's south side. Its end markets are international airlines, airport passengers, concession operators, and the Port Authority as the public-sector counterpart. The operating model is asset-heavy and concession based, with long-duration value creation tied to airport terminal development, financing, construction, and operations. Port Authority materials describe a 23-gate terminal with first gates planned to open in 2026 as part of a broader JFK modernization program. Swiss Life Asset Managers announced in 2022 that it had committed a minority equity stake in the project, and the detailed current ownership split among the sponsor consortium is not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2022-07-29: Swiss Life Asset Managers announced a minority equity commitment to the New Terminal One project.
2022-09: The New Terminal One project broke ground at JFK.
2025-04-14: The project announced the duty-free operator selection for the terminal.
2026: The first gates were scheduled to open as part of the initial operating phase.

oneSentenceDescription: Windmill Farms produces mushrooms with more than one million pounds of weekly output across facilities in Ontario and Washington, including North America's only fully enclosed organic composting facility.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Instar Asset Management | Fund: Instar Asset Management | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Windmill Farms produces mushrooms through controlled-environment agricultural operations serving retail and foodservice customers in Canada and the United States. Its customer base consists of large grocery and foodservice accounts that require year-round supply from scaled indoor agricultural facilities. The operating model is asset-heavy, built around vertically integrated composting and indoor growing infrastructure rather than open-field farming. Instar disclosed at acquisition that the company was headquartered in Ashburn, Ontario, produced approximately 27 million pounds of mushrooms per year, and operated North America's only fully enclosed composting facility in its category. Instar partnered with management to acquire Greenwood Mushrooms in 2022 and Windmill later acquired the assets of Ostrom Mushroom Farms in 2023; the current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2022-06: Instar partnered with management to acquire Greenwood Mushrooms Development Corp.
2022: Instar disclosed annual production of approximately 27 million pounds of mushrooms at acquisition.
2023-06: Windmill Farms acquired the assets of Ostrom Mushroom Farms.
2025: Instar continued to list Windmill Farms as a portfolio company based in Ashburn, Ontario.

oneSentenceDescription: LS Networks provides fiber bandwidth and connectivity in the Pacific Northwest serving 134+ communities and 750+ wireless towers.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Instar Asset Management | Fund: Instar Asset Management | Ownership Interest: 100%
companyOverview: LS Networks provides fiber bandwidth, middle-mile connectivity, and tower backhaul services across the Pacific Northwest. Its customers include carriers, enterprises, public institutions, and wireless infrastructure users that require regional transport connectivity rather than mass-market consumer broadband alone. The operating model is asset-heavy and lease and service based, supported by owned fiber routes and related network infrastructure. Company materials state that LS Networks serves more than 134 communities and over 750 wireless towers across Oregon, Washington, and Northern California. LS Networks was founded in 2005 by electric cooperatives and the Coquille Tribe, and Instar announced in 2020 that it had agreed to acquire 100% of the company.
historicalMilestones: 2005: LS Networks was founded.
2020-10: Instar announced an agreement to acquire 100% of LS Networks.
2021: LS Networks entered the Instar portfolio.
2025: Company materials continued to describe service to 134+ communities and 750+ towers.

oneSentenceDescription: Skyservice US represents the U.S.
profileSubsector: Fixed-base operations and business aviation services
ownershipDetails: Firm: Instar Asset Management | Fund: Instar Asset Management | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Skyservice US provides fixed-base operations, aircraft fueling, maintenance, hangar, and related business aviation services in the Pacific Northwest. Its customers are business aviation operators, aircraft owners, charter users, and other aviation clients requiring ground services and support infrastructure. The operating model is asset-heavy because the business relies on airport leases, hangars, fueling systems, maintenance capabilities, and service facilities across its network. Instar portfolio materials identify Skyservice and Skyservice US as a North American business aviation services platform, while other public materials note that the U.S. operations originated in Leading Edge Jet Center. Leading Edge Jet Center was founded in 2005, Instar added the U.S. platform to its portfolio before integration with Skyservice in 2019, and the detailed current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2005: Leading Edge Jet Center, the predecessor U.S. business, was founded.
2019: Instar disclosed that Skyservice integrated with Skyservice US, formerly Leading Edge Jet Center.
2021-11: Skyservice rebranded four U.S. FBO locations under the Skyservice name.
2025: Instar continued to identify Skyservice & Skyservice US as a North American business aviation platform.

oneSentenceDescription: Steel Reef captures, processes, and redistributes associated gas that would otherwise be flared, managing about 140 million cubic feet per day with C$1.3 billion invested.
profileSubsector: Associated gas capture and processing infrastructure
principalLocationOfOperations: Alberta, Saskatchewan, Manitoba, and North Dakota
ownershipDetails: Firm: Instar Asset Management | Fund: InstarAGF Essential Infrastructure Fund | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Steel Reef captures, processes, and redistributes associated gas that would otherwise be flared, using gathering, processing, and power infrastructure. Its customers include producers, power users, and other industrial and midstream counterparties that require gas takeaway or related low-carbon infrastructure solutions. The operating model is asset-heavy and midstream oriented, with value created through physical gas infrastructure and long-term operating assets rather than commodity trading alone. Company materials describe approximately 140 million cubic feet per day of managed gas and more than C$1.3 billion invested in the platform. Steel Reef was founded in 2012, Instar first invested in 2016, and Instar later disclosed expanded ownership through a Fund II investment in 2020; the detailed current ownership percentages are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 2012: Steel Reef was founded.
2016-04: Instar invested C$75 million in Steel Reef for a 20.6% equity stake.
2020-01: Instar disclosed expanded ownership through a Fund II investment.
2025: Instar continued to identify Steel Reef as an energy-transition platform in its portfolio materials.

oneSentenceDescription: CSV Midstream develops, owns, and operates natural gas and NGL processing infrastructure in Alberta's Montney region with six plants.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Northleaf | Fund: Northleaf Capital Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: CSV Midstream develops, owns, and operates natural gas and NGL processing, compression, and gathering infrastructure in Alberta's Montney region. Its customers are upstream producers that need sour and sweet gas processing, liquids handling, and related takeaway services. The business follows an asset-heavy operating model built around long-lived processing plants and associated field infrastructure, with public materials describing a six-plant platform across northwestern Alberta. Recent company disclosures cite assets including Resthaven, Karr, Pipestone South, Simonette, Valhalla, and the 150 MMcf/d Albright sour gas plant that entered service in 2025. Northleaf infrastructure funds acquired 100% of CSV Midstream in 2019 and supported the concurrent acquisition of the Karr facility, which expanded the company's processing footprint in the basin.
historicalMilestones: 2014: CSV Midstream was founded in Alberta.
Jun 28, 2019: Northleaf acquired 100% of CSV Midstream and announced a strategic investment partnership.
Aug 1, 2019: CSV completed the acquisition of the Karr 6-18 natural gas facility from Paramount Resources.
Oct 25, 2022: CSV announced final investment decision for the Albright sour gas processing plant.
Nov 10, 2025: CSV brought the Albright plant into operations.

oneSentenceDescription: GlidePath develops, owns, and operates standalone battery energy storage and advanced energy assets across the United States.
profileSubsector: Battery energy storage development and operation
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook | Fund: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: GlidePath Power Solutions develops, owns, and operates standalone battery energy storage projects across U.S. power markets. The platform serves utilities, grid operators, and other market participants by providing fast-response capacity and ancillary services that support reliability and renewable integration. Its model is asset-heavy, with projects developed, financed, and operated on a merchant or contracted basis depending on local market structure. Quinbrook describes GlidePath as a Chicago-based platform, and public disclosures tied to its 2018 wind acquisition cited a development pipeline exceeding 600 MW alongside broader distributed power activities. Quinbrook acquired GlidePath in 2017 through its low-carbon power strategy and has since used the platform as the core vehicle for its U.S. battery storage build-out.
historicalMilestones: 2017: Quinbrook acquired GlidePath.
Jan 18, 2018: GlidePath acquired a 223 MW operational wind portfolio from NextEra.
Feb 4, 2020: GlidePath advanced an 80 MW New York storage portfolio after receiving NYSERDA support.
Jan 25, 2022: GlidePath announced construction of the 50 MW Byrd Ranch storage project in Texas.

oneSentenceDescription: Meyersdale Energy Storage is an 18 MW / 12 MWh battery project in Somerset County, Pennsylvania, originally built in 2015 and repowered in late 2024 after GlidePath acquisition in 2019.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook | Fund: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Meyersdale Energy Storage is an operating standalone battery project in Somerset County, Pennsylvania. The facility participates in PJM, with Quinbrook describing it as an 18 MW battery asset that operates in the Reg-D market. Its business model is asset-heavy and merchant-exposed, deriving value from grid-balancing services rather than regulated utility earnings. As a single-site project, its geographic footprint is concentrated in Pennsylvania, but it sits within GlidePath's broader U.S. storage portfolio. The project was originally built in 2015, acquired by GlidePath in 2019 after Quinbrook had acquired the platform, and repowered in late 2024 to extend operating life.
historicalMilestones: 2015: The Meyersdale battery was originally placed in service.
2019: GlidePath acquired Meyersdale as an operating project.
Late 2024: GlidePath completed a repowering of the asset.

oneSentenceDescription: Gemini is a Nevada mega-project with 690 MWac solar and 380 MW / 1,416 MWh storage, delivering about 10% of Nevada's peak demand.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook | Fund: Quinbrook Valley of Fire Fund | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: APG | Fund: ABP | Ownership Interest: 49%
companyOverview: Gemini Solar + Storage is a utility-scale solar and battery project in Clark County, Nevada. The asset serves the Nevada power market under long-term contracted arrangements and follows an asset-heavy generation model pairing solar PV with battery storage to support peak demand and reliability. Public project disclosures describe Gemini as a 690 MWac solar facility with a 380 MW / 1,416 MWh battery system capable of supplying roughly 10% of Nevada's peak power demand. The project is concentrated in Nevada and is operated by Primergy within Quinbrook's broader U.S. clean energy portfolio. Quinbrook originated the project before launching Primergy in 2020, closed $1.9 billion of debt and tax equity financing in 2022, sold a 49% equity stake to APG on behalf of ABP in 2022, and reached commercial operations in 2024.
historicalMilestones: Jun 25, 2019: Quinbrook announced the landmark solar-plus-storage development that became Gemini.
May 18, 2020: Quinbrook launched Primergy Solar to develop large-scale solar and storage projects in North America.
Jul 1, 2022: Quinbrook and Primergy closed $1.9 billion of debt and tax equity financing for Gemini.
Oct 21, 2022: APG agreed to acquire a 49% equity stake in Gemini on behalf of ABP.
Jul 18, 2024: Gemini reached commercial operations.

oneSentenceDescription: Ash Creek Solar is a 408 MWac project in Hill County, Texas with a long-term PPA with Microsoft.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook | Fund: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Ash Creek Solar is a utility-scale photovoltaic project in Hill County, Texas. The facility serves the Texas power market under a long-term power purchase agreement with Microsoft and operates under an asset-heavy contracted generation model. Primergy describes Ash Creek as a 408 MWac project, placing it among the larger single-site solar assets now entering service in Texas. The asset is concentrated in Texas and forms part of Quinbrook's U.S. power portfolio through Primergy. Primergy acquired the project from Orion Renewable Energy Group in 2021, closed $588 million of project financing and a Microsoft PPA in 2024, secured additional tax equity in 2024, and reached commercial operation in 2025.
historicalMilestones: 2021: Primergy acquired the Ash Creek project from Orion Renewable Energy Group.
Mar 12, 2024: Primergy closed $588 million of project financing and a long-term PPA with Microsoft.
Oct 18, 2024: Primergy secured an additional $350 million of tax equity financing for Ash Creek.
Jul 17, 2025: Ash Creek reached commercial operation.

oneSentenceDescription: Prairie Mist Solar is a 100 MWac project in Ashley County, Arkansas with Amazon as offtaker.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook | Fund: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Prairie Mist Solar is a utility-scale solar project in Ashley County, Arkansas. The project serves the regional grid under a long-term offtake arrangement with Amazon and follows an asset-heavy contracted generation model. Primergy identifies Prairie Mist as a 100 MWac facility, and public operating announcements state that the project created approximately 200 construction jobs and is expected to contribute about $10 million of tax benefits to Ashley County over its operating life. The asset is concentrated in Arkansas and sits within Quinbrook's U.S. generation portfolio through Primergy. Primergy closed $300 million of debt and tax equity financing in 2023 and announced commercial operation in 2024, while the timing of Quinbrook's initial project acquisition has not been publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: Nov 29, 2023: Primergy closed $300 million of debt and tax equity financing for Prairie Mist.
2024: Primergy reported that Prairie Mist reached commercial operation.
Dec 18, 2024: Primergy announced the project was fully operational and delivering solar energy to the grid.

oneSentenceDescription: Middlebury Resource Recovery Center is a Vermont anaerobic digestion facility processing about 80,000 gallons per day of liquid organic waste and exporting renewable electricity via a 1.014 MW genset.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook | Fund: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Middlebury Resource Recovery Center is an anaerobic digestion and waste-to-energy facility in Middlebury, Vermont. The project processes food and beverage residuals and wastewater from regional industrial customers and converts those streams into renewable electricity and treated effluent. Its model is asset-heavy and contracted around essential waste-management and utility functions rather than pure merchant power sales. Public project materials describe a 1.014 MW grid-connected generation unit and note that the facility had been in development for around six years before construction began in late 2022. Quinbrook acquired PurposeEnergy in 2023 and now owns the project through that platform, which opened the Middlebury facility in 2024.
historicalMilestones: Dec 16, 2022: PurposeEnergy held a groundbreaking for the Middlebury project.
Apr 12, 2023: Quinbrook acquired PurposeEnergy, the platform that owns and develops Middlebury.
Oct 24, 2024: PurposeEnergy held the grand opening for the Middlebury facility.

oneSentenceDescription: PurposeEnergy Minster is an anaerobic digestion project in Minster, Ohio converting organic waste into biogas for a co-located dairy processor.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook | Fund: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: PurposeEnergy Minster is a biogas project in Minster, Ohio designed to convert industrial organic waste into fuel for nearby industrial use. The asset serves food and beverage processing customers in west-central Ohio and is being developed as part of PurposeEnergy's waste-to-energy platform. Its operating model is asset-heavy and based on anaerobic digestion infrastructure integrated with host industrial operations rather than a purely merchant generation profile. Public materials identify the project as part of the Quinbrook-backed PurposeEnergy platform, which has developed and operated food-and-beverage waste treatment projects for more than 15 years. The timing of the project's original development is not publicly disclosed, but Quinbrook became the current sponsor in 2023 through its acquisition of PurposeEnergy.
historicalMilestones: Apr 12, 2023: Quinbrook acquired PurposeEnergy.
2023: Quinbrook identified Minster as part of the acquired PurposeEnergy project portfolio.
2025: Local reporting noted that PurposeEnergy had been working for several years on the Minster biodigester development.

oneSentenceDescription: PurposeEnergy South Burlington processes brewery waste at Zero Gravity Brewery and generates renewable electricity under a net-metering arrangement.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook | Fund: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: PurposeEnergy South Burlington is an operating anaerobic digestion facility in South Burlington, Vermont. The project processes brewery waste and wastewater, with Zero Gravity Craft Brewery serving as the anchor tenant, and converts those streams into renewable electricity. It is an asset-heavy operating facility integrated with industrial waste handling rather than a pure merchant generation asset. Public case-study materials tied to the original brewery installation describe a 492,000-gallon anaerobic digester, giving the site meaningful scale for a single-customer industrial biogas system. Quinbrook became the current sponsor in 2023 through its acquisition of PurposeEnergy, while the South Burlington facility itself predates that transaction and has been operating since 2010 based on public waste-to-energy case studies.
historicalMilestones: Mar 2008: Magic Hat and PurposeEnergy executed the development agreement for the brewery digester project.
2010: The South Burlington digester began operating at the brewery site.
Apr 12, 2023: Quinbrook acquired PurposeEnergy and became the current sponsor of the South Burlington asset.

oneSentenceDescription: SAINT is a Vermont anaerobic digestion facility co-located with Ben & Jerry's that began exporting electricity in December 2024.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook | Fund: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: St. Albans Industrial Treatment, or SAINT, is a Vermont biogas facility designed to process food and beverage waste and convert it into renewable electricity and resource-recovery outputs. The site serves industrial customers in and around St. Albans, with Ben & Jerry's and related manufacturing activity identified in public project announcements as key counterparties. Its model is asset-heavy, pairing industrial waste treatment with energy recovery rather than relying on a purely merchant power strategy. The project is concentrated in Vermont and reached its first electricity exports in late 2024 before holding a formal grand opening in 2025. Quinbrook became the current sponsor in 2023 through its acquisition of PurposeEnergy, which developed SAINT as part of its Northeast biogas platform.
historicalMilestones: Apr 12, 2023: Quinbrook acquired PurposeEnergy, the platform developing SAINT.
May 23, 2023: PurposeEnergy announced plans to build the SAINT biogas facility with Ben & Jerry's and Unilever.
Dec 2024: SAINT began exporting renewable electricity.
May 29, 2025: PurposeEnergy held the SAINT grand opening.

oneSentenceDescription: Rowan Bauxite is a 626 MW hyperscale data center campus planned in Frederick, Maryland across three sites.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook | Fund: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Rowan Bauxite is a hyperscale data center campus in Frederick County, Maryland. The project is being developed for large cloud and technology customers through an asset-heavy, build-to-suit model that integrates land, power, water, and digital infrastructure. Public materials describe three Bauxite projects at the Quantum Loophole campus with more than 600 MW of aggregate capacity, with Bauxite I delivering an initial 200+ MW. Operations are concentrated in Maryland within Rowan's broader U.S. data center platform. Rowan was founded in 2020 and is backed by Quinbrook, and the Bauxite campus advanced materially after site approval in 2024 and large construction financings for its first phases in 2024 and 2025.
historicalMilestones: Nov 2020: Rowan Digital Infrastructure was founded. (Founding)
Jan 10 2024: Bauxite received site development plan approval in Frederick County. (Development)
2024: Rowan secured $975 million of financing for Bauxite I. (Financing)
2025: Rowan secured $1.24 billion of financing for Bauxite II. (Financing)

oneSentenceDescription: Rowan Cinco is a 220-acre San Antonio data-center campus with 700 MW of secured power capacity.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook | Fund: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Rowan Cinco is a hyperscale data center campus in Medina County outside San Antonio, Texas. The project is being developed for large enterprise and hyperscale customers through an asset-heavy, build-to-suit operating model that combines powered land, campus infrastructure, and long-term customer deployments. Public disclosures describe Cinco as a 300 MW project representing at least $900 million of direct investment in the local community. Its footprint is concentrated in South Texas within Rowan's national development portfolio. Rowan was founded in 2020 and is Quinbrook-backed, with construction on Cinco beginning in 2025 and a $550 million construction financing closing in 2026.
historicalMilestones: Nov 2020: Rowan Digital Infrastructure was founded. (Founding)
Aug 7 2025: Rowan announced the start of construction on the 300 MW Cinco campus. (Development)
Sep 2025: Groundbreaking took place for the first phase of Cinco. (Construction)
Feb 11 2026: Rowan closed approximately $550 million of construction financing for Cinco. (Financing)

oneSentenceDescription: Rowan Temple is Quinbrook's Austin-region hyperscale campus with 1.6 GW of secured power capacity expected online between 2026 and 2030.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Quinbrook | Fund: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Rowan Temple is a hyperscale data center development in Temple, Texas. The project targets large-scale data center users through an asset-heavy, build-to-suit model centered on powered land and supporting campus infrastructure. Rowan's project materials describe Temple as a 300 MW development on roughly 700 acres, with more than $700 million of planned investment. The asset is concentrated in Central Texas and forms part of Rowan's broader U.S. data center pipeline. Rowan was founded in 2020 and is backed by Quinbrook, while UPP completed a co-investment in the Rowan platform in 2024 and construction at Temple began in 2026.
historicalMilestones: Nov 2020: Rowan Digital Infrastructure was founded. (Founding)
Aug 13 2024: UPP completed a co-investment in Rowan Digital Infrastructure. (Financing)
Jan 5 2026: Rowan announced construction had begun on the 300 MW Temple project. (Construction)

oneSentenceDescription: Arco Norte is a 224 km toll road in central Mexico co-owned with IDEAL (51%) and CPP Investments (29%), with OTPP at 20%.
ownershipDetails: Firm: IDEAL | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 51%
Firm: CPP Investments | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 29%
Firm: Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan | Fund: Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan | Ownership Interest: 20%
companyOverview: Arco Norte is a 224-kilometer toll road concession that serves as a transportation link across central Mexico. The road carries passenger and freight traffic around the Mexico City metropolitan area, with revenues generated from toll-paying users rather than from regulated utility tariffs. Its model is asset-heavy and concession-based, with long-lived roadway infrastructure, traffic operations, and maintenance obligations concentrated in central Mexico. Public owner disclosures show that IDEAL, CPP Investments, and Ontario Teachers' have owned the road together since 2016, following the prior phased opening of the concession in 2009 and 2011. Ontario Teachers' current interest is held through its infrastructure portfolio, while detailed fund-level ownership beyond the disclosed consortium stakes is not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: Jul 2009: The first phase of Arco Norte opened to traffic. (Operations)
May 3 2011: The toll road reached full operation. (Operations)
Jun 9 2016: IDEAL, CPP Investments, and Ontario Teachers’ announced the acquisition of Arco Norte. (Acquisition)
Oct 2 2018: The same consortium expanded its Mexican toll-road partnership through the Pacifico Sur transaction. (Strategic expansion)

oneSentenceDescription: Skyway Concession Company manages, operates, and maintains the Chicago Skyway toll road under a concession running through 2104.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Atlas Arteria | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: 66.67%
Firm: Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan | Fund: Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: OMERS Infrastructure | Fund: OMERS Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Skyway Concession Company operates the Chicago Skyway toll bridge and roadway linking Chicago with the Indiana Toll Road. The asset serves passenger vehicles, commercial traffic, and regional freight flows, with revenue derived from toll collections under a long-term concession structure. Its business model is asset-heavy and concession-based, with operations and maintenance responsibilities concentrated on a 7.8-mile corridor in Illinois. Public infrastructure profiles show that SCC assumed operations in 2005 under a 99-year lease, and the asset was acquired by a Canadian pension-fund consortium in 2016. Ontario Teachers' remains an investor in the asset, although detailed current fund-level ownership allocations for the retained interest are not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: Jan 26 2005: Skyway Concession Company assumed operations under the 99-year lease. (Concession)
Feb 2016: Ontario Teachers’ and other Canadian pension investors acquired the Chicago Skyway concession. (Acquisition)
2022: Atlas Arteria acquired a 66.67% stake while the Canadian consortium retained the balance. (Ownership change)

oneSentenceDescription: AES Clean Energy develops, owns, and operates utility-scale solar, wind, and storage with 2.5 GW of operating assets at formation and a 12 GW development pipeline.
ownershipDetails: Firm: AIMCo | Fund: AIMCo | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: AES Clean Energy develops, owns, and operates renewable power and storage assets across the United States. The platform serves utilities, corporate offtakers, and wholesale power markets through a mix of contracted solar, wind, and battery projects. Its model is asset-heavy, combining project development, construction, ownership, and operations across a large national pipeline. AIMCo describes the business as having approximately 7.8 GW of operating or in-construction projects, reflecting material scale in U.S. renewables. The platform was formed in 2020 through the combination of sPower and AES' U.S. clean energy business with AIMCo as the investment partner, and it expanded further through AES' acquisition of Community Energy in 2021.
historicalMilestones: Nov 17 2020: AES and AIMCo announced the formation of the U.S. renewables platform. (Formation)
Dec 20 2021: AES acquired Community Energy Solar, adding development pipeline and team capacity. (Acquisition)
Apr 16 2025: AES Clean Energy secured funding to support construction of 2 GW of projects in the United States. (Financing)

oneSentenceDescription: Puget Energy is the holding company for Puget Sound Energy, Washington's regulated utility.
ownershipDetails: Firm: AIMCo | Fund: AIMCo | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Puget Sound Energy is a regulated electric and natural gas utility serving customers in Washington State. The company serves residential, commercial, and industrial customers through a rate-regulated model rather than a merchant infrastructure model. Its operations are asset-heavy and geographically concentrated in western Washington, with public 2025 fast facts citing 1.26 million electric customers, nearly 900,000 natural gas customers, about 27,000 miles of electric lines, 27,000 miles of gas pipelines, and 7.89 GW of owned and contracted generating capacity. AIMCo has been part of the ownership group since the 2009 take-private transaction and increased its stake in 2018 when Macquarie Infrastructure Partners exited. Current ownership is held by a pension-fund consortium, while the detailed percentage attributable to AIMCo is not publicly disclosed in current company materials.
historicalMilestones: Feb 6 2009: Puget Holdings completed the take-private acquisition of Puget Energy. (Acquisition)
Aug 8 2018: AIMCo increased its stake as the consortium acquired Macquarie Infrastructure Partners’ 44% interest. (Ownership change)
2025: PSE disclosed year-end fast facts including 1.26 million electric customers and nearly 900,000 gas customers. (Scale disclosure)

oneSentenceDescription: Boldyn Networks provides neutral-host wireless and fiber infrastructure for transit systems, venues, campuses, and carriers.
profileSubsector: Neutral-host wireless, DAS, private networks, Wi-Fi, and fiber
ownershipDetails: Firm: AIMCo | Fund: AIMCo | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Boldyn Networks provides neutral-host wireless infrastructure, distributed antenna systems, private networks, Wi-Fi, and fiber connectivity solutions. Its customers include transit systems, venues, enterprises, carriers, and smart-city counterparties that require shared or managed communications infrastructure. The business follows an asset-heavy digital infrastructure model, with long-lived network assets deployed under multiyear commercial arrangements rather than pure retail telecom pricing. Boldyn operates internationally, and company materials describe it as one of the largest neutral-host providers globally. The Boldyn brand officially launched in 2023, and AIMCo disclosed in 2024 that it had increased its commitment to the company alongside CPP Investments and Manulife Investment Management.
historicalMilestones: Jun 28 2023: Boldyn Networks officially launched its new global brand. (Rebranding)
Mar 6 2024: CPP Investments, AIMCo, and Manulife IM announced additional investment to support U.S. growth. (Financing)
2024: The consortium-backed company supported the acquisition of Apogee Telecom to expand in higher education connectivity. (Acquisition)

oneSentenceDescription: ACES Delta is developing a green hydrogen hub in Delta, Utah with 220 MW of electrolyzers.
profileSubsector: Green hydrogen production and long-duration hydrogen storage
ownershipDetails: Firm: AIMCo | Fund: Haddington ESP I, LP | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: ACES Delta is developing a green hydrogen production and long-duration storage hub in Delta, Utah. The project is designed to serve power and industrial users in the western United States by producing hydrogen via electrolysis and storing it in large underground salt caverns. Its model is asset-heavy, combining generation, storage, and related infrastructure rather than relying on a light-capex services model. U.S. Department of Energy materials describe Phase 1 as a 220 MW electrolysis facility paired with two 4.5 million-barrel caverns providing more than 300 GWh of hydrogen storage. The operating project is a joint venture between Chevron and Mitsubishi Power, while AIMCo publicly disclosed its exposure through Haddington ESP I in 2022.
historicalMilestones: 2022: AIMCo disclosed its investment in Haddington ESP I tied to ACES Delta. (Investment)
Jun 2022: The U.S. Department of Energy issued a $504.4 million loan guarantee for ACES Delta. (Financing)
2025: ACES Delta continued development as a Chevron and Mitsubishi Power joint venture with Chevron as majority owner. (Ownership disclosure)

oneSentenceDescription: Velocity FBO Network operates fixed-base operator locations in Michigan, Florida, Georgia, Arizona, and Louisiana.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Tallvine | Fund: Tallvine Partners | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Velocity FBO Network is a fixed-base operator platform providing fueling, hangar, ground handling, and related aviation services at general aviation airports in the United States. Its customer base includes private aviation, corporate operators, cargo users, and transient aircraft movements that require on-airport service infrastructure. The business combines an asset-heavy airport-services footprint with recurring operating revenue from aviation activity at each location. Public company materials show a multi-state network including locations such as Kissimmee, Detroit, Saint Simons Island, Lake Havasu, and Baton Rouge. Tallvine launched the platform in early 2025 through the Odyssey Aviation North America assets, rebranded it as Velocity in June 2025, and expanded the network through subsequent airport acquisitions.
historicalMilestones: Jan 30 2025: Tallvine launched its U.S. FBO platform using Odyssey Aviation North America assets. (Founding)
Jun 12 2025: The platform rebranded as Velocity FBO Network. (Rebranding)
Oct 1 2025: Velocity added the Lake Havasu FBO location. (Acquisition)
Feb 13 2026: Velocity added the Baton Rouge FBO location. (Acquisition)

oneSentenceDescription: Generate Capital invests in, owns, operates, and finances distributed infrastructure with 2,000+ operating projects and 40+ asset creation partners.
ownershipDetails: Firm: QIC | Fund: QIC | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Generate Capital is a sustainable infrastructure investment and operating platform focused on distributed assets. The company works with technology and project developers across power, transportation, water, waste, agriculture, and digital infrastructure end markets. Its model combines capital provision with direct ownership and operation of infrastructure assets, making it more asset-heavy than a pure advisory or financing platform. Public materials from Generate and QIC describe a platform with more than 2,000 operating projects and a network of more than 40 asset-creation partners. Generate was founded in 2014, and QIC remains a disclosed investor in the platform, including participation in the company's 2024 equity raise.
historicalMilestones: Dec 4 2014: Generate Capital launched as a dedicated resource-efficiency finance company. (Founding)
Nov 16 2021: Generate converted to a public benefit corporation. (Strategic change)
Jan 31 2024: Generate announced a $1.5 billion capital raise with new and returning institutional investors including QIC. (Financing)

oneSentenceDescription: Beanfield owns and operates a 4,000-kilometre fiber network in Toronto, Montreal, and Vancouver.
profileSubsector: Metro fiber broadband and enterprise fiber
ownershipDetails: Firm: OMERS | Fund: OMERS Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: DigitalBridge | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Beanfield Technologies owns and operates metro fiber broadband and enterprise connectivity infrastructure in Canada. The company serves commercial buildings, residential developments, and enterprise customers with fiber-based internet, transport, and related telecom services. Its model is asset-heavy and facilities-based, with the company building, owning, and maintaining its own network rather than relying mainly on resale arrangements. Public disclosures tied to OMERS' investment state that Beanfield's network spans about 4,000 kilometers and serves roughly 4,000 commercial buildings and 170,000 residential units, primarily in Toronto, Vancouver, and Montreal. Beanfield was founded in 1988, DigitalBridge acquired the business in 2019, and OMERS Infrastructure made a strategic investment in the parent entity in 2023.
historicalMilestones: 1988: Beanfield was founded in Toronto. (Founding)
Nov 12 2019: Digital Colony acquired Beanfield Technologies. (Acquisition)
Jul 17 2023: OMERS Infrastructure announced a strategic investment in Beanfield. (Financing)

oneSentenceDescription: 55 Hereford Street is a Tier III data center in Brampton, Ontario with 127,000 square feet and expansion plans from 10 MVA to 27 MVA.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Grain | Fund: Grain Management | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: StratCap | Fund: Not publicly disclosed. | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: 55 Hereford Street is a colocation data center asset in Brampton, Ontario serving the Greater Toronto market. The facility targets enterprise, cloud, and AI-oriented data center demand through a colocation model rather than through telecommunications retail subscriptions. Its operating model is asset-heavy, with public marketing materials describing a 215,100 square foot Tier III facility capable of supporting more than 130 kW per rack. The site is concentrated in the Toronto-area data center market and is positioned near 151 Front Street West, Canada's largest carrier hotel and interconnection hub. Grain acquired a majority stake in the property in partnership with StratCap in 2023, and subsequent public materials describe ongoing expansion of the site's power and capacity profile.
historicalMilestones: Jun 15 2023: Grain acquired a majority stake in 55 Hereford Street in partnership with StratCap. (Acquisition)
2025: Public site materials described the facility at 215,100 square feet and high-density liquid-cooling capability. (Expansion disclosure)

oneSentenceDescription: Montreal University Hospital Research Center is a 70,000 square meter P3 research facility accommodating 1,700+ people.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Meridiam | Fund: Meridiam | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: CRCHUM is a hospital research facility affiliated with the Université de Montréal and the CHUM health system. The center supports basic, clinical, and population-health research rather than a commercial end market, making it a social infrastructure asset with institutional users and long-term public-sector relevance. Its model is asset-heavy and facility-based, with public materials describing a 70,000-square-meter building that hosts more than 1,700 people, including around 450 researchers and more than 400 graduate students and postdoctoral fellows. Operations are concentrated in Montréal within Quebec's academic and healthcare research ecosystem. CRCHUM was founded in 1996, construction of the current facility began in 2010, the building opened in 2013, and Meridiam is the long-term infrastructure investor associated with the project.
historicalMilestones: 1996: CRCHUM was founded. (Founding)
Mar 2010: Construction of the current CRCHUM building began. (Construction)
2013: The CRCHUM facility opened in Montréal. (Operations)

oneSentenceDescription: 13.3-mile managed-lanes concession on IH-820 and SH 121/183 in Tarrant County, Texas.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Meridiam | Fund: Meridiam | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: North Tarrant Express Segments 1 and 2 is a managed-lanes highway concession in the Dallas-Fort Worth area. The asset serves daily commuter and freight traffic through a toll-based transportation model, with revenues linked to lane usage under a long-term concession structure. Its operating model is asset-heavy and corridor-specific, covering major portions of I-820 and SH 121/183 with ongoing maintenance and operational obligations. Public project profiles describe approximately 13 miles of reconstructed and expanded roadway with new managed toll lanes in Northeast Tarrant County. Meridiam is part of the long-term investor group associated with the concession, which was awarded in 2009 and reached substantial completion in 2014.
historicalMilestones: Jun 23 2009: The concession for North Tarrant Express Segments 1 and 2 reached commercial close. (Concession)
2010: Major design and construction work began on the corridor. (Construction)
Oct 2014: Phase I opened and reached substantial completion. (Operations)

oneSentenceDescription: 10-mile I-70 Denver DBFOM adding express lanes, replacing a viaduct, and creating a 4-acre cover park.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Meridiam | Fund: Meridiam | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Central 70 is a highway DBFOM concession covering a major stretch of Interstate 70 in Denver, Colorado. The project serves passenger and freight traffic through a long-term transportation concession that includes express toll lanes and supporting corridor improvements. Its model is asset-heavy, combining reconstruction, widening, operations, and lifecycle maintenance across a 10-mile urban interstate segment. Public project materials describe the removal of the legacy viaduct, the lowering of the interstate, and the addition of a four-acre park over part of the corridor. Meridiam is the long-term infrastructure investor associated with the project, which reached financial close in 2017, entered active construction in 2018, and achieved completion in 2023.
historicalMilestones: 2017: Central 70 reached financial close. (Financing)
Summer 2018: Full construction activity began on the corridor. (Construction)
2022: The project held major ribbon-cutting milestones as key sections opened. (Operations)
2023: Central 70 reached completion. (Completion)

oneSentenceDescription: 22.5-mile managed-lanes and corridor improvements on I-66 in Northern Virginia.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Meridiam | Fund: Meridiam | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Transform 66 Outside the Beltway is a managed-lanes highway concession on Interstate 66 in Northern Virginia. The asset serves commuters, freight movement, transit users, and rideshare traffic through a toll-based corridor model with express lanes and multimodal improvements. Its operating model is asset-heavy and long-duration, covering the design, construction, financing, operations, and maintenance of 22.5 miles of highway infrastructure. Public project materials also cite park-and-ride expansion, new transit service support, interchange upgrades, and a shared-use trail as part of the corridor scope. Meridiam is one of the long-term investors associated with the project, which began construction in late 2017 and opened the final section of new express lanes in November 2022.
historicalMilestones: 2017: Transform 66 Outside the Beltway reached financial close and entered construction. (Concession)
Late 2017: Construction began on the corridor improvements. (Construction)
Nov 29 2022: The final section of the new express lanes opened. (Operations)

oneSentenceDescription: 13.4-mile managed-lanes concession on IH-635 in Dallas County under a 52-year DBFOM.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Meridiam | Fund: Meridiam | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: LBJ Express is a managed-lanes highway concession on Interstate 635 in the Dallas-Fort Worth market. The asset serves one of the region's most heavily traveled commuter and freight corridors through toll-based managed lanes under a long-term concession structure. Its business model is asset-heavy, involving reconstruction, operations, and maintenance within the existing corridor footprint. Meridiam's asset page notes average daily traffic of more than 250,000 vehicles on the rebuilt route, indicating the corridor's importance and usage scale. Meridiam is a long-term investor in the project, which reached financial close in 2010, opened in 2015, and was refinanced in 2020.
historicalMilestones: Sep 4 2009: The concession reached commercial close. (Concession)
Jun 2010: The project achieved financial close. (Financing)
Jan 2011: Major construction work began. (Construction)
Sep 15 2015: LBJ Express opened to traffic. (Operations)
Sep 25 2020: The project was refinanced. (Financing)

oneSentenceDescription: 27 km six- to eight-lane highway completing Edmonton's ring road.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Meridiam | Fund: Meridiam | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Northeast Anthony Henday Drive is an availability-based highway concession completing the ring road around Edmonton, Alberta. The project serves commuter, commercial, and regional freight traffic through a long-term public-private partnership rather than through direct tolling. Its operating model is asset-heavy, covering the design, build, finance, operation, and maintenance of a 27-kilometer six- to eight-lane divided highway. The concession is concentrated in Alberta and has been in operation since 2016. Meridiam originally held a 50% stake and increased its interest to 100% in 2019 through the acquisition of its partners' shares.
historicalMilestones: May 8 2012: The DBFO agreement for NEAH was executed. (Concession)
2012: Construction work commenced on the corridor. (Construction)
2016: Northeast Anthony Henday Drive entered operation. (Operations)
Jul 1 2019: Meridiam increased its stake from 50% to 100%. (Ownership change)

oneSentenceDescription: Temperature-controlled storage in Las Cruces and Santa Teresa, New Mexico serving Southern New Mexico, West Texas, and Northern Mexico.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Ridgewood | Fund: Ridgewood Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: Valley Cold Storage & Transportation provides temperature-controlled warehousing and related logistics services in southern New Mexico. Its customers include food, agriculture, food-service, and distribution businesses that need short- and long-term refrigerated storage near the U.S.-Mexico border. The business follows an asset-heavy operating model built around refrigerated warehouse infrastructure and associated transportation support rather than a brokered logistics model. Public company materials describe two strategically located facilities along the I-10 and I-25 corridors serving southern New Mexico, northern Mexico, and west Texas. Valley Cold's website identifies 2020 as the year of the current operating platform's launch, and Ridgewood Infrastructure acquired the company in 2024.
historicalMilestones: 2020: Valley Cold’s website identifies the operating business in its current form. (Founding)
Dec 3 2024: Ridgewood Infrastructure announced its acquisition of Valley Cold. (Acquisition)
2024: Public materials described two operating locations in Las Cruces and Santa Teresa. (Scale disclosure)

oneSentenceDescription: SeaCube has 2.4+ million CEUs, $4 billion+ in assets, 200+ depots worldwide, with 70% refrigerated containers.
ownershipDetails: Firm: Wren House | Fund: Wren House Infrastructure | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
Firm: Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan | Fund: Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan | Ownership Interest: Not publicly disclosed.
companyOverview: SeaCube Container Leasing buys, leases, manages, and sells intermodal shipping equipment used in global trade. Its customer base includes shipping lines, logistics providers, and government users that need refrigerated and dry containers for ocean, rail, and road transport. The business is highly asset-heavy, with a large owned and managed fleet rather than an asset-light brokerage model. Current company materials cite approximately 2.4 million CEUs in service, more than $4 billion of assets, over 200 depots worldwide, and a fleet that is about 70% refrigerated containers. Ontario Teachers' took SeaCube private in 2013, and Wren House completed the acquisition of an equity stake in 2023, with current detailed percentage ownership not publicly disclosed.
historicalMilestones: 1993: SeaCube traces its operating history to 1993. (Founding)
Jan 2013: Ontario Teachers’ agreed to acquire 100% of SeaCube and take it private. (Acquisition)
Oct 4 2023: Ontario Teachers’ announced an agreement to sell an equity stake to Wren House. (Ownership change)
Dec 5 2023: Wren House completed the acquisition of its equity stake in SeaCube. (Ownership change)
